instructions: 1. In a bowl mix together all the ground spices – red chilli powder, turmeric, garam masala, coriander powder, cumin powder, black pepper & fennel powder. Keep aside. 2. Add chicken and ½ tablespoon ginger garlic to a mixing bowl. Sprinkle ½ to ¾ teaspoon salt and half of the ground spices we just combined. Pour lemon juice. 3. Mix together to marinate the chicken. Cover and set aside until used later in the recipe or overnight in the refrigerator. 4. Meanwhile slit the green chilli. Chop onions & begin to cook from the next step. While you sauté the onions deseed & chop tomatoes. Grate ginger & garlic if you do not have the paste. This saves some time. 5. Heat oil in a wok or a skillet/ pan on a medium flame. 6. Add onions and green chilies. Saute on a medium high heat for 6 to 7 minutes and then on a medium low until golden brown but not burnt. 7. Add ginger garlic paste and saute for a minute until the raw smell has gone. 8. Stir in the rest of the ground spices and saute for a minute. 9. Stir in the tomatoes and salt. Cook until they dry out, blend well with onions & begin to release oil. 10. Take 2 tbsps of the hot onion tomato masala and add it to the whisked yogurt. Mix well until well combined. 11. Reduce the heat and slowly add this to the pan and saute until the yogurt cooks down and the bhuna masala turns thick. 12. Meanwhile (while bhuna masala cook) in another pan, heat 1 tablespoon ghee. 13. Add the red chilies and let them fry for 30 to 40 seconds. 14. Add all the whole spices – bay leaf, black cardamoms, green cardamoms, cloves, cinnamon & dried red chilies. (Be careful as the spices splutter) 15. Transfer the chicken. Fry on a medium high heat for 2 to 3 mins and then on a medium to low heat for another 2 to 3 mins. Turn off. 16. Add the bhuna chicken & 2 tablespoons of chopped coriander leaves (optional) to this masala and continue to saute for 2 to 3 minutes. 17. Cover and cook on a low heat, until the chicken is fully cooked and tender. If you feel the masala is sticking to the pan or about to burn you may splash little hot water. I do about ¼ cup, pour on the sides and not over the chicken. 18. Taste test and adjust salt. Sprinkle crushed kasuri methi. Garnish with coriander leaves.

title: Chicken Burger Recipe
ingredients: 3 Chicken breasts, 0.25 cup yogurt, 0.25 tsp all spice powder, 1 cup water, Salt, 1 cup all-purpose flour, 1 tsp chili flakes, 1.25 cup bread crumbs, 1 egg, Oil, 6 burger buns, 0.5 cup Mayonnaise, 2.5 tbsp extra virgin olive oil, 2 tbsp lemon juice, 1.5 tsp dry mustard, 0.5 tsp pepper, 5 lettuce leaves, Tomatoes
instructions: 1. Whisk together yogurt, salt, all spice or ground pepper and water. Slice the breasts if they are too thick. Place them in the buttermilk and rest for a minimum of 4 hours to overnight in the refrigerator. 2. Mix together little salt, red chili powder and flour. Keep aside. Beat egg with 2 tbsp water. Keep aside. 3. Remove the chicken and place them in the flour and coat them well. Dip in the egg mixture and then roll in the bread crumbs. Rest for 10 mins. 4. Deep fry in hot oil on a medium high heat until golden and crisp. Your meat thermometer should read 165F in the thickest part. Remove to a cooling rack. Fry the chicken in batches. 5. Slice and toast the dinner rolls on a griddle. Place the fried chicken, then lettuce and tomato. 6. Prepare a sauce by stirring mayo, mustard, olive oil, pepper, salt and lemon juice. 7. Add this generously over the tomatoes. You can also add tomato ketchup or any other preferred sauce. Place the other half of the dinner roll. 8. Serve chicken burger immediately.

title: Chicken Butter Masala
ingredients: 0.5 cup Greek yogurt, 6 grams chicken thighs, 0.75 teaspoon Kashmiri red chilli powder, 0.125 teaspoon turmeric, 1 teaspoon garam masala, 0.3333333333333333 teaspoon salt, 1 tablespoon ginger garlic paste, 1 teaspoon kasuri methi, 1 tablespoon oil, 1 tablespoon lemon juice, 1 tablespoon oil, 1.5 cup sliced onions, 15 grams ginger, 15 grams garlic, 1 teaspoon salt, 1.5 to 2 teaspoon Kashmiri red chilli powder, 5 cups tomatoes chopped, 25 whole cashews, 1 teaspoon cumin powder, 2 teaspoon coriander powder, 1.5 teaspoon garam masala, 1.5 teaspoon sugar, 0.5 teaspoon cardamom powder, 1.5 cups water, 2.5 tablespoons butter, 1 green chilli, 0.375 teaspoon Kashmiri red chilli powder, 1 cup water, 1 tablespoon ghee, 0.5 teaspoon garam masala, 2 teaspoon kasuri methi, 0.3333333333333333 cup chilled cream
instructions: 1. Cut chicken to ¾ inch pieces and add them to a bowl. Pat dry with kitchen tissues. 2. Add all the ingredients for marinade - Greek yogurt / hung curd, red chilli powder, turmeric, garam masala, salt, ginger garlic paste, kasuri methi, oil and lemon juice. 3. Marinate and rest covered for a minimum of 60 mins, overnight is best (refrigerated). 4. Meanwhile slice the onions and peeled ginger. Heat a pan with oil and begin to saute the onions until transparent (pink), till they lose the raw flavor. While the onions fry, chop the tomatoes. 5. Add ginger and garlic. Saute for 2 to 3 mins. Lower the heat and stir in red chilli powder. 6. Add the chopped tomatoes, cashews and salt. Cook on a medium high heat until the tomatoes break down to a mush. 7. Stir in garam masala, cumin powder (optional), coriander powder, sugar and Cardamom powder. 8. Turn off and cool down (read notes). Pour 1 cup water to the blender and make a super smooth puree. If required pour ½ cup more. (Some blenders do the best job with no extra liquid added, while some need only little, so adjust depending on your blender) 9. Wipe down the pan with some tissues and heat it with butter on a low to medium heat. 10. As the butter begins to melt, stir in green chilli (optional) and red chilli powder (optional). Taste test the onion tomato puree and add the chilli powder accordingly. 11. Strain the puree directly to the pan. Make sure your pan is not too hot else your spices will burn. 12. Pour ½ cup hot water and begin to cook the butter masala until it reaches a thick and creamy consistency. 13. While the butter masala simmers, heat a pan/ skillet with 1 tablespoon oil. When the oil becomes hot, layer the chicken pieces on the pan and cook on a high heat. 14. When the chicken turns brown at the bottom, turn them to the other side and cook until golden and slightly charred. Depending on the size of your pan, you may need to grill the chicken in batches. Chicken may not be cooked fully at this stage. 15. When the butter masala turns thick and creamy, pour ½ cup hot water (to rehydrate the chicken) and 1 tablespoon ghee. 16. Stir in the grilled chicken and let simmer for a 3 to 4 minutes, until the chicken is cooked through and the butter masala reaches a thick and creamy consistency. 17. Taste test and adjust salt. Add garam masala and kasuri methi. 18. Stir in the chilled cream. Turn off the heat. Mix and let rest for at least 5 mins. Garnish chicken butter masala with a tablespoon cream and fine chopped coriander leaves. Serve with Butter naan, Jeera rice or plain basmati rice.

title: Chicken Chettinad
ingredients: 0.5 kg chicken, 0.125 teaspoon turmeric, 0.25 teaspoon red chili powder, 1 tablespoon lemon juice, 1 tablespoon ginger garlic paste, 0.5 teaspoon sea salt, 2.5 tablespoons oil, 1 bay leaf, 1 cup onions, 1.5 cups tomatoes, 2 sprigs curry leaves, 0.75 cup water, 4.5 red chilies, 1 tablespoon coriander seeds, 1 teaspoon fennel seeds, 0.75 teaspoon cumin seeds, 0.5 teaspoon pepper corn, 5 green cardamoms, 1 inch cinnamon stick, 4 cloves 4 cloves, 1 star anise, 1 piece stone flower, 0.25 cup coconut, 8 cashews
instructions: 1. Marinate chicken with chili powder, turmeric, salt, ginger garlic and lemon juice or yogurt. Cover and rest aside until needed. 2. To a pan, add red chilies, cinnamon, cloves, star anise, dagad phool, black pepper corn, fennel seeds, cumin seeds, coriander seeds and cardamoms. 3. Heat on a medium flame for 2 mins. Reduce to low and roast until crunchy and golden, for 7 to 8 mins. Do not burn and keep stirring constantly. Remove to cool down. 4. Add coconut, half of the curry leaves and half tablespoon oil to the same pan and roast until light golden, for 7 to 8 mins. Turn off. 5. Transfer all the roasted spices and make a fine powder. Your chettinad masala is ready. To make the paste, add coconut, curry leaves and cashews if using to the same grinder jar. Pour half cup cold water and make a super smooth paste. This is Chettinad masala paste. 6. Heat oil in a pan, add bay leaf (optional) and onions. Saute until the onions turn light golden, for 7 to 8 mins. 7. Add the chicken and fry on a high heat for at least 4 to 5 mins. 8. Stir in the tomatoes. (and red chili powder if using). Saute until the raw smell of tomatoes is gone and the oil begins to ooze out of the tomatoes. 9. Stir in the rest of the curry leaves and transfer the ground Chettinad masala paste and hot water. 10. Mix well and simmer covered until the chicken is cooked through. If the gravy is runny, evaporate some of the liquid by cooking on a high heat. If it's too thick add some hot water. (A meat thermometer inserted in the thickest part of the chicken should read 165 F or 75 C.) 11. Taste test and adjust salt to your liking. 12. Serve chicken chettinad gravy with rice, chapatti, idli, dosa and appam.

title: Chicken Dum Biryani Recipe
ingredients: 2 cups basmati rice, 2.25 litres water, 1 tsp shahi jeera, 1 bay leaf, 2 inch cinnamon, 3 green cardamoms, 4 cloves 4 cloves (, optional), 3 tbsp oil, 2 onions, 1 star anise, 3 green cardamoms, 1 black cardamom badi elaichi, 4 cloves 4 cloves (/ lavang), 1 inch cinnamon stick, 0.5 tsp shahi jeera, 1 bay leaf, 1 tbsp ginger garlic paste, 0.5 kg chicken, Salt, 0.1875 tsp turmeric, 1.5 to 2 tsp biryani masala, 0.875 tsp red chili powder, 2 green chilies slit, 1 cup curd thick fresh, 1 Handful mint, 1 Handful coriander leaves, 1 pinch saffron, 2 tbsp milk, 1 tsp kewra water, 1 tbsp ghee
instructions: 1. Wash rice few times till the water runs clear. Soak in ample amount of water for 30 mins. Drain and set aside. 2. Bring 2 to 2 ½ liters water to a boil in a pot or a cooker. Add bay leaf and shahi jeera. You can also use cinnamon, cloves and cardamoms. If you do not like to get a bite of the spices in the rice, you can also tie these in a muslin cloth and add to the pot. 3. Meanwhile, Heat 3 tbsp. oil in a biryani pot , handi or pan. Add sliced onions and fry evenly until golden. 4. These onions lend a unique and authentic flavor, so do not skip these. 5. When they turn dark brown, set aside ¼ portion of these fried onions. 6. When the water comes to a boil, add salt and drained rice and cook until al dente or 90 % done. 7. Wash rice few times until the water runs clear. Soak for 30 mins. Drain the water. 8. Bring 8 to 10 cups of water to a boil in a large pot. Add bay leaf and shahi jeera. You can also add cinnamon stick, cardamoms and cloves. You can also tie these spices in a muslin cloth and add to the pot. It can be discarded later 9. When the water comes to a boil, add salt and drained rice. 10. Cook until it is done al dente. Rice should be firm, grainy and you must feel the bite into the grain when you bite it. Drain it off to a colander. 11. While the rice cooks, Saute onions in hot oil to make fried onions. When they are golden and evenly done, set aside 1/4 of them aside on a kitchen towel. 12. Lower the flame and saute bay leaf, star anise, shahi jeera, cloves, cardamom. 13. Add ginger garlic paste and fry until the raw smell has gone completely. 14. Add chicken and fry on a medium heat until it turns pale or white. 15. Sprinkle salt, biriyani masala, red chili powder, slit green chilies and turmeric. Mix well and fry for 3 mins. 16. Cover and cook on a low to medium flame for 3 to 4 mins to let the moisture out from the meat. 17. Pour yogurt and mix well. Cover and cook on a medium heat until the meat is completely cooked and the gravy thickens. At this stage a thick gravy is left for the dum process. Taste and adjust salt as needed. 18. Lower the flame, sprinkle some mint and coriander leaves. Stir well. 19. Layer the rice evenly. Sprinkle fried onions, mint, coriander leaves, saffron soaked milk, kewra water & ghee. You can also skip using saffron, ghee and kewra water but these give an authentic touch. 20. Cover the pot with a foil or a moist kitchen cloth. Place a tight lid to trap the steam or dum. If the lid is not tight then you can place a heavy pot on top of the lid. 21. Heat a old tawa or a pan, place this biryani pot and dum cook for 12 to 15 mins on a medium heat. If using a induction stovetop, you can directly place the pot at 3 No for 15 to 20 mins. 22. Grease an oven safe bowl, Add the cooked chickn mixture, Layer the rice, sprinkle mint, coriander, saffron milk and seal it with a foil, dum in the preheated oven at 180 c for 15 to 25 minutes. 23. Serve chicken dum biryani with salan and raita

title: Chicken fried rice
ingredients: 3 cups cooked rice, salt, 1 tablespoon garlic, 1 tablespoon soya sauce, 0.875 teaspoon rice vinegar, 0.29166666666666663 teaspoon sugar, 0.25 teaspoon crushed pepper, 2 tablespoon oil, 1.5 green chilies, 2 sprigs scallions, 0.375 cup bell peppers, 15 grams chicken, 1 egg, 0.5 teaspoon soya sauce, 1 pinch salt, 1 pinch crushed black pepper
instructions: 1. Wash rice in lot of water and soak for 20 mins. Drain off and cook in 3 to 4 cups water. When the rice is cooked al dente, drain it to a colander. The rice must be firm and almost cooked but not mushy. Cover and rest for a while. Fluff up with a fork and cool completely. 2. Meanwhile prepare the veggies and garlic. You can also use green chilies for extra heat. In a small bowl, stir together 1 tablespoon soya sauce, ¾ to 1 teaspoon rice vinegar & ¼ to ⅓ teaspoon sugar (optional). If you want to use any other hot sauce you may stir in. Keep this aside. 3. Ensure your chicken is cut to bite sized pieces. Add pepper, salt and 2 tbsps egg white. Instead of egg you can also add ¾ tbsp corn starch or corn flour. Mix this up well. 4. Pour 1.5 tbsps oil to a hot wok or pan. When the oil turns hot, add the chicken. Stir fry for 3 to 4 mins stirring often. If the egg sticks to your pan, just scrape off with the spatula. 5. You can check if the chicken is cooked at this stage. Mine gets done under 4 mins. Push the chicken to one side of the pan. Then beat the rest of the egg and pour it. 6. Scramble it and fry until the egg is cooked through yet soft. Do not overcook. Remove it to a plate. 7. Pour another half tbsp oil and fry the garlic on the highest flame. 8. Then add spring onion whites, chilles (optional) and bell peppers. Stir fry for 1 to 2 mins. 9. Add the rice, prepared sauce (soya+vinegar) and pepper. You can also add your favorite sauces. Stir fry for a minute. 10. Add the chicken and the egg. Taste and add more salt or sauce at this stage. 11. Fry until the rice turns hot. Add spring onion greens. Serve chicken fried rice hot. 12. 3 cups cooked rice (or ½ cup uncooked rice) 13. 1 tablespoon garlic chopped 14. ¼ to ½ cup bell peppers chopped 15. 150 grams chicken chopped to bite size 16. Wash rice at least twice and cook it al dente (firm & not mushy). Drain to a colander and cool it completely. 17. Do not add hot rice to the wok while making chicken fried rice, it makes the rice mushy. 18. Add chicken, a pinch of salt, pepper and 2 tablespoons of egg white to a bowl. You can also simply replace the egg with corn starch. Mix together and set aside. 19. In a small mixing bowl, stir together soya sauce, sugar and vinegar. (If you want you may add any other hot sauce you prefer.) 20. Heat 1½ tablespoon oil in a wok or pan and add the chicken. 21. Stir fry for 3 to 4 minutes until the chicken is almost cooked through. Move the chicken aside in the pan. Then whisk the remaining egg and pour it to the pan. 22. Scramble & cook the egg completely until it is firm but still soft. Do not overcook. Remove the chicken & egg to a plate. 23. Wipe off the wok with a kitchen tissue. Pour another half tbsp oil to the same pan. Add garlic and fry for 30 seconds. 24. Add spring onion whites/ scallions, chilies (optional) bell peppers and toss on a highest heat just for 2 mins. The bell peppers must be crunchy and not soft. 25. Transfer the rice, sprinkle black pepper and pour the prepared sauce. Mix and fry on the highest flame for a minute. 26. Stir in the chicken and egg. Continue to fry until the rice turns hot. This takes just a minute or two. Taste test and add more salt or sauce you prefer. Don't over fry, this can make the rice hard. 27. Serve chicken fried rice hot. 28. For best results I cook my rice with very little salt, then add a bit to the chicken. Lastly adjust while frying the rice. 29. You can add any hot sauce for the extra heat. Adding 1 to 2 green chilies along with onions will add heat to your chicken fried rice. 30. You can skip egg & corn starch from the recipe and just brine the chicken at least for 4 hours in ¾ cup of buttermilk. Drain and use it here. This keeps the chicken tender. 31. .kadence-column173268_d8ec65-ba > .kt-inside-inner-col,.kadence-column173268_d8ec65-ba > title: Chicken Jalfrezi Recipe (Restaurant Style)
ingredients: 55 grams chicken, 2 teaspoons lemon juice, 0.25 teaspoon salt, 0.25 teaspoon black pepper, 0.5 teaspoons Kashmiri red chili powder, 2 tablespoons oil, 0.75 cup onions, 0.5 tablespoon ginger grated, 0.625 tablespoon garlic grated, 1 teaspoon Kashmiri red chilli powder, 0.875 teaspoon garam masala, 1.25 teaspoon coriander powder, 0.5 teaspoon cumin powder, 0.5 teaspoon fennel powder, 0.25 teaspoon turmeric, 1 teaspoon sugar, 0.75 cup tomato puree, 1 tablespoon kasuri methi, 0.25 cup water, 1.5 tablespoon ghee, 1 red chilli, 1 green chilli, 1 inch ginger juliennes, 0.5 cup onions, 1.5 cups bell pepper, 1 tablespoon soya sauce, 2 tablespoons coriander leaves
instructions: 1. To a bowl add chicken, salt, black pepper, red chilli powder & lemon juice. Mix and keep aside for about 15 to 20 mins, until you prepare the rest of the veggies. If using fresh tomatoes, puree them in a grinder. 2. Dice the bell peppers and onions. Separate the layers from diced onions. Slit the green chili and deseed if you want. Also fine chop another onion for the sauce. 3. Heat a pan with oil and add onions. Fry them until golden on a medium high heat. Lower the heat and stir in the ginger garlic. Saute for a minute. 4. Add chicken and stir fry on a medium high heat for about 3 to 4 minutes, until it turns pale. 5. Stir in the spices – red chilli powder, turmeric, garam masala, coriander powder and cumin powder. Pour the tomato puree. Mix well and let cook until oil begins to separate from the masala. 6. Pour hot water and mix well. Cook covered on a medium heat until chicken is tender. (If your chicken begins to ooze out lots of moisture, do not add water) 7. Meanwhile, in a different pan, heat 1 to 2 tablespoon ghee/oil. Add green chilies, red chilies and ginger juliennes (optional). When they begin to sizzle in hot oil, add bell peppers and onions. 8. Stir fry on a medium high flame for 2 to 4 mins until slightly tender, making sure they retain their crunch. 9. When the chicken is fully cooked and the sauce is thick, add kasuri methi, sugar & stir fried veggies along with the leftover ghee or oil in the pan. If there is excess liquid you may evaporate by cooking on a high heat. (Optionally, for a fancy flavor, you may add soya sauce/ tamari or Worcestershire sauce). 10. Stir well and cook just for 1 to 2 minutes. Taste test and adjust the salt. Turn off and add chopped coriander leaves (optional). Serve chicken jalfrezi with rice, fried rice, or with noodles, roti or any flatbreads.

title: Chicken Kathi Roll (Chicken Frankie)
ingredients: 3 grams chicken breast, 0.875 teaspoon Kashmiri chili powder, 0.75 teaspoon garam masala, 0.5 teaspoon salt, 2 tablespoons Greek Yogurt, 1 teaspoon lemon juice, 1 teaspoon kasuri methi, 1 tablespoon oil, 1.5 tablespoon oil, 0.75 cup onions, 1 cup bell peppers, 4.5 eggs, 3 parottas paratha roti chapati, 0.41666666666666663 cup onions, 2 green chillies, 1 lemon, 1 teaspoon chaat masala, 2.5 tablespoons Red chili sauce
instructions: 1. In a bowl, stir together all the ingredients mentioned for marinade except chicken. Mix well and taste test. Adjust salt and spice levels. 2. Pat dry chicken with kitchen tissues if needed and add it to the marinade. Mix well and rest until you prepare the rest. You can marinate this up to 48 hours in the refrigerator. 3. When you are ready to make the Chicken Kathi Rolls, chop onions, chilies and bell peppers. 4. Add the sliced onions and green chilies (called to assemble) to a small bowl. Sprinkle little salt and squeeze the lemon juice. Cover and rest aside until we need them to assemble later. 5. Heat half of the oil in a skillet and add the onions and bell peppers. Sauté them on a high heat until they lose the raw flavor, for 3 to 4 mins. Remove to a plate. 6. Add the rest of the oil and spread it all over the skillet. Place the chicken in the pan and fry, turning them to the other side at 3 mins mark (total - 6 to 7 mins). 7. When they are cooked through, add the previously stir fried onions & bell peppers. Taste test and add a sprinkle of salt, garam masala and red chili powder if you want. 8. Mix well and turn off. Cool down the chicken completely to prevent soggy chicken rolls. 9. If using frozen parathas, cook them as per the instructions on the pack. 10. Optional if using eggs: Heat a non-stick pan with 1 tsp oil and pour 1 to 2 beaten eggs per roll. Wait for the egg to set a bit and then place the pre-cooked parotta and press down with a spatula until the egg is cooked through. If in doubt, turn the parotta to the other side and cook again until slightly puffy. 11. Remove to a tray and cool down slightly before wrapping. If you want more egg parotta, make the same way as mentioned above. 12. Place the paratha on a tray/plate. Add chicken in the center, followed by some sliced onions, green chilies, chaat masala and red chili sauce/ green sauce or mint chutney. Bring the sides to the center and wrap them in a parchment paper if you want. 13. Follow the same above steps and make more chicken kathi rolls.

title: Chicken kebab recipe | Chicken kabab recipe
ingredients: 25 grams chicken, 2.5 tablespoons greek yogurt, 2 tablespoons oil, salt, 1.5 tablespoons lemon juice, 0.75 teaspoon garam masala, 1.25 teaspoon ginger garlic paste, 0.875 teaspoon red chilli powder, 1 medium onion
instructions: 1. When you are ready to grill them in the oven. Firstly heat the oven to 240 C for at least 15 to 20 mins. Next rinse the skewers and put them on alternately chicken and onion. 2. Next grill the chicken kebab for 15 to 20 mins and flip them. Grill for another 10 to 15 mins. The last 5 mins I prefer to move the tray to the top rack. At this stage you can also brush the kababs with little butter or oil. I have not done it. 3. The onions will char and impart a good flavor when moved to the top rack. The difference in the color is due to the lighting. They turn out red in color if you use a good chili powder. Serve chicken kebab hot with mint chutney or mint yogurt raita or green chutney . 4. Ensure chicken is free from excess moisture. Drain it completely. 5. Mix all the ingredients in a mixing bowl. 6. Taste the marinade and add more salt & spice if needed. 7. If using wooden skewers, soak them in water for a while. Line the chicken and onions alternately on the skewers. 8. Preheat the oven to 240 C for minimum 15 to 20 mins. 9. Serve chicken kebab hot with mint raita or chutney.

title: Chicken keema
ingredients: 1 bay leaf, 0.5 teaspoon cumin, 1 cinammon stick, 2 green cardamoms, 4 cloves 4 cloves, 4 black pepper corn, 2 tablespoons oil, 0.75 cup onions, 1 green chili, 1 tablespoon ginger garlic paste, 1 tomato, 1.5 teaspoon garam masala, 0.625 teaspoon red chili powder, 8 mint leaves, 25 grams chicken, 0.5 teaspoon salt, 0.25 teaspoon turmeric, 2 tablespoons coriander leaves, 0.25 cup green peas, 0.25 cup cream
instructions: 1. Heat oil in a pan on a low to medium heat. Add the whole spices & let them sizzle. 2. Then add onions, green chilies and fry until golden. 3. Add ginger garlic paste and fry until the raw smell disappears. 4. Transfer tomatoes or tomato puree and fry until the tomatoes turn soft. 5. Add garam masala, red chilli powder and mint or curry leaves. 6. Fry until the raw smell goes off from tomatoes. 7. Set aside the onion tomato mixture to a plate. Add bay leaf back to the pan and then keema, sprinkle salt and turmeric. Most times tomatoes turn the meat tough or hard, hence i prefer to set aside. 8. On a low flame, cover and cook till it is completely cooked and is soft. If needed sprinkle some water. 9. Lastly add the onion tomato mixture back to the pan. Stir well. on a low flame, cover and cook again till every thing blends well. 10. Add chopped coriander leaves. Off the stove. To make a gravy version of this, add 1/3 cup coconut milk or blend 12 cashews to smooth milk and pour it. Cook till the curry thickens. If you prefer to use cream check the recipe below on how to use. Garnish and serve it with rice or roti. 11. ¾ cup onions chopped 12. 1 green chili (chopped or slit) 13. 1 tomato (1 cup fine chopped) 14. 8 mint leaves chopped or 1 sprig curry leaves 15. Pour oil to a pan and heat it. Add the whole spices and saute till they begin to sizzle. 16. Add onions and green chilli. Fry until the onions turn golden. 17. Next add ginger garlic paste and fry until the raw smell goes off. 18. Transfer the chopped tomatoes, fry until softened. If tomatoes are dry, sprinkle very little water. 19. Add chili powder and masala powder, mint leaves and fry until the raw smell of tomatoes go off. 20. Set the onion tomato mixture to a plate. 21. Add back the bay leaf and minced meat, turmeric and salt. 22. Cover and cook on a low heat till the qeema is soft cooked in its own moisture. If needed sprinkle little water. 23. Add back the onion tomato mixture. Mix everything well. 24. Cook covered on a low flame till minced meat and onion tomato mixture blends well. This depends on the quantity of moisture left in the pan. To make dry keema curry, cook tiil all the moisture evaporates. 25. To make a gravy version of the chicken keema recipe, pour coconut milk or cashew milk and cook till the curry thickens. To make smooth cashew milk, blend cashew nuts with little water. 26. If using cream, pour the cream in a bowl, add little keema to the cream first. stir it and then add it to the pan. This way it doesn't curdle. Add chopped coriander leaves.Serve with rice or roti, naan 27. .kadence-column173268_d8ec65-ba > .kt-inside-inner-col,.kadence-column173268_d8ec65-ba > title: Chicken Kolhapuri
ingredients: 5 grams chicken, 0.875 cup onions, 3 tablespoons oil, 0.75 cup tomatoes, 0.25 teaspoon turmeric, 0.75 tablespoon ginger, 0.75 tablespoon garlic, 0.5 teaspoon salt, 0.25 cup coriander leaves, 0.25 teaspoon nutmeg powder, 4 cloves 4 cloves ((laung)), 1 strand mace, 1.5 tablespoons white poppy seeds, 1 tablespoon white sesame seeds, 4.5 black pepper corn, 4.5 red chilies, 0.25 cup coconut, 2 tablespoons coriander seeds, 1 inch cinnamon piece, 3 green cardamom, 1 black cardamom, 1 teaspoon cumin seeds
instructions: 1. Heat a pan on a low flame. Add red chilies, coriander seeds, cinnamon, cloves, cardamoms, pepper, mace and nutmeg. Roast until golden without burning. If using nutmeg powder, add it in the next step. 2. Add coconut, cumin, sesame seeds and poppy seeds. Saute until all the ingredients turn to golden. Do not over roast as the spices will taste bitter. 3. Set these aside and cool. Blend spices to a fine powder. 4. Meanwhile add 1 tablespoon oil to a deep pan or pot & heat it. Add onions & fry/saute them until golden. 5. Add chopped ginger garlic and saute without burning until the raw smell goes off. Set these aside to cool. 6. Transfer onion and ginger garlic to the spice powder and blend again to a fine powder. It will be slightly oily and lumpy but make sure ginger garlic is ground well. 7. Add this masala and turmeric to the chicken. Mix well to marinate. Set this aside for at least 15 mins to overnight (in the refrigerator). 8. Pour oil to the same pan and add tomatoes. Sprinkle salt and saute until the tomatoes turn soft. 9. Transfer marinated chicken and saute well for 3 to 5 mins on a medium heat, until the masala begin to smell good. 10. Add salt to the chicken. Pour hot water just enough to partially cover the chicken. The amount of water to use depends on the kind of chicken, you may start with half cup water. 11. Mix well. Add coriander leaves. Regulate the flame to low flame and cook chicken until fully cooked, fork tender. As you cook, add more hot water if the curry turns too thick. If it is too runny, cook uncovered on a slightly higher heat. 12. You will also see traces of oil over the curry when it is done. Taste test and add more salt if you want. 13. Serve kolhapuri chicken with plain steamed rice, roti or chapati, kachumber, onions slices and lemon wedges.

title: Chicken Kurma
ingredients: 8 whole cashewnuts, 0.25 cup thick coconut milk, 0.75 teaspoon fennel seeds, 2 tablespoons oil, 1 bay leaf, 1 star anise, 0.5 teaspoon cumin seeds, 2 green cardamoms, 4 cloves 4 cloves, 1 inch cinnamon, 1 cup onions, 1 green chili, 0.25 teaspoon turmeric, 0.5 teaspoon salt, 1.5 teaspoon ginger garlic paste, 1 lb chicken, 1 medium ripe tomato, 0.5 teaspoon red chili powder, 1 teaspoon garam masala 1 4 tsp, 1 sprig curry leaves, 1 tablespoon yogurt, 1 sprig coriander
instructions: 1. If you want to skip making a kurma paste simply use about ⅓ cup canned coconut milk. Grind together coconut, cashews & fennel seeds with a few tablespoons water to a smooth paste. Keep this aside (optionally if using poppy seeds, powder them first. Add coconut and blend to a smooth paste with little water.) 2. Chop onions & tomatoes finely. Set aside. 3. Heat oil in a pan. Add the whole spices. Once they begin to sizzle add the onions and green chilies. Sprinkle salt and saute them until golden. 4. Add ginger garlic paste and fry until it turns aromatic, for about a minute. 5. Add chicken, turmeric and little more salt. Fry the chicken on a medium high heat until it turns white or pale in color. 6. Add tomatoes & stir fry until the tomatoes are softened and broken down. 7. Time to add in chili powder and garam masala. Stir and fry for 3 to 4 minutes. 8. Add curry leaves or mint and coconut paste (or coconut milk) and yogurt. Stir and fry until the mixture begins to smell aromatic, for 2 to 3 mins. 9. Pour ½ cup hot water and mix well. Chicken oozes out water as it cooks, so add more hot water as you cook if required. 10. Cook covered till the chicken turns completely tender and soft. Taste test and adjust the salt. Towards the end add ¼ tsp garam masala. Switch off the stove. 11. Transfer chicken kurma to a serving bowl. Best to serve after 30 minutes. Garnish with coriander or mint leaves just before serving.

title: Chicken Majestic
ingredients: 3 grams chicken, 0.5 cup Buttermilk, 1.5 tbsp corn flour, Salt to taste, 1.5 tsp Ginger garlic paste, 0.125 tsp turmeric, 0.375 tsp red chili powder, Oil, 1 tbsp Oil, 2 cloves garlic, 2 green chilies, 1 sprig curry leaves, Handful mint leaves, 1 tbsp soya sauce, 0.375 tsp red chili powder, 0.5 tsp garam masala powder, 3 tbsp yogurt
instructions: 1. Wash and cut chicken to strips to around 2 ½ inches length. 2. Soak them in buttermilk and salt for at least 20 mins if deep frying. Keep for 2 to 4 hours if you wish to pan fry or bake. 3. Drain up the buttermilk and add ginger garlic paste, turmeric, corn flour and red chili powder. Mix well and set aside for at least 10 mins. 4. Deep fry on a medium heat. If you fry on a low heat, the chicken will turn out hard and if you fry on very high heat, the chicken will not be cooked well from inside. So once the oil is hot, reduce it to medium, wait for a min and then add chicken pieces to oil. 5. Fry till these are lightly golden. Remove it to a kitchen tissue. 6. Preheat the oven to 180 C for about 15 to 20 mins. Then place the chicken on a greased tray or put them on skewers. 7. Grill them for 8 mins on each side or until done. Adjust the time as needed depending on the oven. 8. Whisk the yogurt well with a fork and keep aside. Pour 1 tbsp oil to a hot pan. 9. Add garlic, curry leaves and green chilies. Fry till the curry leaves turn crispy. Garlic also smells good by then. 10. Next add mint leaves, red chili powder and garam masala. Saute for a minute. 11. Take the pan down from the heat and pour the whisked yogurt. Mix well and cook on a low flame till the moisture evaporates and becomes thick. Add soya sauce and mix. 12. Add chicken. Mix for the chicken to blend well with the cooked mixture. Off the heat. 13. Serve chicken majestic as a starter or as a side to rasam or rice.

title: Chicken manchurian
ingredients: 25 grams chicken breast, 0.5 egg, 0.125 tsp pepper, 1 tsp soya sauce, 2 pinches chili powder, 2 tbsp corn starch, 1.5 tbsp all-purpose flour, 1 tsp ginger garlic paste, salt, oil, 1 tbsp garlic, 0.5 tbsp ginger, 1 medium onion, 1 tbsp soya sauce, 2 tbsp red chili sauce, 2 tbsp tomato sauce, 1 tsp vinegar, 1 cup water, 2 tsp corn starch, 3 tbsp water, salt, 0.75 tsp sugar, 0.75 cup capsicum, 2 tbsps scallions
instructions: 1. Make sure there is no excess moisture in the chicken before you use it. You can either use cubed chicken or chicken mince (keema). 2. I prefer to chop finely and then use. Add soya sauce, ginger garlic, salt , red chili powder (optional) and pepper to a mixing bowl. Mix well. For Chinese recipes, generally no chili powder is used. But i prefer to use to get a a good color which otherwise is got by food colors in restaurants. 3. Add egg white, rice flour and corn flour. 1 egg white works good for half a kg chicken. I used only half of 1 egg white since i used only 250 grams chicken. Do not add more egg otherwise the mixture will turn soggy. 4. Mix everything well and set aside for at least 1 hour. You can also skip this. 5. We can use the same pan further. Transfer the hot oil to a cup, keep only ½ tbsp oil. Fry chopped garlic ginger till you begin to smell good. 6. Add onions and spring onion whites and saute till they turn transparent. 7. Add bell peppers, these are optional. If you do not have skip them. Fry for 2 to 3 minutes. 8. Pour the sauces. Stir and mix. 9. When the sauces begin to bubble, Stir the corn flour mixture and then pour it to the pan. 10. Pour 1 ¼ cup water and begin to cook. Taste the sauce and adjust salt & spice. This has to be slightly sweet, sour and hot. 11. When the sauce begins to bubble, add the fried chicken and spring onions. Toss well to coat them in sauce. If you have made the chicken balls then simmer for 2 minutes, this helps the balls to absorb the flavors. If the sauce becomes very thick, you can add one to 2 tbsp water and stir. 12. This recipe yields a semi dry chicken manchurian. If you like to have more gravy, just add 1 tsp more of cornflour and ½ cup more water. Serve right away as a appetizer or as a side to fried rice or hakka noodles or simple veg noodles . 13. Mix chicken with soya sauce, salt, ginger garlic paste, red chili powder (optional) and pepper powder. 14. Add egg white and flour. Mix well. Set aside for at least 1 hour. 15. If you have used chicken cubes then skip this step. If you have used the mince divide the mixture to 12 portions. Make balls. If you don’t get round balls, that’s just fine. 16. Heat oil until hot enough to fry. Test by sliding a small portion of the batter to the hot oil. It has to rise without browning. This is the right temperature. 17. Deep fry chicken on a medium flame until golden & crisp. When the chicken is done cooking bubbles will begin to reduce. Remove the fried chicken to a kitchen towel. Please make sure the chicken is cooked completely inside before you add it to the sauce. 18. Stir in the corn flour with 3 tbsp water in a small bowl. To another small bowl, add vinegar,soya sauce, tomato sauce (optional) and red chilli sauce. Mix all of these and set aside. 19. Transfer the oil to a bowl and retain only half a tbsp. Fry ginger garlic until aromatic. Fry onions or scallion whites until transparent. 20. Add bell peppers and fry for 2 minutes. Pour the sauces. 21. When they begin to bubble, Stir the corn flour mixture & pour it along with 1 cup water or stock. 22. The sauce begins to bubble and thickens. Taste the sauce and add in more salt or any sauces you desire. Turn off the heat. Then add fried chicken & spring onions. 23. Stir and coat the chicken with the sauces. If you have made to chicken balls then cook for 2 mins. 24. Garnish with spring onions and serve chicken manchurian as a appetizer.

title: Chicken Masala Recipe
ingredients: 0.5 kg chicken, 0.5 teaspoon red chili powder, 0.75 teaspoon garam masala, 0.5 teaspoon turmeric, 0.75 teaspoon Ginger garlic paste, 0.25 teaspoon salt, 2 tablespoons curd, 2 tablespoons Oil, 1 bay leaf, 2 green cardamoms, 1 inch cinnamon piece, 3 cloves 2 to 4 cloves ( (optional)), 1 cup onions, 0.75 teaspoon ginger garlic paste, 1.5 green chilies, 0.5 cup tomato, 0.625 teaspoon Kashmiri red chili powder, 0.5 teaspoon Salt, 0.625 teaspoon garam masala, 1 Handful coriander leaves finely chopped
instructions: 1. Add chicken to a large mixing bowl. Make sure there is no excess water/ dripping moisture in the chicken. Add ginger garlic paste, turmeric, red chilli powder, salt, yogurt (optional), garam masala or chicken masala. 2. Cover & set aside for about 45 mins to 1 hour. It can also be left overnight in the refrigerator. 3. Heat oil in a pan or pot. Add the whole spices - cloves, cinnamon, cardamoms and bay leaf. (be careful - they can splutter) 4. Add green chilies and onions. Saute them on a medium to medium high heat for 5 to 6 mins. Then reduce the heat and saute for a few more minutes until the onions turn golden. 5. Add ginger garlic paste and saute for a minute, until the raw smell of the ginger garlic goes off. 6. Pour the tomato puree. Then add salt, red chili powder and garam masala as well. 7. Saute this very well until the mixture begins to smell aromatic and release some oil. This takes only 2 to 3 minutes. 8. Add marinated chicken and saute for 4 to 5 mins on a medium to high flame. Turn the flame to completely low and cook covered for 5 mins. (Alternately you can also sear the chicken with 1 tablespoon oil in a separate pan or grill or air fry for 5 mins first & add to the prepared onion tomato masala.) 9. Add coriander leaves or mint leaves and give a good stir. 10. Cover the pan again and continue to cook on a low heat until chicken is completely cooked to soft and tender. For a semi-dry chicken masala, you don't need to add water as the chicken gets cooked in its own moisture. 11. To make a gravy dish: Add little hot water or coconut milk at the previous step, after adding coriander leaves. Cook until the gravy turns thick & chicken is cooked through. If you intend to use coconut milk, add more red chilli and garam masala when you cook the tomato puree earlier. 12. Your chicken is fully cooked when the internal temperature reaches to 165 F. The other way is to pierce the chicken with a fork and should be able to pull out the meat easily from the pieces. 13. Taste test and add more salt or garam masala if you want. You can also add 2 to 3 tbsps. of heavy cream or whipping cream. Garnish with coriander leaves. 14. Serve chicken masala hot with steamed rice, roti, sliced onion wedges and lemon.

title: Chicken Noodles Recipe
ingredients: 2 grams chicken, 0.25 tsp pepper powder, 0.125 tsp salt, 1 tsp soya sauce, 0.75 tsp five spice, 15 grams Noodles, 1 tsp oil, salt, 1.5 tsp oil, 2 tbsp oil, 2 cloves garlic, 2 tbsps spring onion, 0.25 cup bell pepper, 0.25 cup carrots, 0.25 cup cabbage, 0.25 cup spring onion, 1.5 tbsp chilli sauce, 1 tbsp water, 1 tbsp soya sauce, salt
instructions: 1. Cut chicken to thin strips or bite sized pieces. Large chunks of chicken will not get cooked faster and tends to turn hard. Add the chicken to a bowl. Add in pepper powder, salt, soya sauce and spice powder – five spice powder or garam masala. 2. Marinate this well. Cover and set aside till the noodles are cooked. You can also refrigerate this upto 24 hours and use when needed. In restaurants they also use little corn starch or potato starch or even egg to coat the chicken. This helps to retain the juices and yield very tender and soft chicken. 3. Bring 2 to 2.5 liters of water to a boil in a large pot. Add the noodles. Please follow the instructions on the pack for timing, if salt and oil are needed. If needed then add 1 tbsp oil and salt. 4. Boil the noodles until al dente. The noodles must be firm and partially boiled. While the noodles boil, prepare the veggies. 5. When done drain them to a colander immediately. If needed you can rinse the noodles with cold water it depends on the kind used. Add 1 tsp oil and smear it all over the noodles. This prevents them from sticking. 6. Add 1 tbsp oil to a wok. I do not own a wok, so I use a pan. A well seasoned bare cast iron pan works well, if you have one you can use it. When the oil turns hot, add garlic. After 30 seconds, add chicken. 7. Fry the chicken until completely cooked. If you see the pan too dry, then add 1 to 2 tbsp of hot water. Using cold water turns the chicken hard. At the end of this step, chicken should be cooked well and must be able to cut it easily with the wooden spatula. 8. Move the chicken to one side of the pan. If using a wok you can remove the chicken to a plate and set aside. Set the flame/ heat to the highest. Add another tbsp oil to the pan. Fry the spring onion whites for a minute. I do not set the chicken aside as the moisture from veggies prevent the chicken from getting overcooked and it absorbs the flavor of capsicum. 9. Add capsicum and carrots. You can also add cabbage. Fry the veggies just for a minute. 10. Mix chicken and veggies. Fry for another 1 to 2 mins until the aroma of the veggies come out. Do not over fry as it may turn the chicken hard. 11. Add spring onions and chilli sauce + 1 tbsp water. 12. Add noodles and if needed little salt. Then pour soya sauce over it. Chinese noodles are usually low on salt and it comes mostly from the sauces. If you have set the chicken aside add it to the wok/ pan now. 13. Mix well. Stir fry for 1 to 2 mins. Serve chicken noodles hot.

title: Chicken Nuggets Recipe
ingredients: 25 grams chicken breast, 0.3333333333333333 cup all-purpose flour, 0.3333333333333333 teaspoon salt, 0.75 teaspoon garlic powder, 0.75 teaspoon garam masala, 0.75 teaspoon crushed black pepper, 1 egg, 0.75 cup bread crumbs, 0.25 cup yogurt, 1.5 cups water, 0.3333333333333333 teaspoon salt
instructions: 1. Cube the chicken breast to 1 to 1 ¼ inch pieces. 2. Brine: (optional) If you wish to go ahead with brine, whisk together yogurt and salt in a bowl until smooth. Pour water and mix well. Add the chicken and brine overnight in the refrigerator or at least for 3 hours. Later drain completely. 3. To the chicken cubes, add ½ teaspoon each of garlic powder, garam masala or Italian herbs and black pepper to the chicken. Also add ⅓ teaspoon salt and mix well. 4. Add the rest of the seasonings to a bowl along with the flour. Mix well. (check step by step pictures) 5. Break one egg to a bowl and beat it well with a fork. Alternately you may use the brine. 6. Transfer the bread crumbs to another bowl. Arrange all the 3 bowls side by side - flour, egg & bread crumbs. 7. Dredge chicken in flour and then dip it in egg. Then coat in bread crumbs. Repeat this for all the chicken nuggets. Gently press down each nugget with your fingers so the bread coats it well. 8. Rest them for 10 mins. Meanwhile heat oil in a pan for deep frying. 9. When it has reached a temperature of about 350 degrees F, begin frying in batches, taking care not to overcrowd the pan. If you do not have a thermometer, gently drop a piece of bread crumb to the oil. If the oil is hot enough, it will rise. 10. Cook until chicken nuggets turn light golden brown on one side, then turn them carefully to ensure that both sides are thoroughly cooked & fried. 11. Remove them to a wire rack or a kitchen tissue. 12. Serve chicken nuggets hot with your favorite dip or sauce. 13. Place the chicken nuggets into the air fryer basket. Spray them with olive or avocado oil. Air fry them for 5 to 7 minutes at 400 F or 200 C. 14. Place the chicken nuggets on a prepared tray and spray oil. Bake them for 18 to 20 mins at 400 F or 200 C, turning the chicken once after 10 mins.

title: Chicken Pasta (Chicken Spaghetti)
ingredients: 5.5 ounces spaghetti, 6 ounce boneless chicken, 2.5 tablespoons Olive oil, 1 tablespoon garlic cloves, 1 onion, 1.5 cups tomatoes, 1.25 teaspoon red chili flakes, 0.625 teaspoon black pepper, 0.1875 teaspoon allspice, 0.5 teaspoon salt, 0.5 teaspoon herbs
instructions: 1. Bring water to a rapid boil in a large pot. Add salt and pasta. Let the water keep boiling and cook the spaghetti al dente & not too soft. Refer the instructions on the pack for the amount of salt and the exact cook time. 2. Reserve ½ cup spaghetti cooked stock and drain the spaghetti to a colander. 3. Heat half the oil in a pan or pot. Add half of the garlic and let it sizzle for a minute. Add the shredded or diced chicken and saute until soft and fully cooked. 4. Add the ground spices and saute on a high heat for a minute. Set the chicken aside. 5. Pour rest of the oil to the pan and add the rest of the garlic. When the garlic begins to sizzle, add the chopped onions. Saute until golden. 6. Add tomatoes and cook until the sauce becomes thick. 7. Transfer the sautéed chicken and 2 to 3 tbsps of pasta cooked stock. Mix and simmer for a few minutes until the sauce reaches a thick consistency. Taste test and add more salt or spices to taste. 8. Add spaghetti and mix well. If your chicken spaghetti looks dry, you may add a splash of pasta stock or more oil. 9. If you like to make it cheesy add your favorite cheese over the chicken spaghetti. Serve hot with a side of soup or cheese toast.

title: Chicken Patties Recipe (Chicken Tikki)
ingredients: 3 grams chicken, 0.25 cup onions, 1 green chilli, 1.5 teaspoons ginger, 3 tablespoons coriander leaves, 0.75 teaspoon paprika, 0.25 teaspoon turmeric, 1.5 teaspoon garam masala, 0.375 cup bread crumbs, 0.75 teaspoon salt, 3 tablespoons oil, 0.5 cup bread crumbs, 1 egg, 0.125 teaspoon salt
instructions: 1. Cook the chicken: Place the chicken in a pan and cover with stock or water. Optionally you may add 2 tablespoons of yogurt to prevent the chicken from drying out. 2. On a low heat cook covered until tender, turning the chicken to the other side half way. When done the internal temperature reaches 165°F (74°C). 3. Prepare the mixture: Cool the chicken and mince or pulse it in a food processer. Add onions, chili, garam masala, ginger, salt, paprika/red chili powder, turmeric and coriander leaves. 4. Add only half of the bread crumbs and bring the mixture together to form a dough. Do not process the mixture in processor else the onions begin to release moisture. 5. If the mixture is too sticky, add more bread crumbs. Do not add any liquid. Taste test and adjust the spices and salt. 6. Shape to patties: Divide the mixture to 8 to 9 portions. Flatten and shape to patties. 7. Prep to crumb coat: If you want to use egg, beat the egg in a bowl with salt. Or make a thick batter using gram flour and very little salt. (check pics in the post) 8. Dip the patty in the batter or egg. Immediately put it in the bread crumbs to coat the patties well. Keep these aside for 15 mins. Press down the patties with your fingers so the bread crumbs stick well. 9. Cook the chicken patties: Heat a pan with oil. Shallow fry or deep fry or pan fry to suit your liking. When the oil is medium hot, place the chicken patties in the pan and fry until crisp and golden. Remove to a serving plate. 10. Serve Chicken Patties with Mint Chutney or Cilantro Chutney.

title: Chicken Pulao Recipe (Instant Pot and Stovetop)
ingredients: 1.6 pounds bone-in skinless chicken, 1 teaspoon ginger garlic paste, 0.375 teaspoon Kashmiri red chili powder, 0.125 teaspoon turmeric, 0.29166666666666663 teaspoon salt, 1 teaspoon garam masala, 2 tablespoons lemon juice, 2 bay leaf, 2 star anise, 0.625 teaspoon cumin seeds, 6 cloves 6 cloves ((laung)), 2 pieces Cinnamon, 8 green cardamoms, 0.75 teaspoon fennel powder, 2 black cardamom, 1 strand mace, 1 piece nutmeg, 3.5 tablespoon ghee, 1 cup onion, 2.5 green chilies, 2 teaspoons ginger garlic paste, 0.3333333333333333 cup tomatoes, 0.75 teaspoon salt, 0.625 cup mint leaves, 2 cups aged basmati rice, 3.5 cups water, 0.25 cup coriander leaves
instructions: 1. Add chicken to a mixing bowl. Score them if the pieces are too large. Add ginger garlic, red chilli powder, turmeric, salt, garam masala and lemon juice. Marinate the chicken and keep covered aside until you prepare the rest. 2. To a bowl, add rice and rinse at least thrice. Pour fresh water & soak for at least 30 minutes while you slice and follow the rest of the steps. Later drain the rice to a colander. 3. Slice and prepare the onions, green chilies, mint and coriander leaves. 4. To a pressure cooker or pot, pour ghee or oil & heat it on a medium flame. Add all the whole spices & let them sizzle for a minute. 5. Add the onions and green chilies. Stir fry them on a medium heat for 5 to 6 mins. When they begin to brown, reduce the heat slightly and cook further until they turn deep golden/ brown but not burnt. 6. Stir in the ginger garlic paste and saute until aromatic, for a minute. Add the mint leaves and marinated chicken. Stir fry on a medium heat for 5 minutes, until the chicken turns pale. (If using chicken breast cut down to 2 to 3 mins). 7. Meanwhile pour water to another small pot and add salt. Stir and taste test it. It should be slightly salty. Heat this water on another burner. 8. Add fennel or coriander powder, tomatoes to the chicken and cook until all of the moisture from tomatoes evaporates. It is essential to cook until the raw flavor from tomatoes has gone. 9. Pour hot salted water to the pot/cooker and bring it to a rolling boil. 10. Add drained rice and mix gently. Cover partially and bring the water to a rolling boil on a medium high heat (very important step). 11. Cooking in a Pot or Dutch oven: Cover the pot completely and cook on low to medium-low heat for 7 to 10 mins, until the rice is cooked through & all of the water is absorbed. Let chicken pulao rest for another 10 mins. 12. Stovetop Pressure Cooker: Cover the pressure cooker & cook on a medium heat until you hear 1 whistle. Let the pressure drop naturally before you open the lid. 13. Taste test and add more salt if you want and squeeze the lemon juice over the salt to dissolve easily. Garnish chicken pulao with coriander leaves. Use a rice paddle to serve chicken pulao. Onion raita, mint raita or cucumber raita go well with this. 14. Press SAUTE button. Pour oil to the inner pot of the Instant pot. 15. Add all the whole spices & saute for 30 to 60 seconds. Then add the onions and chilli. Saute until transparent or golden. Add the ginger garlic and stir fry for a minute, until aromatic. 16. Next add mint & chicken. Saute for 3 to 4 mins until chicken turns pale. 17. Add fennel or coriander powder & tomatoes. Saute until the tomatoes turn mushy. Ensure the moisture dries up at this stage. 18. Drain the water from the rice completely and add it to the pot. 19. Also pour hot water/ stock or coconut milk and add salt. Mix everything well. 20. Press CANCEL button. Scrape off the bottom of the pot with a spatula to release any bits of spices stuck in there. 21. Secure the Instant pot with the lid. Position the steam release handle to sealing. 22. Press the PRESSURE COOK button (high pressure) for 5 mins. 23. When the IP finishes, it will beep. Allow the pressure to release naturally for 2 to 3 mins. 24. Then release the rest of the pressure manually with the help of a spoon by moving the steam vent from sealing to venting. 25. Remove the steel insert and let cool down for a while. Gently fluff up with a fork. Do not stir the chicken pulao a lot when it is too hot. Garnish with coriander leaves.

title: Chicken Sandwich Recipe
ingredients: 18 grams chicken shredded roast boiled chicken, 1 tablespoon oil, 1.5 to 2 tablespoons mayonnaise, 1.25 tablespoons sour cream, 0.25 teaspoon dry mustard, 0.1875 teaspoon black pepper crushed, 0.75 tablespoon lemon juice, 0.25 cup lettuce, 2 tablespoons cilantro, 2 tablespoons red onion, 4 slices bread, 2 tablespoons unsalted butter, 0.25 teaspoon paprika, 0.25 teaspoon garlic powder, 0.25 teaspoon onion powder, 0.1875 teaspoon black pepper, 0.25 teaspoon salt, 0.25 teaspoon garam masala
instructions: 1. If you want, butter the bread slices on both the sides and toast until crisp. You can do this on a pan or in a toaster/ oven or air fryer. 2. For the chicken filling or salad you will need the following: 3. 2 tablespoons cilantro coriander leaves or 1 tbsp parsley or 1 tsp dill (fine chopped) 4. 2 tablespoons red onion fine chopped 5. Here are the spices to season the chicken. This combination of spices is somewhat similar to a steak seasoning but not the same because this has garam masala. You may replace this with your favorite seasoning. If you are using roast chicken, you may leave out this. 6. To make 2 sandwiches/servings you will need -180 grams (6.5 ounces) boneless chicken (breast/thigh/any). If you have roasted or cooked/boiled chicken then use 1 cup (4 ounces, 140 grams) and leave out the next few steps of roasting. But if it is cold from the refrigerator, make sure you heat it very well. 7. Heat your pan with 1 tablespoon oil. Place chicken in the pan & let cook for 3 to 5 minutes, depending on the thickness. Then turn the pieces to the other side. After the chicken is cooked for 5 minutes, place 1 tablespoon of butter. This keeps your chicken moist and prevents from drying out. 8. When the chicken is fully done, you should be able to cut the thickest part of the chicken easily with a spatula or a butter knife. This is the indication it is fully cooked. 9. Turn down the flame to low and stir in all the spices. Mix well and turn off. Taste test and add more salt or spices to your taste. Cool this completely. 10. Shred/cut the chicken to smaller bits using a wooden spatula or a butter knife. (if using a nonstick take the chicken to a board and chop) 11. Transfer the chicken to a mixing bowl. Add mayo, sour cream or Greek yogurt or cream cheese. Start with smaller amounts 1 tablespoon each. 12. Add chopped lettuce, cilantro, onions, mustard, black pepper and lemon juice. 13. Mix well and adjust the seasonings to taste. The mixture should be thick and not runny. As it sits, the mixture will release some moisture from onions and lettuce, so make sure it is thick. 14. Spread ¼ tsp mayo/ yogurt on the bread slice and place the lettuce so it holds the lettuce well. Spread the chicken filling. 15. Cover with another slice of toasted bread. Slice the Chicken Sandwich. Serve the chicken sandwich immediately.

title: Chicken Shawarma Recipe
ingredients: 25 grams chicken, 1.5 tablespoon extra virgin olive oil, 1 tablespoon garlic, 0.625 teaspoon paprika, 1 teaspoon cumin powder, 1 teaspoon coriander, 0.41666666666666663 teaspoon cardamoms, 0.25 teaspoon cinnamon, 0.375 teaspoon crushed black pepper, 0.125 teaspoon turmeric, 0.5 teaspoon salt, 2 tablespoons Greek Yogurt, 1.5 teaspoon lemon juice, 1 onion, 1 tomato
instructions: 1. Mix all the ingredients for marinade. Add lemon juice only if needed. Taste the marinade and add more salt if needed. 2. Pat dry chicken with kitchen tissues and cut to 1½ inch size pieces. Add the chicken to the marinade. Cover and rest for 12 to 24 hours in the refrigerator. 3. Preheat the oven to 200 C or 400 F for at least 15 mins. 4. Prepare a grilling tray - spread a foil and grease it. 5. Place the chicken pieces, ½ inch apart, over the tray along with onions and tomatoes. 6. Grill for 15 to 20 mins depending on the size of the pieces. After 10 mins turn them to the other side with a tong. 7. Move to the top rack and broil for 2 to 3 mins. Shred the chicken in the tray and serve. 8. Heat a pan with half tbsp olive oil on a high heat. 9. When the pan is hot. Place the chicken and turn to cook on all the sides evenly. 10. When the chicken is tender and cooked through, shred it on the pan with a wooden spatula. 11. Cook further for 1 to 2 mins. Set this aside and use the same pan to grill quartered layered onions and deseeded tomatoes. 12. Serve chicken shawarma as a appetizer with yogurt garlic sauce (recipe in step by step pics section). 13. Spread sauce on the pita bread, place lettuce and then chicken, grilled onions, tomatoes and roll it up.

title: Chicken soup recipe | How to make Indian chicken soup
ingredients: 25 gms chicken, 1 carrot, 0.5 cup Sweet corn, 1 potato, 1 cup any vegetables of your choice, 2 tbsp Lemon juice, 1 tbsp ginger, 1 tbsp garlic, 1 tsp ghee, 1 bay leaf, 4 pepper corn, 2 inch cinnamon piece
instructions: 1. Add chicken, potatoes, mixed vegetables, and 3 cup water to a cooker or pot. 2. Tear the bay leaf to pieces and break the cinnamon. Add them along with pepper corn. 3. Pressure cook for 3 whistles or boil until the chicken is tender & falls off the bone. 4. Set the chicken aside. Shred the meat and discard the bones. 5. Heat a deep pan with oil, sauté ginger and garlic for a minute. 6. Add carrots and sweet corn fry for a min. 7. Strain the chicken stock to the pan and boil for a while until the corn and carrots are tender. 8. Add salt and the shredded chicken. Turn off. 9. Cool down the chicken soup a bit then squeeze in the lemon juice and add more pepper powder. 10. Serve chicken soup warm. You can also add some boiled noodles to this.

title: Chicken Spring Rolls
ingredients: 1 tsp garlic, 3 cups cabbage, 1 medium carrot, 0.5 cup bell peppers, 2 tbsp oil, 25 grams chicken, 0.5 tsp pepper, 0.5 tsp Chinese five spice powder, salt, 1 tbsp soya sauce, 8 spring roll sheets, oil, 1 tsp corn flour, 3 tbsp water
instructions: 1. Pour 1 tbsp oil to a pan. Saute chicken until pale in color for 2 to 3 mins. 2. Sprinkle salt as needed. 3. Cover the pan and cook until fully done. Set this aside. 4. Pour 1 tbsp oil. Next fry the garlic just for 1 min. 5. Add cabbage, carrots and bell pepper. Fry on the highest flame for 2 to 3 mins. Do not overcook. Veggies have to be crunchy. 6. Add the chicken. Next pour soya sauce. 7. Sprinkle pepper and spice powder too. Saute just for another minute. 8. Cool the stuffing. There should not be excess moisture in the stuffing. You can check the salt and add more if needed. 9. Next mix together corn flour and water to make a thin mixture. You can also use plain water to dampen the edges of the sheets for sealing. 10. Keep the spring rolls sheets at room temperature for about 30 to 40 mins. If they are stuck to each other, then you can place a damp cloth over them. They will come off easily. 11. Spread 1 spring roll sheet. Place 2 tbsp of chicken stuffing. 12. Next cover the filling with one edge. Roll it till the center. Bring the 2 sides over to the center. 13. Roll again. Next brush the prepared corn starch over the edges. You can also just apply water to the edges. 14. Roll up and make sure the edges are well sealed. Repeat making all of them. Keep them covered until you fry. 15. Heat oil in a pan for deep frying. 16. When the oil is hot, drop the spring rolls to the oil and fry on a medium heat until crisp. You may not get them golden always since it depends on the brand of sheets used. 17. Remove them to a cooling rack or a steel colander. 18. Serve chicken spring rolls hot with ketchup or schezwan sauce.

title: Chicken Tikka Recipe (Tandoori Tikka Kabab)
ingredients: 1 pound bone-less chicken, 1 teaspoon salt, 1.25 teaspoons Kashmiri Red chilli powder, 0.25 teaspoon turmeric, 0.75 teaspoon roasted cumin powder, 2 teaspoons coriander powder, 1.125 teaspoon garam masala, 1 teaspoon chaat masala, 0.5 teaspoon black pepper, 1 tablespoon kasuri methi, 1 tablespoon lemon juice, 1.5 tablespoon Ginger garlic paste, 1 tablespoon oil, 0.5 cup Greek yogurt, 1.5 tablespoons butter, 2 grams bell peppers, 1 medium onion
instructions: 1. Cut chicken to 1½ inch pieces and add them to a mixing bowl. If possible pat dry the chicken with a few tissues to remove any excess moisture. 2. Add chilli powder, coriander, cumin powder, garam masala, chaat masala, salt, kasuri methi and lemon juice. Mix well and rest for at least 10 to 15 mins. This helps the chicken to absorb the flavors well & this step is optional. 3. Next add ginger garlic paste, oil and yogurt. Mix well. Cover and marinate for a minimum of 8 hours and up to 48 hours in the refrigerator. If you are in a rush you may cut down the resting time to 20 mins. 4. If using bamboo skewers make sure you soak them for 30 mins and later pat dry before use. 5. Prepare a deep baking tray and line with aluminum foil, if you want to grill these in the oven. Preheat the oven to 240 C or 460 F for at least 15 mins. 6. Optional - If using bell peppers and onions, add them to the chicken marinade and mix well. 7. Thread the bell peppers, onions (if using) and chicken to the skewers in alternating patterns. (check photos in the post) 8. Place them on the prepared tray, spacing them apart and bake the chicken tikka for 9 mins. With the help of tongs, turn them to the other side and bake for another 9 to 10 mins, until completely cooked. Every oven is different so the cook time may vary. 9. Turn on the broil mode on your oven and broil for 2 mins. If your oven does not have this setting, simply move the chicken to the top most rack. This chars the chicken slightly. 10. Bring the tray out of the oven and check if the chicken is cooked thru. For this cut the chicken tikka to 2 parts, meat should not be pink in color in the center of the tikka. If it looks pinkish, it means it is still undercooked & bake for a little longer. 11. Brush them with butter. Serve chicken tikka kebab with Cilantro chutney, Mint chutney or raita. You can also sprinkle some chaat masala. 12. Place the chicken tikka skewers in the air fryer basket or in a pan that fits your basket. 13. Air fry for 6 mins at 200 C or 400 F. Turn them to the other side and air fry for another 6 mins. Brush them with butter (or leftover marinade or mustard oil) and air fry for another 2 mins. 14. I prefer to brush the air fryer chicken tikka again with butter before serving. 15. Brush your pan with oil or butter and place the chicken tikka over the pan. Grill them on a medium heat for 4 to 5 mins, turn them to the other side and grill for another 4 to 5 mins until fully cooked and the internal temperature reaches 165 F or 74 C. 16. If you want to char the chicken, take the chicken tikka on metal skewers, over direct fire and grill for 1 to 2 mins. 17. If you want to replicate tandoori flavors, Place the hot grilled chicken tikka along with the tray on your kitchen counter. Place a small steel bowl in the center of this tray. 18. Hold a wood coal with a tong, over direct fire until hot and red. Put that in the steel bowl in your tray. Pour 1 tsp ghee over the hot coal. Immediately you will see a lot of smoke. Cover the tray with another tray or seal with foil to trap the smoke. 19. Leave it for 3 to 5 mins, depending on how smoky you want it to be. Your smoky chicken tikka is done!

title: Chicken varuval (Chicken chukka)
ingredients: 5 grams bone-in-chicken, 7 shallots, 1.5 teaspoon ginger garlic paste, 0.125 teaspoon turmeric, 0.3333333333333333 teaspoon sea salt, 2 tablespoons sesame oil, 1 tablespoon lemon juice, 2 sprigs curry leaves, 1 tablespoon coriander seeds, 0.75 teaspoon cumin seeds, 4 cloves 4 cloves, 1 inch cinnamon piece, 2 green cardamom, 0.375 teaspoon black pepper corn, 1 teaspoon fennel seeds, 4 red chilies, 1 tablespoon coconut, 1 tablespoon roasted gram
instructions: 1. Make the spice blend: On a medium heat, dry roast red chilies, coriander seeds, cumin seeds, fennel seeds, cloves, cardamom, cinnamon and black pepper until aromatic & crunchy. Add 1 sprig curry leaves, dried coconut and roasted gram. Toast for a min until the leaves turn crisp. Cool down and make a fine powder. 2. Preparation: Fine chop, crush or pulse onions in a chopper. 3. Temper: Heat oil in a pan. Add 1 sprig curry leaves and crushed onions. Saute until light golden, till the onions lose the raw flavor for about 5 mins. Add the ginger garlic and saute for 40 to 50 seconds. 4. Add the chicken, salt and turmeric. Saute on a high flame till the chicken turns white in color. This takes around 2 to 3 mins. Cover and cook for 3 to 4 mins to see if the chicken releases moisture. 5. Make chicken chukka: Stir in the powdered masala and let cook until the chicken is tender and cooked through. If the pan is dry, pour ¼ cup hot water and cook. This is optional and depends on how much moisture your chicken releases. Sometimes you may not even need it. 6. Open the lid and evaporate the excess moisture, so the masala clings on to the chicken. Taste test to adjust salt and add lemon juice. Garnish with fine chopped coriander leaves. 7. Serving suggestion: Serve chicken chukka/varuval with rice, alongside rasam or sambar. It also goes well with ghee rice, chapati or dosa.

title: Chickpea Curry Recipe
ingredients: 2 tablespoons oil, 3 cups chickpeas, 1.5 cup onions, 1 tablespoon ginger, 1 tablespoon garlic, 1.5 cups tomato puree, 1.5 teaspoon salt, 1 teaspoon Kashmiri red chili powder, 0.25 teaspoon turmeric, 1 teaspoon cumin powder, 1 tablespoon coriander powder, 1 tablespoon curry powder, 0.75 teaspoon garam masala, 1 teaspoon sugar, 0.75 cups water, 1.25 cups coconut milk, 0.25 cup coriander leaves
instructions: 1. In a large pan, heat 2 tablespoons of oil over medium heat. Add 1 ½ cups of chopped onions. Cook the onions until they turn golden, stirring every 2 minutes to prevent burning. 2. Add 1 tablespoon finely chopped ginger and 1 tablespoon of finely chopped garlic. Sauté with the onions until you can smell the garlic and ginger, about 1 to 2 minutes. Remember to stir to prevent burning. 3. Turn the heat to low and add 4. ½ teaspoons salt 1 teaspoon red chili powder or mild smoked paprika ¼ teaspoon ground turmeric 1 teaspoon ground cumin 1 tablespoon ground coriander 1 tablespoon curry powder (or 1 teaspoon garam masala). 5. Toast the spices for 30 to 40 seconds and stir to keep them from burning. 6. Once the spices become aromatic, stir in the 1½ cups fresh or 1 cup canned/bottled tomato purée. 7. Cook it on medium heat for 4 to 5 minutes to remove the raw flavor of the tomatoes. 8. Add the 3 cups of canned or pre-cooked chickpeas, ¾ cup water or chickpea stock, and 1¼ cups coconut milk. If using canned chickpeas, make sure they’re tender and you’ve drained and rinsed the chickpeas before adding them. 9. Cover the pan and simmer the curry on low heat for 15 minutes, stirring every 5 minutes so the bottom of the curry doesn’t burn. 10. You’ll know it’s done when the chickpeas are melt-in-your-mouth soft and have absorbed the flavors of the curry. If they taste bland or too firm, cook at least another 5 to 10 minutes or until ready, adding more water if required. 11. Stir in ½ teaspoon garam masala and 1 teaspoon sugar (optional), then taste test and add either more garam masala (up to another ½ teaspoon if using store bought) or salt to your preference. If you want you may add more coconut milk to your taste. Turn off the heat and keep the curry covered until serving time; this helps retain the flavors of chickpea curry. 12. Once the curry cools slightly, garnish with ¼ cup cilantro leaves. Top the curry with some freshly squeezed lemon juice and serve.

title: Chilli fish
ingredients: 0.25 cup Oil, 25 gms fish fillet, 1.5 tbsps corn starch, 1.5 tbsps all-purpose flour, 0.125 tsp salt, 0.75 tsp soya sauce, 0.25 tsp black pepper, 1 tsp ginger paste, 1 tsp garlic paste, 3 garlic cloves, 2 stalks spring onions, 0.25 cup green bell pepper, 0.25 cup red bell pepper, 1 tbsp soya sauce, 1 tsp vinegar, 1.5 tbsp red chili sauce, 2 tbsp water, 1 tsp sugar, 0.75 tsp corn starch
instructions: 1. Wash and cut the fish to cube sized pieces. 2. Add ginger garlic, soya sauce and pepper powder. Marinate and Set aside for at least 10 mins. 3. Stir to a bowl vinegar, sugar, soya sauce and red chili sauce. Set this aside. Add more or less sauce as needed. To get a nice red color you can also add little red chili paste. I mix little red chili powder with very little water and then use it. 4. Heat oil in a pan for deep or shallow frying or can even bake these. 5. Add corn flour, all-purpose, little salt and mix well. Sprinkle very little water and mix well to coat the fish with flour. Do not make it runny. Refer the pic. Fish must be coated with a thin layer of batter. 6. When the oil is hot, reduce the flame slightly and then fry these pieces to golden. Drain these on kitchen tissue. 7. Once you finish frying, drain off the oil from the pan retaining 2 tbsp. oil in the same pan 8. Add garlic and fry till you get an aroma. Add capsicum, layered onions (white parts of spring onions) and fry on high for 2 mins. Onions and capsicums must be crunchy (partially cooked) and not soft. 9. Stir the prepared sauce mix from step 3 with a spoon for uniform mixing. Pour this to the pan along with 2 tbsps water. Allow the sauces to bubble. 10. Add fried fish & stir fry on the highest flame for 2 mins. Garnish with spring onions More fish recipe s Apollo fish baked salmon recipe 11. Prepare the sauce for seasoning – Add the soya sauce, chilli sauce, sugar and vinegar to a bowl and set aside. 12. Rinse the fish and cut to cubes. 13. Add ¾ tsp soya sauce, 1 tsp ginger 1 tsp garlic paste, and ¼ tsp black pepper. Mix and set aside for 10 mins. 14. Heat a pan with oil for deep or shallow frying or preheat the oven and bake. 15. Add 1 ½ tbsps each of corn starch, all-purpose flour, salt to the fish. Sprinkle some water and mix to coat the fish with flour. The batter should not be runny. Fish has to be coated with a thin layer of batter. 16. Check if the oil is hot enough by dropping a small amount of batter. It has to rise without turning brown. Gently slide the fish pieces to the hot oil. 17. Fry them to golden on a medium flame. Drain these on a absorbent tissue. 18. After you finish frying fish, transfer the oil from the pan to a bowl retaining 2 tbsps in the same pan. 19. Add garlic and fry till it gets fragrant. Add capsicum, onions and fry on high flame for about 2 mins. Onions and capsicums should be crunchy (not over cooked) and not soft. 20. Stir in the sauces we made at step 1 and pour 2 tbsps water. Give a good stir and taste it. Add more sauces or salt if needed. 21. When the sauces begin to bubble, add the fried fish & give a stir. Stir fry on the highest flame for 2 mins. To make a gravy version, double the sauces and stir ¾ tsp corn flour with half cup water and pour it to the pan. Boil until thick then add the fried fish. 22. Garnish with chopped spring onions

title: Chilli Paneer Recipe (Dry & Gravy)
ingredients: 2 grams paneer, 0.25 teaspoon red chilli powder, 3 tablespoons cornstarch, 3 tablespoons all-purpose flour, 0.125 teaspoon black pepper, 0.125 teaspoon salt, water, 3.5 tablespoon oil, 1 tablespoon soya sauce, 2 tablespoons red chilli sauce, 2 tablespoons tomato ketchup, 0.5 teaspoon vinegar, 0.5 teaspoon red chili powder, 1 teaspoon sugar, 1 tablespoon garlic, 2 tablespoon spring onions, 2 tablespoon spring onion, 0.5 cup capsicum cubed, 1 medium onion, 1 green chilies, 0.25 teaspoon black pepper, 1 teaspoon cornstarch, 0.5 cup water
instructions: 1. Add cornstarch, all-purpose flour, red chilli powder, crushed black pepper and salt to a mixing bowl. 2. Pour water, about 6 tablespoons and begin to mix to make a batter. It should be neither too thick nor too runny. You may add 1 to 2 tablespoons more water if required. The batter has to be smooth without any lumps. 3. On a medium flame, heat 3 to 4 tablespoons oil in a deep pan for shallow frying. 4. Add 200 to 250 grams of cubed paneer to the batter. Gently coat paneer with the batter. 5. Check if the oil is hot enough by dropping a small portion of batter. It must sizzle and come up to the surface without turning brown. This is the correct temperature. 6. Pick up each batter coated paneer with a spoon and gently slide them in the hot oil. Space them apart from each other, do not crowd. Do not disturb them for a minute or until the batter firms up. 7. Later stir and fry them on a medium heat on all the sides until crispy. Drain to a kitchen tissue or a steel colander. 8. Option 1 - For dry chilli paneer : Stir in 1 tsp cornstarch to a bowl and mix with half cup water. Stir well & set aside. To another small cup, add 2 tbsps chilli sauce, 1 tbsp soya sauce, 2 tbsps ketchup, 1 tsp sugar, half tsp vinegar and half tsp red chilli powder (optional). Set this aside. 9. Option 2 - For chilli paneer gravy : Add 2 tsp corn flour & 1 cup water to a bowl. Stir well and make a lump free slurry. Keep aside. Add in 2 tablespoon soya sauce, 4 tbsp chilli sauce, 1 teaspoon of vinegar, 3 tablespoons tomato ketchup and little more salt & sugar. For color add ¾ tsp red chilli powder. Stir well and set this aside. 10. Remove excess oil from the fry pan retaining 1 tbsp. Fry one tablespoon finely chopped garlic until it smells good. 11. Then add in 2 tablespoons of spring onion whites and 1 medium onion cubed & layers separated. 12. Also add in 1 slit green chili and half cup diced capsicum. Saute on a high flame for 2 mins. 13. Stir the prepared sauce in the bowl and pour it to the pan. 14. Next stir the prepared corn slurry and pour it to the pan. Mix every thing & cook on a medium heat till the sauce thickens. 15. Check the taste of the sauce. You can add more sauces or salt if you wish to adjust to your taste. 16. If you want to thin down the sauce, just add some hot water to get the desired consistency. Turn off the heat and cool down slightly. 17. Add crushed black pepper & fried paneer cubes to the chilli sauce and mix. Add the spring onion greens. 18. Serve paneer chilli hot as a appetizer. You can also serve this with hakka noodles or fried rice.

title: Chilli Parotta
ingredients: 2 parathas, 0.5 cup onions, 0.5 cup bell pepper, 1.5 tablespoons oil, 1 teaspoon ginger, 1 teaspoon garlic, 1 green chilli, 0.875 tablespoon soya sauce, 1.25 tablespoon red chilli sauce, 0.1875 teaspoon salt, 0.25 teaspoon red chilli powder, 0.25 teaspoon garam masala, 0.25 cup water, 1 tablespoon coriander leaves
instructions: 1. Cut 2 parathas or 3 rotis to ¾ inch cubes and set aside. Avoid using dry and hard flatbreads. If they are hard, place them in a bowl, cover and steam them first in a steamer or cooker. 2. Heat 1½ tablespoons of oil to a thick bottomed pan on medium. Add 1 teaspoon of chopped ginger, 1 teaspoon of chopped garlic and 1 sliced green chilli. Sauté until aromatic but be careful not to brown it. 3. Now add ½ cup of cubed onions and ½ cup of cubed bell pepper. 4. Stir and sauté on a medium heat, until the veggies are slightly tender and yet crunchy. 5. Add ¾ to 1 tablespoon of soya sauce or tamari according to your preference. Mix well. 6. Then add 1¼ tablespoon of red chilli sauce or any hot sauce that you prefer. I used sweet red chilli sauce. 7. Add ¼ cup of water to the masala and bring it to a boil on a medium heat. 8. Finally add the following ingredients: ⅛ to ¼ teaspoon salt (skip if your parotta is salted) ¼ teaspoon of red chilli powder ¼ teaspoon of garam masala Taste and then add extra salt if required, as the sauces contain salt. 9. Mix well and cook for a few minutes. Taste test and adjust spices and sauce as required. When the liquid starts bubbling and the masala becomes slightly thick, add the chopped parotta or roti pieces. 10. Mix well quickly and fry until heated up. 11. Make sure there is some liquid left or your chilli parotta will be dry and chewy. Remove from heat and add 1 tablespoon of finely chopped coriander leaves. Mix well to combine. Serve chilli parotta hot or warm with chutney, raita , salna or any other gravy or stew.

title: Chilli Potato Recipe (Chilli Aloo)
ingredients: 3 medium potatoes, 2 tablespoons corn starch, 0.25 teaspoon red chilli powder, 0.25 teaspoon salt, 0.3333333333333333 cup oil, 1 tablespoon oil, 1.5 tablespoon garlic, 1 medium onion, 2.5 spring onions, 0.75 cup capsicum, 1 green chili, 1 teaspoon sugar, 0.75 tablespoon soya sauce, 2 tablespoons red chilli sauce, 0.625 teaspoon red chili powder, 0.625 teaspoon vinegar, salt
instructions: 1. Peel potatoes and cut each to half inch slices, Slice each to few half inch sticks. So you will have each stick of about half by half inch thickness. Immerse them in a bowl of water for 30 minutes & drain the water completely. 2. Boil them in fresh water for 3 to 4 mins until they are slightly cooked but firm. Drain them to a colander. 3. Sprinkle flour, red chili powder and salt over the drained potatoes. Toss well to coat the flour evenly. If it seems too dry, sprinkle some water. 4. Heat oil in a deep pan. When hot enough slide the potatoes one by one to hot oil. Deep fry on a medium heat undisturbed for 2 to 3 mins, later stir and fry until crisp & golden. 5. For extra crispness, you may remove them from oil and refry on a low flame. Later remove them to a wired rack or steel colander. 6. With a clean cloth wipe dry the soaked potatoes. Spray some oil all over the them. Sprinkle salt, red chilli powder and flour. Mix gently to coat. If it looks too dry, drizzle some oil and mix. 7. Air Fryer: Spread them in the basket in a single layer and air fry at 400 F or 200 C for 8 to 9 mins. Shake them & repeat for another 8 to 9 mins until crisp and golden. 8. In the Oven: Preheat the oven to 200 C or 400 F. Grease or line a baking tray & arrange the potatoes. Bake them for 23 to 30 minutes until crisp and golden. Adjust the baking time as it depends on the size of your potatoes and oven. 9. Optional for extra color and heat: Mix together red chilli powder, red chilli sauce and 2 tsps water to a smooth paste. Keep this red chilli sauce aside. 10. Heat one tbsp oil on a high heat in a wok or pan. Fry garlic and chilli until they smell good. 11. Add onions and stir fry for a minute followed by capsicums. Fry until they turn slightly tender yet crunchy. 12. Add the sugar, red chilli sauce, soya sauce, vinegar & 2 to 3 tablespoons water. Let the sauces begin to bubble. 13. When the sauce thickens taste the sauce and adjust more salt and sauce if needed. 14. Turn off and cool down slightly. Add fried potatoes and toss. Add spring onion greens. 15. Serve chilli potato hot or warm immediately as they lose the crispness if rested. Serve as an appetizer or as a side with noodles and fried rice.

title: Chilli Prawns (Shrimp Manchurian)
ingredients: 25 grams deveined prawns, 1.5 tablespoon sugar, 0.5 teaspoon salt, 1 cup water, 0.25 teaspoon black pepper, 2 tablespoon corn flour, 2 tablespoon all purpose flour, 1.5 tablespoon oil, 1 tablespoon olive, 0.5 cup capsicum, 0.25 cup spring onions, 1 tablespoon garlic, 1.5 green chili, 1.5 tablespoon Asian Chili sauce, 1.25 teaspoon soya sauce, 1.25 teaspoon rice vinegar, 1 teaspoon red chili powder, 0.5 teaspoon black pepper, 0.5 teaspoon sugar
