instructions: 1. Add ½ teaspoon salt, 1½ tablespoons sugar and 1 cup of water to a bowl. Dissolve the sugar completely and add the deveined shrimp. Use about 9 ounces (250 grams) shrimp. I used about 11 tiger prawns here. Let rest for 20 mins while you prepare the following ingredients: 2. 1 tablespoon (4 medium) garlic coves chopped 3. Drain the water completely from the prawns after 20 mins. Mix together 2 tablespoons corn flour, 2 tablespoons all purpose flour and ¼ teaspoon pepper in a small bowl and sprinkle all over the prawns. Coat them well and let rest for 10 mins so the dry flour absorbs the excess moisture. 4. Heat 1½ tablespoon oil in a pan until hot enough. Place the prawns one by one in the hot oil. 5. Let cook until they begin to swirl. Using a tong, turn them to the other side. Cook until they swirl to C shape, turn crisp and cooked through. It only takes about 3 to 4 mins as they are brined. Remove to a plate. 6. Clean up the pan with a paper towel and add 1 tablespoon oil. Saute garlic until fragrant. Add chilies, capsicum, onion cubes or spring onions whites and a pinch of salt. Saute on a high flame just for one to 2 mins until the bell peppers are half cooked yet retain their crunch. 7. Add 1½ tablespoon red chili sauce, 1 teaspoon red chili powder mixed with 2 tbsps water, 1 to 1½ teaspoon soya sauce, 1 to 1½ teaspoon rice vinegar, ½ teaspoon sugar, ½ teaspoon pepper and 2 tablespoons of water. 8. Bring to a boil and let cook until bubbling. Taste test to adjust sauces as per your preference. 9. Switch off the stove and add the fried prawns. Toss well and garnish with green onions. Serve chili prawns immediately to enjoy the crust. Tandoori Shrimp Prawn Curry (Praw... Prawns Fry Prawn biryani 10. S... Shrimp Pakora (Sh... Shrimp Tikka Masa...

title: Chinese Chilli Mushroom Recipe
ingredients: 1 cup button mushrooms, 0.5 cup bell pepper, 0.3333333333333333 cup spring onions, 1 green chili, 1 tablespoon garlic, 1.25 tablespoon olive oil, 1 tablespoon soya sauce, 1.5 to 2 tablespoons chili sauce, 0.875 tablespoon tomato ketchup, 0.375 teaspoon sugar, 0.25 teaspoon black pepper, 2.5 tablespoons water
instructions: 1. Stir in soya sauce, tomato ketchup and red chili sauce in a small bowl. Taste test and add salt or sugar if you want. 2. Rinse mushrooms well under running water. Slice or halve or quarter them. 3. Heat oil in a wok/skillet, on a medium high heat. 4. Add garlic and sauté for 30 to 60 seconds until aromatic. Do not brown it. 5. Next add mushrooms and slit green chilli. Saute for 2 mins. 6. Add the capsicum and half of the spring onions, toss and fry till the capsicums are almost cooked yet crunchy. 7. Make sure mushrooms are cooked yet not mushy. 8. Add the sauces to the skillet all over and pour 2 to 3 tablespoons of water. Quickly stir well. The water immediately blends with the sauce and evaporates. 9. Next sprinkle black pepper. Toss and fry for another min or until the sauce thickens. 10. Check salt and add more sauces to suit your taste. Chili sauce & ketchup already have salt so I have not used. Off the heat. 11. Serve chilli mushroom with hakka noodles or fried rice.

title: chocolate poke cake
ingredients: 1.5 cups wheat flour, 4 tablespoons cocoa powder, 1 teaspoon Baking soda, 0.5 teaspoon salt, 1 cup sugar, 1 cup milk, 0.3333333333333333 cup melted butter, 1 tablespoon vinegar, 1 tablespoon vanilla extract, 6 tablespoons sugar, 3 tablespoons cocoa powder, 0.75 cup water, 0.5 teaspoon vanilla extract, 1 cup whipping cream, 1 tablespoon powdered sugar, 0.5 teaspoon vanilla extract
instructions: 1. Grease a 6 inch square cake pan. Preheat the oven to 170 C for at least 15 mins. 2. Place a sieve over a mixing bowl and add flour, baking soda, salt and cocoa powder. Sieve it at least thrice. Add sugar and mix it well. 3. Make a well in the center and pour water or milk, oil or melted butter, vanilla and vinegar.Mix the wet ingredients gently and then slowly incorporate the flour with the liquid ingredients. Do not over mix but ensure there are no lumps in the batter. 4. Pour this to the grease tray and bake for 23 to 25 mins. When done a tester inserted comes out clean. Cool down the cake in the pan itself over a wire rack until it cools down a bit but still warm. 5. Mix together cocoa powder, sugar and water in a pot. Mix well to break the lumps and boil the syrup until it thickens. It took me about 7 to 9 minutes. The mixture must turn thick & coat the back of a spoon. If using palm jaggery or jaggery, just filter the chocolate syrup and use. Cool this a bit. 6. Chill the bowls and the beaters for few hours. Pour the whipping cream to the chilled bowl. Begin to whip on a low speed initially and then increase the speed to medium. When the mixture turns bubbly add the sugar and beat until you see stiff peaks. Take care not to over whip as it can turn to butter. Keep this chilled until needed. 7. When the temperature of the cake comes down, poke the cake several times with a chop stick about ¾ inch apart. Avoid poking the cake towards the edges. Make several holes all over the cake. Ensure the holes are deep reaching the base. 8. The temperature of the chocolate syrup should have come down slightly but still warm. Mix well and pour the syrup all over the cake. The syrup must go through the holes. Set this aside to cool the cake completely. You can also chill it in the fridge. 9. Spread the whipped cream generously all over the cake. Decorate with some sprinkles or chocolate shavings. Chill the chocolate poke cake until served. Slice the cake when needed and serve.

title: Chocolate Sandwich
ingredients: 4 slices bread, 2 tablespoons Butter, 0.25 cup chocolate chips, 0.25 teaspoon cinnamon, 3 tablespoons walnuts, 1 ripe banana
instructions: 1. Place 2 bread slices on a board and spread mini chocolate chips or chocolate of choice on one slice. Layer the chopped nuts all over the chocolate or spread peanut butter over the other slice. 2. If you prefer sprinkle some ground cinnamon and place the sliced banana (or maple syrup if required). 3. Cover the chocolate filled bread with the other slice of bread. Spread ½ tablespoon butter over the chocolate sandwich 4. Melt ½ tablespoon butter in the pan. 5. Place the sandwich gently in the hot pan, with the buttered side up. Let toast on a low heat until the bread turns crisp. Gently turn it to the other side with a spatula. 6. Immediately sprinkle chocolate chips on top of the hot toasted chocolate sandwich. The chocolate begins to melt, spread it with a butter knife or a spoon. 7. Remove to a rack once the sandwich is crisp at the bottom. Garnish with chopped nuts or chopped white chocolate. 8. Add ½ tablespoon butter to your preheated sandwich toaster and place the sandwiches with the buttered side up. Cover and toast them until golden and crisp. 9. You can also place the prepared sandwich (buttered on both the sides) in the air fryer. Air fry for 5 mins at 350 F or 180 C. Do not air fry for too long the chocolate can burn on the sides and the bread turns too hard.

title: Chocolate Sponge Cake
ingredients: 0.5 cup all-purpose flour, 2.5 tbsp cocoa, 0.125 tsp salt, 0.5 tsp baking powder, 5 tbsp sugar, 2 eggs, 1 egg yolk, 1 tsp vanilla extract, 4 tbsp oil, 0.5 cup 2 tbsp semi sweet chocolate chips, 1 chocolate bar
instructions: 1. Make sure all the ingredients are at room temperature. 2. Grease & line a 6 inch pan. Alternately grease & dust some cocoa evenly. Invert the pan to remove excess. To make layers for black forest cake you can also use two 6 inch pans. Preheat the oven at 340 F - 170 C for at least 15 mins 3. Sieve together cocoa, flour, baking powder & salt to a mixing bowl. Mix well and keep aside. 4. Break 2 whole eggs to a large mixing bowl. Separate 1 egg yolk and add it as well along with sugar. 5. Begin to beat on a low speed to avoid splashes. Then increase the speed to high and beat until frothy. 6. Pour vanilla & continue to beat until a ribbon consistency is achieved. The eggs turn frothy & foam up. The volume will more than double and will reach a ribbon stage. 7. Lift the whisk & check to find the ribbon consistency. The egg mixture will fall like a ribbon from the whisk when you lift it. If this is not achieved then continue to beat. 8. Next keep beating and pour oil in a slow stream to the egg mixture. Beat the mixture on a high speed just until combined. Do not over beat. 9. The flour needs to be added in 3 small batches. Now sprinkle 1/3 of the dry mixture/ ingredients to the egg mixture. 10. Using a whisk gently mix just until partially incorporated. Do not over mix or beat the batter at this stage as it deflates the mixture & can make a flat dense cake. 11. Repeat adding another 1/3 portion of the flour and mix. Finish off by adding the last part. Mix until just combined well. 12. Transfer to the prepared pans. Tap the pan to the counter twice. 13. Bake at 170 C for 15 mins if using two 6 inch pans. Bake for 20 to 24 mins if using a single 6 inch pan. 14. When the cake is done a tester inserted comes out clean. Transfer the cake pans to a cooling rack and cool down for 15 mins. Invert on a cooling rack and cool completely. 15. Dust some powdered sugar or make a frosting. This chocolate sponge cake can be used to make black forest cake or just melt some chocolate chips and pour it over the cake. 16. I melted little over half a cup of choco chips and frosted the cake. Decorated with some chocolate shavings.

title: Chutney Powder (Pappula Podi)
ingredients: 1 cup peanuts, 0.3333333333333333 cup fried gram, 9 kashmiri dried red chilies, 2 sprigs curry leaves, 1 tablespoon cumin seeds, 2.5 garlic, 1 teaspoon oil
instructions: 1. Dry roast peanuts on a medium flame until golden and crunchy. When they turn aromatic, add fried gram or desiccated coconut. 2. Roast for another minute and remove to a plate and cool. 3. Pour 1 teaspoon oil and heat it. Add curry leaves and dried red chilies. Fry them on a medium heat until crisp. Then turn down the flame to low and add cumin and garlic. Stir just for 30 seconds. 4. Turn off the heat and let them cool down. 5. Once cool, add the curry leaves, garlic, red chilies and cumin to a grinder or spice jar and make a smooth powder. 6. Then peanuts, fried gram and salt. Run the grinder in short intervals of 30 to 50 seconds scraping down the sides. Make sure everything is mixed well. Avoid overgrinding else peanuts will release oil. 7. Taste test and add more salt if needed. 8. Store in an air tight jar. keeps fresh for about 2 to 3 months.

title: Cilantro Chutney (Coriander Chutney)
ingredients: 3 cups cilantro, 1.5 green chilies, 1.5 teaspoon roasted cumin powder, 2.5 medium garlic cloves, 0.5 teaspoon salt, 1 tablespoon lemon juice, 3 tablespoons cold water, 3 tablespoons roasted peanuts
instructions: 1. Rinse cilantro under running water and shake off very well to get rid of the excess moisture. Cut down the stems and roots, discard them. Remove & discard mature stalks and keep the tender/ young ones. Roughly chop leaves & tender stalks. 2. Peel the garlic and make sure they have not sprouted or begun to sprout. 3. Remove the stalks of green chilies. If you prefer low heat, deseed the chilies. 4. Add the peanuts, green chilies, salt, cumin and garlic to the base of the blender jar. If you can't eat spicy and pungent foods, you may start with a little piece of chili and 2 garlic cloves, adjust towards the end. 5. Transfer coriander leaves, lemon juice and water. Start with only a few tablespoons of water (2 tbsps). 6. Blend the ingredients to a smooth cilantro chutney, scraping the sides every 30 to 40 seconds. Add more water only as required while blending. 7. Chutney has to thick, of spreading consistency. If it is too thick add a tbsps more water. 8. Taste test and add more salt, green chilies or lemon juice as required to taste. 9. Transfer cilantro chutney to serving bowl. Serve with any snacks like pakora, samosa and sandwiches. 10. Cover and refrigerate the leftovers for 4 days. You may optionally refresh the chutney the next day with few tbsps of thick strained yogurt. Give a good stir.

title: Classic Chocolate Cake
ingredients: 0.75 cup 2 tbsp flour, 0.25 cup 2 tbsp cocoa powder, 0.5 tsp salt, 0.75 tsp baking powder, 0.75 tsp baking soda, 1 cup sugar, 1 egg, 0.5 cup milk, 0.25 cup oil, 1.5 tsp vanilla extract, 0.5 cup boiling hot water, 0.5 cup butter, 1.75 cup powdered sugar, 4 tbsp cocoa powder, 1.5 tbsp milk, 1 tsp vanilla extract
instructions: 1. Preheat the oven to 175 C or 350 F for at least 15 mins. Grease and line 2 – 6 inch cake pans. You can also make a single layer cake in a 7 or 8 inch pan. You can also just grease and dust with some flour or cocoa. 2. Place a sieve over a mixing bowl. Add flour. 3. Add the rest of the dry ingredients – cocoa, baking powder, baking soda and salt. Sieve them all. 4. Next add sugar. 5. Mix everything very well until uniform. Make a well in the center. 6. Heat half cup water in a pot. (Don’t add the water here). Meanwhile pour milk, egg, oil and vanilla to the dry ingredients. 7. Whisk the wet ingredients first. Then whisk together wet and dry ingredients just until combined. Do not over mix. 8. You will have a slightly thick batter at this stage. 9. Quickly pour the hot water to the batter. The water must be very hot. 10. Immediately whisk the batter just until the water is absorbed and the batter looks uniform. Batter is very runny at this stage. 11. Pour this to the pans. Tap the pan to the counter 2 to 3 times. 12. Bake for 28 to 30 mins or until the tester comes out clean. Please adjust the timing depending on the oven and pan sizes. If baking in a 7 inch pan you may need to bake up to 35 to 38 mins. Cool the pans on a wire rack for 10 mins. 13. Then invert to a cooling rack. Cool completely before frosting.

title: Cluster Beans Recipe (Goruchikkudu Recipe)
ingredients: 25 grams cluster beans, 0.875 cup water, 2 onions, 1 green chilli, 1.5 to 2 tablespoon oil, 0.25 teaspoon mustard, 0.5 teaspoon cumin, 1 sprig curry leaves, 1 teaspoon ginger garlic paste, 0.625 teaspoon red chilli powder, 0.875 teaspoon garam masala, 0.625 teaspoon salt
instructions: 1. Rinse the cluster beans in lot of water and drain them 2. Gently break/ cut one end of the bean. If needed string them by pulling the string lengthwise. 3. Repeat the same with both the ends. Cut them to ½ to ¾ inch sized pieces. I usually hold a bunch of them and chop at one time. 4. Add them to a bowl and pour water. Cover it. 5. Pressure cook for 3 whistles by placing the bowl in the cooker. I do it for 10 mins along with my rice in Instant pot. 6. Meanwhile heat oil in a pan. Add mustard and cumin. 7. When they splutter, add curry leaves, onions and chilies. 8. Saute and fry the onions until deep golden to light brown. 9. Then add ginger garlic paste and saute for 30 to 60 seconds. 10. Stir in red chilli powder, garam masala, salt and turmeric. 11. When the pressure releases from the cooker, remove the cluster beans and check. 12. Option 1 - If the beans are firm yet cooked correctly then transfer the cooked cluster beans along with the stock to the pan. Stir and cook covered on a medium flame until all the water evaporates. 13. Option 2 - If the cluster beans overcooked, then just pour the stock to the pan. Cook until the water is almost absorbed by onions. Then just stir in the cooked cluster beans and stir fry for a while until the masala blends well. 14. Cluster beans should be soft cooked yet should hold shape and not turn mushy. Check salt and add more if needed. 15. Serve goruchikkudu curry/fry with rice, ghee & rasam.

title: Coconut Burfi (Nariyal Barfi)
ingredients: 4.25 cups coconut, 1.5 cups sugar, 1.5 tablespoons ghee, 0.5 teaspoon cardamom powder, 2 tablespoons cashews, 2 cups milk, 1.75 cup desiccated coconut, 0.5 tin condensed milk, 0.5 cup milk, 0.125 teaspoon cardamom powder, 2 cups desiccated coconut, 1.5 cups mawa, 0.5 cup milk, 1 cup sugar, 0.5 teaspoon cardamom powder, 1 teaspoon ghee, 2 tablespoons pistachios
instructions: 1. Grease a pan and optionally line it with a parchment paper. 2. Heat a pan with 1 tbsp ghee. Fry cashews until golden and remove to a plate. 3. Stir together coconut and milk. Bring it to a boil on a medium high flame. 4. Reduce the heat and cook until the milk evaporates. Stir in the sugar and cook until all of the moisture has completely evaporated. 5. Add ghee and continue to cook stirring constantly until the mixture turns dry & leaves the pan. 6. Press down the mixture with a spatula to check if there is any excess moisture. If it still has moisture then cook further to dry it. (check video) 7. Keep stirring continuously at this stage and cook until the mixture turns dense, thick and sticky. It also turns very aromatic and leaves the pan. If you prefer a very soft set burfi then take it off a bit early. 8. Stir in cardamom powder and Transfer the mixture to the greased pan. Press down with a spatula. 9. Sprinkle nuts and keep aside at room temperature for 3 to 4 hours to set. Slice the coconut burfi. Leave it at room temperature for 8 to 10 hours to set completely. Cool and store coconut barfi in a air tight jar. 10. To a pan, add coconut, desiccated coconut and milk. 11. Mix and cook on a medium flame until the entire mixture turns thick and to a mass. Add cardamom powder and mix. Transfer this to a tray. 12. Press down with a spatula. Sprinkle some nuts. Refrigerate for an hour. Cut coconut barfi to squares and enjoy. 13. Add mawa, sugar and milk to a heavy bottom pan. Heat until the mawa and sugar dissolves completely. 14. Stir in the coconut and cook until the coconut burfi mixture turns thick to a mass and begins to leave the sides of the pan. 15. Stir in cardamom powder and transfer to a greased pan. Level with a spatula and press down the nuts with spatula. 16. When the mixture cools down to warm, cut the coconut burfi to desired shapes. Do not wait to cool completely as it turns to powder. 17. Remove from the tray only after cooling completely.

title: Coconut Chutney Recipe
ingredients: 0.75 cup coconut, 1.5 to 2 tablespoons fried gram roasted split chana dal, 1 garlic clove, 1.5 green chilies, 0.3333333333333333 teaspoon salt, 0.5 teaspoon cumin seeds, 0.29166666666666663 cup water, 1.5 teaspoons lemon juice, 0.25 cup coriander leaves, 1 teaspoon oil, 0.25 teaspoon mustard seeds, 1 red chili, 1 sprig curry leaves, 1 pinch hing, 1 pinch split urad dal
instructions: 1. Wash and chop capsicum to cubes, slit green chilies. Pour 1 teaspoon oil to a pan. Add ginger and green chilies. Saute them for a minute, then add the bell peppers. Saute all of them until the bell peppers turn soft. Then add cumin seeds and stir. Turn off the stove. If using frozen coconut you may saute it in the same pan until slightly hot. 2. Cool all the ingredients and transfer them to a grinder jar along with roasted gram, salt & lemon juice. You may want to set a few green chilies aside. You can add them later if needed. 3. Blend to a smooth chutney adding water as needed. The amount of water to add depends on the capsicum. Taste test and add more salt or fried green chilies. 4. I usually don’t temper this. But you can do it by heating 1 teaspoon oil in the same pan. Add a pinch of mustard, 1 pinch of urad dal, 1 broken dried red chilli and crushed garlic clove. When the urad dal turns golden, add curry leaves and hing. Turn off and pour this to the capsicum coconut chutney. 5. Substitutes: Roasted gram can be substituted with chana dal or Bengal. But it has to roasted first on a low to medium heat until lightly brown or deep golden. Cool and add directly to the grinder jar along with other ingredients.

title: Coconut Ladoo Recipe (Nariyal Laddu)
ingredients: 2 cups coconut, 0.25 teaspoon cardamom powder, 8 cashews, 1 teaspoon ghee, 0.75 cup sugar, 1 cup milk, 1.5 cups desiccated coconut, 0.5 cup 2 tbsps condensed milk, 0.25 teaspoon cardamom powder, 2 cups coconut, 1 cup jaggery, 0.25 teaspoon cardamom powder, 1 cashews
instructions: 1. Heat 1 teaspoon ghee in a pan, add 8 broken cashew nuts. 2. Fry them stirring often until they turn golden. Remove these to a plate and set aside. 3. To the same pan, add grated coconut, milk and sugar. 4. If using desiccated coconut set the pan aside for 15 minutes for the coconut to absorb the milk and soften. 5. Mix all of the ingredients well. 6. Begin to cook till the coconut ladoo mixture thickens and all the moisture evaporates completely. 7. The mixture must still be moist and not dry. 8. Add cardamom powder and nuts. Mix well. Cool the mixture slightly. 9. When it comes down to warm temperature, take small portions of this to your palm and press down with your fingers to bind the mixture. This gives the round shape coconut ladoos. (Check video) 10. When they are still warm and moist you can also roll the ladoos in desiccated coconut. 11. Store coconut laddu in an airtight jar and refrigerate. Use within 5 to 7 days. 12. To a pan, add 1½ cups desiccated coconut, ¼ tsp cardamom powder and ½ cup + 2 tablespoons condensed milk. 13. Mix up all of these and cook on a low flame stirring often. Taste test and If needed add more condensed milk for more sweetness. 14. Cook stirring continuously, till the mixture thickens and turns to a mass. (check step-by-step photos above for consistency) 15. Turn off the stove. At this stage the coconut ladoo mixture must be still moist. If you overcook then the ladoos will turn hard. 16. Cool and add chopped pistachios. When it is still warm, take small portions of this mixture to your palms and press down with your fingers to give a round shape to the coconut ladoos. 17. Cool these completely. Store in a air tight box and refrigerate. Use up within a week. 18. Heat 1 tsp ghee and fry cashews. Remove and keep them aside. In the same pan, mix together fresh coconut and grated jaggery in a pan. 19. Once the jaggery melts, taste test and add more if needed. Cook until the mixture smells good , turns thick & binds together. 20. Add cardamoms and fried nuts. Cool down and roll to balls. 21. Store coconut laddus in a air tight jar and store at room temperature. Use up within 7 days.

title: Coconut Ladoo with Condensed Milk
ingredients: 0.5 cup 2 tbsps condensed milk, 0.25 cup milk, 1.5 to 1 cups desiccated coconut, 1 pinch cardamom powder, 2 tablespoons nuts, 2 tablespoons desiccated coconut
instructions: 1. Add half tin (½ cup plus 2 tbsp or about 200 grams) condensed milk to a heavy bottom or non stick pan. 2. Pour ¼ cup milk and add desiccated coconut to suit your taste: If you have a sweet tooth you can use 1 ½ cups or around 130 grams. If you like moderately sweet then 1¾ cups works good. For mildly sweet ladoos, you can use 2 cups equal to 175 grams. It works with all the above mentioned quantities. This time I added about 2 cups to make them mildly sweet. With 2 cups they have a dominating taste of coconut and less of the condensed milk. So judge and use accordingly. 3. Mix it well. Begin to cook on a low flame stirring frequently. 4. If you are using 2 cups, the texture remains same. Cook until the mix turns warm. If you are using lesser than 2 cups, the mixture turns slightly gooey. Continue to cook until it becomes a thick mass, for 2 to 3 mins. Switch off the stove. The timings vary if you are using only 1½ cups coconut. 5. Add a large pinch of cardamom powder and 1½ to 2 tablespoon roasted sliced pistachios. 6. Cool down slightly. While the mixture is still hot (not burning hot), take small portions and roll to balls. 7. Add 2 tablespoons desiccated coconut to a plate and roll them. 8. You can also garnish them with more nuts. 9. Diwali Sweets & D... Gond Ke Laddu, Ho... Badam Laddu (Almo... Ragi laddu | Nac... Rava Laddu Recipe... Dry Fruit Laddu (...

title: Coconut Milk Pulao
ingredients: 1 cup rice, 2 cups coconut milk, 2 tablespoons ghee, 1 onion, 1 green chilli, 0.25 cup carrot, 0.25 cup potato, 0.25 cup green beans, 0.25 cup green peas, 1.25 teaspoon ginger garlic paste, 0.25 cup mint leaves, 0.625 teaspoon salt, 1 cashewnuts, 1 bay leaf, 3 green cardamoms, 4 cloves 4 cloves, 1 inch cinnamon, 1 star anise, 0.75 teaspoon shahi jeera, 1 strand mace, 1 black cardamom, 0.5 teaspoon crushed fennel seeds, 0.25 teaspoon crushed black pepper
instructions: 1. Rinse rice well thrice and soak for 15 mins. Drain to a colander and set aside. Do not over soak. 2. While the rice soaks, prepare the veggies and the spices. 3. Heat ghee in a pot or a pressure cooker. 4. Add all the whole spices. When they begin to sizzle add onions and saute them until deep golden but not burnt. 5. Stir in cashews, fennel seeds and black pepper if using. 6. Add ginger garlic and green chilies. Saute for 30 to 60 seconds until aromatic. 7. Add all the chopped veggies and mint. Fry for 1 to 2 mins until aromatic. 8. Pour coconut milk (mixed with water) and add salt. Stir and taste test to check the salt levels. Add more salt if required. The coconut milk should be slightly salty. 9. Bring the coconut milk to a rolling boil on a high heat. 10. Stir in the drained rice. 11. If cooking in a regular pot, reduce the heat to low or medium and cover. Cook until all of the coconut milk is absorbed & the rice is cooked fully yet not mushy. 12. To cook in a pressure cooker, cover and cook on a medium heat until you hear 1 whistle. Let the pressure drop and open the lid. 13. Fluff up the coconut milk pulao with a fork. Cover and rest for 5 mins before serving with a curry or raita. 14. If using an Instant pot, perform all the above steps on a saute mode. Secure the IP, position the steam release valve to sealing and press the pressure cooker. Set the timer to 5 mins. Let the pressure release for 3 to 4 mins. Release the rest manually. 15. Fluff up with a fork. If the top layer is too dry and looks under cooked, remove the steel insert from the IP. Mix gently with a fork and cover with a plate. Let rest for 5 mins.

title: Coconut Milk Rice Recipe
ingredients: 2 cups Basmati rice, 1.5 cup coconut milk refer notes, 2 cups water 1 cups for IP, 2 teaspoons ginger garlic paste, 1.5 green chili, 1 medium carrots, 1 cup green peas, 6 green beans, 2 tablespoons oil, 0.75 teaspoon salt, 1 bay leaf, 4 green cardamoms, 6 cloves 6 cloves, 2 inch cinnamon, 1 teaspoon cumin seeds, 1 cashew nuts, 4 tablespoons coconut flakes
instructions: 1. Add rice to a bowl and rinse it well thrice. Drain the water. 2. Pour fresh water and let it soak for 20 mins while you prepare the other ingredients. Later after 20 mins, drain the water completely. 3. Add oil or butter to a pot or pressure cooker. Fry cashews on a medium heat until golden. Remove to a small plate and set aside for garnish. 4. Reduce the heat. To the same pot, add whole spices and fry for 30 seconds, until they turn fragrant. 5. Add ginger garlic paste and saute for a minute without burning, until it smells fragrant. 6. Add slit green chili, chopped vegetables and saute for 2 to 3 mins. 7. Pour the coconut milk along with the water and add salt. Taste and adjust the salt. 8. Drain the water completely from the rice and add it to the pot. Mix well and cover with the lid. 9. Cooking in pot: Let the rice cook on a low heat until all of the water is absorbed and is fully tender but not mushy. If the rice is undercooked, sprinkle some warm water and cook until soft. Turn off the heat and let the coconut milk rice rest for 10 mins. Later fluff up with a fork. 10. Pressure Cooker: Cook on a medium heat until you hear 1 whistle. When the pressure drops, open the lid and gently fluff with a fork. 11. Optional: Toast the coconut until golden. Garnish coconut milk rice with toasted coconut and cashews. 12. Press Saute button on the instant pot and pour ghee to the steel insert. Add the whole spices. 13. When the spices begin to sizzle, add ginger garlic and stir well. Add green chilies, cashews and vegetables. Saute for 2 to 3 mins. 14. Add 2 cups drained rice, 1½ cups water, 1 cup coconut milk and salt. Give a good stir. Taste test and add more salt if needed. 15. Secure the instant pot with the lid and position the steam release handle to sealing. Press the pressure cook button (high) and Set the timer to 6 mins. 16. When the IP finishes, let the pressure release manually for 7 to 8 mins. Later do a manual pressure release. Gently fluff up the coconut milk rice with a fork. 17. If you want you may toast the coconut separately and garnish.

title: Coconut Oats Dinner
ingredients: 1.5 cup rolled oats, 1 cup coconut, 1.5 tbsp oil, 0.25 tsp mustard, 0.5 tsp cumin, 0.5 tsp urad dal, 0.5 tsp chana dal, 2 tbsp peanuts, 1 sprig curry leaves, 0.75 tsp ginger, 2 green chilies, salt, 2 tbsp lemon juice, 3 cup coriander leaves, 1 pinch asafoetida
instructions: 1. Rinse rolled oats and drain them in a colander. 2. Gently fluff up immediately and set aside. 3. Heat a pan with oil. 4. Add mustard, cumin , urad dal, chana dal and peanuts. 5. Fry them until the peanuts are golden. 6. Then add in hing, ginger, green chilies and curry leaves. 7. Saute them quickly until the curry leaves turn crisp. 8. Then pour 2 tbsp water. This will soften the dals slightly. 9. Add turmeric and salt. 10. Then add in oats. Gently give a good stir. 11. On a very low heat cook covered until the oats turn slightly hot. This only takes about 1 to 2 mins. 12. Turn off the heat. Add coconut and coriander leaves. 13. Mix well and check salt. If needed add more and sprinkle lemon juice over the salt. 14. Mix well and serve coconut oats with pickle or yogurt.

title: Coconut Rice Recipe (Thengai Sadam)
ingredients: 1 cup uncooked aged rice, 2 cups water, 1.25 cup coconut, 0.5 teaspoon salt, 1.5 green chili, 1 red chili, 2 tablespoons oil, 0.5 teaspoon mustard seeds, 0.5 teaspoon cumin seeds, 1 pinch hing, 1 tablespoon chana dal, 0.5 tablespoon urad dal, 1 teaspoon ginger, 13.5 cashewnuts
instructions: 1. Wash rice at least thrice and drain the water. If using basmati rice, soak it for 20 to 30 mins. 2. To cook rice in pot, bring water to a rolling boil and add the rice. Cook covered on a low heat until fully done but not mushy. 3. To pressure cook basmati rice, pour water & pressure cook for 1 whistle. To pressure cook normal rice, pour more water & pressure cook for 2 whistles. The rice must be grainy and not mushy. When the pressure releases, open the lid. 4. Fluff up the cooked rice with a fork and cool completely. 5. Slit the green chilies, fine chop ginger, and grate the coconut. If using frozen coconut, break up the lumps and loosen the coconut. 6. Pour ghee or oil to a pan and heat it. 7. Fry cashews or peanuts until golden. Remove to a plate and set aside. 8. Add mustard seeds, cumin seeds, red chilli, chana dal and urad dal. Saute them and fry until the dals turn golden and aromatic. 9. Then add ginger, slit green chilli & curry leaves. Saute for 30 to 60 seconds until the curry leaves turn crisp. Add hing. 10. Optional - Quickly pour 3 tablespoons water to the pan. This will soften the dals and bring out the flavors of the ginger and curry leaves. If you want your dal to be very soft, then you can add little more water. Cook until all of the water evaporates. 11. Then add fresh or frozen coconut and sprinkle salt on it. Then add rice. Off the heat. If using frozen coconut, then saute the coconut a bit until it becomes hot. Then add the rice. 12. Mix well and taste test. Add more salt if needed. Serve coconut rice with a simple curry or plain yogurt.

title: Cooker Cake, How to Make Cake in Pressure Cooker
ingredients: 1.5 cups all-purpose flour, 1.25 teaspoons baking powder, 0.5 teaspoon baking soda, 1 pinch salt, 0.75 cup sugar, 0.5 cup oil, 1 cup milk, 1.25 tablespoon vinegar, 2 teaspoons vanilla extract, 1 grams chocolate
instructions: 1. Grease a 7 inch cake tray. Dust with flour evenly, shake it and invert it in the kitchen sink to remove excess flour. Or simply line a parchment paper. Set this aside. 2. Add salt to a pressure cooker or any heavy bottom large pot/ kadai and spread it evenly. 3. Cover the salt with a plate. Place a ring/trivet or hot pot stand over the plate. 4. Remove the gasket and weight, if using a cooker. Cover the cooker or pot with a lid and begin to heat it on a medium heat. 5. Pour milk, sugar, vinegar, melted butter (or oil) and vanilla to a mixing bowl. Stir well until sugar dissolves. 6. Place a sieve over the bowl. Add flour, baking powder, baking soda and salt to the sieve. Lightly mix them up with a spoon. Sieve it. (check video for help) 7. Gently mix the wet and dry with a spatula or a whisk, until the flour is just combined well. Do not over mix the batter. 8. Pour this to the cake pan. Knock it a few times over the counter. Brush milk on top to get a golden top. This is optional. 9. Hold the cake tray with a tong and place inside the cooker/ pot, on top of the trivet/ring or stand. The cooker has to be hot inside at this stage. 10. Cover the cooker with the lid. Pls note the gasket & weight have to be removed. 11. Bake for 50 to 60 mins on a low flame. When the cake is completely baked, a tester inserted comes out clean. 12. The cake top will be fairly dry when you touch it. If it is still moist, bake for few more mins. 13. Cool the cake tray for 10 to 15 mins over a wire rack, so it won't sweat at the bottom. Later invert it on to a wire rack. 14. Cool completely before frosting or slicing. 15. Preheat the oven to 180 C or 350 F for at least 15 mins. 16. Bake it for 30 to 35 mins. Tester inserted comes out clean. Adjust the time to suit the size of your pan and oven. 17. Pour water to a pot and heat it. The water level has to be less than half. 18. Place another bowl or pan over the pot. The bottom of the bowl or pan must touch the hot water but not the pan inside. 19. Also make sure water doesn’t seep into the top bowl or pan. 20. Add 100 grams of chocolate or chips. Keep stirring until the chocolate melts and turns smooth. 21. Make sure the cake and the chocolate both are cool and not hot. 22. Pour the chocolate over the cooled cake and spread it evenly. Top with some sprinkles if you have. Slice the cake and enjoy.

title: Corn Sandwich Recipe
ingredients: 6 slices bread, 2.5 tablespoons Butter, 0.5 cup cheese, 0.5 cup sweet corn kernels, 0.25 teaspoon crushed black pepper, 0.625 teaspoon chaat masala, 0.25 cup green chutney, 2 tablespoons coriander leaves, 0.5 carrot, 2 tablespoons onions, 0.25 cup tomatoes, 1.5 green chilies
instructions: 1. Prepare the corn kernels. If using corn on the cob, boil, grill or bake until tender. I use the frozen corn so I cook for 1 to 2 mins in a pan until cooked completely. Turn off and cool. 2. To the cooled corn, add grated carrots, onions, tomatoes & green chilies. All these are optional and you may skip. 3. Add black pepper, chaat masala and cumin powder. Mix well. Taste test and adjust the spices if you want more. Avoid adding salt. 4. Butter your bread on the inner side and spread chutney or mayo or any spread of your choice. 5. Place the cheese slice over the bread. [If using grated cheese, simply mix that with the veggie corn kernels we prepared earlier.] 6. Spread the corn filling on the prepared bread slice. Smear some chutney or spread on the other bread slice too. Cover the sandwich with the chutney side in. Spread little butter on the outside too. 7. Heat a toaster or a pan and drizzle little softened butter. Place the corn sandwich and toast it till golden. 8. If making on a pan, press down the sandwich with a spatula, toast on a low heat until the bread becomes crisp on one side. Carefully turn it to the other side and toast until the cheese begins to melt. 9. Transfer the corn sandwich to a wooden board and cut before serving.

title: Crispy Corn Recipe (Crunchy Corn Snack)
ingredients: 3 cups corn kernels, 6 tbsp corn flour, 5 tbsp rice flour, 0.5 tsp pepper powder, 0.375 tsp salt, 4 cups water, 0.75 tsp jeera powder, 0.875 tsp chaat masala, 0.625 tsp red chili powder, 2.5 tbsp onions, 2 tbsp coriander leaves, 1 tbsp lemon juice
instructions: 1. Choose corn that have firm and plump kernels. Avoid too young, tender or juicy ones. Also do not use hard kernels. 2. Cut the corn cob to 2 parts. Begin to pick the whole kernels from the cob without cutting them with a knife. Add them to a bowl. Do not cut them with a knife as the cut kernels tend to splutter while frying. 3. Next add them to salted hot water and boil for 2 minutes. You can skip this step if the kernels are moderately milky and not hard. 4. Drain them and rinse under running water. When excess water drains up, add them to a wide bowl. 5. While you prepare the corn also prepare to deep fry them or preheat your oven to 400 F or 200 C for at least 15 mins. 6. Corn have to be moist at this stage. Then sprinkle salt, pepper, 3 tbsps corn flour and 3 tbsps rice flour. Toss them to coat with the flour. If using egg, pour 1 egg white and mix well. Then sprinkle flour. Toss well. 7. Next repeat adding the rest of the flour as needed. Toss them again. If needed sprinkle 1 tbsp water. Toss. Avoid water if using egg. 8. If you like more crispy flour coated on the corn then repeat coating them once more, sprinkling 1 tbsp more water if needed. The corn kernels must look floury at the same time they should not be dry. I did not do it the third time. 9. Spread the prepared corn on a parchment paper or greased sheet. Grill them for 24 to 30 mins until they turn golden and crisp. 10. Pour oil to a hot deep pan for frying. Heat it on a medium flame. 11. When the oil turns hot, drop one corn kernel and check. The oil must be quite hot at this stage otherwise the flour may disperse in the oil. 12. I added about 2 to 3 ladles of corn for each batch and quickly covered with the splatter screen. 13. Regulate the flame to medium and do not disturb until you see them floating on top. If they turn lumpy gently separate them. 14. When they turn crunchy remove to a colander. A plate or kitchen tissue tends to make them soft quickly. 15. Before adding the next batch of corn to oil, ensure the oil is hot enough again. 16. Add all the fried crispy corn to a bowl. Sprinkle red chili powder, cumin powder and chaat masala. 17. Immediately remove to serving plates. Sprinkle some onions and chopped coriander leaves.. 18. Serve crispy corn immediately. If desired just squeeze in few drops of lemon juice.

title: Cucumber Raita Recipe
ingredients: 1 cup cucumber, 1.5 cup yogurt, 0.25 teaspoon salt, 0.5 teaspoon roasted ground cumin, 0.25 teaspoon black pepper, 2 tablespoons coriander leaves, 0.5 teaspoon chaat masala, 4.5 mint leaves, 1 green chili pepper
instructions: 1. Add thick yogurt to a mixing bowl. Very runny yogurt with lot of whey is not suitable to make this cucumber raita. Chopped cucumbers will let out moisture and make the raita more runny. If you do not have thick yogurt then you may strain the whey following this hung curd recipe . 2. Whisk it well with a fork or a hand whisk until smooth. We want a smooth yogurt here. Add salt and black pepper. You may also add cumin powder and chaat masala at this stage. But I prefer to keep it for the garnish. 3. Mix well and taste test at this stage. If your yogurt is too sour, you can balance it with very little sugar at this step but otherwise it is not required. We don’t want to sweeten the cucumber raita. We just want to balance the taste. 4. Rinse cucumbers well and peel them if you don’t want to keep the skin. 5. You can chop to bite sized pieces or grate them to your liking. Grated cucumber is going to make the raita runny, so grate only if you are using greek yogurt. 6. Also deseed and chop the green chilies. You may skip them if you don’t prefer heat in your raita. The other option is to just add the whole slit green chili so the raita won’t become hot but will add some flavor. Also fine chop coriander leaves and mint if using. 7. Add the cucumbers, herbs and chilli. Mix well. I also add a teaspoon of ginger juice at this stage. Ginger aids digestion so we prefer it. 8. Mix all of them and then sprinkle roasted cumin powder and coriander leaves. If you want you can also add chaat masala and black pepper. Serve cucumber raita with Chicken biryani Mutton biryani Veg biryani

title: Cumin Powder (Roasted Jeera Powder)
ingredients: 0.5 cup cumin
instructions: 1. Clean cumin seeds and add them to a heavy bottom pan. Turn on the heat to medium and keep stirring until the seeds become hot to touch. 2. Reduce the heat to the lowest and roast them. Keep stirring continuously so the seeds roast evenly. 3. When done, they will emit a strong earthy, pungent and smoky aroma. Remove from heat. Do not over roast they will burn and taste bitter. 4. Cool them completely and add to a grinder. Grind in short intervals of 30 to 50 seconds until ground to a fine powder. 5. Cool completely and transfer to a air tight glass jar. If you plan to store for longer, store in smaller jars and use them one after the other so most of the aroma is retained. 6. Use cumin powder as needed in curries, stir fry dishes, soups, salads and chaats.

title: Curd Rice Recipe
ingredients: 0.5 cup rice, 1.5 cup water, 1.25 cup curd, salt, 2 tablespoon carrot, 2 tablespoon cucumber, 1 teaspoon coriander leaves, 1 teaspoon oil, 0.5 teaspoon mustard seeds, 0.5 teaspoon cumin seeds, 0.875 teaspoon chana dal, 0.875 teaspoon urad dal, 1 red chilli, 1 sprig curry leaves, 1 pinch hing, 0.5 teaspoon ginger, 1 green chili, 7 roasted cashews
instructions: 1. Add rice to a bowl and rinse it well a few times. Then pour 1½ cups water and ½ teaspoon salt. Keep it in a pressure cooker. Pressure cook rice for 3 whistles until soft. You can also cook the rice directly in the cooker for 2 whistles. 2. When the pressure releases, remove the rice & mash it lightly. You can also fluff up & use if you do not like mushy rice. If you prefer to use milk, refer my notes below. Cool it completely. 3. Add curd to cooled rice. Mix well until combined. If you have cooked the rice without salt, then add it now. 4. Stir in coriander leaves, grated carrots and cucumbers (not gourd) to the curd rice. Taste the curd rice & add more salt if needed. 5. Heat oil in a pan. Then add cumin & mustard. When they begin to splutter, add red chilli, chana dal, urad dal & cashews if using. Fry until the dal turns golden. 6. Add ginger, green chili and curry leaves. When the curry leaves turn crisp, add hing. 7. Optional to soften the lentils - pour 2 to 3 tablespoons water to cook down the lentils, until slightly soft. If the lentils are hard, add a bit more water & cook until they soften to your liking. 8. Pour the tempering over the curd rice and mix. South Indian style curd rice is ready. 9. Garnish with pomegranate seeds.

title: Curry Leaves Chutney
ingredients: 2 cups curry leaves, 3.5 green chilies, 0.75 teaspoon cumin seeds, 0.5 teaspoon salt, 3 garlic cloves, 1 tablespoon oil, 1 tablespoon lemon juice, 1.5 tablespoon chana dal, 0.75 tablespoon urad dal, 0.5 cup coconut
instructions: 1. Rinse curry leaves in plenty of water at least thrice. If you are using store bought curry leaves that are not organically grown, simple spray some vinegar and sprinkle baking soda. Leave them for 5 mins. Later Rinse them in water. 2. Drain them to a colander and shake off the excess water. Spread them on a cloth or kitchen tissues and wipe dry. You can also leave them to air dry for a few minutes. 3. Pour ½ tablespoon oil to a pan and add the chana dal, urad dal and cumin seeds. Let the dal turn light golden. Then add the chilies and garlic 4. Fry them until blistered or crisp. Remove to a plate to cool down. 5. Add another ½ tablespoon oil and add the curry leaves. Fry them on a low to medium heat until the leaves are crisp but not burnt. 6. Stir in the coconut and saute for a minute or 2 until the coconut turns hot. 7. Cool and add to a grinder. Squeeze some lemon juice (or tamarind paste) and add salt. 8. Blend all these starting with ¼ cup water, adding more up to ¾ cup in total. Taste test and adjust salt and lemon juice. 9. Curry leaves chutney tastes good on its own without a tempering. But if you want you may temper it optionally & it is not a part of the recipe. 10. For this heat ½ tablespoon oil in a small tadka pan. Add ¼ teaspoon mustard seeds, 1 pinch of urad dal, 1 broken dried red chili, 1 to 2 small garlic cloves and 5 to 6 curry leaves. When the leaves turn crisp, add a pinch of hing and pour this over the curry leaves chutney.

title: Curry Powder Recipe
ingredients: 2 tablespoons coriander seeds, 0.5 teaspoon black pepper corn, 2.5 red chilies, 0.5 tablespoon cumin seeds, 1.5 teaspoon fennel seeds, 0.5 teaspoon fenugreek seeds, 0.5 teaspoon mustard seeds, 1 tablespoon Bengal gram, 1.5 sprigs curry leaves, 0.25 teaspoon turmeric, 0.5 teaspoon ginger, 1 star anise, 1 two inch piece Ceylon cinnamon, 4 cloves 4 cloves, 5 green cardamoms
instructions: 1. Heat a pan and add 2 tablespoons (10 grams) coriander seeds, ½ teaspoon (1 gram) black pepper, 3 to 4 dried red chilies (deseeded for low heat), 1 star anise, 1 (1 gram) two inch piece cinnamon, 4 cloves and 5 (2 grams) cardamoms. Toast them (do not brown) on a low to medium heat until coriander seeds turn crunchy. Add the ½ tablespoon (5 grams) cumin seeds and 1½ teaspoon (4.5 grams) fennel seeds. Toast until they begin to smell earthy. Transfer to a cooling tray. 2. To the same pan add ½ teaspoon (2 grams) fenugreek seeds and roast on a low heat until they change their color to deep golden & begin to smell nutty/aromatic. Be careful as these can lend a bitter taste to your curry if they are overtoasted or undertoasted. Add the ½ teaspoon (2 grams) mustard and keep stirring just for a minute. Remove them to the tray. 3. Roast 1 tablespoon (14 grams) Bengal gram/ urad dal – lentils/raw rice on a low to medium heat until aromatic, deep golden or light brown. If using rice, you won’t brown them. Transfer to the cooling tray. Adding more lentils will take you close to sambar powder. 4. Add 10 to 20 curry leaves and toast them until crisp. Turn off and transfer to the tray. The picture shows more curry leaves but don’t use more than 20. 5. Add ¼ teaspoon organic turmeric and ½ teaspoon dried ginger (optional) to grinder jar along with all the cooled ingredients. Powder them fine. Cool down if the curry powder is warm. If you use a lot of turmeric it can stain your grinder jar. Also you may keep aside some of the toasted chilies and use after taste testing the powder. I use only 2 deseeded chilies. 6. Transfer to an air tight glass jar and use as needed. If making a larger batch, store in smaller jars so it remains fresh for longer. This recipe makes about 6 tablespoons. 7. Lentils: Also known as dal are another key ingredient in the curry powder. It not only adds flavor and a creamy texture to the curries but also works as a healthy thickener. Here we use chana dal – Bengal gram & it can be substituted with split pigeon peas (toor dal), skinned urad dal or red lentils. But the flavor changes. 8. Dried Red Chilies: I use low heat Mathania chilies. You can use Kashmiri for low heat or Byadgi for medium heat or any other of choice. To make a hot curry powder, use hot chilies instead of increasing the quantity. We found this way the flavors are more balanced. Using a lot of chilies will impart more chili flavor to the powder & will go closer to the traditional thool/ karam. 9. Ginger: A lot of times I do not include dry ginger in my curry powder because I love using fresh ginger over dried. Also note that both add different kind of flavors. So you can use dried here and still use fresh while cooking. Using dried ginger too often can dehydrate and cause constipation in a lot of people. So use with caution.

title: Curry Sauce Recipe (Instant Pot and Stovetop)
ingredients: 2 cups onions, 3.5 cups tomatoes, 0.875 cup carrots, 0.875 cup red bell pepper, 1 inch ginger, 6 medium garlic, 2 cup water, 0.25 cup oil, 0.41666666666666663 tablespoon Kashmiri red chilli powder, 0.5 tablespoon garam masala, 1 tablespoon coriander powder, 0.5 tablespoon cumin powder, 1 teaspoon fennel powder, 0.625 teaspoon salt, 0.5 teaspoon sugar, 0.5 cup cream, 1 medium bay leaf, 2.5 green cardamoms, 1 black cardamom, 2.5 inch cinnamon piece, 1 star anise
instructions: 1. Heat oil in a large pot. Add the whole spices, onions, ginger & garlic. 2. Saute/fry them on a medium heat until transparent. For deeper flavors saute them until light golden brown on a low flame, 3. Stir in red chilli powder, coriander powder, garam masala, cumin powder & fennel powder. 4. Stir in the tomatoes, carrots and bell peppers. Stir fry for 2 to 3 mins on a medium heat. 5. Pour 1½ cups water or broth. Bring it to a rolling boil. 6. Cover and cook on a medium heat until all the veggies are mushy. Add more water/broth at this stage, as you cook. 7. Add salt and sugar when they are soft. 8. Cool this and remove the bay leaf, black cardamom, star anise and cinnamon. Blend to a smooth thick sauce, adding more liquid as required. To use the sauce as a dip or on pizza, in sandwiches and marinades, keep the consistency thick. 9. Taste test and more garam masala if you want. Transfer to a glass freezer safe containers. 10. Vegetable Curries: Grill, roast or stir fry your favorite veggies until tender. Season with salt and spice. Add the required amount of sauce and simmer for 2 to 3 mins. For a loose consistency, add some broth, coconut milk or cashew cream or heavy cream and bring it to a gentle boil. 11. Meat Curries: Marinate prawns, chicken or lamb with spices, yogurt/buttermilk & herbs or with this sauce. Grill, roast or stir fry them until tender and add to the hot sauce. If the sauce is too thick, add some broth/coconut milk or cashew milk. Simmer for a few minutes until the flavors are absorbed. 12. Perform the steps till you saute the onions on a SAUTE mode. Turn off the IP. Stir in all the ground spices, followed by the veggies and 1½ cups water. 13. Pressure cook on high pressure for 16 minutes. Let pressure drop naturally, discard bay leaf, cinnamon, star anise and black cardamom. Blend the ingredients & pour back to the IP. 14. Add sugar (optional) and salt. Taste test and add more garam masala if required. If the sauce is too thick you may add little water. Cook on a saute mode until you see traces of oil/fats on the sauce and the curry sauce turns thick. This takes about 5 minutes.

title: Daddojanam
ingredients: 0.5 cup rice, 1.5 cups water, 1.5 cups curd, 0.5 cup full fat milk, 1.5 tsp oil, 0.5 tsp mustard seeds, 0.5 tsp cumin, 1 Pinch hing, 1 sprig curry leaves, 0.75 tbsp chana dal, 0.5 tbsp urad dal, 0.5 tsp pepper
instructions: 1. These are the ingredients to use. If you are not making this for offering to God, then you can also use slit green chilies and half tsp of grated ginger. 2. Wash rice a few times and soak it for about 20 minutes. You can skip soaking and cook the rice for longer. Pressure cook it until soft and mushy. I pressure cook for 2 whistles on a medium heat. 3. Once done, open the lid and mash the hot rice very well. 4. When the rice is still very hot, add boiled milk. Using boiled milk instead of unboiled will keep the rice good for longer. The milk can be very hot to warm. 5. Mix up everything very well. The rice has to look very moist at this stage. If needed you can add in more milk. If the rice is aged, then it usually needs little more milk. Set this aside to cool completely. 6. When the rice is cooled, you will see the milk has been absorbed completely and it looks dry. Add salt at this stage. 7. Then the curd. 8. Mix up everything well. Rice must not be dry, it has to be gooey and creamy. Add in more curd if needed. 9. Heat oil in a kadai. Add mustard and cumin. 10. When they begin to splutter, add chana dal and urad dal. Fry until the dals turn golden. 11. Add curry leaves, pepper and hing. Switch off the stove. 12. Cool down & Pour this over the curd rice. Mix very well. 13. Temple style daddojanam is ready. After offering to the Goddess, I garnished with some pomegranate seeds. You can also use grated carrots and cucumber.

title: Dahi Kabab | Dahi Ke Kabab
ingredients: 2 cups curd, 1.25 cup paneer, 1 cashewnuts, 0.25 cup onions very finely chopped, 1.5 teaspoons ginger paste, 2 green chilies, 1.125 teaspoons garam masala, 3 tablespoons coriander leaves, Salt as needed, 0.5 cup bread crumbs, 2.5 tablespoons besan, Oil as needed for shallow frying
instructions: 1. Place a strainer over a large deep bowl. Spread a thin muslin cloth or cheese cloth over the strainer. Pour the curd and bring together the edges of the cloth. Then wring the cloth to drain up excess whey from curd. (check pics in the post) 2. Place 2 to 3 heavy ceramic bowl or a heavy object over the pack of yogurt. Refrigerate the whole thing for 12 to 14 hours. The whey drips and gets collected to the bowl. Later hung curd is ready. 3. Crumble paneer and add it to a mixing bowl along with hung curd. 4. Add all the other ingredients - ginger, garlic, chilli, garam masala, onions, cashews and coriander leaves. 5. Mix well just to make a non sticky dough. If the dough is still sticky add more bread crumbs. 6. Divide the dough to equal sized balls. Flatten them. 7. Then roll them in besan or any flour you prefer. Shake them off gently to remove excess flour. Set aside for 5 to 7 mins. 8. Heat up oil for shallow frying. Test if the oil is hot enough by dropping a small portion of the mixture. It should sizzle, rise up and not turn brown. 9. Gently drop the dahi kabab one after the other. Regulate the flame to medium. 10. Do not disturb them for a few minutes. When one side turns golden, flip them and fry on the other side as well. 11. Fry all the dahi kababs in batches until golden and crisp. 12. Serve dahi kabab hot with coriander chutney, green chutney or mint chutney.

title: Dahi Vada Recipe (Dahi Bhalla)
ingredients: 0.5 cup urad dal, 0.5 inch ginger, 1 green chili, 0.625 teaspoon cumin seeds, 0.25 teaspoon salt, 0.125 teaspoon hing, 3 tablespoons chilled water, 6 cashew nuts, Oil, 1 cup coriander leaves, 0.25 cup mint leaves, 1 green chili, 0.25 inch ginger, 1 garlic, 0.5 teaspoon cumin seeds, 1 tablespoon Lemon juice as needed, 1 tablespoon roasted gram, 0.25 teaspoon salt, water very little if needed, 0.25 cup tamarind, 0.5 cup jaggery, 1 cup water, 0.5 teaspoon salt, 0.25 teaspoon dry ginger powder, 0.25 teaspoon fennel powder, 0.375 teaspoon roasted cumin powder, 0.375 teaspoon red chili powder, 0.5 teaspoon coriander powder, 0.125 teaspoon hing, 2.5 to 3 cups curd, 1 tablespoon powdered sugar, 0.5 teaspoon salt, 1 teaspoon roasted cumin powder, 0.5 teaspoon red chili powder, 1 teaspoon chaat masala, 0.25 cup coriander leaves
instructions: 1. Wash urad dal thrice and soak for at least 4 to 6 hours. Chill 1 cup water in the refrigerator to blend the lentils later. 2. Add 1 cup water, tamarind, jaggery and dates to a pot. Simmer for 4 to 5 mins until the tamarind turns completely mushy. 3. Stir in red chili powder, salt, cumin powder, fennel powder, dry ginger powder and coriander powder or garam masala. Mix and boil for 2 minutes until the aroma of spices comes out. If the mixture becomes too thick, pour ¼ cup hot water. 4. Taste test to adjust salt and jaggery to your preference. Cool down, blend and pass through a sieve to a wide bowl. 5. Optionally temper - Heat a teaspoon of oil or ghee in a pan. Add hing and then pour the tamarind chutney. Simmer for 2 to 3 mins until bubbling hot & thick. Cool this completely. 6. Transfer this to a dry glass or ceramic jar. Store this in fridge and use up within 4 to 6 weeks. 7. Add coriander leaves, mint leaves, green chili, ginger, garlic, cumin seeds, lemon juice, roasted gram & salt to a blender jar. Grind to a smooth chutney, adding water (2 to 3 tbps) only as needed. Taste test to adjust salt and spice. 8. Drain the water from soaked urad dal and add to a blender jar. Also add cumin, salt, green chili, ginger, hing and pour 2 tbsps chilled water. 9. Grind for a minute and scrape the sides, repeat blending adding 1 tbsp water each time only as needed until the batter turns thick, fluffy and light. (Avoid adding too much water as it makes the batter runny. We want a thick & fluffy batter here.) 10. When the batter is ground perfectly, it looks lighter in color. Remove to a bowl. Using your hand or a whisk, whisk/ aerate it well for 1 min in one direction. 11. Test the consistency – drop ¼ tsp batter to a bowl of water. If it floats it means the batter is aerated well else blend it a little longer or whisk a little longer. 12. Stir in the cashews if using. 13. To a large bowl, pour 2 to 3 cups water and ½ teaspoon salt. Keep this aside on the kitchen counter. 14. On a medium flame, heat oil in a kadai. Test if the oil is hot enough by sliding ½ tsp batter. It should sizzle and come to the surface steadily without turning brown. 15. Grease your fingers or a tablespoon or a fruit scooper. Take small portions of the batter and gently slide into the oil. 16. Fry on a medium heat, untouched for 3 minutes and then stir them. Continue to fry until golden and crisp. Remove to a steel colander or lined rack. 17. Transfer the hot fried vadas to the bowl of water. Rest them for 15 to 20 mins, rotating them with a spoon in between. Timing may vary depending on the size of the vadas. 18. While these rest fry the next batch of vadas. Repeat the same process of soaking them in water. 19. Add 2½ cups yogurt, a tbsp of powdered sugar and ½ teaspoon salt to a large wide bowl (big enough to hold all your vadas). Beat well with a whisk until smooth. It should not be too thick like Greek yogurt and not very runny. Taste test and adjust salt. 20. Gently press down the vadas in between spatulas or your palms one after the other to remove most of the water. Drop these to the bowl of yogurt & ensure all of the vadas are dunked in the yogurt well. 21. Cover this and chill in the refrigerator for at least 2 hours. This is when the dahi vadas soak up a bit of yogurt and flavor. 22. They will be softer by 2 hours. If you like more yogurt, whisk another half cup yogurt with sugar and salt. Pour that as well. Transfer the dahi vadas to serving tray or plates. 23. Top with a generous amounts of green chutney and sweet tamarind chutney. 24. Sprinkle cumin powder, chaat masala, black salt and red chili powder. Garnish with coriander leaves. Adjust the quantity of chutneys, chaat masala, cumin powder and red chili powder to suit your taste. 25. Serve dahi vada immediately after you sprinkle the spice powders.

title: Dal Palak
ingredients: 0.5 cup toor dal, 1.25 cup water, 1 tomato, 2 cups palak, 1.5 to 2 tablespoons ghee, 0.75 teaspoon cumin seeds, 1 red chilli, 1 teaspoon ginger chopped, 1.5 green chilies, 1 pinch hing, 0.1875 teaspoon turmeric, 0.5 teaspoon salt, 0.5 teaspoon kasuri methi, 1 tablespoon Lemon juice
instructions: 1. Add dal to a bowl and rinse it well thrice. 2. Option 1 - Cook dal in a pressure cooker: Transfer to a pressure cooker or a bowl. If using tomato, add it as well and pour water. Pressure cook for 2 whistles on a medium heat or in the instant pot for 10 minutes. When the pressure releases naturally, open the lid and mash or whisk dal well until slightly smooth. 3. Option 2 - Cook in a pot: Add the rinsed dal & tomato (optional) to a sauce pan and pour water. Bring to a rolling boil on a high heat and reduce to medium. Cook until mushy, adding more hot water if required. 4. On a medium flame, heat a pan with ghee or oil and add cumin seeds. When they begin to sizzle, add ginger, garlic, red and green chilies. Let them fry for a minute and add hing. 5. Add spinach and saute for 3 to 4 mins on a medium heat, until the leaves wilt & soften. Transfer the cooked dal, salt and turmeric. Mix well and stir in more hot water if you want a slightly loose consistency (to serve with rice). 6. Stir in kasuri methi, taste test and adjust salt. Sprinkle lemon juice and serve with rice or roti. If you want to make a special tadka, check the special tadka section in the post. 7. Add 1 to 1½ tablespoon ghee/butter to a small tadka pan and heat on a medium flame. Stir in a large pinch of mustard seed, ¼ teaspoon cumin seeds, 1 broken dried red chili and 1 sliced garlic. 8. When the mustard seeds begins to splutter, turn off the stove and add 8 to 10 curry leaves (pat dry first). Stir in a pinch of hing and ¼ teaspoon red chili powder. Mix well and pour over the spinach dal. 9. Press SAUTE button on the Instant pot and pour ghee to the steel insert. Add cumin, dried red chilies, garlic/ginger and green chilies. When the cumin seeds splutter, add tomatoes (optional) and saute for 1 minute. 10. Add dal, turmeric and salt. If you prefer to use chilli powder as a substitute to green chilies, add it now. 11. Press CANCEL button. Pour 1½ cups water and deglaze with a spatula to release any bits of food stuck at the bottom. Optionally you can also place a trivet and keep your rice bowl over it & cover. 12. Secure the Instant pot with the lid. Press PRESSURE COOK button (high pressure) and set the timer to 10 mins. Let the pressure release naturally for at least, 15 mins. Release the rest manually. 13. Add the chopped spinach to the dal and give a good mix. Press SAUTE button and cook for 2 mins until the spinach wilts and softens. 14. Press CANCEL and sprinkle kasuri methi (optional). Cover the IP until you serve. This helps spinach to cook completely with the residual heat in the IP. 15. Taste test and add more salt if needed. When the spinach dal cools a bit, add lemon juice. Serve with rice or roti.

title: Dal Tadka Recipe (Restaurant Style)
ingredients: 1 cup toor dal, 3 cups water, 0.75 cup water, 1.5 to 2 tablespoon oil, 0.5 teaspoon cumin seeds, 1 teaspoon garlic, 1 teaspoon ginger, 1 medium onion, 1 green chilli, 2 medium tomatoes, 0.25 teaspoon turmeric, 0.625 teaspoon red chilli powder, 0.5 teaspoon garam masala, 1 teaspoon salt, 1 tablespoon kasuri methi, 1.5 tablespoon coriander leaves, 1 medium lemon, 2 tablespoons ghee, 0.625 teaspoon cumin seeds, 2 red chilies, 0.75 tablespoon garlic, 0.125 teaspoon hing, 0.3333333333333333 teaspoon red chilli powder, 0.25 teaspoon mustard seeds, 1 sprig curry leaves
instructions: 1. Add dal to a pot or pressure cooker and rinse them very well with lots of water. Rub the lentils well with your hand while you rinse. Drain completely. 2. Pour water and pressure cook the dal on a medium heat for 3 to 4 whistles. To cook in instant pot, pressure cook on high for 10 minutes. To cook in pot check my notes below. 3. When the pressure drops naturally, open the lid. Dal should be soft cooked. 4. Lightly mash keeping some of the dal intact. Add more water only if required to bring to consistency. 5. While the lentils cook, to a medium pan pour oil and heat it. Add cumin seeds to the hot oil. 6. They will begin to splutter, then add ginger garlic and saute on a low heat until fragrant. 7. Add onions and green chilies. Fry them until slightly golden. 8. Reduce the heat. Add red chilli powder, turmeric and garam masala. Give a quick stir. 9. Stir in the tomatoes along with salt. Saute until the tomatoes become soft. The mixture begins to smell good at this stage. 10. Transfer this to the cooked dal and simmer for about 6 to 7 minutes. Taste test and add more salt at this stage. Then add kasuri methi and give the boiling dal a good stir. Add coriander leaves and turn off the heat. 11. Place a tall steel cup in the center of the pot. With a tong hold a wood coal over direct fire like a candle or lighter until a portion of it becomes red and hot. This will take about 2 minutes. 12. Place this gently into the cup and pour ½ teaspoon ghee directly over the hot coal. It will begin to release smoke. Quickly cover the pot and let the dal smoke for 3 to 4 minutes. 13. Transfer the hot cooked lentils to a serving bowl. Pour 2 tbsps ghee to a small pan & heat it on a medium heat. 14. Add cumin seeds and mustard seeds (optional). Let them fry & splutter on a low heat until aromatic. 15. Next add red chilies and give a good stir. Add garlic and curry leaves (optional). Fry until light and aromatic. Do not brown. 16. Stir in asafoetida and red chili powder. Quickly remove from heat and pour the tadka over the dal. 17. Garnish dal tadka with coriander leaves. Squeeze some lemon juice over the dal while serving. Serve with Basmati rice, roti, paratha, naan, jeera rice or ghee rice.

title: Dibba Rotti
ingredients: 0.5 cup urad dal, 1 cup rice rava, 0.5 tsp Cumin, Salt as needed, 3.5 tbsp Oil
instructions: 1. Wash urad dal at least thrice and soak in enough water for 4 hours. 2. If using rice, then rinse it well and drain it to a colander. Allow it to dry completely until the dal is soaked. You can also spread it on a cloth. Then pulse the rice in a blender to get a coarse rice rava texture. If using idli rava, rinse it a few times and drain the water completely. Squeeze excess water with both your palms. Set this aside until the dal is soaked. 3. Drain the water from urad dal and add it to a blender jar. Pour little water and add salt. 4. Grind the dal to thick smooth & frothy consistency. Transfer this to a bowl. 5. Fluff up the rice rava well. Add it to the batter along with cumin and mix well. The consistency of the batter should be thick to get the right crust. 6. Set this aside for about 15 to 45 minutes if you desire. Resting the batter makes the dibba rotti soft from inside. 7. Heat a heavy bottom Kadai or a cast iron pan with oil. Spread the oil all over the kadai to coat it well in oil. 8. When the oil becomes hot pour the batter to the center in the oil and level it with a ladle. Pour the batter till 1½ to 2 inches thickness or height. There should be enough oil in the kadai which spreads around when the batter is poured to the center. 9. Cover and cook on a medium flame till the batter firms up. For even crust, the flame should reach till the edges of the batter under the kadai. 10. Remove the lid and cook till you get a crusty thick golden base. 11. Flip it to the other side and cook till the other side also cooks well and gets a crusty base. 12. Off the flame and leave it in the kadai for about 5 minutes. I move it to a wire rack to retain the crust. Then slice the dibba rotti. 13. If you are left with more batter, repeat adding more oil to the kadai and heat it. When the oil turns hot, pour the batter and make more dibba rotti following the same method. 14. Serve dibba rotti with any chutney or pickle.

title: Dill leaves recipe (shepu bhaji)
ingredients: 25 grams dill leaves, 1.5 tablespoons oil, 0.25 teaspoon mustard seeds, 0.5 teaspoon cumin seeds, 2 garlic, 1.5 green chilies, 1 sprig curry leaves, 1 tablespoon chana dal, 1 teaspoon urad dal, 0.375 cup moong dal, 0.125 teaspoon turmeric, 0.25 teaspoon salt
instructions: 1. If using moong dal, add it to a bowl and rinse well. Soak it well for a minimum of 30 mins in warm water. Later drain the water before using. 2. Add dill leaves along with the stems to a large pot filled with water. Let them rest for about 5 mins. Repeat the rinse well. Drain to a colander. 3. Pour oil to a pan and heat it. 4. Add mustard, cumin, chana dal and urad dal. Fry them until the dals turn golden. 5. Then add garlic, chilli and curry leaves. Stir fry until garlic turns fragrant. 6. Drain the water from moong dal and add it to the pan. Cover and cook on a low heat until tender. If needed sprinkle water in between. 7. The moong dal must be cooked well but not mushy. This takes only a few minutes as the lentils are presoaked. 8. While the lentils cook, chop the dill as desired. If you do not like to use the stems you may remove them. 9. When the lentils are tender and done, add salt and turmeric. Evaporate any excess moisture in the pan. 10. Add the dill to the pan and stir fry until the leaves wilt. The stems also will turn slightly tender. 11. Serve dill stir fry with rice or roti.

title: Dondakaya Curry | Tindora Curry
ingredients: 25 grams ivy gourd, 2 tablespoons oil, 1 sprig curry leaves, 0.5 teaspoon cumin seeds, 0.5 teaspoon mustard seeds, 1 Pinch turmeric, 1 Pinch hing, 2 onions, 1 tomato, 0.625 teaspoon red chili powder, 0.75 teaspoon garam masala powder, 2 tablespoons coriander leaves, 0.3333333333333333 teaspoon salt, 2.5 tablespoons coconut
instructions: 1. Add ivy gourds to a large pot filled with water. Add 2 teaspoons of rock salt and a few tablespoons of vinegar. Let them rest for 5 to 10 mins. Rinse them well under running water. 2. Nip off both the ends and chop them to desired size. An easier way is to slice them in a food processer (with a slicing attachment) or dice them in a onion chopper (with a dicing attachment). 3. Heat oil in a pan, Add mustard and cumin. 4. When the seeds crackle, add curry leaves, green chilies and hing. 5. When the curry leaves turn crisp, add onions and fry until they turn slightly golden. 6. Add tomato, salt and turmeric. Saute until they become mushy and blend well with onions. 7. Add ivy gourd and fry for 2 to 3 mins on a medium high heat. Add red chili powder and saute for another 2 to 3 minutes. 8. Cover and cook on a very low flame stirring occasionally to prevent burning. 9. When the gourd is half cooked, add garam masala and saute for 2 mins. Taste test and adjust salt. 10. Cover and simmer until cooked & tender. Sprinkle coriander leaves and coconut. Stir well. Serve dondakaya curry with rice or chapathi.

title: Dondakaya Fry (Tindora Fry)
ingredients: 25 grams tindora, 1 red chili, 1 sprig curry leaves, 0.25 teaspoon mustard seeds, 0.5 teaspoon cumin seeds, 1 teaspoon channa dal, 1 teaspoon urad dal, 2 tablespoons Oil, 1.5 garlic cloves, 1 pinch hing, 0.125 teaspoon turmeric, 0.3333333333333333 teaspoon sea salt, 0.375 teaspoon red chili powder, 2 tablespoons peanuts, 0.75 tablespoon chana dal, 0.75 tablespoon urad dal, 0.75 tablespoon coconut, 0.75 tablespoon sesame seeds, 1 red chili, 1 garlic, 0.75 teaspoon cumin seeds
instructions: 1. Wash 250 grams ivy gourd very well in your preferred way. I rinse them under running water and add them to a bowl filled with water, little vinegar and salt. Let soak for 5 to 10 mins and rinse them well again. Wipe dry with a cloth, chop both the ends and discard. You can slice to thin round slices or lengthwise as shown above. But I simply add them to a processor/chopper and use the pulse button (do not process). 2. Heat 1 to 2 tablespoons oil in a kadai. Add the following ingredients one after the other 3. 1 teaspoon chana dal (soak in hot water for 15 mins & pat dry, if you want) 4. 1 teaspoon urad dal (soak in hot water for 15 mins, if you want) 5. Let fry until the lentils turn golden and aromatic. Add a pinch of hing (optional) and the chopped dondakaya or slices. Also add in ⅛ teaspoon turmeric and ⅓ teaspoon salt. Saute on a medium high flame for 3 mins and cook covered on a low heat until tender and fully cooked through. Keep stirring in between to prevent burning. 6. You will need 2 to 4 tablespoons grated raw coconut here. Or use idli podi , peanut podi or vepudu karam. If you want to make my aromatic vepudu karam make it from the recipe card below. Here I am using raw coconut and 1 tablespoon raw sesame seeds ground coarsely. 7. When the gourd is tender add in the coconut. Mix and fry until it smells good, for 2 mins. 8. Sprinkle ¼ to ½ teaspoon red chilli powder. You can also add some 1 to 2 tbsps roasted skinned peanuts at this stage (optional). 9. Stir fry until the raw smell is gone, for another 2 to 3 mins. Taste test to adjust salt or chili powder. Serve dondakaya fry with hot rice and ghee or with chapati. Dondakaya Curry 10. ... Dondakaya Pachadi... Ladies Finger Fry... Raw Banana Fry (A... Yam fry recipe 11. ... Beerakaya fry

title: Dosakaya Pachadi | Cucumber Chutney
ingredients: 1 cucumber, 4 green chili, 0.5 teaspoon cumin seeds, 0.5 teaspoon salt, 1.5 garlic, 1.5 tablespoons Lemon juice, 2 tablespoons coriander leaves, 1 sprig curry leaves, 0.25 teaspoon mustard seeds, 0.25 teaspoon cumin seeds, 0.5 red chili, 1 garlic, 0.5 teaspoon urad dal, 0.5 teaspoon channa dal, 1 pinch asafoetida, 1 tablespoon oil, 0.5 tablespoon chana dal, 0.5 tablespoon urad dal
instructions: 1. Wash cucumber and peel off the skin. 2. Cut it to 2 large pieces. Remove the seeds and discard. Check if the cucumber is bitter. 3. Fry green chilies in 1 tsp oil for 1 to 2 min. Cool them. If using chana dal and urad dal, fry them too until golden & aromatic in the same pan. Cool completely. 4. Add green chilies, dal, cumin, salt and garlic to a grinder jar and make a fine powder. 5. Then add 2 tbsp of the chopped cucumber pieces and tamarind paste to the same grinder jar. Grind them to a fine chutney. 6. Add the blended mix with the rest of the cucumber pieces. Then add salt and chopped coriander leaves. Mix well. 7. Heat the same pan with the remaining oil. 8. Add mustard seeds, cumin seeds and red chili. 9. When the seeds pop, add chana dal and urad dal. Fry till the dals turn golden. 10. Next add the curry leaves and hing. 11. Pour this to the dosakaya chutney, check salt and spice. If needed you can add lemon juice or more tamarind paste. 12. Serve cucumber chutney as a side with rice, roti or dosa.

title: Drumstick Curry (Drumstick Vegetable Recipe)
ingredients: 2.5 drumsticks, 2.5 tbsps oil, 2 onions, 2 tomatoes, 1 green chili, 1 sprig curry leaves, 1 pinch mustard seeds, 0.5 teaspoon cumin seeds, 2 garlic cloves, 1 teaspoon red chili powder, 0.25 teaspoon turmeric, 0.5416666666666666 teaspoon salt, 1 teaspoon coriander powder, 1 cup water
instructions: 1. Heat oil in a deep pan. When the oil turns hot add mustard & cumin. They will begin to splutter, then add curry leaves, garlic and green chili. Saute until the leaves turn crisp. 2. Add the onions, sprinkle salt and fry till they turn transparent. You don’t need to brown the onions but the raw smell should be gone. 3. Add drumsticks & fry for 2 to 3 mins to remove the raw smell. 4. Add tomatoes, some more salt &turmeric. Fry till the tomatoes become completely soft and pulpy. Do not cover and cook but fry in a open pan as it tastes better. 5. Add red chili powder & coriander powder(optional). Saute well until the raw smell of the chilli powder goes away. 6. Pour water just enough to cover the drumsticks. Stir well. 7. Cook on a medium flame till they are thoroughly cooked. If you have enough time, try cooking on the lowest heat for a long time. I sometimes do that, it yields a very tasty curry. Serve drumstick curry with rice or chapathi. 8. You can also make a variation to this by adding boiled eggs. When the drumsticks are almost cooked but not fully, prick boiled eggs with a fork and add them here. Cook till the drumsticks are completely done. You can also fry the eggs in 1 tsp oil and then add them here If you are new to cooking, then the following tip may help you: 9. Choosing drumsticks: Always choose young and fresh looking drumsticks. The veggies that are harvested long time ago will usually dry up and it is harder to cook them. They will also lack the flavor.

title: Drumstick Leaves Stir Fry | Moringa Leaves Recipe
ingredients: 3 potatoes, 2 cups drumstick leaves, 3 garlic cloves, 1 sprig curry leaves, 1 teaspoon urad dal, 1 tablespoon chana dal, 3.5 green chilies, 0.125 teaspoon hing, 1 red chili, 1.5 to 2 tablespoon Oil, 0.25 teaspoon turmeric, 0.625 teaspoon cumin, 0.25 teaspoon mustard, 0.5 teaspoon salt
instructions: 1. Separate the leaves from the stalks and wash thoroughly in enough water, drain the leaves in a colander to remove water thoroughly. 2. Option 1 - Boil potatoes al dente. I usually boil them in the instant pot for 7 to 9 minutes depending on the size. The potatoes must be just cooked and not mushy, Once done, peel the skin and cube them. 3. Option 2 - You can also simply stir fry the potatoes first instead of boiling. For this cube the potatoes to ½ to ¾ inch pieces and keep them immersed in a bowl of water until used. When you are ready to add them to the pan, drain the water and rinse them once. 4. Heat a pan with oil. Add cumin, mustard, dal, garlic and red chili. Fry till the dal turn golden. Add curry leaves and green chilies fry for 1 to 2 mins. 5. Add hing, boiled potatoes, salt, turmeric and fry for 3 to 4 mins. If using raw potatoes, stir fry them on a medium heat for 2 to 3 mins. Cover and cook on a low heat until fork tender. If required you may splash little water to help them cook faster. 6. Increase the flame to medium high. Add the moringa leaves and fry till they wilt off. Keep stirring and fry. If the leaves are not tender, you may cover and cook. 7. Serve drumstick leaves stir fry with rice and sambar or rasam.

title: Dry Chicken Ghee Roast
ingredients: 2 tablespoons ghee, 3 sprigs curry leaves, 2 cloves garlic, 0.5 tablespoon black pepper, 0.5 kg chicken, 1.5 tablespoon lemon juice, 1 tablespoon mint leaves, 0.25 teaspoon turmeric, 1.5 teaspoon Garam masala, 1.5 teaspoon red chili powder, 1 onions, 1 tablespoon ginger garlic paste, 1 tablespoon ghee, 0.5 teaspoon salt
instructions: 1. To a pot, add chicken along with the ingredients mentioned under to marinate. 2. Mix everything well and cook on a low flame till the chickn is tender and cooked completely. 3. If you find moisture in the pot, Cook on a slightly high flame and evaporate it. Set this aside for about 30 to 45 minutes. 4. Heat a pan with ghee. Add chopped curry leaves and garlic. Fry until the curry leaves turn crisp. You can set aside few leaves for garnish if you want. 5. To the pan, add the cooked chicken and begin to roast until all of the moisture dries up. 6. When the masala gets roasted well but not burnt, sprinkle black pepper and salt if needed. 7. Fry till you get a nice an aroma of pepper chicken ghee roast, for about 2 mins. The dish is done when the masala becomes aromatic. Switch off the flame. 8. Garnish with curry leaves. Serve dry chicken ghee roast with rice and rasam or with roti. Squeeze some lemon juice if you want.

title: Dry Fruit Laddu
ingredients: 0.25 cup cashews, 0.25 cup almonds, 0.25 cup pista, 8.5 dates, 7 apricots, 4 figs, 2 tbp dried coconut, 1 tsp poppy seeds, 1.5 tbsps sesame seeds
instructions: 1. On a medium heat dry roast almonds and cashews until slightly aromatic. Add pistas and fry for another 2 mins. Turn off the stove. You can also do this in oven. Dry roast nuts in microwave for 1 minute on high. 2. Remove to a plate to cool. Add dates, apricots and figs to the hot pan and just stir them until slightly hot. This is done for shelf life. You can also skip this to prevent slight loss of nutrition. 3. If using seeds, toast them until they turn aromatic. Set these aside. 4. When all of them cool down. Add dates, apricots and figs to a blender along with pistachios. Blend them to a slightly coarse mixture. Pieces of pista in the mixture is just fine. Please use steel or glass blender jars. You can also use a food processor. 5. Remove to a plate and add the cashews and almonds. Pulse them a few times to get coarse mixture. If your blender doesn't have sharp blades then it is good to chop and add them. 6. Remove the entire blended mixture to a plate. If using seeds add them now and just knead or mix well. 7. Divide the mixture to 12 portions and roll to balls. Do not roll them too tight as the ladoo will turn hard. Gently shape them. 8. For garnishing - roll them in fine chopped pistachios or desiccated coconut or toasted poppy seeds or sesame seeds. 9. Store dry fruit laddu in airtight jar.

title: Dum Aloo Recipe (Restaurant Style)
