Place a clean and dry strainer/filter over a clean jar. Pour the ghee through the strainer/ filter. Press down the milk solids. I usually strain the ghee to several small jars. This helps the ghee to remain fresh for longer. 9. Culture the cream : Follow either method (A) or (B) or (C) based on your convenience. Methods B and C are traditionally followed. 10. Method A – Heat up 2 cups of heavy cream until warm enough. Stir in 2 tablespoons of yogurt (at room temperature). Cover and keep it in a warm place for 8 to 16 hours as you would keep to make yogurt. When your cultured cream is ready, it smells like yogurt. Chill it for few hours and then proceed to step 2 to make your own cultured butter. 11. Method B – Boil non-homogenized full fat milk. When the temperature comes down you will see a layer of cream over it. Make yogurt using both the cream and milk, following this post on homemade yogurt or curd . 12. When the yogurt is set remove the top portion of the cultured cream and store in a separate jar. Refrigerate and keep adding the cream to it every time you make yogurt. You will need at least a cup of this cultured cream to go ahead and make your butter, followed by ghee. 13. Method C – Boil milk and let it cool down for a few hours. Remove the milk cream that forms on top & store in a separate jar. Refrigerate this and keep adding more cream every time you boil the milk. 14. When you have a cup of this cream, heat it to warm temperature and then add a tbsp of yogurt/curd culture to this and mix well. Allow this to set in a warm place like regular yogurt for 5 to 8 hours depending on the temperature.

title: How to make Paneer
ingredients: 2 liters full fat milk, 2.5 tablespoons vinegar, 1 colander, 1 cheese cloth, 1 bowl, 1.5 heavy objects
instructions: 1. Heat Milk: Pour milk to a heavy bottom pot & bring it to a gentle boil on a medium heat. Stir occasionally to ensure the milk doesn't get scorched at the bottom. 2. Curdle Milk: When the milk comes to a boil, turn off the stove and stir in 2 tablespoons of vinegar. You should see the milk begins to split, separating the solids and the whey. If done correctly, whey should be clear or yellowish but not milky. 3. Troubleshooting: If the milk did not curdle fully or still looks milky, stir 1 tbsp more vinegar. Boil on a medium heat till it curdles fully. Turn off immediately and do not overcook else paneer can become hard. 4. Strain & Rinse: Place a colander over a large bowl to collect the whey. Spread a cheese cloth over it. Transfer the paneer and the whey in 2 batches. Pour cold water all over the paneer to rinse off the vinegar really well. 5. Drain the Liquid: Gather the edges of the cloth in your hand and bundle the paneer to form a round shape. Squeeze off any excess liquid. Make a knot and hang the paneer on to your faucet, for 30 mins to drain completely. (see post for pictures) 6. Set the Paneer: Remove the knot and twist the edges of the cloth. Place bundle on a flat colander or a wooden board. Press down the cloth & place a heavy object (like a cast iron pot & rice bags) on it. (check the video below) Let the paneer set at room temperature or in the refrigerator (if hot) for 3 to 4 hours, 7. Dice the Paneer: Unpack the cheese from the cloth. You will see a dent and it is normal. Cut the paneer to 1 inch cubes. Refrigerate in a air tight container and use within 2 to 3 weeks or freeze up to 3 months. 8. Recipes to try: Use the diced cheese in curries, kebabs or rice pilafs. You may check out these 60 amazing Paneer Recipes for ideas.

title: How to make Rice Flour at Home
ingredients: 1 cup rice, water
instructions: 1. Add rice to a bowl and pour water. Rub the rice well and discard the water. 2. Repeat the rinse a few time, 3 times. Drain to a colander. Shake off gently to remove the excess water and leave it in the colander for 5 mins. 3. Transfer to a absorbent cloth and pat dry. 4. Spread the rice and let it air dry on the cloth until crunchy. Or alternately add it to a pan and roast the rice on a medium heat for 2 to 3 mins. Later reduce the heat and roast until crunchy. It takes about 6 to 7 mins. 5. Cool and add to a grinder jar. Grind in intervals of 1 minute until the rice grains are ground to a fine powder. If you want coarse flour you can instead pulse the rice in the grinder. 6. Place a fine flour sieve over a large mixing bowl and transfer the flour to the sieve. 7. Sieve the flour and add the coarse grains if any to the grinder. Grind again. 8. If your grinder is not powerful enough, grind the sieved flour again. This gives you very very fine flour. 9. Store rice flour in a air tight dry glass jar at room temperature for 2 months. You can refrigerate for an year or more.

title: Hung Curd Recipe
ingredients: 2 cups curd, 1 pinch salt
instructions: 1. Place a strainer over a deep bowl and spread a thin muslin or cheese cloth. Pour yogurt in the center of the cloth. 2. Bring all the 4 edges together to the center and make a knot with one edge to secure the yogurt. Squeeze gently to drain excess whey. Check pics in the post. 3. Hang it just above your kitchen sink for 30 to 45 mins, so that the whey drips to the sink. 4. Place back the yogurt pack in the strainer. Place an heavy object such as a ceramic bowl or mortar pestle over it. 5. Move the entire setup (bowl, strainer, yogurt and the heavy object) to the refrigerator. Rest for 4 to 8 hours, until most of the why is strained. 6. Open the know and check. Hung curd must be thick and creamy without any whey. 7. Transfer to a air tight container, preferable glass container and refrigerate until used. You can sprinkle some salt and also eat it right away.

title: Hyderabadi Biryani
ingredients: 1.5 cups basmati rice, 0.5 kg chicken, 0.375 teaspoon biryani masala, 1 onions, 4 tablespoons coriander leaves, 4 tablespoons mint leaves, 4 tablespoons oil, 2 tablespoons ghee, 0.25 teaspoon saffron strands, 1.25 tablespoons ginger garlic paste, 0.25 teaspoon turmeric, 1.5 green chilies, 0.6666666666666666 cup yogurt, 1 tablespoons lemon juice, 1.25 teaspoon red chili powder, 1.25 teaspoon biryani masala powder, 0.375 teaspoon green cardamom powder, 0.75 teaspoon salt, 7 cups water, 1.125 teaspoon salt, 1 teaspoon oil, 1 bay leaf, 3 inch cinnamon, 6 cloves 6 cloves ((laung)), 4 green cardamom, 1 star anise, 1 black cardamom, 2 strands mace, 0.5 tsp caraway seeds
instructions: 1. Slice onion thinly, separate the layers and set aside. 2. Heat 4 tbsps oil or ghee in a deep pan. 3. Fry the onions stirring often until deep golden to brown. 4. Do not burn else they turn bitter. Remove them from oil & set aside. 5. Cool & pour this oil to the marinade later. 6. Add yogurt, salt, turmeric, chili powder, biryani or garam masala powder, cardamom powder, green chilli, ginger garlic paste & lemon juice to a mixing bowl. 7. Mix well & taste this. If needed add more salt or spice. 8. Next add in chicken & marinate for 2 hours to overnight for best results. 9. Overnight marination in fridge yields best soft succulent chicken. 10. Wash rice till the water runs clear. Soak for 30 minutes. Drain & set aside. 11. Heat 6 to 7 cups water in a pot. Add 1 tsp oil, whole spices & salt. 12. Taste this. The water has to be slightly salty. 13. Boil water rapidly for 5 mins to release the aroma of spices. 14. Add rice & cook till it is ¾ or 75% done. 15. The center of the rice must be still slightly uncooked. 16. Do not make the rice mushy. Drain off to a colander. 17. While the rice cooks, transfer marinated chicken to a heavy bottom casserole or non-stick pan. 18. Add oil, ½ of the fried onions, mint & coriander leaves. Mix & spread chicken in a single even layer. 19. Layer the cooked rice evenly, sprinkle fried onions, mint & coriander leaves over the chicken. 20. Sprinkle ¼ tsp masala powder. Pour the saffron milk all over & then ghee. 21. Cover the pot with a foil to trap the steam or dum. Then cover with a tight & heavy lid. 22. Bake for 30 to 40 mins at 390F or 200C in a preheated oven. The timings will slightly vary depending on the depth of the casserole/pot used. Once done, if the chicken is too moist then bake for another 10 mins. 23. Place this over a thick hot griddle or tawa. Set the flame to medium high such that the flame reaches all over the diameter of the casserole. Cook this way for exactly 20 mins. 24. Reduce the flame to lowest. Cook for exactly 15 mins. Off the flame. Rest for 20 to 30 mins. 25. Garnish hyderabadi biryani with fresh chopped coriander leaves & serve with raita.

title: Hyderabadi Marag (Mutton Soup)
ingredients: 0.5 kg lbs Bone-in mutton, 0.25 cup ghee, 1 cup onions, 3 green chilies, 1 cup mint leaves, 1 cup coriander leaves, 0.75 tablespoon ginger, 0.75 tablespoon garlic, 1 teaspoon salt, 0.5 cup yogurt, 2 cashews, 2 blanched soaked almonds, 3.25 cups water, 4 cups water, 0.625 cup full fat milk, 0.5 teaspoon garam masala, 2 cinnamon pieces, 1 black cardamom, 8 green cardamoms, 6 cloves 6 cloves, 0.5 teaspoon all-spice, 0.5 teaspoon shahi jeera, 1 teaspoon black pepper corn
instructions: 1. Heat ghee on a medium flame and saute onions and green chilies, until light golden. (not brown, Takes about 6 to 7 mins) 2. Lower the heat and stir in all the spices. Saute for a minute and add the mutton. Increase the heat to medium and saute for 5 to 6 mins. 3. Add salt, ginger garlic, mint and coriander (reserving 1 tablespoon each for later). Saute for 2 mins. Pour half of the hot water and cook for 1 hour. 4. Reduce the flame to low to bring down the temperature slightly. Whisk the yogurt well with a fork to a really smooth texture. Pour a few tablespoons of the stock, in batches. Mix well and pour back to the marag. 5. Cover and cook until the meat is completely tender (takes me 30 mins). Meanwhile grind cashews and almonds in a grinder with ½ cup milk, to a smooth puree. Stir in the rest of the milk and mix. 6. Stir in this nut puree and more hot water during the last 10 minutes along with the rest of the mint and coriander leaves. 7. Taste test and add more salt as needed along with garam masala (optional) if you want. 8. Garnish Marag with fried onions and serve hot. It is usually eaten with Hyderabadi Square naan but artisan bread, pav, brioche or naan also make great sides.

title: Idli Chutney (Dosa Chutney Recipe)
ingredients: 1 grams raw coconut, 1.5 tablespoons chana dal, 0.5 tablespoon urad dal, 3 green chilies, 0.875 teaspoon cumin seeds, 1.5 sprigs curry leaves, 3.5 garlic, 0.3333333333333333 inch ginger, 1 tablespoon oil
instructions: 1. Heat 2 teaspoons oil in a medium pan and add 2 tablespoons chana dal (or 1½ tablespoons chana dal and ½ tablespoon urad dal). Alternately, you can use 3 tbsps peanuts. 2. Fry them until lightly golden. 3. Add 2 to 4 slit green chilies, 3 to 4 small crushed garlic and 1 to 2 sprigs curry leaves. Do not forget to slit or chop the chilies else they will burst in the pan. Also do the same with garlic and pat dry the curry leaves to avoid splatters. 4. Fry until the chilies blister and curry leaves turn crisp. Turn down to low and stir in the cumin seeds. Fry for 40 seconds. Be careful as they can burn very fast. 5. As soon as you smell the roasted cumin, add the raw coconut -100 grams (1 cup loosely measured). 6. Stir and saute for 2 to 3 mins until really hot. Turn off and cool down. 7. Transfer to a grinder with salt and pour water in batches. Start with half cup and add more as you grind. I usually reserve one to 2 chilies aside and add them if required while blending. This way it won’t become too hot/spicy. 8. Grind to a thick chutney. Taste test to adjust salt. 9. If you want you may temper – heat 1 tsp oil and add a pinch of mustard seeds, 1 pinch of split urad dal, 1 broken chili and 2 to 3 torn curry leaves. When the urad dal turns golden, turn off and add hing. Pour this over the idli dosa chutney. Serve with idli dosa or any snacks.

title: Idli Podi Recipe (Milagai Podi)
ingredients: 0.25 cup chana dal, 0.25 cup urad dal, 0.25 cup sesame seeds, 0.875 teaspoon salt, 12 red chilies, 1 tablespoon cumin seeds, 2.5 sprigs curry leaves, 0.25 cup coconut, 1.5 garlic cloves, 0.25 teaspoon hing
instructions: 1. To make idli podi prepare the following ingredients: 2. Here are the optional ingredients which are our favorite. These ingredients are going to take your podi to the next level. 3. 2 to 3 sprigs curry leaves (optional, rinse and pat dry) 4. Since the roasting time of each ingredient is different, you need to dry roast them individually / separately for best results. To a heavy bottom pan, add chana dal. On a medium heat, dry roast them stiring often until they change their color to light brown. 5. Roasting them on a high heat is only going to change their color fast without roasting them from inside. So it is important to roast on a low to medium heat. Transfer them to a plate when they change to light brown color. 6. To the same pan, add urad dal and stir fry on a medium heat. 7. These lentils tend to burn fast if you don’t stir often. So be careful. Remove them to the same plate once they turn to light brown color. 8. Add sesame seeds and roast them the same way. They may sizzle or steam when they are about to be done. Remove to the same plate. 9. Add cumin seeds and roast until deep golden to light brown. They begin to smell earthy and pungent when done. Remove to the same plate. 10. Next add red chilies and roast until crisp. Keep regulating the flame from low to medium and vice versa as needed. 11. They turn slightly crisp when done but will turn fully crisp upon cooling. Transfer to a plate. 12. To the same pan, add curry leaves. Please make sure you pat dry them before using. 13. Roast the leaves until crisp. This takes only a few minutes. Remove them to the plate. 14. Add garlic and dry roast for 2 to 3 mins. 15. Stir in the coconut and roast until hot and aromatic. Keep this in the pan itself 16. Cool all the ingredients. 17. If you try to grind all the roasted ingredients together, you may end up with a too coarse powder. First grind the red chilies, salt and curry leaves in a grinder to make a fine powder. A lot of people also add hing. If you want you may add ¼ teaspoon at this stage. 18. Try to grind as fine as possible because this also gives a nice color to your podi. You may set aside some of this chilli podi so you can adjust the heat level later. 19. Scrape off the sides of the blender jar, then add dal, cumin and sesame. 20. Grind to a fine or coarse powder as per your liking. Add coconut and garlic. 21. Grind again. Taste test and add more salt. If the idli podi is not spicy, you may add little red chilli powder and grind again. You can also heat the chili powder, cool and add it here for better shelf life. Store idli podi in an airtight glass or steel jar at room temperature. It stores well for several months.

title: Idli Sambar Recipe (Tiffin Sambar)
ingredients: 0.75 cup dal, 2 cups water, 2 tablespoon tamarind, Salt as needed, 13.5 shallots, 7 french beans, 1 medium carrot, 0.5 cup pumpkin, 1 tomato, 0.125 teaspoon turmeric, 0.375 teaspoon red chilli powder, 2 tablespoons coriander leaves, 4.5 cups water, 1 tablespoon oil, 2 sprigs curry leaves, 1 red chili, 0.75 teaspoon cumin seeds, 0.75 teaspoon mustard seeds, 1 pinch methi seeds, 1 pinch hing, 0.5 tablespoon urad dal, 1 tablespoon chana dal, 3 red chilies, 1 tablespoon coriander seeds, 0.5 teaspoon cumin seeds, 0.25 teaspoon methi seeds, 2 tablespoon coconut
instructions: 1. Rinse and pressure cook dal with 2 cups of water for 2 whistles or until soft. 2. Or alternately cook in a pot until soft. If using a Instant pot, pressure cook on high pressure for 10 mins & wait for natural pressure release. 3. Mash it and set aside. If you like the hotel style tiffin sambar, you can also add pumpkin to the dal for cooking. 4. Mash everything together and set aside. 5. Soak tamarind in little hot water, squeeze and extract the pulp. Keep this aside. 6. In a pan, dry roast red chilies, chana dal and urad dal on a medium flame until lightly golden. 7. Next add coriander seeds and roast all of them until a nice smell comes. 8. When the dals turn deep golden remove to a plate. 9. Next in the same pan saute methi seeds until slightly dark then add cumin. 10. When the cumin turns aromatic remove to a plate. Cool these completely and make a fine powder. 11. Add oil to a pot and heat up. Throw in the cumin, mustard and methi. 12. When they pop, add red chilies, curry leaves and hing. 13. When the leaves turn crisp, set aside half the tempering if you wish to add at the end. 14. Add veggies and saute for 2 to 3 minutes. Add tomatoes and saute until soft. 15. Sprinkle sambhar powder and red chilli powder if using. Saute for a minute without burning. 16. Pour water and cook until the vegetables are soft cooked. 17. Next pour filtered tamarind and then the mashed dal. Stir and bring the idli sambar to a boil. 18. Taste test and add more tamarind or salt as needed. Add the rest of the tempering and coriander leaves. 19. When it begins to bubble. Switch off. You can also make a fresh tempering but I don't do. 20. Serve idli sambar hot with soft idli, dosa, uthappam, pongal & vada.

title: Indian Butter Chickpeas
ingredients: 8.5 oz cans chickpeas, 0.125 teaspoon baking soda, 0.3333333333333333 cup raw cashews, 3.5 tablespoons unsalted butter, 1 tablespoon ginger garlic paste, 1.5 teaspoon garam masala, 1.5 to 2 teaspoon Kashmiri chili powder, 0.125 teaspoon turmeric, 1.5 teaspoon coriander powder, 0.75 teaspoon cumin powder, 0.75 teaspoon sea salt, 4.5 oz tomato paste, 1 cup water chickpea cooked liquid, 1 tablespoon kasuri methi, 0.29166666666666663 cup heavy cream, 2 tablespoons coriander leaves, lemon juice
instructions: 1. You will need the following ingredients: 2. 2 – 15 oz cans chickpeas (drained or 3 cups boiled chickpeas from 1 cup dried) 3. 1½ to 2 teaspoon Kashmiri chili powder (adjust to heat tolerance, I use 2 tsps home ground) 4. Omit this step if you are using nut butter or heavy cream as a cashew substitute. Soak cashews in ¼ cup hot water for 15 mins. Grind with the same water, in a small grinder to a smooth thick puree. Stir in another half cup water and reserve aside for later. (While they soak, proceed with the next step.) 5. Begin to melt 2 tablespoons butter on a low flame. Before it melts completely, add ginger and garlic. Sauté for 1 to 2 mins without burning. 6. Stir in the ground spices – red chili powder, turmeric, garam masala, coriander powder, cumin powder and salt. 7. Add the tomato paste, baking soda (optional), 1 cup water (or chickpea cooked liquid) & chickpeas. (Or Add 1¼ cup passata/tomato puree and omit water) 8. Mix well and bring to a gentle boil on a medium heat. 9. Cover and cook for 10 mins, until your sauce is really thick and the chickpeas are tender. (While this cooks, grind the soaked cashews.) 10. Stir in the cashew puree/nut butter and heavy cream (start with lower amount of cream and adjust as per taste & consistency). If you want, you may temper the cream by mixing 3 to 4 tbsps of cooked sauce with the heavy cream. This way you will make sure the cream is not split after adding to the sauce. 11. Continue to simmer for another 4 to 5 mins, or a little longer until slightly thick, yet runny. 12. If you have used cashew cream, the sauce will thicken after cooling down, so make sure you turn off when the sauce is slightly runny/ flowy. The sauce won’t taste good if it is too thick. 13. Crush kasuri methi and add it along with the reserved butter and more salt or garam masala if required. Taste test first before making the adjustments. (I erred in adding these ingredients early before adding the cream and nut puree, but add them when you are done with the cooking) 14. Let the butter chickpeas cool down a bit. Squeeze lemon juice. Garnish with cream and coriander leaves. Serve it hot over rice, quinoa, millets or flatbreads like Naan or kulcha. (If you have used baking soda, I highly recommend adding lemon juice before serving)

title: Indian Drumstick Vegetable Recipe
ingredients: 3 drumsticks, 1.5 cups onions, 2 medium potatoes, 2 tomatoes, 2 green chilies, 0.5 teaspoon red chili powder, 1.5 tablespoon Oil, 1 sprig curry leaves, 0.5 teaspoon mustard, 0.5 teaspoon cumin, 0.125 teaspoon turmeric, 1 teaspoon coriander powder, 0.5 teaspoon garam masala, 2 tablespoon coriander leaves
instructions: 1. Wash drumsticks under running water. If not tender scrape off the skin lightly. Wash and chop to finger length pieces. Cube potatoes to medium sized pieces, and add them to a bowl of water until used. 2. Add oil to a pan and heat. Add mustard and cumin. When they begin to splutter, add curry leaves, green chilies and finely chopped onions. Fry until transparent or pink stirring often. 3. Add drumsticks and potatoes. Fry for 2 to 3 minutes. Add chopped tomatoes, salt and turmeric. I usually deseed the tomatoes and use for this recipe. Fry until the tomatoes turn mushy. 4. Then add red chili powder and fry for 2 minutes. If you wish to add garam masala or coriander powder add it now. 5. Pour water just enough to cover the drumsticks and potatoes. Cover and cook till the veggies are soft cooked. Garnish drumstick gravy with coriander leaves. Serve with plain rice & ghee.

title: Indian Lamb Curry Recipe
ingredients: 2.2 lbs bone-in lamb, 0.25 cup yogurt, 2.5 teaspoons Kashmiri red chilli powder, 0.5 teaspoon turmeric, 2.5 teaspoons garam masala, 0.875 teaspoon cumin powder, 1 tablespoon coriander powder, 2 teaspoon salt, 6 tablespoons oil, 1 bay leaf, 6 green cardamoms, 3 inch cinnamon piece, 2 black cardamom, 1.5 star anise, 2 cups onions, 1.5 cup tomatoes, 1.5 green chilli slit, 2 tablespoon ginger garlic paste, 4 tablespoons mint leaves, 4.5 cup water, 0.5 tablespoon kasuri methi, 0.25 cup coriander leaves
instructions: 1. Add all the ground spices to a small bowl and mix them. 2. Marinate lamb with half of this spice mix, half of the ginger garlic, salt, kasuri methi and yogurt (or lemon juice/ vinegar). Cover and set aside for about 2 hours to overnight in the refrigerator. 3. Heat 1 to 2 tablespoon oil/ghee in a heavy bottom pan and add star anise, bay leaf, black cardamom, green cardamom & cinnamon. 4. Once the spices begin to sizzle, add the mint leaves and stir in the marinated lamb and braise on a medium heat for about 8 to 10 minutes. 5. Pour half of the hot water and mix well. Cover and let cook on a low to medium heat for 1 hour. Keep stirring every 15 mins and add more hot water as you cook. 6. Meanwhile in another pan, heat the rest of the oil/ghee. Add the onions & green chilies. Saute on a medium heat for 6 to 7 mins. 7. Reduce the heat and stir fry for a few more minutes until deep golden but not burnt. 8. Add rest of the ginger garlic & saute until the raw smell of ginger garlic goes away. This takes 1 to 2 minutes. 9. Turn off the heat & stir in the rest of the ground spices, immediately pour the tomato puree. Cook on a medium heat until the raw flavor of tomatoes is gone. 10. Transfer this to the lamb after 1 hour of cooking lamb. Cook covered on a low heat until the meat is fully cooked and fork tender, for another 50 mins or more. Pour more hot water if required as you cook & keep stirring often, every 5 to 7 mins. 11. Taste test and add more salt or garam masala & kasuri methi if needed. If you want more heat in your lamb curry, add some freshly crushed black pepper. 12. Garnish lamb curry with coriander leaves and serve with roti/ naan/ rice or with any artisan bread. 13. Lamb curry tastes best after resting for a few hours or even the next day. Reheat & adjust the salt when you are ready to serve.

title: Indian Rice Pudding (Better than restaurant)
ingredients: 0.5 cup rice, 5 cups whole milk, 0.29166666666666663 cup sugar, 0.5 teaspoon cardamom, 0.25 cup unsalted pistachios, 1 tablespoon rice flour, 1 tablespoon ghee, 0.25 cup cashews cup sweet raisins
instructions: 1. Pour 5 cups milk to a heavy bottom pot. Bring to a gentle boil on a medium heat. Meanwhile rinse half cup rice, drain the water and add it to the hot milk. 2. Cook on a medium heat, stirring often at the bottom and sides. until the rice is soft, and slightly mushy. It takes me 20 mins to reach this stage. 3. This step is optional but helps thicken the pudding faster. Add 1 tablespoon rice flour (not glutinous flour) to a small bowl and mix with half cup milk/ water. 4. Mix well to make a lump-free slurry. 5. When the rice is slightly mushy, mash it slightly with the back of the ladle/ spatula to thicken. 6. Pour the rice slurry slowly to the rice pudding, stirring well with your other hand to incorporate it. The temperature falls here, bring it back to a boil and simmer for 3 minutes or until it turns thick and creamy. 7. Add ¼ to ⅓ cup sugar (or 3 to 4 tbsps condensed milk) & cook until the pudding is thick, yet of flowing consistency. It will become thicker after cooling. Taste test and add more sugar if you want. 8. Stir in ½ teaspoon ground cardamom. If you want you may also add 1 pinch of saffron (crushed) or/ and 1 tsp rose water. 9. Turn off the heat. Add ¼ cup chopped unsalted pistachios. (If you have only salted, blanch them in hot water for 1 to 2 mins. Drain, pat dry in a cloth and chop.) 10. This is the consistency we prefer. It will become slightly thicker after cooling down.

title: Indian Tofu Kadai Recipe
ingredients: 225 grams extra firm tofu, 1.5 cup tomatoes, 0.75 cup onion, 1 cup bell peppers, 1 teaspoon ginger, 1 sprig curry leaves, 2 tablespoon oil, 1.25 teaspoon Kashmiri red chili powder, 1.5 to 2 teaspoons garam masala, 1 teaspoon salt, 1 teaspoon kasuri methi, 1 tablespoon soya sauce, 1 teaspoon sugar
instructions: 1. Thaw the frozen tofu in the refrigerator & press down gently in between your palms to remove excess liquid/water. 2. Wrap in a kitchen towel/ papers to get rid of excess moisture. Chop to 1 inch portions of ½ to ¾ inch thickness. 3. On a low flame, heat half of the oil in a pan. Add curry leaves and ginger. Stir fry until aromatic without burning, for 30 to 60 seconds. 4. Stir in red chili powder and half of the garam masala. Add the tomatoes and let cook, partially covered until thick and oil begins to separate. 5. Meanwhile, heat 1 tsp oil in a non-stick or cast iron pan. Place the tofu and fry them until golden and crisp. Turn them to the other side with a spatula & fry until crisp. Transfer this aside to a plate. 6. Add the rest of the oil and add onions and bell peppers. Stir fry on a high heat until aromatic and slightly soft, for 3 mins. 7. Stir in tofu, onions, bell peppers, soya sauce (optional), sugar, salt & kasuri methi (optional). If it is too thick & dry, pour ¼ cup hot water. Stir well and let cook covered for 3 mins. 8. Taste test to add more salt & garam masala as needed. If you want squeeze some lemon juice while serving. 9. Serve tofu stir fry with rice or chapatti/roti & a vegetable salad. Alternately stir in some cooked noodles or pasta for a complete meal.

title: Instant Pot Brown Rice Recipe
ingredients: 2 cup brown rice, 2.5 cup water, 0.75 tablespoon oil, 0.125 teaspoon salt
instructions: 1. Rinse 1 cup brown rice thrice & drain the water completely. You can also soak it up to 30 mins for softer rice. 2. Add it to a pot along with 1¼ cups water and 1 tsp oil (optional). 3. Pour 1 cup water in the inner pot of instant pot. 4. Secure the lid and set the steam release handle to sealing position. 5. Pressure cook for 20 mins & allow the pressure to release naturally. 6. Following this method I cook up to 2 cups of brown rice along with any one of these – ¾ cup dal, 1 cup soaked chana, 1 cup soaked rajma or up to 4 chilled eggs at one time in my 6 Qt IP. Some times I also cook whole potatoes, yam, beetroot or sweet potatoes along with the brown rice. 7. This method is great as you don’t need to clean the large pot of the IP. You will be left with just the inner pots for a clean up. 8. Cooking in the inner pot : I cook brown rice directly in the inner pot when I have to make over 4 servings or over 2 cups. Usually rice tends to get mushier when made in large quantity following the pot-in-pot method. 9. Cooking it directly in a larger pot prevents the mushy texture. Each grain gets enough space to expand and cook well in a larger pot. 10. Adding a small amount of oil or ghee to the pot prevents the grains from turning sticky & mushy. So absolutely no mushy brown rice even when you cook a lot of it! 11. Sometimes I saute the brown rice in little oil or ghee for 2 mins before pressure cooking. I always feel this sauteing also brings out the nutty flavor of brown rice. 12. Most times I prefer not to flavor my brown rice as we mostly serve it with curry. However you can also flavor it with some garlic, cumin, whole spices, lemon zest or herbs. All you need to do is just saute your flavoring ingredient before adding the rice to the pot. 13. Heat the instant pot by pressing saute button. When it displays hot, pour oil or ghee in the inner pot. 14. Add the rinsed & drained brown rice. Saute for 2 to 3 mins. If you like saute some spices or garlic prior to adding the rice. Cumin, star anise, mace, cinnamon, cloves & cardamoms go well. 15. Pour water and add salt if you like. I do not add salt. You can also use broth or stock instead of water. Give a good mix. 16. Secure the lid of the Instant pot. Set the steam release handle to sealing position. 17. Pressure cook on high pressure and set the timer to 23 mins. 18. When the pressure releases naturally open the lid. If you see a layer of rice being hard, then quickly fluff up with a fork and cover the lid back. Rest for 2 to 3 mins. 19. You will get fluffy, flavorful & perfect instant pot brown rice. Serve brown rice with any curry or dal. You can also use this for fried rice or any tempered rice.

title: Instant Pot Tikka Masala Recipe
ingredients: 1.5 tablespoon ghee, 1 pound chicken, 0.25 cup Greek yogurt, 1 teaspoon ginger, 1 teaspoon garlic, 1 tablespoon lemon juice, 0.5 teaspoon salt, 0.75 teaspoon Kashmiri red chili powder, 1 teaspoon garam masala, 1.5 tablespoons oil, 1.5 cups yellow, 1 inch ginger, 4.5 garlic, 1 teaspoon salt, 1 pound ripe tomatoes tomato puree, 1 teaspoon Kashmiri red chili powder, 1.5 teaspoon garam masala, 1 tablespoon coriander powder, 1 teaspoon cumin powder, 1 teaspoon sugar, 0.25 cup water, 1 tablespoon kasuri methi, 0.5 cup heavy cream, 2 tablespoons coriander leaves
instructions: 1. Marinate chicken with the ingredients mentioned under “to marinate”. Cover and rest in the refrigerator for at least 30 mins to overnight. (If you refrigerate overnight, leave it on the counter for 30 mins before you begin to cook it.) 2. Add ghee/oil to the steel insert of the instant pot and press saute button. Spread it all over the pot. When the ghee is hot, transfer the chicken and spread it to a single layer. 3. Let cook for 2 to 3 mins and stir to turn the pieces to the other side. Cook for another 2 to 3 mins, until the spices begin to brown slightly. Press cancel and remove the chicken to a bowl. It’s alright if the chicken is slightly undercooked. 4. Pour 2 to 3 tablespoons of water and deglaze the pot well. Pour the liquid over the chicken. 5. Add the rest of the ghee or oil and add onions, salt, ginger and garlic. Saute for 3 to 4 mins. If you want you may saute until golden. 6. Stir in the tomatoes and all the ground spices – chili powder, 1 tsp garam masala, coriander powder and cumin. Pour water. 7. Secure the lid and pressure cook for 10 mins. Release the pressure slowly and blend the sauce with a hand/stick blender to a smooth or coarse sauce. Pour the deglazed liquid to the IP and simmer the sauce for 5 mins, until thick. 8. Add the chicken & mix well. Cook for 3 mins until the chicken is fully cooked through and tender. 9. Crush kasuri methi in your palms and add it along with sugar, more salt & garam masala if required (taste test first). Press cancel and stir in the cream. Garnish Instant Pot Tikka Masala with chopped coriander leaves.

title: Irani Chai Recipe (Hyderabadi Irani Tea)
ingredients: 1.5 cups water, 2 tablespoons black tea, 6 green cardamoms, 1.5 to 2 tablespoons refined sugar, 1.5 cup full fat milk
instructions: 1. Pour 1½ cups milk to a pot and add 1 to 1½ tablespoons sugar. Bring it to a boil & reduce the heat. Let simmer until it reduces to ½ the quantity. 2. You can leave a spoon in the pot to avoid overflowing. 3. Meanwhile crush 4 green cardamoms and keep aside. 4. Add the cardamoms, 2 tablespoons black tea (loose tea leaves or dust) and 1½ cups water to a pot. 5. Cover the pot and bring it to a boil. Reduce the heat and simmer for 7 to 8 minutes, until the decoction reduces to 1 cup. This decoction is actually made in a special closed pot which traps the steam and retains the flavors of chai. Since we don’t have that kind of pots at home, it can be made in sauce pan. You can place an heavy object so the lid sits tightly on the pot and does not let the steam escape. This is a optional step. 6. This is how it looks when it is done. 7. Strain the chai to a kettle. It should be around a cup. 8. Once the milk has reduced to half the quantity, you will see layers of cream on top. If you don’t like the cream, take a ladle full of milk and pour it back to the pot. Repeat this a few times until the cream breaks down & the milk turns frothy & is aerated. Keep doing this a few times. 9. Divide the milk among 2 to 3 serving cups. 10. Top with the decoction. You can play with the quantity of decoction and milk to suit your taste. Serve Irani Chai hot or warm with your favorite tea-biscuits.

title: Jackfruit Seeds Kurma Curry
ingredients: 1 cup jackfruit seeds, 1 cup onions, 1 green chili, 1.5 teaspoon Ginger garlic paste, 1 bay leaf, 0.5 teaspoon cumin, 2 tablespoons Oil, 1 teaspoon Garam masala powder, 0.25 tsp Red chili powder, 0.25 teaspoon turmeric, 0.625 teaspoon Salt, 1 tbsp tamarind paste, 0.25 cup coconut, 2 tbsps poppy seed
instructions: 1. Chop each jack fruit seed to half or quarters. Peel off the white skin. 2. Scrapping off the brown skin is optional. It can be scraped off with a knife if you prefer. 3. Rinse the seeds and boil them till tender in enough water. Drain the water and set them aside. 4. Dry roast the poppy seeds on a low heat until crunchy. Cool and make a fine powder in a blender. Add coconut to the same blender and make a smooth paste with little water 5. Pour oil to a hot pan and add cumin & curry leaves (or bay leaf). 6. Once the seeds crackle, add ginger garlic paste. Saute till it turns aromatic. 7. Add chopped onions , slit green chilies and fry till the onions turn golden. 8. Saute jack fruit seeds, turmeric, garam masala and red chili powder for about 2 mins. 9. Add the coconut paste and fry for another 3 minutes. 10. If using add the tamarind paste, salt and water just enough to cover the seeds. 11. Cover and cook jackfruit seeds curry on a low flame for 5 to 8 minutes or till you see traces of oil on top. 12. Serve the curry with rice or roti.

title: Jalebi recipe
ingredients: 1 cup all-purpose flour 125 grams, 2 tablespoons corn starch white corn flour, 0.125 teaspoon turmeric, 0.5 cup curd, 0.5 cup water, 0.5 teaspoon baking soda, 1 teaspoon lemon juice, oil, 1 cup sugar, 0.625 cup water, 1 pinch saffron, 0.25 teaspoon cardamom powder, 1 teaspoon lemon juice, 1 sauce bottle
instructions: 1. Add sugar and water to a pot. 2. Boil on a medium heat until it reaches a 1 string consistency. 3. Take a small portion of the syrup in a spoon. Cool it slightly. 4. Take it in between your thumb and fore finger. 5. Gently move the fingers away from each other, you must see a single string. 6. Pour lemon juice, cardamom powder and saffron. Remove from heat. Stir and set aside. 7. If your syrup goes beyond this stage, then sprinkle some water and mix. Recheck for the 1 string consistency. 8. A. Method 1: For Instant jalebi : Add maida, cornflour and turmeric to a mixing bowl. Mix everything well until uniform. Next add curd. Pour water & make a thick lump free batter. The batter has to be thick but of flowing consistency. If needed add more water. Beat the batter well with a whisk in one direction in a circular motion for 4 mins. The batter will turn smooth. 9. B. Method 2: For fermentation : Mix together maida, besan & turmeric in a bowl. Pour water & make a thick batter of pouring consistency. Cover and ferment for 12 to 24 hours until the batter gets a very mild sour flavor. The batter will not rise, but will have tiny bubbles on top. 10. Heat ghee or oil on a medium heat to fry jalebis. 11. If using oil, then add 1 to 2 tbsp ghee to the oil. This enhances the flavor. 12. Pour 1 tsp lemon juice to the batter & mix. Skip the lemon juice if you have fermented the batter. 13. Add soda and mix gently just until combined. 14. Check batter consistency: The prepared batter must be smooth free flowing and thick. Spoon just 2 to 3 tbsp of batter to the sauce bottle to check if the consistency is right. 15. Next check if the oil is hot enough by dropping a small portion of the batter. It has to come up immediately without browning. 16. Now squeeze the bottle gently and move in circular motion to get spiral. If you are getting very thick jalebi, then the batter is thick. Next if you are getting very thin flat jalebis the batter is thin. 17. To fix thick batter, add a tbsp or more water. Next to fix thin batter, add a tbsp of maida (all purpose flour). Take a look at the pic in the FAQ section to fix the batter. 18. Mix the batter well. Spoon it to the bottle. 19. Ensure oil is hot and the flame set to medium high heat. 20. Squeeze in the batter gently in circular motions starting from the center moving outside. 21. You can also do it the other way. You will get properly shaped ones after making a few. 22. While the jalebi is getting fried, check the syrup. It must be slightly hot to warm when the jalebi is dipped into it. If not heat up a bit. 23. The last 1 minute, turn the flame to low and fry the jalebi. This helps to make them extra crisp. 24. When the jalebi is done it turns crisp. Remove it with a skewer and add to the warm sugar syrup directly. 25. Allow to rest for 2 mins. Remove to a plate. Continue to make more jalebis. 26. Serve jalebi hot.

title: Jeera Water (Cumin Water)
ingredients: 0.5 teaspoon Jeera, 1.25 cups water
instructions: 1. Pour water to a non-reactive pot or pan and then add jeera. You can also crush the seeds before adding to the water, so they release their flavor and volatile oils. You can do this easily by using a rolling pin and a board or using a mortar pestle. 2. On a medium heat, bring the water to a gentle boil. 3. Cover and simmer for 5 minutes. 4. Increase the heat slightly and let it come to a boil again. You will see the seeds being swollen up and begin to release the aroma. 5. Turn off the stove and cover it. Remove pan from heat and let cumin water cool somewhat. 6. It is best to have this warm. You may leave it on the stovetop so it remains warm longer. 7. Strain out the seeds from the water with a fine strainer or muslin. Strain it to serving cups. If the flavor is too strong for your taste you can also dilute it with more warm water. 8. Traditionally jeera water is consumed plain & more often at room temperature. This is how I always have it. But if you are trying to incorporate this for weight loss then you may squeeze in some lemon juice. Remember not to add lemon juice to hot jeera water. 9. Add honey if you prefer, if possible Manuka honey. Both lemon juice and honey are optional. Serve cumin water warm and fresh, the same day for maximum health benefits. 10. When mint or basil is added to the water, it enhances its digestive benefits. 11. Lemon juice added to jeera water helps with weight loss. 12. Honey and cinnamon powder added to cumin water fights respiratory infections. 13. Adding cinnamon powder to jeera water balances blood sugar levels. 14. While you can have jeera water at any time of the day, you get best results when you have it in the morning, on an empty stomach. Lastly if you are having it fresh, drink it at room temperature or when it is still warm.

title: Jowar Dosa Recipe
ingredients: 0.5 cup urad dal, 0.5 cup jowar, 0.5 cup raw rice, 0.25 teaspoon methi seeds, 1 cup urad dal, 3 cups cups jowar flour, 0.25 cup thick poha, 1.5 cups water, 1 cup jowar flour, 2 tablespoons onions, 1 green chili, 2 tablespoons coriander leaves, 5.5 curry leaves, 1 teaspoon ginger, 0.3333333333333333 teaspoon sea salt, 0.5 teaspoon cumin seeds, 2.5 cups water, 2 tablespoons ghee
instructions: 1. Wash jowar several times until the water runs clear. Soak it for about 6 hours. Wash rice, dal and methi. Soak them separately for 4 hours. Soaking the lentils and rice for longer can make the batter sour quickly with in few hours. (you may experiment to see what works) 2. Drain the water and blend dal, rice and methi with water just as needed until batter is smooth, fluffy and bubbly. 3. Drain water from jowar. Blend with water as needed, try to use less water. Since the grains are soaked well, the batter gets done well to smooth. 4. Transfer this to the urad dal batter. Mix well. The consistency should be moderately thick like the regular dosa batter. 5. Ferment overnight in a warm place or for 4 to 6 hours depending on the weather conditions. When you are ready to make the dosa, pour water as required and bring to a pouring consistency. (jump to make dosa section below) 6. Wash and soak urad dal for 6 hours. Rinse and soak poha in 1/3 cup water for at least 15 mins. Later rinse the urad dal and blend with poha and water to a bubbly, smooth and thick consistency. 7. To a large mixing bowl, add the jowar flour and pour water. Mix well with a whisk or hand to a smooth batter. Transfer the urad dal batter and mix both, very well. 8. Cover and ferment in a warm place overnight to 12 hours, until slightly bubbly and raised. Avoid over fermenting. (Read tips in the post to ferment well) 9. When you are ready to make your dosa, add salt as required and water (only if necessary) to bring to a spreading consistency. I add about 3 to 4 tbsps only. Mix very well to collapse some of the bubbles. 10. Grease if using a cast iron pan, a non-stick pan does not require greasing. Heat on a medium flame until hot enough, a sprinkle of little water should sizzle and evaporate. 11. Pour a ladle of batter in the center of the pan and begin to spread in circular motion, from the center moving to the edges. (see pictures) 12. Drizzle some butter/ghee/oil and let cook on a medium high flame until golden. The sides of the dosa will begin to leave the pan when it is ready. Gently remove to a plate or turn it to the other side and cook for a minute. 13. Turn it to the other side again and cook for 30 to 60 seconds until crisp. Remove to a serving plate. Make sure the pan is hot before making the next one and do not forget to stir the batter in the bowl. 14. In a mixing bowl, stir together flour, onions, chilies, curry leaves, coriander leaves, cumin, salt and ginger. Pour half of the water and begin to mix to a lump free batter, adding more water as needed. 15. The consistency of the batter should be runny for a thin dosa, slightly thicker for a thicker dosa. Rest aside for 15. 16. Grease your pan and heat on a medium high flame until really hot. A few drops of water sprinkled on the pan should sizzle and evaporate instantly. 17. Stir the batter very well with a ladle, as the solids settle at the bottom. Regulate the flame to medium high and begin to pour the batter, moving forward – starting from the edges in a circular pattern to cover the entire pan. 18. You will see some unfilled pockets that you need to fill up with the batter. (Check the pictures) 19. Drizzle 1 teaspoon ghee or oil across the edges & over the dry spots. Let cook for 3 mins on a medium high until the edges begin to leave the pan. 20. Gently turn with a spatula to the other side and cook for another 1 min. Lastly turn it again and cook until the base is crisp, for another minute. This is just a guideline for timing, it may vary depending on the kind of stove and cookware used. 21. Remove to a serving plate. Before you make the next dosa, make sure the pan is really hot (not smoking) and give the batter a good stir else the solids settle at the bottom.

title: Kadai Chicken Recipe (Chicken Karahi)
ingredients: 0.5 kg chicken bone-in chicken curry-cut, 0.75 cup onions, 1.25 cup tomato, 0.75 tablespoon ginger garlic paste, 2.5 tablespoons oil, 1 Kashmiri dried red chili, 0.5 teaspoon cumin seeds, 0.625 teaspoon salt, 0.5 teaspoon Kashmiri red chili powder, 0.25 teaspoon turmeric, 0.75 teaspoon garam masala, 1 teaspoon kasuri methi, 3 tablespoons heavy cream yogurt, 2 tablespoons coriander leaves, 1 tablespoon butter, 0.5 cup onions, 0.5 cup capsicums, 1.5 tablespoons coriander seeds, 1 Kashmiri red chili, 1 teaspoon fennel seeds, 2 cloves 2 cloves, 0.5 inch cinnamon, 3 green cardamoms, 2.5 black pepper corn
instructions: 1. On a medium heat, dry roast red chili and coriander seeds for 2 minutes. Add cloves, cardamoms, fennel seeds and cinnamon. Roast until they smell aromatic, for 1 to 2 mins. 2. Cool this and make a fine powder. Keep this aside. 3. On a medium flame, heat ghee/oil in a pan. Add cumin seeds and red chili. 4. When the seeds begin to sizzle, add the onions and saute until golden for 4 to 5 mins. 5. Stir in ginger garlic paste and saute for 30 to 60 seconds. 6. Add chicken and stir fry for 3 to 4 minutes, until the chicken turns pale. Lower the heat and stir in turmeric, red chili powder, salt and kadai masala. 7. Mix well and pour the pureed tomatoes. Cook on a medium heat until the tomato puree turns slightly thick & loses its raw flavor, for 3 to 4 mins. 8. Partially cover the pan and cook further until the chicken is tender and fully cooked through. The gravy should be thick at this stage. 9. Optional: Meanwhile melt butter in a wok. Add the bell peppers and onions, stir fry on a high heat until the capsicum is slightly blistered, for 2 to 3 mins, Avoid overcooking, keep them crunchy. Add this to the kadai chicken. 10. Taste test and add more salt if needed along with garam masala and kasuri methi. 11. Stir in the cashew puree. If using heavy cream or yogurt, pour it to a small bowl. Take a portion of the gravy and mix with it. Add back the tempered cream/yogurt to the kadai chicken. 12. Simmer for 2 mins & avoid overcooking to retain the crunch of bell peppers. Garnish with coriander leaves or ginger juliennes and slit green chilies. 13. Serve kadai chicken with flatbreads or basmati rice.

title: Kadai Paneer Recipe
ingredients: 1.5 tablespoons coriander seeds, 1.5 Kashmiri dried red chilies, 2.5 green cardamoms, 1 teaspoon fennel seeds, 2 cloves 2 cloves, 0.25 inch cinnamon, 1 tablespoon oil, 1 cup onions, 1.5 to 2 cups tomatoes, 6 cashew nuts, 1.25 tablespoon oil, 1 teaspoon ginger garlic paste, 0.5 teaspoon Kashmiri red chili powder, 0.5 teaspoon garam masala, 1 teaspoon kasuri methi, 0.625 teaspoon salt, 1.5 to 2 cups water, 1 onion, 0.5 cup capsicum, 225 grams paneer, 0.75 teaspoon ginger, 2 tablespoons coriander leaves
instructions: 1. To a small grinder jar, add red chilies, cardamoms, fennel seeds, coriander seeds, cloves & cinnamon. If you add more cinnamon it can ruin the dish. Make a fine powder and keep aside. 1½ tablespoons coriander seeds , 1 to 2 Kashmiri dried red chilies, 2 to 3 green cardamoms, 1 teaspoon fennel seeds, 2 cloves, ¼ inch cinnamon 2. To a small grinder jar, add red chilies, cardamoms, fennel seeds, coriander seeds, cloves & cinnamon. If you add more cinnamon it can ruin the dish. Make a fine powder and keep aside. 3. Heat oil in a pan. Add onions & cashew nuts. Saute them on a medium high flame until the onions turn transparent or pink. 1 tablespoon oil, 1 cup (125 grams) onions , 6 cashew nuts 4. Heat oil in a pan. Add onions & cashew nuts. Saute them on a medium high flame until the onions turn transparent or pink. 5. Add tomatoes and saute until completely mushy. 1½ to 2 cups (300 grams) tomatoes 6. Add tomatoes and saute until completely mushy. 7. Cool this completely and add to a blender jar. Blend to a smooth paste or puree. Set this aside. 8. To the same pan, add oil and heat it. 1 to 1½ tablespoon oil 9. To the same pan, add oil and heat it. 10. Saute onion and capsicum on the highest flame until half done. They must be slightly tender yet crunchy. 1 onion, ½ cup capsicum 11. Saute onion and capsicum on the highest flame until half done. They must be slightly tender yet crunchy. 12. Add paneer and toss for 1 to 2 mins. Set all these aside. Do not over fry these. 200 to 250 grams paneer 13. Add paneer and toss for 1 to 2 mins. Set all these aside. Do not over fry these. 14. Heat the rest of the oil and saute ginger garlic paste until the raw smell goes off. 1 teaspoon ginger garlic paste 15. Heat the rest of the oil and saute ginger garlic paste until the raw smell goes off. 16. Next transfer the ground kadai masala and saute on a low to medium heat for 2 to 3 mins until it turns fragrant. Do not burn. 17. Add the ground onion tomato paste and saute for 2 mins. 18. Next add garam masala and red chili powder. Saute for another 2 mins until the masala turns fragrant. ½ teaspoon Kashmiri red chili powder, ½ teaspoon garam masala 19. Next add garam masala and red chili powder. Saute for another 2 mins until the masala turns fragrant. 20. Pour 1 cup water and salt. If you feel the gravy is too thick, pour ¼ to ½ cup more water. Allow it to boil until the gravy thickens and traces of oil appear on top. ½ to ¾ teaspoon salt 21. Pour 1 cup water and salt. If you feel the gravy is too thick, pour ¼ to ½ cup more water. Allow it to boil until the gravy thickens and traces of oil appear on top. 22. Then crush and add kasuri methi. Stir well again. Taste the gravy and add more salt or garam masala as needed. 1 teaspoon kasuri methi 23. Then crush and add kasuri methi. Stir well again. Taste the gravy and add more salt or garam masala as needed. 24. Add the sauteed paneer, capsicum and onion. 25. Stir well & finally add ginger juliennes and coriander leaves. ½ to 1 teaspoon ginger , 2 tablespoons coriander leaves 26. Stir well & finally add ginger juliennes and coriander leaves. 27. Cover and rest for 2 mins. Transfer to a serving bowl and rest for at least 15 mins for the paneer to absorb the flavors. 28. Enjoy kadai paneer gravy with roti, butter naan , paratha or jeera rice.

title: Kadala Curry Recipe
ingredients: 1 cup kadala, 2 cups water, 1 medium onion, 1 medium tomato, 1 green chilli, 0.75 tablespoon ginger, 1 tablespoon garlic, 1 tablespoon coriander powder, 0.875 teaspoon kashmiri red chilli powder, 0.25 teaspoon turmeric, 1 tablespoon oil, 1 sprig curry leaves, 0.5 cup raw coconut, 3 cardamoms, 1 teaspoon fennel seeds, 0.25 teaspoon black pepper, 4 cloves 4 cloves, 2 inch Ceylon cinnamon piece, 0.1875 teaspoon nutmeg, 0.5 cup water, 1.5 tablespoons coconut oil, 0.5 teaspoon mustard seeds, 1.5 red chilli, 3 shallots, 1 spring curry leaves
instructions: 1. Rinse and soak kadala/chickpeas overnight. Later drain the water and rinse them well before cooking. 2. Add 1 tablespoon oil to a pressure cooker or pot. Heat it. 3. Saute onions and green chilies slightly until transparent. Add ginger garlic and saute for 1 minute. 4. Add the tomatoes, salt, turmeric, coriander powder and red chilli powder. Mix and saute for a few minutes. 5. Add soaked kadala and pour 2 cups water. Pressure cook for 10 whistles on a medium heat, until kadala is soft cooked. 6. While the kadala cooks, on a low heat, dry roast cardamoms, fennel seeds, pepper, cloves, cinnamon and nutmeg, until they begin to smell good. 7. Add the curry leaves and coconut. Increase the flame to medium and roast all of this until for 5 mins, stirring continuously. If you want you can also roast this until golden without burning. 8. Cool and grind to as smooth as possible. Pour half cup water and blend to a very smooth paste. If required you may add 1 to 2 tbsps more water. 9. When the pressure drops, check if the kadala is cooked well. They must be soft cooked. 10. Add the coconut paste. Mix well. Stir and continue to cook for 4 to 5 minutes or until the curry reaches a desired consistency. Taste test and adjust salt. 11. Pour 1 ½ tbsps oil to a small pan. When it is hot add mustard seeds and 1 broken red chilli. As soon as they splutter add curry leaves and sliced shallots. 12. Stir fry until the onions turn golden and aromatic. Pour this directly over the hot kadala curry and cover it.

title: Kadhi Pakora Recipe
ingredients: 1 cup curd, 0.25 cup besan, 3.125 cups water, 1.5 tablespoon oil, 0.25 teaspoon methi seeds, 0.5 teaspoon cumin seeds, 1 red chili, 2.5 tablespoons onions, 1.5 green chilies, 0.5 tablespoon ginger, 0.5 tablespoon garlic, 0.29166666666666663 teaspoon turmeric, 0.25 teaspoon Kashmiri red chili powder, 0.5 teaspoon coriander powder, 0.29166666666666663 teaspoon garam masala, 0.875 teaspoons salt, 1.5 tablespoons coriander leaves, 0.75 cup besan, 0.75 tablespoon corn starch, 0.75 cup onions, 0.5 teaspoon ajwain, 1.5 green chilies, 0.5 tablespoon ginger, 0.25 teaspoon salt, 0.25 teaspoon red chilli powder, 0.29166666666666663 teaspoon garam masala, 0.125 teaspoon baking soda, 2 tablespoons coriander leaves, 3.5 tablespoons water, oil for deep frying, 1.5 tablespoon ghee, 0.29166666666666663 teaspoon cumin seeds, 2 red chilies, 1 sprig curry leaves, 0.25 teaspoon Kashmiri red chili powder, 0.25 teaspoon Hing
instructions: 1. To a large mixing bowl, add gram flour and half of the yogurt. Mix well with a whisk until smooth and lump-free. Add rest of the yogurt & mix. Pour water and mix well. 2. Pour ghee/oil to a large deep pot and heat it. Add cumin, methi seeds & dried red chilli. 3. When they begin to sizzle add onions and green chilies. Saute on a medium heat until the onions turns transparent & lose the raw flavor. 4. Reduce the heat to low and add ginger garlic. Saute until aromatic for a minute. Stir in red chilli powder, coriander powder, turmeric and garam masala. 5. Mix the gram flour and yogurt mixture with a spoon and pour that into the pot. 6. Mix well and bring to a gentle boil on a low heat. As the mixture begins to heat, keep stirring so the besan does not settle at the bottom. 7. Once the mixture is hot and turns slightly thicker, increase the heat slightly and bring to a boil. 8. Regulate the heat to medium and simmer until the kadhi becomes thick and you see spiced oil floating over it. It takes about 30 mins. Keep stirring the kadhi in intervals of 5 to 8 mins throughout the process. 9. When the kadhi is done, stir in the salt and taste test. At this stage kadhi should be thick but not too thick. It becomes more thick as it cools down and also note that pakoras need some moisture to soak up. If it is too thick you may add some boiling hot water. (Check Video for consistency) 10. While the kadhi cooks, you can begin to make the pakoras. Heat oil for frying in a deep pan/pot. 11. To a mixing bowl, add onions, green chilies, salt, besan, corn starch (optional), crushed ajwain, red chili powder, garam masala, ginger, baking soda & coriander leaves. 12. Mix well to a thick pakora batter, adding water as required. Start with 3 tablespoons water and add more as required. 13. Taste test and adjust salt and spice to taste. 14. To test if the oil is hot enough, drop a small portion of pakora batter to the oil. If should sizzle and rise to the surface without turning too brown. 15. Regulate the heat to medium, Drop portions of pakora batter to the hot oil and do not disturb them for a minute or 2. 16. Later stir and fry them on a medium heat until crisp and golden. When they are done, the bubbles will reduce. At this stage, reduce the heat and fry them for a minute or 2 to make them crisp. 17. Remove the pakoras to a cooling rack or a steel colander. Meanwhile make the pakoras with the rest of the batter. 18. Optional - To keep the pakoras extra crisp for longer, after frying all the pakoras, fry them again for a minute on a medium heat. Be careful not to burn them. 19. It is advised to add the pakoras and temper the dish only before serving. Add the coriander leaves to the kadhi. 20. Add the pakoras to the hot kadhi if you want softer pakoras. If you prefer them crisp, add them while serving. 21. Pour ghee/oil in a small tadka pan. When it becomes hot, add cumin seeds and dried red chilies. When the seeds begin to sizzle, stir in the fine chopped curry leaves until crisp. 22. Turn off the heat and add red chili powder and hing. 23. Pour this over the kadhi pakora and serve with rice/chawal or with naan.

title: Kakarakaya fry
ingredients: 25 grams karela, 1.5 to 2 tablespoons Oil, 0.25 teaspoon salt, 1 tablespoons garlic, 5 grams jaggery, 3.5 tablespoons copra, 1 tbsp Tamarind, 1 sprig curry leaves, 1 Pinch hing, 0.5 teaspoon cumin, 0.125 teaspoon Turmeric, 1.25 teaspoon red chili powder, 1 tablespoon coriander powder
instructions: 1. Skip this step if using curd. Soak tamarind in ¼ cup hot water. 2. Wash bitter gourd thoroughly. Cut off both the ends. Cut to thin round slices. 3. If desired remove the seeds & discard. 4. Squeeze up the tamarind very well and make a thin liquid by adding another 2 to 4 tbsp water. 5. Make sure tamarind water is thin otherwise it may prevent the gourd from getting cooked. 6. Heat a pan with oil and add cumin. 7. When they begin to splutter, add garlic and fry for 2 mins. 8. Add the curry leaves, hing and turmeric. Saute for a minute. 9. Next add the bitter gourd and begin to stir fry for 2 to 3 minutes. 10. Add salt and filter the tamarind across the pan. Use only as needed. 11. Mix well. Cover and cook on a medium heat until the bitter gourd is cooked well. 12. Keep stirring in between to prevent burning 13. Add coriander powder, red chili powder and mix. Saute for 2 mins. 14. Add coconut and jaggery. On a low to medium heat stir fry until the jaggery completely dissolves. Takes about 3 minutes. 15. Check the taste. We prefer to make it slightly hot, sweet and tangy. You can add in more chili powder, jaggery and tamarind water at this stage. 16. Cook until all moisture dries up well. 17. Kakarakaya fry is ready to serve with rice and ghee.

title: Kalakand Recipe (Kalakand Sweet)
ingredients: 1 can tin condensed milk, 4 grams paneer, 0.5 teaspoon cardamom powder, 2 tablespoons nuts, 1 teaspoon edible rose water
instructions: 1. Skip this section if you have paneer or ricotta. Your homemade paneer/chenna has to be moist and unset with some amount of whey in it. 2. Pour full fat milk to a pot and bring it to a boil. Add 2 tablespoon lemon juice or half cup yogurt to the hot milk. 3. Stir and continue to boil till the milk begins to curdle. Turn off when you see the whole pot of milk curdled well & whey has separated completely. 4. Pour 2 cups cold water immediately. Drain this to a cheese cloth lined over a colander. Hold the cheese cloth carefully under running water and rinse it well to remove the lemon flavor. 5. Bring the edges together and squeeze off to drain all the excess whey from the cheese. Hang it to the faucet for about 10 to 20 mins. It should weigh about 350 to 400 grams. 6. Grease a small tray and line with parchment paper. Keep this aside. 7. Pour condensed milk to a heavy bottom non-stick pan. Add the paneer or ricotta cheese. Mix them well and begin to cook on a medium flame stirring continuously. 8. The mixture thins down initially and then slowly begins to thicken. Continue to stir and cook until the mixture begins to thicken. 9. Then reduce the flame to low and continue to cook stirring until it becomes thick. It took about 8 to 9 mins for me. If you are using store bought ricotta it will take longer. (Check the video or step by step photos) 10. Do not overcook. The mixture has to be moist and juicy but not dry. 11. When you see the mixture begins to leave the sides yet remains juicy (not runny) transfer the kalakand immediately to the greased tray. 12. Spread it to ½ to ¾ inch thickness. Sprinkle nuts and gently level with a spatula. 13. Refrigerate kalakand for at least 2 hours. It will set to a soft set crumbly cake. Then cut to desired sizes. I cut mine to 12 (¾ inch thickness) or to 16 (½ inch thickness) 14. Remove it from the fridge 30 to 60 mins before serving. Kalakand is best served warm or chilled but the texture won't be good if it is too cold.

title: Kashmiri Pulao Recipe
ingredients: 2.25 tablespoon ghee, 1 cup basmati rice, 1 onion, 2 pinch saffron, 3 tablespoon milk, 9 cashew nuts, 1 tablespoon raisins, 1.5 cup water for pressure cooker, 1.5 tablespoon sugar, 0.25 teaspoon salt, 0.5 teaspoon fennel seeds, 1 bay leaf, 0.5 teaspoon caraway seeds, 1 black cardamom, 1 cinnamon, 3 green cardamoms, 4 cloves 4 cloves ((laung)), 0.5 teaspoon dry ginger powder, 0.25 cup pomegranate arils, 0.5 cup apple and pineapple
instructions: 1. Rinse rice well a few times to remove the surface starch. Pour fresh water and soak for at least 30 mins. After 30 mins, drain completely. Slice an onion and squeeze them gently to separate the layers. 2. Optional : You may skip this step if you want. Heat up milk in a small bowl and add saffron. Stir and let soak. You can microwave milk for 20 to 30 second to heat up the milk or heat on stovetop. Crush fennel seeds coarsely. Set aside. 3. Heat 1 tbsp ghee in a pot or pressure cooker. Add nuts and fry till light golden. Then add raisins and fry until they become plump. Remove them to a plate for later use. 4. To the same pot, add 1 tbsp ghee and heat up. Add sliced onions (layers separated) and fry till light golden. Then turn down the heat to lowest and continue to fry until deep golden but not burnt. Set these aside as well. 5. Transfer any ghee left in the nuts and fried onions to the pot. Add all the spices and fry till they begin to smell good. Do not burn. 6. Add the drained rice. Sprinkle salt, sugar, ½ tbsp ghee and fry for about 2 to 3 minutes. 7. Pour the saffron milk and water. If you prefer to skip the milk, you may add the saffron strands directly. Stir and check salt. The water has to be slightly salty. 8. Cover the pressure cooker and cook on a medium heat until you hear 1 whistle. If cooking in pot bring the water to a rolling boil and then reduce the heat. Cover and cook until the rice is cooked. 9. When the pressure drops, open the lid and fluff up the Kashmiri pulao with a fork. 10. Transfer Kashmiri pulao to a serving bowl. 11. Garnish with fried onions, nuts, raisins, and pomegranate, apple and pineapple chunks. 12. Follow all the saute steps in saute mode. While frying the onions switch to saute low so the onions get fried evenly. Pressure cook for 5 mins and wait for natural pressure release for 5 to 6 minutes and then open the lid. 13. Fluff up the kashmiri pulao with a fork and let cool down for sometime before moving to serving dish. Later garnish with all the fried garnishes and fresh fruits.

title: Kathi Roll Recipe
ingredients: 1.25 cup whole wheat flour, 0.25 teaspoon salt, 0.3333333333333333 cup water, 1 teaspoon oil, 1 potato, 0.375 cup peas, 0.75 cup bell peppers, 1 tablespoon oil, 0.5 teaspoon cumin seeds, 0.25 teaspoon mustard seeds, 1 Pinch asafoetida, 0.625 cup onion, 0.75 teaspoon ginger garlic paste, 0.75 teaspoon coriander powder, 0.5 teaspoon red chili powder, 0.5 teaspoon garam masala, 0.125 teaspoon turmeric, 0.5 teaspoon salt, 2 tablespoon coriander leaves, 2.5 tablespoons Green chutney
instructions: 1. Steam or boil potato until just done without making mushy. 2. Add atta to a mixing bowl along with salt and oil. Pour water just as needed and make a soft dough. Knead well until it becomes soft and pliable. Cover and set aside for 20 mins. 3. Divide the dough to 4 to 5 equal parts. Lightly sprinkle the rolling area and the dough ball with flour. Using a rolling pin roll the ball evenly to a round layer. 4. Heat a pan / griddle or tawa on a medium high heat. Gently transfer the roti to the tawa. When you see tiny bubbles, flip it and press down gently with a spatula. 5. Cook on both the sides flipping until both the sides are cooked with golden to brown spots. Add ghee or oil now. 6. Spread a kitchen cloth or tissue over a plate. Transfer the roti to it. Make all the rotis and stack them in the plate. 7. Heat a pan with oil. Allow mustard and cumin to crackle. Add hing, onions and a pinch of salt. Saute until onions turn golden. 8. Fry ginger garlic paste until the raw smell goes off. Add all the veggies. Next add turmeric, chili powder, coriander powder, garam masala & the rest of the salt 9. Saute for 3 mins. Cover and cook on a low flame until the veggies are cooked to your liking. Taste test and add more salt if you want. Add the coriander leaves if using and set this aside to cool. 10. Smear chutney or sauce over the roti. If you are making these ahead to serve later, do not smear the chutney as the rolls can easily turn soggy. Instead drizzle a little over the filling and serve the chutney/ sauce at the time of serving. 11. Add a sufficient amount of cooked veggies to one side of the roti. Begin to roll it tightly and prick a sandwich pick to hold in shape. Or wrap them with a parchment paper to cover half of the kathi roll and tuck the bottom. 12. They are ready to serve with more chutney or sauce of choice.

title: Keema Naan
ingredients: 2.5 cups all-purpose flour, 0.5 teaspoon sea salt, 2 teaspoons sugar, 0.5 teaspoon instant yeast, 0.5 cup yogurt, 2 tablespoons oil, 0.3333333333333333 cup luke warm milk, 1 teaspoon kalonji onion seeds, 3 tablespoons unsalted butter, 25 grams keema, 2 tablespoons oil, 0.5 cup onions, 1 green chili, 1.5 teaspoon ginger garlic paste, 0.25 teaspoon turmeric, 0.5 teaspoon Kashmiri red chili powder, 0.5 teaspoon garam masala, 1 teaspoon coriander powder, 0.5 teaspoon cumin powder, 1 teaspoon tomato paste, 0.5 teaspoon sea salt, 1 teaspoon kasuri methi, 0.25 cup coriander leaves
instructions: 1. Stir in flour, salt, sugar and yeast in a mixing bowl or food processor or stand mixer. Pour yogurt, oil and half of the milk/water,. 2. Mix to form a soft dough, adding small quantities of the remaining milk or water only as required. Knead the dough for 8 to 9 minutes on a low speed or with your hands, until soft and pliable. 3. Oil the bowl and the dough ball with a tablespoon oil. Cover and let rise until doubled or for 2 to 3 hours in a warm place. 4. Heat oil in a pan and add onions and green chilies. Saute until light golden for 6 to 7 mins. Stir in the ginger & garlic. Saute for a minute and add the ground spices – turmeric, red chili powder, garam masala, coriander powder and cumin powder. 5. Deglaze with 2 tbsps water. Add the keema & saute for 4 to 5 mins on a medium high heat. 6. Pour ¼ cup hot water and cook covered until the meat is tender and fully cooked to your liking. (adjust hot water as needed) It is cooked when it reaches 161℉ or 72℃. (read notes) 7. If using tomato paste, dilute it with 2 tbsps water and add it along with salt and kasuri methi. Cook until all the moisture evaporates. Taste test to adjust salt and heat. 8. Turn off and cool down completely. Mix in the coriander leaves and divide to 6 portions. 9. Your dough should have doubled. Divide to 6 portions and roll to smooth balls. Keep them covered until used up. 10. Lightly grease your rolling area. Spread/roll a dough ball to a 4 to 5 inch disc/roti. 11. Place a portion of keema in the center and bring the edges together to seal them. Shape to a round ball. Stuff all the dough balls with keema and place them in a bowl, untouched to each other. 12. Preheat your oven for atleast 10 mins, on a broil mode, with top bottom heating elements ON. Place a dough ball on the rolling area and sprinkle with 2 pinches of seeds and few coriander leaves. 13. Roll it to a 6 inch keema naan. You may keep them thicker or thinner to make larger naans. 14. Place 2 to 3 naans on a tray, lined with foil. Bake in the center rack, for 5 to 6 mins or until golden and crisp. Adjust the baking time as it varies by oven and temp of your dough. 15. Remove and brush with melted butter. Serve keema naan hot or warm with mint chutney, raita and salad. Bake the rest of the naans this way adjusting the baking time.

title: Keema Recipe (Mutton Keema Curry)
ingredients: 2 tablespoons oil, 1 bay leaf, 1.5 inch cinnamon, 2.5 cloves 2 to 3 cloves ((laung)), 2.5 green cardamoms, 1 green chili, 1 cup onions, 1 tablespoon ginger garlic paste, 3 grams keema, 0.25 teaspoon turmeric, 0.875 teaspoon Kashmiri red chili powder, 1.25 teaspoon garam masala, 0.3333333333333333 teaspoon salt, 2 tablespoons mint leaves, 0.375 cup tomato puree, 0.25 cup water, 2 tablespoons coriander leaves
instructions: 1. Heat a pan with oil and add the whole spices - bay leaf, cinnamon, cloves, cardamoms. 2. When they begin to sizzle, add green chilies and onions. Saute them well on a medium flame until evenly golden. 3. Add ginger garlic paste & saute for a minute or until the raw smell goes away. 4. Add keema and break down any lumps. Saute for 3 to 4 mins on a low to medium heat. 5. Sprinkle salt & all the ground spices - turmeric, red chili powder and garam masala. Also add mint now. 6. Saute for 3 to 4 mins on a low heat. 7. Cover and cook for about 8 to 10 mins on the lowest heat. You will see some moisture being released and the keema is slightly tender. 8. Add tomato and saute until the tomato blends well with keema completely. This takes about 5 to 6 mins. The raw smell of tomatoes should go away by now. 9. Pour hot water, stir and cook covered until the keema is completely tender meaning it's cooked. 10. Taste test and add more salt and garam masala if you want. Open the lid, evaporate any excess liquid left to suit your taste. We personally love the semi-dry keema so I evaporate the excess liquid by sautéing a little longer. 11. Keep mutton keema covered until served. Garnish with coriander leaves. 12. Sprinkle some lemon juice before serving with plain rice, saffron rice, paratha and fresh cut onions, cucumbers and carrot.

title: Kerala Chicken Curry (Nadan Kozhi Curry)
ingredients: 0.5 kg chicken, 0.25 teaspoon red chili powder, 0.3333333333333333 teaspoon salt, 0.25 teaspoon turmeric, 0.5 teaspoon garam masala, 0.375 teaspoon crushed black pepper, 2 tablespoons coconut oil, 0.75 cup onions, 1 tablespoon ginger garlic paste, 0.5 cup tomatoes, 0.75 cup coconut milk, 1.5 tablespoons coriander seeds, 1 inch cinnamon piece, 4 cloves 4 cloves ((or ⅛ tsp ground cloves)), 2 pods green cardamom, 0.25 teaspoon black pepper corn, 1 teaspoon fennel seeds, 0.25 teaspoon cumin seeds, 3 red chilies, 1 tablespoon coconut oil, 1 red chili, 3.5 pearl onions, 1 sprig curry leaves
instructions: 1. Marinate chicken with garam masala, salt, turmeric, red chili powder, lemon juice and black pepper. Cover and keep aside until you prepare the rest. If you want you may use store bought garam masala and leave out making the spice blend here. Dry roast the whole spices on a low to medium heat until aromatic and crisp. Cool them and make a fine powder. Pour little water and make a paste of pouring consistency. Keep this aside. 2. Add coconut oil to a hot pan and saute sliced onions till lightly golden. Stir in the ginger and garlic. Saute for a minute until the raw smell is gone. If you want add tomatoes at this stage and cook down until they lose the raw smell. I don’t use them in this recipe. 3. Add the marinated chicken and saute for 3 to 4 minutes on a high flame. Pour the ground garam masala paste and mix well. 4. Cook covered on a medium to low flame till the chicken is tender and cooked completely. I didn’t pour water to cook the chicken, the moisture in the spice paste was good enough. But if your pan is too dry, you may add a splash of hot water. When the chicken is tender/ cooked, pour coconut milk. If you are using homemade fresh extracted, use thin coconut milk to prevent splitting. 5. Simmer uncovered for 5 minutes. You should have a thick consistency like seen in the picture below. Taste test and adjust salt and black pepper if you want. Heat a small pan with coconut oil for tempering. Add sliced onions/shallots and fry till they turn pink. Add curry leaves and broken dried red chili. When they turn crisp. Switch off the stove. 6. Pour this hot tempering over the Kerala Chicken Curry.

title: KFC fried chicken
ingredients: 5 grams chicken, 1.5 cup buttermilk, 0.25 teaspoon salt, 2 tablespoons cornstarch, 1 egg white, 1 cup bread crumbs, 2.5 cups Oil, 0.5 teaspoon Salt, 1 teaspoon red chilli powder, 0.5 teaspoon black pepper, 0.25 teaspoon allspice, 0.75 tablespoon garlic powder, 1 teaspoon onion powder, 1 teaspoon mixed herbs
instructions: 1. Choosing chicken : For starters, you can choose what kind of chicken you prefer. Be it boneless thighs, or a whole bird that is cut into pieces, you can be selective. Most people love fried drumsticks. But you can also use chicken breasts & thighs. 2. Brining : Next is the decision to brine or not to brine. The benefit of using a buttermilk brine is that it tenderizes the chicken and adds moisture while contributing a pleasing kiss of acidity. 3. While skipping this step is an option, a soak in buttermilk for at least 4 hours will take your fried chicken to new heights which is certainly worth the extra planning. 4. To make the butter milk, add ½ cup plain yogurt to a bowl and whisk it with 1 cup water. Add about ¼ teaspoon salt & give a good mix. You can also add some crushed black pepper if you like. 5. Rinse chicken and add it here. Cover the bowl with a cling wrap and refrigerate 4 hours to overnight. Overnight gives best results. I do not suggest skipping this as the chicken won’t cook through properly without brining. 6. When you are ready to make the fried chicken prepare the crumbs for coating. To a bowl add, 1 cup bread crumbs or panko crumbs. I used ¼ teaspoon allspice & ½ teaspoon red chilli powder or paprika. These are optional and you may add any other seasoning if you prefer here. 7. Break an egg to a bowl and separate the white. 8. Remove the chicken pieces one after the other from the buttermilk and add them a large mixing bowl. Or drain the buttermilk from the chicken. We don’t want the buttermilk here. 9. Pour the egg white to the chicken and 3 to 4 tablespoons buttermilk. Mix well. Chicken must be moist enough to hold the bread crumbs at the next stage. Pick up each piece of chicken & roll them in the bread crumbs. 10. Press down each piece of chicken so the bread crumbs stick to the chicken well. Set aside for at least 10 minutes. A better way is to place these on a wired rack.

title: Khara Bath Recipe (Masala Rava Bath)
ingredients: 0.75 cup rava, 2 tbsp ghee, 0.25 tsp mustard, 0.75 tsp urad dal, 11 cashews, 1 green chilli, 1 onion, 3 tbsp capsicum, 0.5 carrot, 3 tbsp green peas, 1 tomato, 2.25 cups water, 0.875 tsp MTR vangi bath powder, 0.125 tsp turmeric powder, 0.5 tsp salt, 1 tsp ginger, 2 tbsp coriander leaves, 1 tsp lemon juice
instructions: 1. Roast rava: Dry roast rava on a low to medium flame stirring often until crunchy and light. Do not brown. Keep aside to cool down. 2. Tempering: Heat 1½ tbsp ghee in a pan and add mustard seeds, dal and cashews. Let fry until golden and add the curry leaves, ginger and hing. Add onions and chilies. Saute until transparent and add all the veggies. Stir fry for 2 mins and cook covered until tender. 3. Make khara bath: Add vangi bath powder, turmeric and salt. Pour water and mix. Taste test to adjust salt. Bring the water to a rolling boil, reduce the heat to low. Pour the rava in a slow stream with your left hand while you stir the water with your other hand to incorporate it into the water 4. Quickly stir to break down any lumps formed. Cover and simmer until the water is absorbed completely. Turn off and let rest for 5 mins. Sprinkle coriander leaves and the rest of the ghee. Optionally you may also add some lemon juice and grated coconut. 5. Serve khara bath hot with coconut chutney.

title: Khichdi Recipe | Moong Dal Khichdi
ingredients: 1.5 tablespoons ghee, 0.875 teaspoon cumin seeds, 1 bay leaf, 0.875 teaspoon ginger, 0.25 cup onion, 1 green chili, 0.5 cup tomatoes, 0.25 teaspoon turmeric, 0.3333333333333333 teaspoon salt, 0.5 cup rice, 0.5 cup moong dal, 4 cups water, 1 pinch asafoetida, 0.5 tablespoon ghee, 1 medium carrot, 8 french beans, 1 potato
instructions: 1. Add rice and moong dal to a pot. Rinse them at least thrice & drain water completely. If you do not have a cooker, then soak them for 30 to 40 mins. This will help them cook faster. 2. Heat ghee in a pressure cooker or pot on a medium heat. Once the ghee is hot, add bay leaf (optional) and cumin seeds. 3. Soon the seeds begin to splutter, then add ginger & saute until fragrant, for about 30 to 60 seconds & sprinkle hing. 4. Add onions and green chilies. Saute them until the raw smell of onions goes away & they begin to turn golden. 5. Next add tomatoes, carrots, beans, salt and turmeric. Saute for 3 to 4 mins. 6. Then add drained daal and rice. Saute for 3 to 4 mins. 7. Pour 4 cups water for porridge like consistency or 3 cups for mushy consistency. 8. Stir well & taste the water. Add more salt if needed. 9. Cover the pressure cook and let it cook for 2 whistles on a medium flame for a mushy khichdi. Cook for 4 to 5 whistles on a medium flame for porridge consistency. 10. Let the pressure release on its own, then open the lid and mix well. If the consistency is too thick for your liking, pour about ½ to ¾ cup hot boiling water and simmer for 1 to 2 mins until dal khichdi begins to bubble. Taste test and adjust salt. 11. If cooking in a pot, bring water to a boil on a high flame. Then reduce the flame to medium to low and continue to cook. 12. Leave the pot uncovered or partially covered so the froth won't overflow. Keep checking and add more hot boiling water (about ½ to ¾ cups) as needed until the lentils are fully soft cooked. 13. When both are done, mash the khichdi slightly so you get a thicker consistency. Adjust salt as needed. 14. Transfer to serving plates and pour 1 tsp ghee on the hot khichdi. Serve with papad and pickle. 15. Press saute button (medium) on your Instant pot. Pour ghee to the steel insert. 16. As soon as the ghee melts, add bay leaf and cumin seeds. 17. When cumin begins to sizzle add onions & chopped chili. 18. Saute until onions turn transparent. Next add in ginger, hing, tomatoes, all veggies & turmeric. 19. Saute for 2 to 3 mins until tomatoes turn mushy. 20. Add drained rice & lentils. Saute them for another 2 to 3 mins. 21. Next add salt & pour water. Deglaze the pot by scraping the bottom with a spatula to remove bits of food stuck in there if any. Mix well & taste the water. 22. Add more salt if needed. Secure the lid & move the steam release handle to sealing. 23. Press the pressure cook or manual button (high pressure). Set the timer to 8 mins. Or you can also press porridge button & set timer to 9 mins. 24. Once it is done, the instant pot beeps, then press cancel and switch off. 25. Wait for a natural pressure release for 10 minutes & release the rest manually. 26. When the float valve drops, open the lid & stir gently. 27. If you like soupy khichdi, add some hot boiling water to bring to consistency you like. Saute for 1 minute if you prefer. 28. Top dal khichdi with little ghee & serve with pickle or papad or chutney.

title: Kofta Curry (Meatball Curry)
