ingredients: 1.5 cups onions, 0.5 cup tomato, 0.75 tablespoon ginger, 1 tablespoon garlic, 1 green chili, 2 tablespoons coriander leaves, 0.3333333333333333 cup yogurt, 0.25 cup oil, 0.25 teaspoon shahi jeera, 4 green cardamoms, 1 black cardamom, 4 cloves 4 cloves ((omit if you don't like)), 1.5 to 3 teaspoons Kashmiri red chili powder, 0.875 teaspoon garam masala, 0.875 teaspoon cumin powder, 1 tablespoon coriander powder, 0.75 teaspoon sea salt, 2.25 cups water, 15 almonds cashews, 25 grams minced ground meat, 2 tablespoons onion, 0.5 inch inch ginger, 2 garlic cloves, 1 green chili, 2 tablespoons mint leaves, 2 tablespoons coriander leaves, 2 teaspoons oil, 2 tablespoons egg, 0.25 cup bread crumbs, 0.3333333333333333 teaspoon sea salt, 1 teaspoon red chili flakes, 0.5 teaspoon garam masala, 0.5 teaspoon cumin powder
instructions: 1. Omit this step to use heavy cream or coconut cream. If you are using nuts or seeds, soak them in half cup boiling hot water. While the curry simmers, drain the water and grind with ¼ cup water (adjust as required) to a smooth thick cream. 2. Heat oil/ghee in a kadai or deep pot and add the whole spices - shahi jeera, cloves, green and black cardamoms. When they begin to sizzle, add onions and saute for 10 minutes on a medium heat. Do not burn. 3. Turn down the flame to low and continue to saute until deep golden/ brown but not burnt. It takes me another 4 minutes. 4. Pour 3 tablespoons of water and deglaze. Saute until the onions look jammy, for another 3 mins. 5. Stir in the ginger garlic and green chilies. Saute on a medium heat for another minute or 2, until aromatic. 6. Stir in all the ground spices, followed by tomato puree. Mix and cook down until thick, for about 3 to 4 mins. 7. Lower the flame and stir in a tablespoon of the cooked masala in the whisked yogurt and mix well. Pour it into the pan and cook until it blends well with the rest of the ingredients. 8. Saute this masala for another 3 mins and pour water. Bring to a rolling boil and simmer for 5 mins. 9. Pulse or mince, onion, ginger, garlic, green chili (optional), mint and coriander leaves until slightly coarse. Don’t overdo to the extent they release too much moisture. I use a food processor or a onion chopper, you may do it on a board and transfer to a bowl. 10. Mix or pulse it together with meat, ground spices, egg and salt, until well mixed and comes together. (if using ground meat (not keema/mince), use your hands to mix all the ingredients, you don't need a processor) 11. Stir in the flour/bread crumbs and oil. Mix just until combined. Do not process or overwork here. Grease your hands generously and divide to 12 equal portions. Roll to kofta/ balls. 12. Option 1 - Heat ¼ to ½ cup oil in a deep pan, until hot enough to fry. Slide the koftas and fry them for 6 to 7 mins, in 2 batches. Remove to a plate. 13. Option 2 - Preheat your air fryer for 2 mins at the highest heat. Spray the basket with oil and place the kofta in the basket. Brush them with oil. Air fry at 380 F/190 C for 10 to 12 mins, as per your preference. Shake the basket, halfway through. 14. You should have your curry cooked down for at least 5 to 7 mins before you proceed. Reduce the flame to low and add the hot koftas to the simmering curry. Mix gently, cover and cook for 25 mins on a low heat, stirring at 5 to 7 mins interval. 15. Stir in 2 to 3 tablespoons almond cream/ khus khus paste and let simmer for another 3 to 4 mins, or until thick enough. (You may use the entire amount or lesser cream as per your preference) 16. Taste test and adjust salt. For heat you may add more black pepper. Garnish with coriander leaves.

title: Kulcha Recipe
ingredients: 2.25 cups all-purpose flour, 0.5 cup yogurt, 0.25 cup warm milk, 2 tablespoons oil, 2 teaspoon sugar, 0.625 teaspoon sea salt, 0.25 teaspoon baking soda, 1 teaspoon baking powder, 1 tablespoon ghee, 2 grams potato, 2 grams paneer, 0.25 cup onion, 0.25 cup carrot, 2 green chilies, 1 teaspoon ginger, 1 teaspoon white sesame seeds, 0.5 teaspoon red chili powder, 0.5 teaspoon cumin powder, 1 teaspoon coriander powder, 0.375 teaspoon garam masala, 0.3333333333333333 teaspoon sea salt, 0.5 teaspoon amchur, 4 tablespoons coriander leaves, 2.5 tablespoons butter
instructions: 1. In a mixing bowl, stir together flour, baking powder, baking soda, sugar, salt and instant yeast if using. Pour warm milk, yogurt & oil/ melted butter. 2. Mix well to make a soft non-sticky dough, adding more water or milk if required. Knead until smooth & elastic for 6 to 7 mins. Shape to a ball. 3. Grease the bowl and dough ball with a tsp of oil. Place in the bowl. Cover and rest in a warm place for 20 mins or for 1 to 2 hours if using yeast. 4. Heat ghee in a pan. Saute green chilies and onions until golden and soft. 5. Add ginger and saute for a minute. Stir in the sesame seeds if using and toast for a minute. Stir in chili powder, cumin, coriander, garam masala, amchur and salt. 6. Add carrot and saute for 1 to 2 mins. Stir in the paneer and cook for 2 to 3 mins, until heated through. 7. Add half of the coriander leaves and potato. Mix well & taste test to adjust the seasoning. Cool down completely. 8. Punch down the dough with your hand once and divide to 6 portions. Shape and smoothen them to balls, tucking the ends in the center. Cover until used up. 9. Take a portion of the dough and spread to a 4 inch disc, with your fingers. Place 2 tablespoons of the stuffing in the center, keep pushing it down into the dough and bring the sides over the stuffing. Seal the edges well and shape to balls. 10. Grease a rolling board, place the dough and roll it to a 6 inch kulcha. Sprinkle some coriander leaves and sesame or kalonji seeds. Roll lightly. Transfer to the kitchen counter. 11. Heat a pan on a medium high heat. Transfer a kulcha to the pan and cook until you see bubbles, for 3 to 4 mins. Turn it to the other side. Cook until brown spots are visible and the edges are cooked through. 12. Optional - transfer the cooked kulcha (upper side facing down) over a wired rack, placed on the fire/ stove. Cook gently on a low heat, until you see some charred spots. 13. Smear butter all over the kulcha. Serve them hot on their own or with a curry and raita on the side. 14. Store the leftover filling in the refrigerator for 2 days or in the freezer for a month. Use it to make another batch of kulchas or fill your sandwiches & dosas.

title: Kulfi Recipe
ingredients: 1 cup milk, 2 teaspoons corn starch, 2 cups full fat milk, 0.25 cup sugar, 0.25 teaspoon cardamom powder, 1 Pinch saffron, 12.5 pistachios, 0.25 cup cream, 4 pistas
instructions: 1. Bring milk to a boil in a heavy bottom pot. Allow it to boil further for 10 mins on a medium flame. Keep stirring often to prevent the milk solids getting burnt at the bottom. 2 cups full fat milk 2. Bring milk to a boil in a heavy bottom pot. Allow it to boil further for 10 mins on a medium flame. Keep stirring often to prevent the milk solids getting burnt at the bottom. 3. Meanwhile add pistachios along with saffron to a blender jar and make a slightly coarse powder. Set this aside. 10 to 15 pistachios, 1 Pinch saffron 4. Meanwhile add pistachios along with saffron to a blender jar and make a slightly coarse powder. Set this aside. 5. After 10 minutes of boiling the milk, add sugar and continue to boil for another 10 to 12 mins. Keep stirring often to prevent the milk getting scorched at the bottom. ¼ cup organic sugar 6. After 10 minutes of boiling the milk, add sugar and continue to boil for another 10 to 12 mins. Keep stirring often to prevent the milk getting scorched at the bottom. 7. Add corn flour to the reserved milk and stir well. Make sure there are no lumps. 1 cup milk, 2 teaspoons corn starch 8. Add corn flour to the reserved milk and stir well. Make sure there are no lumps. 9. Lower the flame completely and pour this to the pot. Keep stirring and boil. 10. Add the nuts powder and cardamom powder. Mix well and make sure there are no lumps. ¼ teaspoon cardamom powder 11. Add the nuts powder and cardamom powder. Mix well and make sure there are no lumps. 12. Pour cream if using and Cook for 6 to 8 minutess more. Keep stirring. Under 8 mins it turns thick. It should be of pouring consistency. ¼ cup cream 13. Pour cream if using and Cook for 6 to 8 minutess more. Keep stirring. Under 8 mins it turns thick. It should be of pouring consistency. 14. Switch off the stove & Cool this completely. The mixture thickens further upon cooling. 15. Pour this to kulfi molds, steel cups or Popsicle molds. Cover with a foil and gently insert a ice cream stick. I use 120 ml steel cups and it fills about 3 cups. 16. Freeze overnight. Immerse these in a bowl filled with water for 1 min or rub the moulds in between your palms for 30 seconds. 17. Gently hold the stick and demould it. Sprinkle chopped pistas and serve kesar pista kulfi immediately.

title: Ladies Finger Fry (Bendakaya Fry)
ingredients: 25 grams ladies finger, 1 green chili, 1 medium onion, 1 sprig curry leaves, 2 cloves garlic, 1 tsp urad dal, 1 tsp chana dal, 0.25 tsp mustard seeds, 0.5 tsp cumin seeds, 0.375 tsp red chili powder, 0.625 tsp coriander powder, 0.25 cup coconut, 1 tbsp lemon juice, 1 pinch turmeric, 0.3333333333333333 tsp Salt, 2 tbsp Oil, 0.25 cup peanuts
instructions: 1. Rinse 250 grams ladies finger under running water. Set them aside to dry. Then wipe off with a clean cloth or absorbent papers. I usually spray some vinegar, let them sit for about 10 minutes and then rinse off well. Then dry them and wipe with a fresh kitchen towel. 2. Chop the ladies finger to thin circles. Before you chop make sure they are not wet otherwise they will become slimy while chopping and while cooking too. 3. Heat a pan with 2 tablespoons oil. Next add ¼ teaspoon mustard seeds, ½ teaspoon cumin seeds, 1 teaspoon chana dal and 1 teaspoon urad dal. Fry till the dal turns golden. Add 2 crushed garlic cloves, 1 sprig curry leaves and 1 dried broken red chili or 1 slit green chilli as well. Fry until the leaves turn crisp. 4. Add 1 medium sliced onion and fry till golden. They should not be raw anymore. While the onions fry, roast ¼ cup peanuts in a heavy bottom pan until golden, aromatic and crunchy. Cool them completely. Add them to a cloth and rub off to roughly remove the skin. Set these aside. 5. Transfer chopped ladies finger and squeeze the lemon juice. I add about a tablespoon. It helps to get rid of the slime faster. 6. On a medium heat, fry till the sticky substance goes away from the ladies finger. It takes about for about 5 to 7 minutes. 7. Next cover and cook till the ladies finger becomes tender for 2 to 3 mins. Covering helps to keep them moist without drying out. Cover only after the bhindi is fried well till it loses its stickiness. Otherwise they will become more slimy and mushy. 8. Sprinkle ⅓ to ½ teaspoon salt, ¼ to ½ teaspoon red chili powder, 1 pinch of turmeric, ½ teaspoon coriander powder or garam masala (optional) , ¼ cup fresh coconut and ¼ cup roasted peanuts. You can also crush the peanuts slightly if you like. If you want them more crunchy, add them while serving. 9. Lastly fry on medium flame till the coconut becomes aromatic takes around 3 minutes. Serve bendakaya fry with rice, phulka or roti. 10. Aloo Bhindi Recip... Bhindi Masala Rec... Bhindi Ki Sabji 11. ... Bhindi Do Pyaza Bhindi Fry Recipe... Brinjal Fry (Andh...

title: Lamb Karahi (Mutton Karahi)
ingredients: 2 tablespoons coriander seeds, 1.5 teaspoon cumin seeds, 3.5 Kashmiri red chilies, 1 teaspoon fennel seeds, 4 cloves 4 cloves, 3 inch cinnamon, 4 green cardamoms, 1 black cardamom, 0.625 teaspoon black pepper corn, 6 grams lamb goat leg shoulder, 3 tablespoons ghee, 1 black cardamom, 0.75 cup onions, 1.25 tablespoon ginger garlic paste, 4 grams tomatoes, 1 teaspoon salt, 0.25 teaspoon turmeric, 1 cups water, 0.25 cup yogurt, 0.75 tablespoon kasuri methi, 1 green chili, 1 inch ginger slice, 2 tablespoons coriander leaves
instructions: 1. On a medium heat, dry roast red chilies and coriander seeds for 2 minutes. Add cloves, black cardamoms, green cardamoms, cumin seeds, fennel seeds and cinnamon. Roast until they smell aromatic but not burnt, for 2 to 3 mins. Cool this and make a fine powder. Keep this aside. 2. On a medium flame, heat ghee/oil in a pan. Add the black cardamom and onions. Saute until transparent for 4 to 5 mins. 3. Add lamb and saute for 5 to 6 minutes, until the color of the mutton changes. Stir in ginger garlic paste and saute for 1 to 2 mins without burning. 4. Lower the heat and stir in turmeric, half of the salt and half of the prepared kadai masala. Pour hot water (start with less) and cook covered for an hour. If the pan dries out add more water and keep stirring in between. 5. After an hour of cooking, pour the pureed tomatoes. Cook covered on a medium heat until the lamb is tender and fully cooked. This step takes about 35 to 40 mins. The sauce should have reduced and turned thick. 6. Add yogurt to a small bowl & stir in 2 tbsps of the kadai gravy. Add back the tempered yogurt to the lamb karahi along with the rest of the kadai masala. 7. Mix well. If it is too thick you may add a splash of hot water. Simmer covered until you see traces of fats on top. This takes about 3 to 4 mins. 8. Taste test. Stir in more salt if needed along with crushed kasuri methi. For more heat you may add some freshly cracked black pepper or chili flakes. 9. Garnish with slit/ sliced green chilies, ginger juliennes and coriander leaves. Serve lamb karahi with tandoori roti, naan or kulcha.

title: Lamb Madras
ingredients: 1.1 lbs lamb, 1.5 tablespoon ginger garlic, 2 tablespoons coriander leaves, 1.5 tablespoon lemon juice, 1.5 teaspoon Kashmiri red chili powder, 0.5 teaspoon sea salt, 1.5 tablespoon Madras curry powder, 1 teaspoon garam masala, 3 tablespoons ghee, 1 star anise, 2 inch cinnamon piece, 1.5 cup onions, 1 cup tomato puree, 0.3333333333333333 cup carrots, 0.3333333333333333 cup red bell pepper, 0.5 cup water, 1 tablespoon tamarind paste, 0.75 teaspoon sugar, 0.5 teaspoon salt
instructions: 1. To a mixing bowl, add lamb, lemon juice, salt, ginger garlic, curry powder, chili powder, garam masala and coriander leaves. Mix well and cover. Keep aside until needed. 2. Puree tomatoes, carrots, bell peppers & half cup water or broth in a blender, to get a smooth puree. 3. Pour oil/ghee to a pot and add star anise, cinnamon and onions. Saute for 8 to 9 mins until transparent. Deglaze with 2 tablespoons water. Add the lamb and sear on a medium high heat for 6 to 7 mins. 4. Add the pureed tomatoes and cook covered on a medium heat for about 25 mins. 5. Pour half cup hot water or broth if needed (in batches) and cook covered until the lamb is tender (takes me about 1 hour 10 mins). Keep stirring every 10 to 15 mins to prevent burning. 6. Stir in tamarind and sugar. Taste test to adjust salt, curry powder & garam masala to your preference. Lamb madras should be hot, spicy and tangy. 7. Sprinkle coriander leaves and serve hot with Naan, paratha, chapati, roti, plain rice, saffron rice or turmeric rice.

title: Lamb Vindaloo Recipe (Goat Vindaloo)
ingredients: 1.25 lbs lamb, 2 tablespoons coriander seeds, 1 teaspoon cumin seeds, 1 black pepper, 6 cloves 6 cloves, 2.5 inch cinnamon piece, 0.5 teaspoon mustard seeds, 2 Kashmiri dried red chilies, 2 teaspoons Kashmiri red chilli powder, 0.25 teaspoon turmeric, 0.625 teaspoon salt, 0.25 cup palm vinegar, 1 teaspoon jaggery, 0.25 cup water, 25 grams garlic, 25 grams ginger, 4 tablespoons oil, 1 onion, 1 sprig curry leaves, 1.5 teaspoons jaggery, 1.5 to 2 cups hot water
instructions: 1. First deseed the dried red chilies and soak them in vinegar and ¼ cup hot water. If using red chilli powder in place of red chilies you may skip this soaking step. Keep this aside. 2. Meanwhile add all the spices – coriander, cumin, black pepper, cinnamon, cloves, mustard and salt to a small grinder jar. Make a fine powder. 3. Add jaggery, ginger, garlic, soaked red chilies (or chilli powder), & vinegar. Grind to a smooth paste adding 1 to 2 tablespoons more water if required. 4. Taste test this and add more salt or vinegar or red chilli powder to taste. 5. Marinate the lamb with turmeric and this vindaloo paste. Cover and rest overnight or at least for 6 hours. 6. When you are ready to cook the vindaloo, take it out of the refrigerator and keep aside. 7. Chop the onion very finely if using. 8. Heat oil in a pan or a dutch oven or a braiser. 9. Add the onions and saute them on a medium heat until golden. 10. Add the marinated lamb and begin to saute on a medium high heat for 5 minutes. You will see the vindaloo paste begins to smell good & leave the sides of the pan. At this stage cover and cook for 3 mins on a medium to medium-low heat. 11. Add the curry leaves and saute for 2 mins. Pour 1 cup boiling hot water and mix well. 12. Let lamb vindaloo cook for 1 ½ hours or until tender. Keep stirring every 15 mins. You will need to add more hot water in batches after 40 mins. 13. You know the lamb vindaoo is done when the meat is soft and fork tender. Taste test and add salt and jaggery (or brown sugar) at this stage and cook further without covering to evaporate the excess water. 14. Vindaloo is a semi-dry dish with thick sauce. Check pictures in the post to see the consistency of the sauce. 15. Lamb vindaloo tastes best the next day. Serve it over Basmati Rice, bread or with naans. If you are lucky enough to make or buy the Goan Poee bread, go for it.

title: Lassi Recipe (Sweet and Salt Lassi)
ingredients: 2 cups curd, 0.75 cup water, 3.5 tablespoon sugar, 0.375 teaspoon cardamom powder, 1 teaspoon rose water, 1 pinch saffron strands optional, 1 tablespoon nuts, 0.5 teaspoon roasted cumin powder, 0.375 teaspoon salt, 2 pinches chaat masala, 0.625 cup mint, 0.5 teaspoon cumin powder, 0.125 teaspoon chaat masala, 2 tablespoon lemon juice, 1 tablespoon sugar, 0.375 teaspoon salt, 0.25 green chili deseeded
instructions: 1. Add 2 cups chilled curd to a deep bowl or a blender. Do note that using sour curd may need addition of milk and more sugar. I do not prefer the combination of curd and milk together. So I make sure to use fresh curd. 2. Whisk it well until smooth. You should not see any grainy yogurt. I used a whisk here. You can also use a immersion blender or mixing rod. 3. Next add 3 to 4 tablespoons sugar, 1 pinch of saffron strands or 1 teaspoon rosewater. If you do not have saffron strands or rose water, simply skip both. 4. Add ¼ to ½ teaspoon cardamom powder. I removed the husks of the 10 whole green cardamoms & powdered them with little sugar in a grinder. Use only a bit for this. 5. Whisk everything well until sugar dissolves. 6. Add half to 1 cup chilled water or whey from yogurt or milk. Adjust water as required to suit your taste. If you want very thick consistency then use half cup. Start with ¾ cup water if you are using Greek yogurt. If you are not serving it right away then you can also use boiled and chilled milk in place of water. Whisk very well until frothy. Pour this to the serving glasses. Taste test and add more sugar or cardamom powder to suit your taste. Or simply blend in a blender until frothy. You can garnish with any of these – saffron, chopped nuts or cardamom powder. 7. On a low heat, dry roast jeera/ cumin seeds until they begin to smell good and aromatic. Cool them completely and powder finely in a spice jar. 8. Whisk 2 cups chilled curd in a bowl or jug. Add half teaspoon black salt or Himalayan salt and ½ to 1 teaspoon roasted cumin powder. Whisk everything well until smooth. 9. Pour half to 1 cup chilled water and whisk again until you see froth on top. Taste test and add more salt or cumin as required.

title: Lauki Halwa | Dudhi Halwa
ingredients: 3 cups bottle gourd, 5 tbsp sugar, 3 tbsp ghee, 1.5 cups milk, 0.25 tsp cardamom powder, 1 tbsp almonds, 1 tbsp pistachios
instructions: 1. Bring 1½ cups full fat milk to a boil in a heavy bottom pan or pot. 2. While the milk boils, rinse 500 grams of bottle gourd under running water. Peel the skin slightly deep and taste the bottle gourd to make sure you are not using bitter or very sour one. Then grate it either using a hand grater or a food processor. I prefer using the grater with the smallest holes. I discarded the center portion of the lauki as it was not young. After grating I had 3 cups of gratings. 3. Keep checking the milk, stir occasionally to make sure it doesn’t get scorched at the bottom. The milk must reduce to half or at least to 1 cup. 4. Heat 2½ tbsps ghee in a heavy bottom pan. 5. Add the grated lauki and saute it well in ghee. Fry well until the raw smell goes away and the moisture evaporates. 6. This may take about 7 to 10 mins. At the end of this step, there should be no excess moisture and the ghee begins to separate from the lauki. 7. Now time to check the milk. Make sure it is very hot, whisk it well with a fork or whisker. Whisking helps to break up the cream. Make sure both milk and lauki are hot at this step. 8. Pour the hot milk to the sauteed lauki. 9. Cook stirring consistently on a medium to low flame. 10. You will see the milk is absorbed fully in some time, then add 4 to 6 tablespoons sugar depending on the level of sweetness you want. 11. Lauki halwa will turn gooey as the sugar melts. Again continue to cook stirring continuously until the halwa turns thick. Reduce the flame completely. 12. Sugar, milk and ghee in the lauki halwa will begin to smell great as you cook. Add ¼ to ½ teaspoon cardamom powder. If you don’t have the powder, crush 4 green cardamom seeds in a mortar pestle and add it. 13. Pour 1 tsp ghee. If you want you can add more. 14. Cook further just for 1 to 2 mins. 15. Garnish lauki ka halwa with sliced almonds and pistas. Serve warm or chilled. It keeps good in the refrigerator for 2 days.

title: Lauki Sabzi (Dudhi Recipe)
ingredients: 1 lb lauki, 0.5 cup onion, 1 cup tomato, 0.5 inch ginger, 1 green chili, 2 tablespoons oil, 0.5 teaspoon cumin seeds, 1 sprig curry leaves, 0.625 teaspoon salt, 0.625 teaspoon Kashmiri red chili powder, 0.125 teaspoon turmeric, 0.75 teaspoon coriander powder, 0.5 teaspoon garam masala, 0.5 teaspoon fennel powder, 2 tablespoons coriander leaves, 0.5833333333333333 cup water
instructions: 1. Often lauki with darker skin (dark green skin) indicates tougher skin which requires peeling deeper. This kind may also have larger seeds, which you may need to discard while chopping. 2. Prepare the following ingredients: If you have a chopper or dicer, use that to save time. 3. 1 lb (450 to 500 grams, 3 cups) lauki (chopped to ½ to ¾ inch pieces) 4. ½ cup (1 medium) onion (fine chopped) 5. 1 cup (1 large) tomato (chopped, deseeded if preferred) 6. ½ inch (1 teaspoon) ginger or garlic (peeled and fine chopped) 7. 1 sprig curry leaves (optional, pat dry rinsed leaves) 8. If you want you may chop the lauki to larger pieces but that increases the cook time slightly. 9. Pour 2 tablespoons oil to a hot pan. Add half teaspoon cumin seeds. When they begin to sizzle, add the curry leaves (pat dry first). 10. Add the onions, ginger/garlic and green chili. 11. Saute until transparent on a medium heat. 12. Add tomatoes and ¼ teaspoon salt. Mix and cook on a medium heat until soft and mushy. 13. Stir in the following ground spices: 14. ¾ teaspoon coriander powder (optional, I don’t add) 15. Mix and saute for a minute. 16. Add the chopped lauki and ¼ teaspoon salt. 17. Stir fry for 3 to 4 minutes on a high heat. 18. Pour ½ cup water and mix well. When the water comes to a boil, reduce to medium and cover. Let cook until tender, adding more water only as required. I used ⅔ cup water in total but that change depending on your gourd. 19. When the lauki is soft and tender, taste test and add salt as required. If there is a lot of water in the pan, evaporate by cooking on a high heat for 2 to 3 mins. Add ½ teaspoon garam masala and ½ teaspoon teaspoon fennel powder (saunf powder). Turn off and keep covered for 5 mins before serving. Garnish Lauki sabzi with 1 tablespoon chopped coriander leaves. Serve hot with rice, roti or bhakri.

title: Lemon Chicken Recipe
ingredients: 3 grams chicken, 1.5 tbsp oil, 1 tsp ginger, 1.5 to 2 tbsp lemon juice, 0.25 tsp salt, 0.25 tsp lemon zest, 0.75 tsp Kashmiri red chili powder, 0.125 tsp turmeric, 0.5 tbsp oil, 1 tbsp ginger, 0.5 tbsp garlic, 1 green chili, 1 medium yello onion, 0.5 tsp sugar, 1 tsp cajun seasoning, 0.5 tbsp soya sauce, 9 mint leaves
instructions: 1. Add diced chicken, ginger paste, red chili powder, salt, turmeric, lemon juice, lemon zest and oil. Do not omit oil, it is the key to tenderize chicken along with lemon juice. I used half of a large lemon. I suggest using fresh lemon and freshly ground ginger or grated ginger. 2. Marinate the chicken and set aside for at least an hour up to overnight in the refrigerator. 3. Heat oil in a pan and saute ginger and garlic for 1 minute. 4. Add onions, slit green chili, sugar and salt. 5. Saute until the onions turn transparent. 6. Add marinated chicken along with the lemon juice. Turn down the flame to low, Stir and saute till the chicken is half cooked, for 2 mins. 7. Sprinkle garam masala or cajun spice mix or all spice. Stir Fry for another 2 to 3 mins. 8. Pour soya sauce and cook on a slightly high heat till the chicken is cooked through for another 1 to 2 mins. Evaporate the soya completely. Taste test and adjust salt, sauce or spices to your taste. 9. Add finely chopped mint leaves or cilantro/coriander leaves. 10. Stir through and saute until the herbs wilt, for another 1 minute. Garnish lemon chicken with mint or cilantro/coriander leaves. Serve with onion wedges.

title: Lemon Pepper Fish Recipe
ingredients: 1 lb fish, 1 teaspoon black pepper, 0.5 teaspoon fennel seeds, 2 sprigs curry leaves, 1 teaspoon ginger garlic paste, 1.5 green chilies, 2 tablespoon oil, 0.625 teaspoon garam masala, 1.5 to 2 tablespoon lemon juice, 0.25 teaspoon turmeric, 0.5 teaspoon red chilli powder, 0.5 teaspoon salt
instructions: 1. Add the fish pieces to a mixing bowl. Pour lemon juice all over and sprinkle salt, turmeric and red chilli powder. Set this aside for minimum 15 mins. 2. Powder together fennel seeds and pepper corn in a small grinder. 3. Heat a wide pan with ghee or oil. 4. Once it becomes hot, reduce the heat and add ginger garlic paste, green chilies. Fry until it turns aromatic, for about 60 seconds. Do not burn this. 5. Spread the oil well in the pan. Place the fish pieces. Fry them for 2 to 3 mins. 6. Turn them to the other side and fry again for another 2 to 3 mins. 7. Sprinkle half of the pepper and saunf powder evenly. Add curry leaves. 8. Turn them and sprinkle the rest of the powder. Fry until the fish is cooked through. To test, prick it with a fork at the thickest part, it should flake easily. The internal temperature should reach 145F or 65C. 9. Taste test and add more salt if required. If using garam masala powder you can sprinkle now. If you want more lemony flavor, add more lemon juice while serving. 10. Lemon pepper fish is ready to serve with roasted veggies or rice, rasam or as appetizer with soup.

title: Lemon Rasam (Nimmakaya Pappu Charu)
ingredients: 0.5 cup toor dal, 1.5 cups water, 3.5 cups water, 1 tomato, 1 inch ginger, 1 green chilli, 1.25 teaspoon salt, 1 pinch turmeric, 2.5 teaspoons rasam powder, 1 handful coriander leaves, 1 tablespoon ghee, 1 sprig curry leaves, 0.5 teaspoon mustard seeds, 0.5 teaspoon cumin seeds, 1 pinch methi seeds, 1 red chilli, 1 pinch hing, 1 lemon
instructions: 1. Pressure cooker: Add dal to a bowl and rinse it well at least thrice. Drain the water and pour 1½ cups water. Place it in the cooker and pressure cook for 3 to 4 whistles on a medium flame. When the pressure releases remove the dal and mash it well to smooth. 2. Pot: To cook in a sauce pot without a pressure cooker, simply cook on a medium heat until tender and soft, skim off any foam that comes on the surface. You may require more water, if so pour more boiling water and cook. 3. To cook in Instant Pot, place the bowl in the IP, over a steam rack and pressure cook for 10 mins. Let the pressure drop naturally. 4. Dal must be soft cooked and mushy. Mash it well with the back of a ladle to smooth texture. 5. While the dal cooks, pour 4 cups water to a pot. Add tomatoes, turmeric, rasam powder, green chilli (optional) and crushed ginger. Cook until the tomatoes are soft cooked and mushy. 6. Add the mashed dal and salt. Bring it to a boil & add chopped coriander leaves. 7. Heat a small tadka pan and add oil or ghee. When it is hot, add mustard seeds, cumin seeds, methi seeds and dried red chilies. 8. When the seeds begin to crackle, add curry leaves and hing. Pour it to the lemon rasam. Simmer the rasam for 2 mins on a very low flame. 9. Allow the lemon rasam to cool down a bit then squeeze off lemon as needed. Adjust salt as well. 10. Serve lemon rasam with soft cooked rice or enjoy as a soup.

title: Lemon Rice Recipe (Chitranna)
ingredients: 1.5 cups raw rice - uncooked, 2 tablespoon oil, 0.25 cup peanuts, 0.75 teaspoon mustard seeds, 1.5 teaspoon chana dal, 1 teaspoon urad dal, 2 green chilies, 0.5 tablespoon ginger, 1.5 sprigs curry leaves, 0.5 teaspoon turmeric powder, 0.5 teaspoon salt, 1 pinch hing, 2.5 tablespoons lemon juice
instructions: 1. Omit this if you have precooked rice - Add raw rice to a strainer and rinse under running water. Transfer to a pot and pour water (read notes). Bring to a rolling boil and reduce the heat to medium. Cook covered until the rice is cooked through. (takes me 15 mins) 2. Cool the rice completely & fluff with a fork. 3. Pour ½ tablespoon oil to a pan and add the nuts. Fry them on a medium heat, until golden and aromatic. Remove to a plate and reserve aside to garnish. wipe down with a kitchen paper. 4. Pour the remaining oil and add mustard, urad dal & chana dal. Fry on a medium flame until the dal turns golden & aromatic. (for 2 to 3 mins) 5. Add ginger, green chilis, red chilies(optional) and curry leaves. Fry them until the leaves turn crisp for about 30 seconds. 6. Stir in hing, turmeric and salt. Immediately pour 3 tbsps water & mix. Cook until the water evaporates and the lentils soften. (If you want the dal very soft, add 3 tbsps more and cook down). Turn off the stove. 7. Add the cooled rice and squeeze the lemon juice. Give a good mix. 8. Taste test to adjust salt and lemon. Garnish with roasted nuts. Serve lemon rice with plain yogurt, papad or pickle. 9. Add 1½ cups basmati rice to a strainer & rinse under running water. Press the saute button and pour 2 tablespoons oil to the steel insert. Add ¼ cup peanuts or cashews and fry until golden. Remove to a plate. 10. Add 1½ tsp chana dal, 1 tsp urad dal, ¾ tsp mustard seeds, 1 to 2 slit green chilies & 1 sprig curry leaves. Fry until lightly golden. Stir in ½ tbsp ginger, pinch of hing and ½ tsp turmeric. 11. Pour 1¾ cups water and add ½ tsp salt. Deglaze the pot and add rice. Mix well. 12. Secure the lid & position the steam release vent to sealing position. Press the PRESSURE COOK button and set the timer to 5 mins. 13. When the IP finishes (it beeps), wait for 5 mins and release the pressure manually with a long spoon (move the steam release handle from sealing to venting) 14. Remove the steel insert to the counter and let cool down for 2 to 3 mins. Taste test and sprinkle more salt if required. Pour the lemon juice all over and mix gently. 15. Garnish lemon rice with roasted nuts and serve with yogurt.

title: Maladu recipe | Fried gram or roasted gram ladoo recipe
ingredients: 1 cup roasted bengal gram, 0.5 cup sugar, 0.25 cup ghee, 3 green cardamoms, 7 cashews
instructions: 1. Add sugar and cardamoms to a blender jar. It is good to use seeds than the entire cardamom pods. 2. Make a fine powder. 3. Transfer this to a bowl. If you are using a bigger jar, you can just keep it in the jar. 4. Add fried gram. 5. Make a fine powder. You may need to scrape the sides and blend again to make very fine powder. Make sure sugar and fried gram turn to a very fine powder. 6. Set this aside together with the sugar. 7. Heat 1/4 cup ghee and fry cashews lightly if using. The ghee turns very hot. 8. Add the flour and powdered sugar. Off the stove. 9. Begin to mix well. The mixture turn really hot and begins to emit a nice aroma. At this stage the flour must get coated with the ghee well. If you see dry powder, just go on mixing. Sugar begins to melt and helps to bind the flour well. If you see any dry flour, then immediately add 1 tbsp more ghee and mix. Then add another tbsp if needed. I used 2 tbsp extra ghee apart from 1/4 cup. 10. Allow the mixture to cool down a bit. When it is still warm take small portions of this and make balls. You don’t need to grease your palms. Do not allow to cool down completely.

title: Malai Chicken Recipe (Creamy White Chicken Curry)
ingredients: 0.5 kg chicken, 1.5 teaspoon ginger garlic paste, 0.5 teaspoon salt, 3 tablespoons Greek yogurt, 0.75 teaspoon garam masala, 1 teaspoon coriander powder, 0.5 teaspoon black pepper, 0.5 teaspoon kasuri methi, 1 tablespoon oil, 2.5 green cardamoms, 1.5 cups onions, 0.75 inch ginger, 2 garlic, 15 cashew nuts, 0.5 cup full fat milk, 2.25 tablespoon ghee, 1.5 green chili, 1 black cardamom, 2 inch cinnamon, 2 cloves 2 cloves ((optional)), 2 strands mace, 1.5 to 2 cups water, 0.625 teaspoon salt, 0.75 teaspoon Garam Masala, 0.5 teaspoon kasuri methi, 0.1875 teaspoon nutmeg, 0.5 teaspoon black pepper, 0.25 cup heavy cream
instructions: 1. Soak cashews in half cup hot water until needed later. 2. To a mixing bowl add Greek yogurt (hung curd), salt, black pepper, ginger garlic paste, kasuri methi, coriander powder and garam masala. 3. Mix well and taste test to adjust salt and spice. Add the chicken and marinate. Cover and set aside at least for 30 minutes to overnight in the refrigerator. 4. Heat oil in a pan. Add cardamoms, sliced onions, ginger and garlic. Saute until transparent for about 4 to 5 mins. Do not brown them. 5. Cool down and add them to a grinder jar along with cashews and milk/cream (added in 2 batches if needed). Grind to a smooth puree. 6. Pour half of the ghee to a pan & heat it on a low flame. Add all the optional ingredients – chopped green chili, cloves, black cardamom, cinnamon and mace. Be careful, the spices can splutter. 7. Pour the blended mixture along with water. Bring it to a gentle boil on a medium heat. Cook covered until thick & you see specks of oil/fats on top. This takes about 12 to 13 minutes. Keep stirring every 5 mins. 8. While the gravy simmers, heat a pan with the rest of the ghee and place the chicken pieces. Fry them until almost cooked, for 3 mins on each side. 9. It’s okay if the chicken is undercooked, if you want you may brown the chicken. 10. When the sauce is thick, stir in the garam masala, nutmeg, salt, kasuri methi, black pepper and the pan-fried chicken. 11. Cover and cook for 2 to 3 mins. Taste test and adjust salt and more black pepper or garam masala. If you want you may add heavy cream. 12. Garnish with black pepper. Serve Malai chicken with Tandoori roti, Naan, Yellow rice or Saffron rice. If you want you may squeeze some lemon juice before serving. 13. Heat 1 tbsp oil in a pan. Add all the whole spices and let them sizzle for 30 seconds. Be cautious as they may splutter. 14. Add ginger garlic paste and sauté until a nice aroma comes out. Do not brown and burn this. 15. Add chicken and fry on medium heat till it turns white or pale in color. This takes around 3 to 4 mins. Transfer this to a plate. 16. Pour 1 teaspoon oil to the same pan, add pureed tomatoes and sprinkle salt. Cook this until all of the moisture evaporates and turns thick like a sauce. This step takes around 4 to 5 mins. 17. Turn off the stove and transfer the chicken to the tomato mixture. Cool all of these completely. Cooling them is very important. 18. Once cool, combine the chicken, tomato mixture, yogurt, fried onions, mint, coriander leaves, turmeric, green chili, red chili powder, salt & half of the chicken masala powder. 19. Marinate the chicken, set aside for at least 1 hour. For best flavor, taste & tender meat marinate overnight in the fridge. 20. Heat 1 tablespoon butter or oil in a pan. Then add marinated chicken. 21. Turn down the flame to lowest, Cover and cook till the chicken turns tender. Stir occasionally to prevent burning. 22. I do not add any water as the chicken releases some juices. However this depends on the kind of meat. 23. Once the chicken is tender, add the rest of the chicken masala powder & kasuri methi. Cover and cook for 2 to 3 mins. If the curry looks runny, evaporate the excess moisture by cooking further (uncovered) on a higher heat. At the end of this step the curry must be quite thick since it will thin down after adding the cream. 24. Add the cream and mix well. Taste test and add more salt or cream to taste. Garnish with more cream and coriander leaves. 25. Serve malai chicken hot with roti, naan, chapati or basmati rice.

title: Malai Paneer (White Curry)
ingredients: 25 grams paneer, 1 teaspoon ginger garlic paste, 0.29166666666666663 teaspoon sea salt, 0.25 cup Greek yogurt, 0.625 teaspoon garam masala, 0.5 teaspoon black pepper, 0.5 teaspoon kasuri methi, 1 tablespoon oil, 2.5 green cardamoms, 2 cups yellow onions, 0.75 inch ginger, 3.5 garlic, 0.25 cup cashews, 0.5 cup cold water, 1 black cardamom, 2 inch cinnamon, 2 cloves 2 cloves ((optional)), 2 strands mace, 1.5 to 2 tablespoon ghee, 1.5 green chili, 1.5 cups water, 0.625 teaspoon sea salt, 0.875 teaspoon Garam Masala, 0.5 teaspoon kasuri methi, 0.1875 teaspoon nutmeg, 0.5 teaspoon black pepper, 0.25 cup heavy cream
instructions: 1. Soak cashews in ¼ cup hot water and keep aside until needed. 2. In a bowl, mix together Greek yogurt, salt, black pepper, ginger garlic paste, kasuri methi and garam masala, Coat the paneer well with the marinade and rest aside. (or up to 24 hours in the refrigerator) 3. Heat oil in a pan and add the green cardamoms, sliced onions, ginger and garlic. Sprinkle salt and saute for about 7 to 8 mins. Pour ½ cup water and cook covered for 5 to 6 mins. 4. Cool down and add to a blender. Also add the soaked cashews and cold water or milk. Blend to a super smooth puree. (If your blender can't make a fine puree, strain it.) 5. On a low flame, heat half of the ghee in the same pan. Add black cardamom, cinnamon, cloves, mace and green chili, wait until they begin to sizzle. 6. Transfer the onion cashew puree along with water. Bring it to a gentle boil on a medium heat. Cover partially and cook on a low heat, for 12 to 13 minutes, until the raw flavor of onions is completely gone. (Keep stirring every 5 mins.) 7. Meanwhile, heat a non-stick pan with rest of the ghee. Place the paneer pieces and fry them until golden. Turn them to the other sides halfway. Fry until most of the yogurt is cooked through and paneer is golden. (takes about 5 to 6 mins) 8. When the sauce is medium thick, stir in the garam masala, nutmeg, salt, kasuri methi and black pepper. Cook for 2 to 3 mins, taste test to adjust salt, garam masala and pepper. Turn off the heat. 9. Mix in the heavy cream and add the fried paneer. (If you use milk to blend, start with only 2 tbsps and adjust) 10. Garnish with black pepper if you want. Serve Malai paneer with Tandoori roti, Naan, Yellow rice or Saffron rice. If you want you may squeeze some lemon juice before serving.

title: Malpua Recipe (Crisp & Fluffy)
ingredients: 1.25 cup all-purpose flour, 0.5 teaspoon elaichi, 1 teaspoon saunf, 1.5 cup milk, 3.5 tablespoons Ghee, 3 tablespoons milk powder, 0.5 cup sugar, 0.25 cup water, 2 teaspoon lemon juice
instructions: 1. Pour milk to a mixing bowl. If using mawa or milk powder, add it and mix well. 2. Add flour, fennel seeds and elaichi powder. 3. Make a batter mixing everything well. 4. Use more milk as needed to make a batter that is not too thick or too runny. 5. But should be of flowing consistency. By adjusting the consistency of batter, you can make thick or thin malpuas as per your liking. 6. If the batter is too thin, the batter spreads a lot and makes thin ones. Very thick batter makes thick & dense malpuas. 7. If you prefer fluffy malpuas, beat the batter for a few minutes with a fork or whisk. 8. Keep the batter aside until the sugar syrup is ready. 9. If you have time, you can set this aside for 4 hours. 10. Add sugar and water to a pot. 11. Dissolve the sugar on a medium flame. 12. Boil the sugar syrup until it turns sticky or reaches a 1 string consistency. 13. To prevent crystallization in sugar syrup: Add 2 tsps lemon juice to the syrup or In another large pot, bring 3 cups of water to a boil. Turn off the stove. Place the sugar syrup pot in the hot water. 14. Heat ghee for frying malpuas. 15. When the ghee is hot, pour 2 to 3 tbsps of batter. 16. The batter spreads depending on the consistency. 17. You can add more milk at this stage to thin down the batter to make thin malpuas. 18. Allow them to fry until golden. Flip and fry on the other side as well. 19. Remove them from the pan and add directly to the syrup. 20. Give a quick stir to the sugar syrup before adding the malpuas. 21. If the sugar crystallizes then add 2 tbsp water and heat it. 22. You can allow them to soak in the syrup for 10 mins if you have made thick malpuas or remove to a plate just after dipping them. 23. Garnish malpua with chopped pistas. 24. You can also top these with rabdi and then garnish with pistas.

title: Manchow Soup Recipe (Chinese Soup)
ingredients: 1.25 tablespoons oil, 0.75 tablespoon ginger, 0.75 tablespoon garlic, 1 green chilli, 0.25 cup onions, 2.5 stalks spring onions, 0.3333333333333333 cup carrot, 0.875 cup cabbage, 2.5 tablespoons French beans, 2.5 tablespoons bell peppers, 3.5 mushrooms, 1.5 tablespoons coriander stems, 2 tablespoons coriander leaves, 0.625 teaspoon salt, 3 tablespoons soya sauce, 0.5 teaspoon pepper, 1.5 tablespoon cornstarch, 2 teaspoons rice vinegar, 15 grams noodles, 1 cup oil, 2.25 tablespoons corn starch
instructions: 1. Pour oil to a hot pan. When the oil turns hot, regulate the flame to high and add fine chopped garlic, ginger, chilli and coriander stems. 2. Saute for 30 seconds and add spring onion whites and onions. Fry until onions turn golden. 3. Add shredded cabbage, chopped beans, bell peppers, mushrooms and carrots. Fry stirring constantly for 2 minutes until you smell the veggies aromatic. 4. Pour 4 cups of water or broth and bring it to a rolling boil. 5. Meanwhile stir together 1½ tablespoon cornstarch with 3 to 4 tbsps of water to make a smooth slurry. Reduce the heat slightly and keep stirring with a ladle and slowly incorporate the corn slurry into the boiling manchow soup. 6. Cook until the soup turns slightly thick. Add coriander leaves and spring onions. 7. Pour soya sauce and add ½ teaspoon salt and crushed pepper. Stir and taste test. Adjust the quantity of soya sauce, salt and pepper to your taste. 8. Turn off when the manchow soup thickens, do not overcook we want the veggies crunchy. Keep this covered until served. Stir in the vinegar when you are ready to serve. 9. Boil the noodles following the instructions on your pack of noodles. Make sure you cook them al dente and not mushy. 10. Once cooked, drain to a colander and rinse them under running water to get rid of the starch. 11. You can also skip all of the following steps and serve the soup with boiled noodles. To fry them proceed further. Shake off the colander to remove all the excess water and spread the noodles on a tray. Let them air dry for 30 minutes until completely dry. 12. Sprinkle 2 to 2½ tablespoons cornstarch all over the noodles and mix gently to coat the noodles with the flour. 13. Heat 1 cup oil in a frying pan until hot enough. When the oil is hot, drop some noodles to to the oil and fry until they turn crisp on the base. 14. Turn them to the other side and fry again until crisp and light golden. Remove them from oil when they are crisp. Continue to fry all of the noodles in 4 to 5 batches ensuring the oil is hot enough before every batch. 15. Ladle the soup to serving bowls. Top with crispy noodles and green onions. If you prefer you may add some chilli vinegar or any hot sauce.

title: Mangalore Bajji Recipe (Goli Baje)
ingredients: 0.75 cup all-purpose flour, 1 tablespoon rice flour, 6 tablespoons curd, 0.25 teaspoon salt, 0.5 teaspoon ginger, 1.5 green chilies, 2 tablespoons coriander leaves chopped finely, 1 sprig curry leaves, 2 tablespoons coconut, 0.25 teaspoon soda, oil, 3.5 tablespoons water
instructions: 1. Mix together flour, rice flour, soda & salt in a mixing bowl. Add ginger, chilies, curry leaves, coconut, coriander leaves, curd and mix well. Pour water in small quantities only as needed and make a thick batter. 2. It should be thick, sticky & of dropping consistency. Cover and rest for at least 2 hours. 3. Beat the batter very well with your hand in a circular motion for 30 seconds to aerate it. Do not over beat. 4. It should be of dropping consistency and not pouring or flowing consistency. If it is not, then add some flour and mix. I did not add any. 5. On a medium flame, heat oil in a pot or kadai until hot. Test by dropping a small portion of batter to the oil. It should sizzle and rise to the surface without turning brown. If it sinks, it means the oil is not hot enough. 6. Take small portions of batter in your hand, shape them with your fingers and drop to the oil. They will double or triple in size so do not make them too big. 7. You can add as many as possible without overcrowding. Do not touch them for a minute or 2. Later stir them with a spoon/spider and fry on a medium heat until golden and crisp. 8. Remove them to a cooling rack or a steel colander. Repeat and fry in batches till you finish the batter. 9. Serve goli baje hot with coconut chutney or any other chutney.

title: Mango Pickle Recipe
ingredients: 1.25 cup mango pieces, 1 tablespoon mustard seeds, 0.5 teaspoon methi seeds, 3 tablespoon red chili powder, 1.5 teaspoon salt, 4 garlic cloves, 3 tablespoon oil
instructions: 1. Choose raw, fresh and unripe green mango that is firm and has a clear skin. Wash and dry mango thoroughly. Wipe clean with a cloth. Do this the previous night. 2. If possible sun dry the jar and other utensils used for making pickle, Make sure all the bowls, spoons, chopping board and knife are dry. These precautions will increase the shelf life of the mango pickle. 3. Dry roast methi seeds on a low flame until deep golden and aromatic. Transfer to a plate and cool them completely. 4. To the same pan, pour oil and heat it up until slightly warm. Set aside to cool completely. You can skip warming the oil if you intend to finish the mango pickle within 10 to 12 days. Skip heating if using cold pressed oil. 5. Blend methi seeds along with mustard seeds to a fine powder. Crush garlic. 6. Chop mangoes to slightly large cubes of 1 inch, using a large knife. 7. Optional for shelf-life: Sun dry each separately - spice powders, salt, garlic and chopped mangoes. Alternately you may heat them in oven at 60 to 70 C (140 to 160F) for 1 hour (avoid keeping salt in oven) 8. Add them to a dry bowl, along with red chili powder, mustard powder, salt and lightly crushed garlic. 9. Pour the oil and mix well. The pickle will be dry at this stage. Cover and set aside for 24 to 28 hours in a dry place. 10. After 24 to 28 hours, mango pieces will release juices and the consistency of the pickle will change. Check the step-by-step photos above. Stir mango pickle well and taste test it. If needed add more salt. 11. Store this mango pickle in a clean dry glass jar. Store it in the refrigerator after 24 hours. If storing at room temperature avoid storing in the kitchen or places with high moisture. 12. It will be ready to serve after 3 days but it tastes best as it ages. 13. Use dry clean spoons every time you use. After every use, wipe off the sides of the jar with a clean tissue or dry cloth to avoid mold.

title: Masala Oats Recipe
ingredients: 1 cup rolled oats, 3.5 cups water, 0.25 cup onions, 0.5 cup tomatoes, 0.5 cup carrots, 0.25 cup green beans, 0.25 cup green peas, 0.25 cup sweet corn, 0.75 teaspoon ginger, 0.75 teaspoon garlic, 1 green chili, 1.5 tablespoons ghee, 0.625 teaspoon cumin seeds, 0.125 teaspoon turmeric, 0.625 teaspoon Kashmiri red chili powder, 0.625 teaspoon garam masala, 1 teaspoon salt, 1 medium lemon, 2 tablespoons coriander leaves
instructions: 1. Prepare the following vegetables 2. ¼ cup onions chopped (optional) 3. ½ cup tomatoes (deseeded & chopped or pureed) 4. 1 green chili (slit or chopped, Optional, leave out for kids) 5. ½ cup carrots (peeled and fine chopped) 6. ¼ cup green beans or French beans (fine chopped) 7. ¾ teaspoon ginger (peeled, crushed or chopped) 8. ¾ teaspoon garlic (peeled, crushed or chopped) 9. Pour 1 to 2 tablespoons ghee or oil to a pot & heat on a medium flame. When the oil becomes hot, add ½ to ¾ teaspoon cumin seeds. Soon the seeds will begin to sizzle, add the onions and green chili. Stir fry until lightly golden. 10. Add the ginger garlic and saute for a minute. 11. Stir in the tomatoes and saute until they turn soft. You can also cover and cook at this stage. I feel using pureed tomatoes cook faster so I often do that. 12. Stir in the following ground spices 13. ½ to ¾ teaspoon Kashmiri red chili powder (use ¼ for low heat) 14. Add the veggies and stir fry for 2 mins. Pour 3½ cups water and bring to a rolling boil. Reduce the heat to medium and cook until the veggies are slightly tender, for 3 mins. If using instant oats use 2½ cups. 15. Add the 1 rolled oats. 16. Stir and leave the spoon in the pot to prevent overflowing. Let cook on a medium heat until thick. Stir occasionally. 17. Stir in the salt. Taste test and add more garam masala if you want. For more heat you can also add crushed black pepper. If it is too thick for your liking you may add little more boiling hot water and simmer for a minute or 2. 18. Oats Recipes f... Savory Steel Cut ... Oats Upma Recipe Oats Khichdi Reci... How to Cook Oatme...

title: Masala Pasta Recipe (Indian Style Pasta)
ingredients: 1.5 cups Penne Pasta, 2.25 tablespoon olive oil, 0.5 teaspoon cumin seeds, 2.5 cloves garlic, 0.5 inch ginger, 0.625 teaspoon salt, 0.5 teaspoon Kashmiri red chili powder, 0.125 teaspoon turmeric, 0.5 teaspoon garam masala, 1 tablespoon kasuri methi, 1.25 tablespoon tomato ketchup, 2.5 tablespoon cream, 1.5 tablespoons coriander leaves, 0.5 cup onions, 1.25 cup tomatoes do not use sour tomatoes, 0.5 cup carrots, 0.25 cup green peas, 0.25 cup capsicum, 3 tablespoons sweet corn kernels
instructions: 1. Fine chop 1 medium onion (½ cup), 2 medium tomatoes (1¼ cup deseeded & chopped or 1 cup pureed). I use 1 cup pureed tomatoes. Grate or mince 2 to 3 garlic cloves & ½ inch ginger to make 1½ to 2 teaspoons ginger garlic. 2. Peel & chop 1 medium carrot (½ cup) & ¼ cup bell peppers. I also use ¼ cup green peas & 2 tablespoons tender/young sweet corn. If you don’t prefer veggies in your masala pasta you may simply skip all of these. 3. You will also need 4. I also use an additional ½ teaspoon roasted cumin powder & fennel powder. If you want you may use, they are optional. 1 to 1½ tablespoons tomato ketchup or 2 tablespoons passata or 1 tsp tomato paste (optional). This adds umami flavor to your masala pasta. 5. In a large pot, bring 6 cups of water to a boil. 6. When the water begins to boil rapidly, add half teaspoon salt and pasta. 7. Cook till al dente on a medium to high flame. 8. While the pasta cooks, rinse and chop the veggies. 9. Drain off the pasta to a colander when done and rinse in cold water. 10. Set aside. Reserve 3 to 4 tablespoons of pasta cooked water. 11. Heat 1 tablespoon oil in a pan. When the oil turns hot, add cumin. 12. When they begin to splutter, then add finely chopped garlic. 13. Fry till it begins to smell good for a minute or two. 14. Add onions and fry until they turn pink. 15. Then add chopped veggies and fry for about 2 minutes. 16. Next mash the tomatoes well and add it to the pan along with salt. 17. Cook until the tomatoes turn soft and blend well with veggies. The raw smell of tomatoes should disappear. 18. Add kasuri methi, red chili powder and masala powder. 19. Stir well and fry for a minute. Switch off the stove. If using cream add now and stir. 20. Add cooked pasta and 1 tablespoon oil. Toss well. Adjust salt as needed. 21. Add coriander leaves. If the pasta looks dry, then pour 1 to 2 tablespoons pasta cooked water. 22. Serve masala pasta hot.

title: Masala Vada | Chana Dal Vada
ingredients: 1 cup chana dal, 0.625 teaspoon salt, 1 inch cinnamon, 1.5 red chili, 0.75 teaspoon cumin seeds, 0.75 teaspoon saunf, 1 onion, handful pudina, 1 sprig curry leaves, 1.25 teaspoon ginger garlic paste, 1.5 green chilies, oil
instructions: 1. Add chana dal to a bowl and wash it with lot of water a few times. 2. Soak it in good amount of water for 2 hours. Drain completely. Chana dal must be fully tender and soaked without any hard center. 3. Add cinnamon, red chili, jeera and saunf to a blender jar. Make a coarse powder. Keep this aside with 2 tbsp soaked chana dal. 4. Next add the drained chana dal & salt to the same blender jar. 5. Pulse it a few times until it turns to a coarse mixture. The dough must have some uncrushed chana dal. Transfer to a bowl. 6. Next add finely chopped mint, curry leaves, onion, masala powder we just made above. 7. Also add green chilies and ginger garlic paste. Mix everything well. Taste and add more salt if needed. 8. Take a handful of the dough & check if it binds well. To check make a ball & then flatten it to a masala vada. The mixture must have a good binding to shape them to vadas. 9. Remove this to your fingers & check if the masala vada is binding well. If the mixture doesn't bind well, then take a handful of this mixture and blend to a smooth paste. 10. Mix that with the rest of the dough. 11. Heat oil in a deep pan on a medium flame. 12. While the oil heats, divide the dough to 10 parts. 13. Make small balls and flatten them on your palm. You can also make these on a greased plastic sheet or dry cloth. 14. Check if the oil is hot enough by gently dropping a small portion of the dough to the oil. The dough must rise without browning quickly. This is the right temperature. 15. Slide masala vada one by one gently to the hot oil. You can add about 3 to 4 vadas in each batch. 16. Do not disturb them immediately otherwise they will break. Wait until they firm up for 2 to 3 mins before turning them over to fry the other side. 17. On a medium flame deep fry the masala vadas until golden & crisp on both the sides. 18. Transfer the masala vadai to a kitchen tissue. Fry the rest of them in batches following the same procedure. 19. Serve masala vada hot with coconut chutney or ketchup. 20. If your air fryer needs preheating, then preheat it for 15 mins at 180 C or 350 F. Place the masala vadas on the rack. I don't brush mine with oil or ghee in the beginning. You may do if it is okay with your air fryer. 21. Air fry for 10 mins at 180 C or 350 F. Turn them to the other side and air fry for another 6 to 7 mins. Please adjust the time as needed depending on your air fryer. They will turn hard if you over fry them. Once done brush the air fried masala vadas lightly with oil or ghee.

title: Matar Mushroom | Mutter Mushroom
ingredients: 1.5 tablespoon oil, 0.5 teaspoon cumin seeds, 0.875 cup onions, 1 green chili, 1 teaspoon ginger garlic paste, 0.75 cup tomatoes, 0.25 tsp salt, 0.625 cup green peas, 2 grams mushrooms, 0.875 teaspoon garam masala, 0.125 teaspoon turmeric, 0.875 teaspoon coriander powder, 0.625 teaspoon red chili powder, 2 tablespoons coriander leaves
instructions: 1. Wash and chop onions, green chilies and set aside. Deseed and chop tomatoes or puree them. 2. Rinse the mushrooms and set aside. Slice them when you are just about to add them to the pan. 3. Heat oil in a pan. Add cumin and allow to splutter. 4. Fry onions and chili until golden. 5. Add ginger garlic paste and saute until the raw flavors go away. 6. Next put in the tomatoes with salt and fry until mushy. 7. Add garam masala, coriander powder, turmeric and chili powder. Saute until aromatic and thick. (To make a gravy dish with more sauce, read my notes below from the recipe notes) 8. Then add peas and mushrooms. Fry for 4 to 5 mins on a medium heat. 9. Cover and cook on a low flame until the mushrooms are tender but not mushy and soggy. Keep stirring in between. 10. As the mushrooms cook, they release moisture. When the mushrooms are tender, open the lid and mix well. Saute on a slightly high heat for a minute. 11. Taste and add more salt if needed. If you prefer you may add crushed kasuri methi (optional). 12. Garnish with coriander leaves. Serve matar mushroom with rice or chapathi or with toasted bread.

title: Mayonnaise Sandwich Recipe (Veg Mayo Sandwich)
ingredients: 4 slices bread, 1.5 tablespoons butter, 3 tablespoons Mayonnaise, 0.25 teaspoon black pepper, 1 teaspoon red chili flakes, 0.25 teaspoon garlic powder, 0.25 teaspoon dry ground mustard, 1 pinch salt, 1 teaspoon Lemon juice, 2 tablespoons Onions, 0.25 cup bell pepper, 3 tablespoons Cabbage, 2 tablespoons carrots, 2 tablespoons sweet corn, 1 Green chili
instructions: 1. Butter the bread slices and toast on a large griddle until golden and crisp. You can also skip toasting. You can also just butter the bread, then fill the sandwich and toast in sandwich maker. For this recipe you will need 4 bread slices. 2. Add the following ingredients to a mixing bowl: 3. 2 tablespoons Onions or shallots or green onions chopped finely 4. 1 Green chili (deseeded and chopped optional) 5. Here are the veggies you will need – about ½ cup: 6. ¼ cup red bell pepper (capsicum, or firm ripe tomatoes, chopped) 7. ¼ cup lettuce or cabbage fine chopped 8. 2 tablespoons sweet corn (Boiled, optional) 9. Add ¼ teaspoon crushed black pepper, ¼ to ½ teaspoon red chili flakes, 3 tablespoons mayo and ¼ teaspoon dry mustard powder (or 1 tsp Dijon mustard). Mix all the ingredients. The filling is ready. Taste test and add more of chilli, pepper or mayo etc if you want. You may add any other sauce like red chilli sauce, mustard sauce or schezwan sauce.

title: Medu Vada Recipe (Garelu)
ingredients: 1 cup urad dal, 0.3333333333333333 teaspoon salt, Ice cold water, Oil, 0.375 teaspoon pepper corn, 2.5 green chilies, 4 tablespoon onions, 1 sprig curry leaves, 0.5 teaspoon cumin seeds, 1 teaspoon ginger
instructions: 1. Wash dal several times and soak in lot of water for at least 4 to 5 hrs. 2. Drain the water completely and transfer the dal to a mixer grinder jar. If you have a wet grinder, you can use it. 3. Sprinkle 3 tablespoons water. Next add salt and start grinding. 4. Next scrape off the sides and then grind again. Whenever needed sprinkle only as much as 1 ½ tsps. Ice cold water evenly. 5. Again continue to grind to a coarse thick batter. Ensure your grinder does not become hot. 6. Sprinkle water. Grind again till the batter looks fluffy and white. 7. Transfer this to a bowl. Aerate the batter by beating it with your hand in a clockwise direction for 30 to 60 seconds. 8. Test the batter if it is aerated well. Drop a small batter ball in bowl full of water. The ball has to float meaning it is light. This is needed to get fluffy medu vada. 9. If the batter doesn't float well, means either the batter is runny or is not aerated well. Read the notes below to know what to do with runny batter. 10. If the batter is in right consistency still it is not floating well. Then beat the batter once more. 11. Add crushed pepper, cumin, ginger,scurry leaves, green chilies and onions. Mix everything well. 12. Heat oil in a deep heavy bottom kadai or pan on a medium heat till hot enough. 13. Drop a small amount of batter to check if the oil is hot. The batter raises if the oil is hot enough. 14. Dip your fingers in water or oil to moisten your fingers. Water should not be dripping. 15. Take small portions of batter. Shape to a ball, slightly flatten with thumb. 16. Dip the thumb in water, again no dripping water. Make a hole in the center. 17. Gently slide the vada in the hot oil by gently shaking off the fingers. 18. Or place the ball on a greased sheet. Flatten it slightly and make a hole in the centre. 19. If the oil becomes too hot, regulate the flame to medium. 20. Moisten your fingers again. Lift the corner part of the sheet and transfer the vada on to your fingers. Then drop it just by shaking your fingers. 21. Fry medu vada till they turn golden on both sides. 22. Make them and fry them in batches till you use up all the batter. In between every batch, wait till the oil heats up, if not hot. 23. Serve medu vada with coconut chutney or tiffin sambar.

title: Methi Chaman Recipe
ingredients: 112.5 grams paneer, 2 cups fenugreek leaves, 0.75 cup onion, 1 teaspoon ginger, 1 teaspoon garlic, 1 green chili, 1 cup tomatoes, 12 whole raw cashews, 2 tablespoons ghee, 0.25 teaspoon cumin seeds, 0.25 teaspoon turmeric, 0.625 teaspoon red chili powder, 1 teaspoon coriander powder, 0.5 teaspoon fennel powder, 0.25 teaspoon ginger powder, 0.625 teaspoon garam masala, 0.5 teaspoon sea salt, 0.75 cup water, 0.125 teaspoon cardamom powder, 0.5 tablespoon kasuri methi, 0.25 cup cream, 2 tablespoons coriander leaves
instructions: 1. Soak cashews in half cup boiling hot water for 10 mins, cool down and blend with ¼ cup soaked water to a smooth puree. 2. Heat ghee in a pan and add cumin seeds. When they begin to sizzle, add the onions and saute until deep golden, for 7 to 8 mins. Stir in the green chili, ginger & garlic. Saute for a minute. 3. Add the tomatoes and salt. Saute until they break down and turn mushy. If you want, you may splash little water and cook covered. 4. Stir in turmeric, red chili, coriander powder, ginger powder, garam masala and fennel powder. Move the sauteed masala to a side in the pan and add the fenugreek leaves. Saute on a medium heat for 4 to 5 mins, until aromatic. 5. Pour the cashew puree and hot water. Mix well. Cook covered on a medium heat until thick & the leaves become tender. 6. Add the paneer, cardamom powder and kasuri methi. Let the paneer heat through and turn off. Stir in the cream and taste test to adjust salt and garam masala. Add more cream if you want, to adjust the consistency. The curry turns very thick after cooling down. 7. Garnish methi chaman with coriander leaves. Serve it hot with flatbreads or with plain basmati rice.

title: Methi Chicken Recipe
ingredients: 0.5 kg chicken, 2 tablespoons oil, 1 cup onions, 1.5 teaspoon ginger garlic paste, 1 medium tomato, 0.5 cup curd, 0.625 teaspoon chilli powder, 0.875 teaspoon garam masala, 0.3333333333333333 teaspoon salt, 0.125 teaspoon turmeric, 1.5 to 2 cups methi leaves, 0.25 teaspoon pepper powder, 1 green chili, 0.25 teaspoon fennel seeds powder saunf powder, 1 teaspoon kasuri methi, 0.5 bay leaf, 2 cloves 2 cloves ((laung)), 4 green cardamoms, 1 inch cinnamon
instructions: 1. Pluck the methi leaves only and not the stems or stalks. Rinse the leaves well a few times in ample amount of water. Drain them completely and set aside. 2. Rinse onions, tomatoes and green chili. Chop them finely and set aside. 3. Deseed tomatoes and fine chop or puree them. Once the methi leaves are drained measure 1½ to 2 cups and chop them roughly. 4. Heat oil in a pot. Add bay leaf, cinnamon, cloves and cardamoms. 5. When they begin to sizzle, add chopped onions and green chili. Saute until the onions turn golden. 6. Add ginger garlic paste & saute until a nice aroma comes out, for about 50 to 60 seconds. 7. Lower the flame and stir in the chilli powder and turmeric. 8. Quickly add chicken and stir fry for 3 to 4 mins. Cover and cook the chicken on a medium flame for about 3 to 4 mins or until tender. 9. Meanwhile whisk the chilled curd with a fork until smooth. Keep it ready. 10. Then add tomatoes, salt and curd. Cover and simmer the chicken on a low heat until the tomatoes dissolve completely. 11. Sprinkle garam masala and fennel powder if using. Then add the chopped methi leaves & mix well. 12. Cover and simmer until the methi leaves wilt completely. It just takes a few minutes for the leaves to get cooked. 13. Sprinkle kasuri methi and pepper powder. Transfer methi chicken to a serving bowl. Garnish with coriander leaves or cream (optional).

title: Methi Dal Recipe (Menthi Kura Pappu)
ingredients: 0.5 cup dal, 2 tablespoons moong dal, 1.5 cups water, 2 cups methi leaves roughly chopped, 0.75 cup tomato, 1.5 green chili, 0.5 teaspoon salt, 0.125 teaspoon turmeric, 1 lemon, 1.5 tablespoon Ghee, 0.5 teaspoon cumin seeds, 0.25 teaspoon mustard seeds, 1 sprig curry leaves, 1 pinch hing, 2 garlic cloves, 1 red chili, 0.25 teaspoon Kashmiri red chili powder, 0.25 teaspoon garam masala
instructions: 1. Add dal to a pot or pressure cooker. Rinse it very well. 2. Pressure Cooker Method: Add water, turmeric, tomatoes and chili. If using dried or fresh unripe mango, add few pieces. 3. Pressure cook for 2 whistles. If cooking in a separate bowl, place it in the cooker and pressure cook for 4 whistles. If cooking in Instant pot, Press the pressure cook button & set the timer for 10 minutes. 4. Without Pressure Cooker: Pour water and bring it to a rolling boil. Reduce heat and cook on a medium heat until the lentils are almost tender, adding more hot water if required. Add tomatoes, turmeric and chilli. Cook until the tomatoes break down & the lentils are soft. 5. While the dal cooks, pluck methi leaves and add them to a large pot filled with water. Rinse them and discard the water. Repeat rinsing and drain. 6. When the dal is cooked, mash it with the back of a ladle, if you like smooth dal. 7. Pour ghee or oil to a small hot pan. 8. Add cumin, mustard, red chili. When they begin to splutter add hing, curry leaves and garlic. 9. When the curry leaves turn crisp, add the methi leaves & fry until they wilt off completely. Turn off and add the red chili powder and garam masala (optional). 10. Add salt & the tempered methi leaves to the dal. If the methi dal is too thick pour some hot water and simmer for 2 to 3 mins. Taste test and adjust salt and garam masala. If you want it more hot, you can mash the green chilies in the dal. 11. Let the dal cool down slightly and squeeze the lemon juice. Serve methi dal with rice, phulka or mudde. 12. Press saute button on the Instant pot and pour ghee or oil to the inner pot / steel insert. 13. Then add mustard, cumin, red chilli & garlic. When the spices pop, add curry leaves and hing. 14. Add methi leaves and green chillies. Saute for a minute. 15. Press cancel button & then add tomatoes, dal, turmeric, red chili powder and salt. If you have small kids, leave out the red chili powder or reduce the quantity. 16. Pour 2 cups water and deglaze the pot by scrubbing the bottom of the pot with a spatula. 17. You can also keep a trivet in the pot and place your rice bowl over it. 18. Secure the Instant pot with the lid & position the steam release handle to sealing. 19. Press the pressure cook button (high pressure) and set the timer to 10 minutes. 20. When the IP is done wait for the natural pressure release. If you want you may add garam masala. 21. If the dal is too thick stir in some hot water. Taste test and add more salt if required. Squeeze some lemon juice. 22. Serve methi dal with rice or roti.

title: Methi Matar Malai Recipe
ingredients: 1 tablespoon oil, 0.5 teaspoon cumin seeds, 2 green cardamoms, 0.1875 teaspoon black pepper corn, 0.75 inch cinnamon piece, 2 cloves 2 cloves ((optional)), 0.3333333333333333 inch ginger, 2 garlic cloves, 1 green chili, 1 cup yellow onion, 15 cashews, 0.5 cup water, 1 tablespoon butter, 2.25 cups methi leaves, 0.5 cup green peas, 0.3333333333333333 teaspoon sea salt, 0.5 teaspoon coriander powder, 0.25 teaspoon fennel seed powder, 0.41666666666666663 teaspoon Garam masala, 0.41666666666666663 cup water, 2.5 tablespoons heavy cream
instructions: 1. Pluck 2 to 2½ cups leaves (50 grams) from the fenugreek stalks (125 grams). Add them to a large pot of water and rinse them 2 to 3 times. Drain the water completely and sprinkle 1 teaspoon salt. Gently spread the salt over the leaves and rest for 5 mins. Later rinse them once more & drain to a colander. This salt step is optional but will cut down the bitter taste from the leaves. 2. Meanwhile prepare the following: 3. 1 cup yellow onion sliced (125 grams, or red onion but the gravy turns out deeper in color) 4. 1 green chili (chopped to 2 portions, omit for kids) 5. ⅛ to ¼ teaspoon black pepper (cut down for low heat) 6. On a medium flame, heat 1 tablespoon oil in a pan. Add cumin, cinnamon, cardamom, black pepper and cloves. (be careful, they can splutter if the oil is too hot). When they begin to sizzle, add ginger & garlic. 7. Followed by onions and green chilies. Saute them for 6 mins until transparent. Add cashews and pour half cup water. 8. Cook covered until very soft, for 5 to 6 mins. 9. Cool down and remove the chilies for kids-friendly/ low heat option. 10. Transfer the onions (along with the leftover liquid in the pan), to a blender jar and blend to a super smooth puree. 11. Run a spoon and make sure the spices are ground well. 12. Optional – If you want to omit cream, you may add ⅓ to ½ cup milk and run the blender once more. Use pasteurized or raw milk that is boiled and cooled previously. Do not add raw milk here. I have not used milk here. If you want a step by step help for this part, take a look at this Malai chicken post.

title: Methi Paratha Recipe
ingredients: 2 cup whole wheat flour, 1 cup methi leaves, 0.25 teaspoon salt, 0.375 teaspoon garam masala, 1.5 teaspoon garlic, 0.25 teaspoon chilli powder, 1 teaspoon oil, 2.5 tablespoons ghee, 0.3333333333333333 cup water, 2.5 tablespoons yogurt
instructions: 1. Pluck methi leaves from the stems. Spray some vinegar and sprinkle salt all over and leave them for 5 minutes. Later rinse them well in a pot of water. Repeat rinsing a few times. Drain them to a colander and shake off so the excess water drains off. 2. Chop the methi leaves and grate the garlic. If using green chilies fine chop them. 3. In a bowl mix together, atta, salt, finely chopped methi leaves, curd (optional), garam masala, chili powder or green chilies and garlic. 4. Begin to knead, adding water little by little to make a soft pliable non-sticky dough. 5. Pour 1 tsp oil & knead again for a min or 2 until soft. You may rest the dough for 30 minutes if you have time. Resting also helps in getting softer parathas. 6. Divide the dough to 8 portions. Roll them to smooth balls. Set aside. 7. Flour the rolling area, place a ball of dough on the flour. Dust it with flour evenly on both the sides. 8. Roll the ball to round shape parathas that are not very thin or thick. 9. To make layered methi paratha, smear half teaspoon ghee or oil all over the paratha. Fold it to half. Then repeat smearing ghee. Fold to a triangle. Roll this to a paratha of triangle shape. Refer stepwise pictures for understanding. 10. This way make 3 to 4 parathas and set aside. 11. Heat a tawa till hot on a medium high flame. Dust off the excess flour from the paratha. Transfer the methi paratha on the tawa. 12. Soon you will see tiny bubbles on the paratha, then flip it. Press down with a wooden spatula to help in puffing. 13. Drizzle some oil or ghee all over the methi paratha. 14. Flip it to the other side, repeat to press and cook till they are slightly browned and done thoroughly. Stack them one over the other to keep them soft. 15. Clean up the flour on the griddle with a kitchen towel before you proceed to toast the next one. 16. Serve methi paratha with yogurt, chutney, dal or pickle.

title: Methi Pulao Recipe
ingredients: 1 cup basmati rice, 1 cup methi leaves, 1 teaspoon ginger garlic paste, 0.3333333333333333 teaspoon salt, 1.5 to 2 tablespoon oil, 0.875 cup green peas, 1 onion, 1 green chili, 2 cups water, 0.5 teaspoon cumin seeds, 1 star anise, 1 bay leaf, 3.5 cloves 3 to 4 cloves, 1 inch cinnamon piece, 2.5 green cardamoms, 1 strand mace
instructions: 1. Add rice to a bowl and rinse it well in lots of water at least thrice. Drain the water and soak it in fresh water for at least 30 minutes. 2. Pluck the methi leaves from the stems and add them to a large pot of water. Remove them to a colander and then repeat the rinse. I sprinkle salt and vinegar and leave them for a while before rinsing them to get rid of the pesticides. 3. Drain them completely to a colander. Shake the colander to remove any excess water in the leaves as it can make your methi pulao mushy. 4. Heat a cooker or pot with oil or ghee. When the oil turns hot, add bay leaf, cinnamon, cloves, cardamoms, cumin seeds and mace. 5. As soon as the spices begin to sizzle, add sliced onions and green chilies (optional). 6. Saute until the onions golden. Add ginger garlic paste and fry till you get an aroma or till the raw smell disappears. 7. Pour water and add salt. Stir and taste test the water. It has to be slightly salty. Bring the water to a boil. 8. Transfer the drained rice, green peas and methi leaves. Give a good stir. 9. Cooking in pot: Cover and cook on a low heat till the rice is fully done. Turn off and let the methi pulao rest in the pot for at least 10 to 15 minutes. Tip - if the rice is undercooked, then add little hot water and cook further. 10. Pressure cooker: close the lid and cook on the lowest flame for about 5 minutes. Alternately cook on a medium heat until it whistles once. Let the pressure release on its own, then remove and fluff up the methi pulao. 11. Press saute button on the Instant pot and pour oil to the steel insert. 12. Add all the wholes spices. When they begin to sizzle, add onions and green chilies. 13. Saute until they turn light golden or transparent for about 2 minutes. 14. Stir in the ginger garlic and saute for 30 seconds. Then add green peas and methi leaves. 15. Saute until the methi leaves wilt off for about 1 minute. Then press cancel and add rice. 16. Pour water and add salt. Stir and deglaze. Taste test. Water must be slightly salty. 17. Secure the Instant pot with the lid and position the steam release valve to sealing. 18. Press pressure cook button and set the timer to 5 minutes. The IP beeps when it is done. Wait for the pressure to release naturally for 2 minutes. 19. Release the rest manually. Give a gentle mix with a fork and keep the methi pulao covered until you serve.

title: Methi Rice Recipe (Methi Leaves Rice)
ingredients: 1 cup rice, 2 tablespoon oil, 1.5 garlic cloves, 0.5 teaspoon cumin seeds, 1.5 green chili, 0.5 cup green peas, 1.25 cup methi leaves, 0.375 teaspoon garam masala, 0.125 teaspoon turmeric, 0.3333333333333333 teaspoon salt, 1.5 tablespoons lemon juice
instructions: 1. Wash and soak rice for about 20 minutes. Bring 2 cups water to a boil and add rice. Cook covered until the rice is done to a grainy texture and not mushy. Cool completely. 2. Pour oil to a hot pan and add cumin, green cardamoms, green chilli and garlic. 3. Saute garlic until a nice aroma comes out, then add green peas and methi leaves. Saute for 3 to 4 minutes until the leaves wilt completely. 4. Add garam masala powder, turmeric and mix well. Turn off the stove. 5. Transfer cooled rice and sprinkle salt. Mix everything well. Add some lemon juice. Taste test and add more salt and lemon juice if needed. 6. Serve methi rice with raita or a veggie salad. 7. Rinse 1 cup basmati rice and soak it. Set aside for 20 mins. Meanwhile pluck the methi leaves. 8. Press SAUTE button on the instant pot. Pour oil to the steel insert. 9. Add cumin, green cardamoms, garlic and green chilies. When they begin to splutter, add green peas and methi leaves. 10. Saute for a minute or 2 until the leaves wilt. Add garam masala, turmeric, salt and drained rice. Pour 1¼ cup water. 11. Press CANCEL button and scrape the bottom with a spatula to release any bits of food stuck. 12. Press PRESSURE COOK button and set the timer to 5 minutes if using basmati rice. If using regular rice, set the timer to 10 minutes. 13. Allow natural pressure release for 5 minutes. Release the rest manually. Let the methi rice cool down a bit and then taste test it. Then add more salt if needed and squeeze in lemon juice.

title: Mint Raita Recipe (Raita Chutney)
ingredients: 0.75 cup mint leaves fresh, 0.25 cup coriander leaves, 0.5 teaspoon cumin, 0.375 teaspoon sugar, 0.25 teaspoon salt, 1.25 cup curd, 1.5 green chili, 1 teaspoon lemon juice, 1.5 tablespoon cucumber, 1.5 tablespoon onions
instructions: 1. This recipe requires dry roasted cumin powder. You can make a batch of the powder following this Cumin powder recipe or make a quick powder from my instructions below. On a medium heat, dry roast half teaspoon cumin seeds in a pan until aromatic & deep golden but not burnt. Cool and powder it in a small grinder. 2. Rinse ¾ cup mint leaves/pudina and ¼ cup coriander leaves (optional) in surplus water at least thrice. Drain off the water. Add the leaves to the grinder along with 1 to 2 green chili (or serrano peppers), ¼ to ½ teaspoon sugar, ¼ teaspoon salt and one tbsp. of yogurt. We add yogurt to help in grinding. If you want you can also add a small garlic clove (peeled). 3. Grind to a super smooth consistency. Pour 1¼ cup yogurt to a bowl and whisk well until it is smooth. Add the mint puree to yogurt. Whisk everything together. Taste test and add more salt if required. If you want you may add 1 teaspoon lemon juice or half teaspoon chaat masala. It is optional to use them. We prefer to stir in a few tablespoons of peeled & chopped cucumbers or onions. This goes well with biryani. Serve with any flavored rice like Veg biryani , Mushroom biryani , Chicken biryani Hyderabadi chicken biryani 4. Wash pudina and coriander in enough water. Drain them to a colander completely. 2. Dry roast jeera and cool it. Powder it in a blender jar. You can also use roasted powder and skip this step. 3. Add 1 to 2 tbsp of curd, coriander, mint, salt, sugar, ginger, garlic and green chili to the blender jar. 4. Blend them all well until smooth. Add it to curd. Stir well. Serve with kababs or Indian snacks .

title: Mirchi Ka Salan Recipe (Biryani Salan)
ingredients: 4 tablespoons peanuts, 3 tablespoons sesame seeds, 3 tablespoons coconut, 1 cup onions, 1 inch ginger, 4 medium garlic, 0.375 cup oil, 3 tablespoons tamarind, 1 cup water, 7 green chilies, 0.75 teaspoon mustard seeds, 1 teaspoon cumin seeds, 1 sprig curry leaves, 1 teaspoon sea salt, 0.5 teaspoon turmeric, 1 teaspoon Kashmiri red chili powder, 1 teaspoon garam masala, 1 tablespoon jaggery, 2 cups water
instructions: 1. Rinse the green chilies and make a slit in the center, lengthwise, keeping the stem and the other end intact. For low heat you may deseed. 2. Dry roast peanuts on a medium heat until golden and aromatic, for 6 to 7 mins. 3. Stir in the sesame seeds and coconut. Roast for 2 mins, until aromatic. Do not burn. Remove from the pan to a plate and cool aside. 4. To the same pan, add half of the oil. Saute onions, ginger and garlic until golden, for 9 mins. Remove to the same plate, retaining any excess oil in the pan. Cool down. 5. Add the sautéed onions, ginger, garlic, roasted peanuts, coconut & sesame to a blender jar. Pour water and blend to a smooth puree. 6. Add the green chilies to the same pan and fry them on a medium heat until blistered. If your pan is dry, add a tsp of oil. Mine takes about 5 to 6 mins.
