Remove to a plate. 7. Pour remaining oil to the pan and heat it. Add mustard, kalonji, cumin seeds and curry leaves. 8. When they begin to pop, stir in the red chili powder, turmeric, salt, garam masala, followed by the salan paste/puree. 9. Mix well and add tamarind, followed by water. Bring to a rolling boil. Cook covered on a medium heat for a minimum of 15 to 20 mins, until thick, smooth, and glossy and you see traces of oil on top. Keep stirring in between. 10. Add the fried green chilies and jaggery. 11. Cover and cook until the chilies wilt but not mushy, for another 5 to 10 mins. Mine cook down by 10 mins and this can vary for you, depending on the kind of chilies. If your salan is too thick and your chilies are undercooked, add some hot water. 12. Taste test and adjust salt and tamarind. Mirchi ka salan should typically taste tangy, spicy, hot and slightly sweet. Turn off when you see oil on top of the gravy and the consistency is slightly runny. 13. Serve Mirchi Ka Salan with biryani, plain rice, pulao, chapati, roti or naan.

title: Misal Pav Recipe
ingredients: 2 cups matki sprouts, 3.5 tablespoon oil, 0.75 teaspoon mustard seeds, 0.75 teaspoon cumin seeds, 1 sprig curry leaves, 0.1875 teaspoon hing, 1 cup onions, 3.5 garlic cloves, 1 inch ginger, 2 tablespoons desiccated coconut, 0.75 cup tomatoes, 3 tablespoons coriander leaves, 0.625 tablespoon salt, 0.25 teaspoon turmeric, 1.25 teaspoon red chili powder, 3 tablespoons goda masala, 1 tablespoon jaggery, 1 teaspoon tamarind paste, 4.5 cup water, 8 pav buns, 2 tablespoons butter, 0.25 cup onions, 1 lemon, 2 tablespoons coriander leaves, 0.5 cup farsan thick sev
instructions: 1. Rinse half cup matki well and soak them in enough water for 8 hours. Later, drain the water completely and wrap them in a clean cloth or add to a box. Cover well and keep it in a dark warm place for 12 to 24 hours to sprout. 2. Depending on the temperature they will sprout. If the weather is too hot, sprinkle some water on them when the cloth dries up. 3. Grate or grind together ginger garlic and coconut in a small grinder. Keep this aside. 4. Heat oil in a pan. Add mustard and cumin seeds. When they splutter add curry leaves. 5. Saute till the leaves turn crisp. Then stir in hing. Add onions and saute until translucent. Then add ginger garlic coconut paste & saute till it turns aromatic for 2 to 3 minutes. 6. Add tomatoes and salt. Cook until the tomatoes turn completely mushy and soft. 7. Add chili powder, misal masala or goda masala & turmeric. Mix and cook until oil begins to separate, just for 2 minutes. 8. Next add the sprouts, tamarind and pour water as needed to make the kat or tarry. If you prefer more kat you can pour upto 5 cups like I did. For a less kat, add 3 cups. 9. Mix everything and bring it to a rolling boil. Reduce to medium heat and cook for 5 minutes till the oil floats on top and the sprouts are cooked, yet firm. 10. Taste test and add more salt or misal masala or goda masala if needed. 11. Slice the each pav to 2, keeping one of the sides intact. Spread soft butter on one side and toast them on a pan. Alternately you may place all the buttered pavs on a prepared baking tray and bake for 3 minutes in the top rack. Adjust the time as required, no preheating required. 12. Transfer misal to 4 serving bowls. Sprinkle lemon juice, onions, coriander leaves and then farsan. 13. Serve misal pav immediately.

title: Mix Veg Paratha (Vegetable Paratha)
ingredients: 1 cup atta, 0.5 teaspoon salt, 0.5 teaspoon ginger, 0.5 teaspoon red chilli powder, 0.25 teaspoon garam masala, 0.5 teaspoon ajwain, 2 tablespoons ghee, 0.5 cup potatoes, 0.5 cup carrots, 0.25 cup cauliflower, 0.25 cup green peas, 0.25 cup methi leaves, 1 Handful coriander leaves
instructions: 1. Steam or boil veggies as desired until soft. I do over a pressure cooker. It just takes around 5 to 7 mins to steam the veggies. 2. Begin to mash them using a masher. They don’t need to cool down, you can do it right away when they are still hot. 3. Make sure there are no bits and chunks of veggies. The veggies must be mashed to very smooth. 4. Add finely chopped leafy greens. I used methi and coriander leaves. Also add ginger garlic paste, garam masala, red chilli powder, salt and ajwain 5. Mix up well, taste it and check if there is enough salt. It must be moderately salty. If needed add more. 6. Add whole wheat flour. 7. Begin to mix without using any water. If it doesn’t come together to form the dough, then sprinkle some water or the vegetable stock and form the dough. 8. The dough must be soft and non sticky. 9. Divide the dough to 5 to 6 balls. 10. Sprinkle flour over the rolling board and a ball. Flatten and begin to roll it. 11. Roll to a 6 to 7 inches paratha of even thickness. 12. Heat a griddle or tawa. When hot enough, transfer the paratha to the tawa. 13. Flip it when you see tiny bubbles. Drizzle few drops of ghee or oil. Spread it all over the paratha. 14. Cook on both the sides until well cooked with golden to light brown spots on them. Place a kitchen cloth or tissue in a plate. Stack the vegetable parathas over it to keep them soft. Continue to make the rest of the parathas and fry them. Serve veg paratha with chutney or pickle.

title: Mooli Paratha (Radish Paratha)
ingredients: 2 cups wheat flour, 1 teaspoon oil, 2.5 tablespoons ghee, 4 cups radish, 0.625 inch ginger, 1.5 green chilies, 3.5 tablespoons coriander leaves, 0.125 teaspoon turmeric, 0.41666666666666663 teaspoon Kashmiri red chili powder, 0.625 teaspoon garam masala, 0.5 teaspoon roasted cumin powder, 0.625 teaspoon salt, 1.25 teaspoon chaat masala, 0.29166666666666663 teaspoon ajwain
instructions: 1. Mix together grated radish with salt and let rest for 7 to 8 mins. Squeeze out the juices from the radish using your hands and place it in a mixing bowl. 2. To another mixing bowl, add the flour and oil. Start adding the radish juices little by little and begin to form a soft non-sticky dough. (You will only need about ½ cup+ 2 tbsps juices. Store the rest in the fridge to use later) 3. Knead the dough well until smooth and divide to 8 portions. Cover and keep aside. 4. Squeeze the grated radish once more to remove any more moisture in it. Add the grated radish to the bowl along with chili, ginger, coriander leaves, cumin powder, turmeric and garam masala. Mix everything well. The stuffing should be dry and not too wet. 5. You may taste test and adjust any other spice but do not add salt. Roughly divide the filling to 8 portions in the bowl. 6. Take 2 to 3 tbsps of flour to a small bowl. Dip a dough ball in the flour and shake off the excess. Sprinkle some flour on the rolling area. 7. Flatten the dough ball and spread it with your fingers to form a small cup (about 4 inches, check pics in the post) 8. Place one portion filling in the center, in the cup and press down the stuffing gently. Sprinkle chaat masala if you want. Bring the edges higher and seal the cup. 9. Flatten with your fingers and place it on the rolling area. Sprinkle a little flour on it and spread gently. Roll it to a 5 to 6 inch paratha. If the paratha breaks, apply little dry flour. This way make 4 parathas and cook them first, later follow the same steps. 10. To fry the paratha, heat a pan on a medium to medium high heat. Dust off the excess flour from the paratha and place it gently on the tawa/pan. 11. Wait until you see bubbles on the mooli paratha. With the help of a turner, turn it to the other side. Spread ghee and let cook until you see bubbles. 12. Reduce the heat to medium. Turn them again and spread more ghee. Fry them pressing down with the turner, until the paratha has some deep golden spots. Make sure they are cooked on the edges as well. 13. Remove and stack on a plate. It is important to dust off the uncooked paratha before placing on the pan. If required, wipe off the pan with a tissue after cooking each paratha. 14. Serve Mooli Paratha hot with yogurt, pickle or chutney. If you want you may sprinkle some chaat masala on the hot parathas.

title: Moong Dal Dosa
ingredients: 1 cup moong dal, 0.875 inch ginger, 1.5 green chilies, 2 red chilies, 0.875 teaspoon cumin, 0.3333333333333333 teaspoon salt, 1.125 cup water, 2 tablespoons Ghee
instructions: 1. Rinse moong dal thrice and drain the water. Soak them for 15 to 20 mins. If your lentils are too old, use warm water. 2. Drain the water and rinse the lentils well. Add them to a grinder jar along with salt, ginger, green chilies, red chilies, cumin and 1 cup water. If you soak the lentils longer, use less water. 3. Grind to a smooth thick, pouring and spreadable consistency. If the batter is too thick, pour more water and blend. I use up ¼ cup more water. (check stepwise photos in the post for consistency) 4. If using cast iron pan, heat it well. Once hot drizzle a few drops of oil and spread it well with a cut onion or a kitchen tissue (careful! hot pan). Let the pan become smoking hot, then sprinkle little water and turn down the heat to low. To use a non-stick you just need to heat the pan until medium hot and not smoking hot. 5. Pour a ladle full of batter to the center of the pan. Spread the batter in circular pattern to make a thin moong dal dosa. 6. Sprinkle little ghee towards the edges. Let cook on a medium high heat until golden. When it is cooked you will see the edges come off the pan on their own. 7. Turn the moong dal dosa to the other side and cook for a minute or 2. Turn them back and cook on a low heat for a minute until crispy. Fold the dosa and remove to a serving plate. 8. To make the next dosa, make sure your pan is not too hot but it should be medium hot. If it is too hot, the batter clumps up in the center and won't let you spread it. So it is essential to reduce the heat during the last 2 mins while you are cooking the previous dosa. 9. Serve moong dal dosa hot with your favorite chutney.

title: Moong Dal Halwa Recipe
ingredients: 0.5 cup moong dal, 6 tablespoons ghee, 0.5 tablespoon suji, 0.5 cup water, 0.5 cup milk, 1 pinch saffron strands, 7 tablespoons sugar, 0.5 teaspoon cardamom powder, 6 almonds, 6 pistachios
instructions: 1. Rinse moong dal thrice and soak it for 30 mins. Drain the water completely and add it to a grinder jar. 2. Blend it to a almost smooth but very slightly coarse texture. Scrape the sides and blend. If needed you may sprinkle little water if your grinder gets jammed. 3. To a heavy bottom nonstick pan, add 6 tbsps ghee and heat it. 4. Add semolina and fry for about a minute until it gets roasted well. 5. Next transfer the ground moong dal and begin to incorporate it into the ghee stirring constantly. 6. Keep the flame to medium. Continue stirring & frying. 7. After 10 to 15 mins the dal mixture will turn crumbly and may have small lumps. 8. Begin to break down the lumps and continue to fry on a low flame. 9. When the moong dal is done frying, you will begin to smell it good and it turns to deep golden color. 10. Pour milk & water (both at room temperature) slowly stirring the dal. Also add the saffron. 11. Cook stirring and ensure there are no lumps. 12. The entire liquid will be absorbed soon. 13. When the mixture turns thick add sugar and continue. The mixture turns gooey and then turns thick like a mass. 14. Add 2 tbsps ghee and cardamom powder or rose water. Adding ghee at this stage makes sauteing the halwa easier. You may skip if you are able to do it. 15. Mix and cook till the moong dal halwa begins to leave the sides of the pan. 16. Toast the nuts until crunchy in a different pan. Garnish the halwa with the nuts. 17. Serve moong dal halwa hot or warm.

title: Mullangi Curry (Radish Stir Fry)
ingredients: 25 grams radish, 1 medium tomato, 1 sprig curry leaves, 0.25 teaspoon mustard seeds, 0.5 teaspoon cumin seeds, 1 pinch hing, 1 teaspoon chana dal, 1 teaspoon urad dal, 0.125 teaspoon turmeric, 1 green chilli, 0.5 teaspoon Kashmiri red chili powder, 1 garlic clove, 2 tablespoons coriander leaves, 1.5 tablespoon oil, 0.25 teaspoon salt, 2 tablespoons roasted peanuts, 1 teaspoon Sesame seeds, 2 tablespoons Coconut
instructions: 1. Rinse 2 to 3 medium radish (about 250 grams) thoroughly under running water. If you prefer to use the leaves, open them up and rinse thoroughly. Peel the skin and rinse the radish well. If you are using organic vegetables then you may keep the skin on and just rinse them well. 2. Grate in a food processer or chop them finely. Shake off the water from the leaves and chop them. Keep them separately. 3. On a medium flame, heat 1 to 2 tablespoons oil in a fry pan. Add 4. 1 teaspoon urad dal (optional). Let fry until the dal turns golden. 5. Add 6. 1 slit or chopped green chilli 7. Next add 8. 1 medium tomato chopped (optional) 9. ⅛ teaspoon turmeric. Sauté till the tomatoes turn pulpy and dry. 10. Stir in half teaspoon Kashmiri red chili powder or red chili flakes (optional) and saute for a minute. 11. Next add the grated radish. 12. Saute on a high flame till it is cooked, half cooked or full cooked to suit your choice. 13. Add radish leaves or coriander leaves, Fry till they wilt, for about 3 minutes or longer. 14. Sprinkle 2 tablespoons roasted crushed peanuts or 2 tablespoons raw or desiccated coconut or 1 teaspoon toasted sesame seeds. Serve mullangi curry with rice or roti.

title: Mushroom Biryani Recipe
ingredients: 1 cup aged basmati rice, 2 cups white button mushrooms, 0.3333333333333333 cup onion, 0.25 cup tomatoes, 2 tablespoon mint leaves fine chopped, 2 tablespoon coriander leaves fine chopped, 1 green chili, 1.5 teaspoon ginger garlic paste, 2 tablespoon Oil, 0.29166666666666663 teaspoon red chili powder, 3 tablespoons curd, 0.3333333333333333 teaspoon sea salt, 0.125 teaspoon turmeric, 0.75 teaspoon garam masala, 1 bay leaf, 1 star anise, 0.5 teaspoon shahi jeera, 2.5 green cardamoms, 1 cinnamon, 3.5 cloves 3 to 4 cloves ((laung)), 2 cups water, 1.25 cups water, 1.75 cups water
instructions: 1. Rinse and soak rice until you prepare the vegetables. If you do not have a good masala, make it from my notes below. 2. Heat a pan or pressure cooker with oil (sauté mode if using Instant Pot). Stir in the whole spices - bay leaf, cinnamon, cloves, cardamom, star anise and shahi jeera. 3. When they begin to sizzle, add onions and chilies. Saute till golden & stir in the ginger garlic paste. Saute for a minute till the raw smell disappears. 4. Add the chopped tomatoes, turmeric and salt. Saute until they turn soft & breakdown. 5. Stir in red chili powder, garam masala, mint and coriander leaves and saute for about a minute (do not burn). Pour yogurt or coconut milk and mix well to deglaze. 6. Add the mushrooms, saute for 2 to 3 minutes. Drain the water from the rice completely and add the rice here. Stir in the salt and pour water. Taste test to adjust salt. 7. Instant Pot - Press cancel, followed by PRESSURE COOK button (high pressure) and set the timer to 5 minutes. When it is done, IP will beep. Wait for 5 mins, release the pressure manually by moving the steam valve from sealing to venting. 8. Stovetop Pressure Cooker: Cook for 1 whistle on a medium flame. Wait for the pressure to drop naturally. 9. No Cooker - cook in a pot: Bring the water to a rolling boil and reduce the heat to low. Cover and cook until the rice is fully cooked and the moisture is absorbed. 10. Cool down the mushroom biryani for 4 to 5 mins and fluff up gently with a fork. Garnish with coriander leaves & sprinkle lemon juice if you want. Serve mushroom biryani with a raita.

title: Mushroom Fried Rice Recipe
ingredients: 1.25 tbsp oil, 2 cloves garlic, 1.5 sprigs spring onions, 0.5 cup bell peppers, 1 cup mushrooms, 1 green chili, 0.875 tablespoon soya sauce, 0.625 teaspoon vinegar, 0.25 teaspoon black pepper, salt, 1.25 cups cooked rice
instructions: 1. For this recipe you require fluffy and al dente cooked rice. If you have precooked rice you may proceed to the next section. 2. Pour 3 cups of water in a large pot and bring it to a boil. Add 1 tsp oil to the water. 3. Meanwhile rinse rice well a few times in a colander or in a bowl to remove all of the surface starch. Soak the rice until the water comes to a boil. 4. When the water begins to boil rapidly, drain the water from the rice and add it to the water. 5. Let the rice cook until al dente, meaning fully cooked yet firm and not mushy. Drain the rice to a colander. Cover and set aside. Let cool completely. If the rice is overcooked by mistake, simply drizzle some oil over the rice and spread it on a tray and cool completely. 6. Heat a wok with oil on a high heat. Fry garlic until it turns aromatic without burning it. 7. Fry spring onion whites or sliced onions for 1 min. 8. Add mushrooms, capsicum and chili. Stir fry until they are cooked al dente and not mushy. 9. Pour soya sauce , vinegar and saute for a minute . You may also skip soya sauce and vinegar and use any other preferred sauce. 10. Add cooked rice, salt if required, black pepper and spring onions greens. Saute for 2 mins. 11. Serve mushroom fried rice hot.

title: Mushroom Fry
ingredients: 1.5 cup white button mushrooms, 0.25 cup cashews, 1 onions, 1 tomato, 1 teaspoon ginger garlic paste, 1 green chili, 1.5 sprigs curry leaves, 0.5 teaspoon cumin seeds, 2 tablespoons Oil, 0.75 teaspoon red chili powder, 0.125 teaspoon turmeric, 1 teaspoon garam masala, 0.3333333333333333 teaspoon salt, 1 teaspoon lemon juice
instructions: 1. Chop onions, slit green chilies. Soak cashews for 15 minutes in water or rinse green peas, drain them and set aside. 2. Heat oil in a pan. Add cumin seeds. When they begin to splutter add the ginger garlic paste and saute on a medium heat until aromatic. Do not burn. 3. Add onions and green chili (option). Stir fry them evenly till they turn brown, not burnt. While the onions fry, rinse and chop the mushrooms. 4. Next add tomato and stir fry until the moisture dries up and the raw smell goes away. 5. Add chili powder, garam masala, curry leaves and turmeric. Saute until the onion tomato mixture turns aromatic and comes together. 6. Add chopped mushrooms, drained cashews and salt. Stir fry on a high flame till you begin to get an aroma of roasted masala and mushrooms. 7. If the masala begins to stick to the pan, reduce the heat and constantly stir fry until the mushrooms are tender, cooked and not soggy. If the mushrooms let out moisture, immediately increase the flame to highest and stir fry. 8. Do not overcook as mushrooms tend to become soggy very fast. Turn off the heat. Taste test and add more salt if required. 9. Sprinkle some lemon juice and give a good mix while serving. Serve mushroom fry with rice and ghee, roti, chapathi or bread.

title: Mushroom Manchurian
ingredients: 2 grams button mushrooms, 0.25 cup all-purpose flour, 3 tablespoons white corn flour, 0.5 teaspoon black pepper powder, 0.5 teaspoon red chilli powder, 0.25 teaspoon salt, 0.25 cup water, Oil, 1 tablespoon oil, 0.625 tablespoon garlic, 0.625 tablespoon ginger, 0.25 cup spring onion, 0.375 cup capsicum, 1 teaspoon corn flour, 0.5 cup water, 1.5 tablespoons soya sauce, 2 tablespoons tomato sauce, 0.875 teaspoon sugar, 0.625 teaspoon vinegar, salt, 2 tablespoons Spring onion greens
instructions: 1. Ensure mushrooms are at room temperature before using them in this recipe. Using too cold mushrooms to fry will result in splashes of oil. 2. Add corn flour, all-purpose flour, salt, pepper and chilli powder to a bowl. 3. Pour ¼ cup water and mix to make a free flowing batter. Add more water, a few tablespoons as needed to adjust the consistency. The consistency has to be moderately thick, not too thick or too runny. (check video) 4. On a medium flame, heat oil in a deep pan for deep frying. You will need about ¼ to ½ cup oil depending on the size of your pan. 5. Rinse the mushrooms and pat dry with a paper napkin. There should be no traces of moisture. Slice them slightly thick. 6. Check if the oil is hot enough by gently dropping a small portion of batter to the hot oil. If the temperature of oil is right, the batter will come up without browning. If the batter does not rise then it means oil is not ready yet. 7. Add the sliced mushrooms to the batter only when the oil is almost hot. Coat them well. 8. Gently slide the mushroom slices one after the other to the oil. Deep fry on a medium high heat without disturbing them for a few minutes. 9. Stir often and fry until golden and crisp. Remove them to a steel colander. 10. To Air Fry: Make the batter thicker than we make for the deep frying version. This helps the batter to stay on the mushrooms without dripping a lot. 11. Cut each button mushroom to 2 parts. Dip each portion in the batter, lift and place it in the powdered cornflakes (slightly coarse, read notes). Coat each mushroom well. 12. Place the mushrooms directly ¾ inch apart in the basket. Air fry at 360 C or 180 C for 8 to 9 mins, shake a bit and continue to air fry for another 5 to 6 mins. Adjust the timing depending on the air fryer. 13. Add 1 tsp corn flour to a bowl and pour half cup of water. Mix and make a lump free slurry. Keep this aside. 14. Heat 1 tbsp of oil in a wide pan. Regulate the flame to high. 15. Add ginger and garlic. Saute for a min. 16. Add spring onion whites and fry for a min or two. 17. Next add capsicum and saute for another minute. 18. Pour soya sauce and tomato sauce. Stir the corn flour slurry once and pour it to the pan. 19. Stir well and cook to make a sauce. Add more water if required. 20. Reduce the flame to medium and add sugar, little salt. Stir and check the taste of the sauce. 21. Add pepper and vinegar if needed. 22. Allow the sauce to turn thick. Turn off the flame. Allow to cool it a bit. Do not add hot mushrooms to the manchurian sauce. 23. Add the fried mushrooms and coat them well in the sauce 24. Garnish with spring onion greens. 25. Serve mushroom manchurian hot as a appetizer or with noodles or fried rice.

title: Mushroom Omelette Recipe
ingredients: 2 eggs, 0.25 teaspoon salt, 0.5 cup button mushrooms, 2.5 garlic cloves, 1 green chilies, 1 onion, 0.25 cup capsicum sliced, 0.125 teaspoon turmeric, 1 tablespoon oil, 1 pinch cumin seeds, 0.125 teaspoon black pepper, 2 tablespoons coriander leaves, 0.25 teaspoon herbs
instructions: 1. Rinse onions, bell peppers (optional) and green chilies (optional) under running water. Slice the onions and bell peppers thin. Fine chop green chilies and garlic. 2. Pluck and rinse coriander leaves in a bowl of water, a few times. Drain and fine chop. Set aside. 3. Remove long stems if any from the mushrooms. Rinse them under running water and slice them slightly thick, not too thin. 4. Break the eggs to a bowl and add salt, turmeric and pepper. Beat until frothy and keep aside. 5. Heat 1 teaspoon oil in a 6 inch pan. Add cumin, when they sputter, add garlic & green chili. 6. Saute them for 30 to 60 seconds. Then add onions, mushrooms & bell peppers(optional). Saute them on a slightly high heat until cooked and roasted. 7. Add coriander leaves. Mix and remove to a small plate. 8. To the same pan, add the rest of the oil and reduce the flame to medium. Pour the beaten eggs. 9. Cook until the eggs are almost set, yet slightly runny, spread the roasted onions and mushrooms. Flip and cook on the other side until eggs are cooked well. Remove to a serving plate and sprinkle dried herbs if desired. 10. Serve mushroom omelette hot with toasted bread or roll it in roti or tortilla. It also goes well as a side in any meal.

title: Mushroom Sandwich Recipe
ingredients: 4 slices bread, 1 tablespoon butter, 1 tablespoon oil, 2 garlic cloves, 1 medium onion, 1 cup mushrooms, 1 pinch salt, 1 teaspoon herbs, 1 teaspoon chilli flakes, 0.25 cup mozzarella cheese
instructions: 1. Add 5 garlic cloves, 5 red chilies, 1 tsp sugar, 1/4 tsp salt and 3/4 tbsp vinegar (or lemon juice) to a small grinder jar. Pour 1 to 2 tbsps water and make a slightly coarse paste. You can also deseed the chilies and use. 2. Heat 1 tbsp oil in a pan and add half tsp cumin seeds. When they sizzle, add 1 medium onion sliced and saute until golden. Add the ground garlic chilli paste and saute on a low to medium heat for 2 to 3 mins until it turns aromatic. 3. Add 1.5 cups sliced mushrooms and saute until they are cooked to your liking. If the mushrooms do not let out moisture cover and cook. They will release some moisture. Taste and add more salt and sugar if needed. Set aside to cool. 4. Slice and butter the bread. Toast it on a griddle or in a preheated oven at 240 C for 5 mins. Spread the filling evenly. Mix together mayo or Greek yogurt with little black pepper and mustard. Spread it evenly. I used this hung curd with mustard & pepper just the way I did for this veg burger . 5. Place your shredded lettuce or any greens you like. Some onions and deseeded tomatoes over it. Little bit of mayo over it again and place your other slice of bread. The filling will be good enough to make 2 large sandwiches or 3 regular sandwiches. Mushroom sandwich is ready. You can also wrap this and take it to work.

title: Mushroom Spaghetti
ingredients: 1 cup tomato puree, 2.5 cloves garlic, 1.5 to 3 cups mushroom, 1 onion, 162.5 grams spaghetti, 1 teaspoon salt, 2 tablespoons Olive oil, 1 teaspoon Oregano, 0.375 teaspoon black pepper, 0.5 teaspoon red chili flakes, 2.5 tablespoons cream
instructions: 1. Bring water to a rolling boil in a large pot and add 1 teaspoon salt. 2. Add spaghetti and cook on a medium heat until al dente, following the instructions on the pack. Spaghetti should have a bite to it and not be overcooked. 3. Set aside some pasta cooked stock. Drain the spaghetti to a colander. Optionally if you want you may rinse them under cold running water. 4. Heat 1 tablespoon oil in a pan. Add half the garlic and saute until aromatic. 5. Add mushrooms and fry them on a high heat until tender yet crunchy. Transfer them to a plate. 6. Pour the rest of the oil to the pan and saute the rest of the garlic. 7. (optional) Add onions if using. Saute them until golden. 8. Pour the tomato puree, salt and then the chili flakes. Cook until the tomato sauce thickens and the raw flavor goes away. 9. Add herbs, red chili flakes, pepper and mushrooms. Saute for a minute. Turn off the heat. If using cream then stir in. 10. Add the cooked spaghetti and toss well. If it looks dry add in a bit more of oil or spaghetti cooked stock. 11. Serve mushroom spaghetti hot with Clear soup.

title: Muslim biryani
ingredients: 1 nutmeg, 4 green cardamoms, 0.375 tsp Pepper corn, 5 cloves 5 cloves (/ laung), 1 cinnamon stick, 0.5 tsp fennel seeds, 0.5 tsp stone flower, 13.5 mint leaves, 13.5 stems coriander leaves, 1.5 inches ginger, 3 pods garlic, 2.5 green chilies, 1 onion, 1 bay leaf, 1 black cardamom, 4 green cardamoms, 4 cloves 4 cloves (/ laung), 1 Cinnamon stick, 1 Star anise, 2 strands mace, 0.375 tsp shahi jeera, 0.5 kg chicken, 2 cups basmathi rice, 3.25 to 3 cups water, 3 tbsp Oil, Salt, 0.25 tsp turmeric, 0.25 tsp red chili powder, 1 tomato, 0.5 cup curd, 1 tsp rose water
instructions: 1. Slice onions and chop tomatoes, set them aside separately. Soak rice in water for atleast 20 minutes. 2. Marinate chicken or mutton with little lemon juice or curd, salt and turmeric. Set this aside to tenderize. If your mutton is not tender you can use meat tenderizer or set aside for about 1 hour to overnight. 3. Add pepper corn, cinnamon stick, cloves, nutmeg, fennel seeds, stone flower and cardamoms to a blender and make a fine powder. Keep this aside in a plate. 4. To the same jar, add onions, ginger, garlic, mint and coriander leaves, make a fine paste. 5. Heat a heavy bottom utensil, add bay leaf, cinnamon, cloves, star anise, mace, black and green cardamons, shahi jeera. 6. When they begin to splutter, add the mint and coriander paste. Fry till the paste begins to smell nice. It takes around 2 to 3 minutes 7. Add tomatoes, salt and turmeric. Fry or cover and cook till the tomatoes turn fully mushy. 8. Add chicken or mutton and fry on low flame for 3 to 5 minutes. 9. Add masala powder from step 3, and red chili powder. Fry for about 3 minutes on a low flame without burning. 10. Add curd and stir well. 11. Cover and cook for about 5 minutes for the chicken. If using mutton cook till soft and tender, it doesn’t get cooked well with rice later. you can even pressure cook mutton for 1 to 2 whistles at this stage. The oil begins to separate and the gravy thickens. We do not want watery gravy. 12. Soak rice for at least 20 mins. 13. Wash chicken or mutton under running water, drain off the water completely and marinate with little curd or lemon juice, salt and turmeric. Rub the meat with the marination for about 5 minutes keep this aside. 14. Chop tomatoes, slice onions, keep them aside for later use. 15. Pluck the mint and coriander leaves and wash in lot of water, set them to drain. 16. Powder all the ingredients mentioned under “To powder” in a blender. Transfer this to a plate and keep it for later use. 17. Heat a heavy bottom pan, add spices mentioned under “To temper”. Let them crackle. 18. Add the onion mint paste to the pan and fry for about 2 to 3 minutes 19. Add tomatoes, turmeric and salt. Cook or fry till the tomatoes turn soft. If needed you can cover the pot and make the tomatoes soft. 20. Add chicken or mutton and fry on a low heat for 3 to 4 minutes. 21. Add powdered masala and red chili powder and fry till it becomes fragrant 22. Pour curd and mix. Cook until the meat turns tender, if mutton you can pressure cook for 1 to 2 whistles till it turns tender and soft. Cook till the oil begins to separate and the gravy thickens. 23. Pour 3 ¼ cups water and add salt 24. When the water begins to boil rapidly, drain off the water from the rice completely otherwise the rice turns mushy. Add the rice to the water and cook till it is 75% cooked. If the water is not sufficient, you can sprinkle hot water and stir. 25. Serve muslim biryani with onion raita and a salan.

title: Mutton Biryani | Lamb Biryani
ingredients: 0.5 kg lamb, 1 tsp red chili powder, 1 tsp biryani masala powder, 1 tbsp ginger garlic paste, 0.25 tsp turmeric powder, 0.5 cup curd, 1.5 tbsp lemon juice, 1 tbsp oil, 0.5 tsp Salt, 2 tbsps raw papaya paste, 2 cup basmati rice, 8 cups water, 1 bay leaf, 0.5 tsp shahi jeera, 3 cloves 3 cloves (or laung (optional)), 1 cinnamon, 3 green cardamom, 3 tbsp Oil, 1 bay leaves, 0.75 tsp shahi jeera, 4 green cardamoms, 1 star anise, 2 inch cinnamon, 8 cloves 8 cloves ( or laung), 1 cup onions, 2 green chilies, 4 tbsp coriander, 4 tbsp mint leaves, 0.5 cup yogurt, 1 pinch saffron
instructions: 1. Marinate mutton with red chili powder, biryani masala, yogurt, ginger garlic, turmeric, lemon juice, oil and salt. 2. Refrigerate for at least 4 hours. Overnight is best. 3. If you are using raw papaya paste, you can just set aside for 1 hour & begin to prepare. 4. Wash rice and soak for 30 to 40 minutes. 5. Drain it completely to a colander and set aside. 6. Heat a heavy bottom pan or a pressure cooker with oil. Add the whole spices. 7. Skip this section if using store bought fried onions. 8. Add onions, green chilies and fry till they turn brown almost about to caramelize. 9. Keep stirring to prevent burning. This is crucial to get a good aroma and taste. 10. There must be enough oil for the onions to fry. Set aside half of the fried onions for garnish. 11. Reduce the flame to low. Add the marinated meat. 12. Add half of the coriander and mint leaves saute for 3 to 4 mins. 13. Cook covered on a low flame till the mutton turns soft and tender. Or cook for 2 to 3 whistles in a pressure cooker. Turn off the stove. 14. If cooking in the pan without pressure cooker, stir occasionally to prevent burning. Remember the flame has to be very low otherwise mutton may turn hard. 15. While the meat is cooking, Bring water to a boil in a large pot. Add the whole spices and salt. Make sure the water is slightly salty. 16. When the water comes to a boil, add the drained rice. 17. Cook until 90% done or aldente. When you bite the grain , it must be cooked completely but you must be able to feel the bite into it. Drain it off to a colander. 18. Soak saffron in little milk and set aside. 19. When the pressure goes off, open the lid. You will be left with a thick gravy. Mutton must be cooked completely now. Check it , if still undone. Cook until it is soft and tender. 20. Add the rest of the half cup curd to the cooked mutton. 21. Next add few chopped coriander and mint leaves. 22. Few fried onions. Mix everything well. 23. You can check the salt and spice at this stage and adjust. 24. Layer the rice evenly over the cooked lamb meat. 25. Sprinkle some chopped coriander, mint leaves, fried onions and tbsp of ghee. 26. Lastly pour the saffron milk. 27. Cover the rim of the biryani pot with a moist thick clean cloth or foil. 28. Place a heavy lid over it. If you do not have a heavy lid. Just place a bowl filled with water over the lid. You can also seal it with atta. 29. Heat a old tawa until very hot. Place this biryani pot over the hot tawa and cook on a low flame for 15 mins. 30. Allow to rest for at least 15 mins. 31. Serve mutton biryani with raita.

title: Mutton Curry Recipe (Mutton Masala Gravy)
ingredients: 0.5 kg mutton, 2 tablespoons yogurt, 0.75 tablespoon ginger garlic paste, 0.75 teaspoon salt, 0.25 teaspoon turmeric, 1 teaspoon meat masala, 0.5 teaspoon Kashmiri red chili powder, 2 tablespoons oil, 3 green cardamoms, 1 inch cinnamon, 1 cup onions, 0.75 cup tomatoes, 1.5 green chilies, 2 teaspoons meat masala, 2 sprigs curry leaves, 0.5 teaspoon salt, 1.5 tablespoons coriander leaves, 0.75 cup water, 2.25 cups water
instructions: 1. Marinate mutton with salt, ginger garlic, turmeric, garam masala or meat masala, chili powder and curd. Yogurt & ginger garlic paste are natural meat tenderizers so they make the mutton succulent. 2. Set aside until you prepare the rest or leave it in the refrigerator to marinate overnight. (When you are ready to cook, leave the cold mutton out on the counter for 30 mins to bring the meat closer to room temperature.) 3. On a low to medium flame, gently heat oil in a pan or pressure cooker. Add the whole spices - cinnamon, cardamoms and bay leaf (optional), followed by onions & green chilies. 4. Saute until onions turn golden. Lower the flame to very low as much as possible. Then transfer the marinated mutton and saute for 5 to 7 mins. 5. Stir in the meat masala & curry leaves (optional), followed by pureed tomatoes. 6. Increase the heat to medium and saute for another 5 to 7 mins, until the raw flavor of tomatoes has reduced. Pour hot water & mix well. 7. To cook in a regular pot: Bring it to a gentle boil and cook covered on a medium heat for 1 hour 30 minutes. Stir well every 12 to 15 minutes and pour more hot water in batches as needed. 8. Stovetop Pressure Cooker: Pressure cook on a medium heat for 4 to 5 whistles, depending on the kind or cooker, meat and size of the mutton pieces. 9. When the pressure drops, open the lid and mix well. If the curry is runny, cook/saute for a few more minutes to evaporate some of the moisture. Your mutton is cooked through when it is tender, flakes easily with a fork and falls off the bone easily. 10. Taste the curry and add more salt if needed. If you feel it is low on flavor, you may sprinkle little garam masala. Garnish with fine chopped coriander leaves. 11. Serve mutton curry hot with rice, tandoori roti, naan or plain paratha.

title: Mutton Fry | Mutton Sukka | Mutton Roast
ingredients: 3 grams mutton, 1 teaspoon oil, 0.25 cup onions, 1.5 teaspoon ginger garlic paste, 0.25 teaspoon salt, 0.25 teaspoon turmeric, 0.375 teaspoon red chili powder, 1 tablespoon meat masala, 0.25 cup water, 2 tablespoons curd, 1 tablespoon oil, 2 sprigs curry leaves, 2 green chilies, 1 clove garlic minced, 1 teaspoon meat masala, 0.25 teaspoon salt, 0.375 teaspoon crushed black pepper, 4 red chilies, 3 tablespoons coconut, 2 tablespoons coriander seeds, 2 inch cinnamon, 5 cloves 4 to 6 cloves, 6 green cardamoms, 1 teaspoon fennel seeds, 0.75 teaspoon cumin seeds
instructions: 1. To make this dish, we will need to dry roast & grind the spices. Alternately you can also use any other readymade meat masala or garam masala. Add the following spices to a pan and dry roast on a low flame until lightly aromatic: 2. 6 green cardamoms. Then add 1 teaspoon fennel seeds and ¾ teaspoon cumin. Roast for a min. Add 3 tablespoons coconut or poppy seeds. Turn off the heat & roast with the residual heat in the pan. Cool all these and make a fine powder. 3. Wash 300 grams boneless mutton (or 500 grams bone-in) and drain completely. Add ginger garlic paste, salt, turmeric and half of the masala powder prepared above. I use 1 tsp ghee for flavor and this is optional. You can also use ¼ to ½ teaspoon chili powder if you prefer the dish hot. If using any other masala powder please check the instructions on the pack. For most brands you can use about 3/4 to 1 tbsp masala powder. 4. Mix everything well. You can also use curd/ yogurt or any meat tenderizer. I have not used any here as I have used lamb meat which was tender. Marinate and refrigerate for 3 to 4 hours. I usually set aside for 30 mins and carry on. But it is highly suggested to use curd and marinate for 3 to 4 hrs if the meat used is not tender. 5. Pour water and mix. 6. Cook on a low heat for 10 mins and then on a medium heat until soft. You can do this in a pot or pressure cooker. I pressure cooked for 5 whistles, this may vary depending on your cooker. 7. Mutton must be cooked soft and must fall off the bone easily. If using boneless mutton then try to cut a piece with fork you must be able to do it easily. 8. Heat a pan with ghee or oil. If not going to consume right away then use oil. Add garlic (optional), green chilies and curry leaves. Saute well until all the 3 ingredients smell good. 9. Add pepper powder and the other half meat masala powder. If using ready made powder, take care not to use a lot. You can also skip using masala at this stage if you do not prefer. Stir the masala in ghee/oil. 10. Pour the cooked mutton along with the stock (mutton cooked water). 11. Add salt if needed. Mix and cook until the gravy thickens and masala clings on to the mutton. You can turn off the stove at any stage 12. Andhra mutton fry is made dry, all the masala clings to the mutton. Turn off the heat. If you like you can also add some chopped coriander leaves. Serve mutton fry with lemon juice sprinkled. It can be served with rice or roti or bread.

title: Mutton Kabab Recipe | Mutton Keema Kabab Recipe
ingredients: 4 grams mutton, 1 medium onion, 0.5 cup roasted besan, 0.5 cup bread crumbs, 1.5 tsp garam masala, 1 tsp coriander powder, 0.5 cup mint leaves, 1.5 green chilies, 0.75 tsp salt, 1.5 tbsp ginger garlic paste, 2 tbsp lemon juice, oil
instructions: 1. If using besan or gram flour roast it well on a medium flame until it turns fragrant. Set this aside. Cool this completely. You can skip this step by replacing gram flour with bread crumbs. 2. Add mutton to a mixing bowl. 3. Transfer the flour, bread crumbs, garam masala, coriander powder and salt. 4. Next also add in finely chopped mint, coriander leaves, ginger garlic paste and green chilies. 5. Pour lemon juice. 6. Mix everything well to make a dough like mixture. Make sure it is not soggy and is firm. If the mixture is soggy add in more bread or besan. 7. Cover and set aside for 30 mins to 1 hour. You can also refrigerate overnight. 8. Divide the dough to 12 portions and make balls. 9. Then flatten them to 1/3 inch kabab. Set them aside in a plate. 10. Deep fry them in hot oil until golden. Regulate the flame to medium after half done. When golden remove to a kitchen tissue. 11. These can be grilled over the charcoal fire or grilled in oven at 240 C for 25 mins on each side. To grill in oven place them on a greased tray. Brush them lightly with oil. 12. Serve mutton kabab with mint chutney or with biryani rice.

title: Mutton Korma (Goat Korma)
ingredients: 1.1 lbs mutton, 1 cup yogurt, 1 tablespoon ginger garlic paste, 0.625 teaspoon sea salt, 1.25 teaspoon Kashmiri red chili powder, 1 tablespoon coriander powder, 0.25 teaspoon turmeric, 1 green chili, 5 cloves 4 to 6 cloves ((laung)), 2 inch cinnamon piece, 6 green cardamoms, 2 black cardamoms, 0.5 teaspoon black pepper, 2 cups sliced onions store bought fried onions, 0.29166666666666663 cup ghee to shallow fry, 0.875 cup water, 0.125 teaspoon mace, 0.125 teaspoon nutmeg, 0.25 teaspoon cardamom, 0.625 teaspoon kewra water
instructions: 1. To a large heavy bottom pot or Dutch oven, add mutton, cloves, cinnamon, black cardamom, green cardamoms, black pepper, salt, turmeric, red chili powder, coriander powder, green chili, ginger garlic paste and yogurt . 2. Mix well to marinate. Cover and rest aside until your fried onions are ready. Alternately you may refrigerate overnight. (Bring it close to room temperature, place on the counter for 1½ hours before cooking) 3. Heat ghee in a wide heavy bottom pan. Separate the layers of onions and slide them to the ghee. Saute on a medium heat for 7 mins, lower the flame and saute for another 4 to 5 mins, until golden. Remove to a plate, retaining most of the ghee in the pan. 4. Cool these completely and pulse them coarsely in a small grinder with a few tablespoons water or yogurt. (if your grinder is unable to grind, add more liquid) 5. Make sure your mutton is close to room temperature before you begin to cook. Add 3 tablespoons ghee/oil to the marinade. (Use the leftover shallow fried onion ghee and add more to top up if required.) 6. Begin to saute/braise the mutton on a medium heat for about 12 to 15 mins. Yogurt and mutton lets out lot of moisture. Cover and let the mutton cook in this for 30 mins. 7. Add the crushed onions and pour hot water. Mix well and cook covered on a low heat stirring every 10 mins, until mutton turns tender to your liking. It takes me another 1¼ hours for tender and fall off the bone meat. (add more hot water as required while you cook). Turn off the heat. 8. Stir in mace, nutmeg, cardamom powder and kewra water. Taste test to adjust salt. 9. Mutton korma tastes best if you let it rest for at least an hour. Serve with naan, roti, paratha, sheermal and rice varieties.

title: Mysore Bonda Recipe
ingredients: 1 cup wheat flour, 0.25 cup rice flour, 0.5 teaspoon baking soda, 0.5 teaspoon salt, 0.5 cup curd, 3.5 tablespoons water, 0.5 teaspoon cumin seeds, 1 tablespoon onions, 1.5 green chili, 0.375 teaspoon ginger, 2 tablespoons coriander leaves, oil
instructions: 1. Add 1 cup flour to a mixing bowl. I have used atta, but you can also use maida. Please note that the flavor of atta is prominent in these bondas. We like it so I use atta. 2. Add rice flour and baking soda. 3. Add salt. I added half tsp sea salt. 4. Add cumin seeds, green chili and ginger. 5. Add finely chopped onions or coconut and chopped coriander leaves. 6. Mix them up once. Add curd. 7. Pour water. 8. Make a thick batter. Sprinkle more water or curd as needed. 9. It should have a dropping consistency (not flowing). Keep the batter aside and heat up the oil in a deep pan to fry. 10. To check if the oil is hot enough, drop a small portion (1 tsp) of batter. It should sizzle and steadily rise to the surface and not sink. Regulate the flame to medium high (not low or too high) when the oil is hot enough. Take a portion of the batter to your hand, shape small portions, about 1 tbsp batter each and gently leave it to the hot oil. 11. They will puff up in few seconds. Keep stirring and fry on a medium flame. 12. Mysore bonda takes little longer to fry than other fritters. Fry them until crisp and golden. 13. Remove them to a cooling rack/ steel colander or kitchen papers. Mysore bonda is best served hot with a chutney. We served with this Onion Tomato Chutney . 14. Egg Puff Recipe Diwali Snacks Rec... Maddur Vada Recip... Churumuri Recipe ... Gobi 65 (Cauliflo... Punugulu Recipe (...

title: Nankhatai Recipe
ingredients: 0.75 cup wheat flour, 0.25 cup besan, 1 tablespoon semolina, 0.25 teaspoon baking powder, 3 green cardamoms, 0.5 cup powdered sugar, 0.3333333333333333 cup ghee
instructions: 1. Preheat the oven to 360 F or 180 C at least for 15 mins. 2. Prepare a tray with parchment paper. Powder sugar along with cardamom seeds. 3. Sieve the flours, semolina, sugar and baking soda together. Set this aside. 4. You can also just mix up all of them together and skip sieving. Ensure the baking powder is mixed well. 5. Pour ghee in parts and bring the flour together to make a stiff dough. 6. Use ghee only as needed for mixing. You may need a tsp more or less depending on the temperature where you live. Do not knead the dough. 7. Make 12 balls out of the dough. Slightly flatten and press with a fork lightly to make a design. 8. Bake exactly for 10 mins. Or until light golden and crisp. The timing may vary depending on many factors - size of your oven, kind of the tray etc. 9. Remove to a wire rack immediately. Cool the nankhatai completely and store in an airtight jar. They keep good for 3 weeks. 10. Enjoy nankhatai as a snack with milk or tea.

title: Navratan Korma Recipe (Navratan Curry)
ingredients: 1 cup onions, 13 cashew nuts, 1.5 green chilli, 0.5 cup peas, 0.5 cup carrots, 0.75 cup cauliflower, 0.75 cup potato, 0.5 cup beans, 2 tablespoons oil, 1 bay leaf, 1 inch cinnamon piece, 2 cloves 2 cloves ((optional)), 0.5 teaspoon cumin seeds, 2 green cardamoms, 0.625 teaspoon ginger paste, 0.5 teaspoon red chili powder, 1 pinch turmeric, 0.375 teaspoon garam masala, 0.75 teaspoon coriander powder, 0.5 teaspoon salt, 1 cup water, 0.75 cup paneer, 6 cashew nuts, 6 Almonds, 0.25 cup pomegranate arils, 0.3333333333333333 cup pineapple chunks, 1 tablespoon raisins, 0.25 cup cream, 0.5 teaspoon kewra water
instructions: 1. Bring 3 cups of water to a rolling boil in a pot. Add onions and cashews to it and boil for 3 mins until the raw smell of the onions has gone. Turn off the stove. 2. Remove the onions and cashews with a slotted spoon. Set them aside to cool and later grind it along with green chili to a smooth paste in a grinder jar. 3. To the same hot water, add the cauliflower florets and leave them for 1 minute. Later drain the water and rinse them under running water. This blanching is done to get rid of any worms in the florets so you may skip this step if not required. Set aside. 4. Heat 1 teaspoon oil or butter in a pan. Add cashews and almonds. Fry until light golden and then add raisins. As soon as the raisins swell remove all of the nuts and raisins to a small plate. 5. To the same pan, add paneer and fry for 2 mins. Do not over fry they turn hard and chewy. If you want soft paneer, Keep them immersed in little warm water until we use later. 6. There are 2 ways you can prepare the veggies. The first method is to immerse all the veggies in 1.5 cups of water and boil them until fork tender. 7. The other method is to steam them. For this bring 2 cups of water to a boil in a steamer. Place the potatoes in a steam basket and stem until half done. Later add the rest of the veggies and steam together until just cooked. Do not over cook them. 8. On a low flame, heat oil or butter in a pan. Add cinnamon, cumin, bay leaf, cardamom and cloves to the hot oil. 9. When they begin to sizzle Add ginger garlic paste and fry until the raw smell has gone. 10. Then add the ground onion cashew paste and cook until it turns thick to a mass and begins to leave the pan, meaning the paste is cooked well. 11. Add the ground spices - chili powder, garam masala, turmeric and coriander powder along with salt. 12. Saute until oil begins to separate from the mixture. 13. Pour ¾ to 1 cup water and bring to a boil. Reduce the heat and simmer until slightly thick. Taste test and add more salt if required. 14. Add all the steamed veggies and cook covered for 2 mins. Pour cream and stir. 15. Add fried paneer and nuts. Turn off the heat and add kewra water or rose water if using. If using pineapple cool down the navratan korma slightly and add the pineapple chunks. 16. Garnish navaratan korma with coriander, pomegranate arils and raisins.

title: Neer Dosa Recipe (Mangalorean Rice Crepes)
ingredients: 1 cup rice, 0.3333333333333333 teaspoon salt as needed, 1.5 to 1 cups water, 1 teaspoon Oil
instructions: 1. Wash rice a few times until the water runs clear. Soak it in water for at least 4 hours to overnight. 2. Drain the water and rinse well if soaked for over night. 3. Add the rice to a blender jar and pour ¾ to 1 cup water. Blend until smooth. Transfer to a batter bowl. 4. Add salt and another ½ cup water. Mix well and check the consistency. It has to be like thin or watery. Add more water if needed. (Check video for consistency) 5. Heat a pan and drizzle few drops of oil. Using a kitchen tissue or a piece of onion smear the oil all over the pan. Wipe off excess oil with a kitchen tissue. 6. When the pan is hot enough, stir the batter well from the bottom. 7. Take a ladle full of batter and pour it over the tawa in a circle. Tilt the pan to spread the excess batter all over. Do not spread the batter like regular dosa batter. 8. Fill any gaps in the dosa to make it even. 9. Cover it with a lid and cook for 2 to 3 mins until the top part turns non sticky meaning it is cooked. 10. Continue to cook until you see the sides come off easily from the pan. 11. Take the dosa to a plate and allow to cool for 30 secs to a min and then fold it to halve and then to triangle. Serve neer dosa with chutney or curry. 12. To make next dosa, repeat greasing the pan with oil and then wipe off the excess. 13. Stir the batter every time you make a fresh neer dosa as the botton part of the batter will be thick.

title: Ney Choru | Nei Choru
ingredients: 1.5 cups rice, 2.5 cups water, 0.25 cup ghee, 0.5 cup onions, 0.25 cup onions, 0.75 tsp ginger paste, 0.375 cup cashews, 0.25 cup raisins, 1 star anise, 0.5 tsp saunf, 1 bay leaf, 4 cloves 4 cloves (/ laung), 2 inch cinnamon sticks, Salt, Green chilies
instructions: 1. Wash rice a few times and drain. If using jeerakasala rice, soaking is not needed. If using basmati soak it for about 30 mins. Drain completely. 2. Make a paste of ginger and if desired you can also crush green chili along with it. Slice the onions. 3. Bring 2 ½ cups water to a boil. Meanwhile carry on with the next steps. 4. Heat ghee and fry the cashews. When they are about to be golden, add raisins and saute until they swell up. Remove them to a plate. 5. In the same pan, fry onions stirring continuously until golden. Transfer these as well to a plate. Fried onions, cashews and raisins are for garnish. 6. In the same pot or pan, add the spices and saute until they turn fragrant. Take care not to burn. 7. Add onions and fry until golden. 8. Add ginger paste and saute just for a while. 9. Add drained rice and fry for 2 to 3 mins on a medium flame. Be gentle while stirring else the rice the break. 10. Add salt and pour the hot water. 11. Stir gently and check salt. If needed add more. 12. Regulate the flame to medium. 13. Cover and cook until all the water is absorbed. It takes about 5 to 8 mins depending on the kind of pot used. 14. When the water is absorbed completely fluff up the rice. If the rice is under cooked, sprinkle some water and cook. 15. Switch off and keep the rice covered for another 5 mins. 16. Garnish ney choru with fried onions, cashews and raisins.

title: Nihari Recipe
ingredients: 2.5 bay leaves, 1.5 tablespoon coriander seed, 1 teaspoon black pepper, 3 inches Ceylon cinnamon piece, 6 cloves 6 cloves, 1 black cardamom, 8 green cardamom, 0.75 teaspoon shahi jeera, 0.75 teaspoon fennel seeds, 1 star anise, 0.25 teaspoon nutmeg, 2 strands mace, 0.5 teaspoon all-spice, 0.75 inch long pepper, 0.75 gram stone flower, 1.5 tablespoons rose petals, 0.5 inch galangal, 5.5 vetiver roots khus, 0.5 kg bone-in mutton, 0.25 cup ghee, 2 cups onions, 0.75 tablespoon ginger, 0.75 tablespoon garlic, 1.5 teaspoon Kashmiri red chili powder, 0.5 teaspoon turmeric, 1.5 teaspoon sea salt, 0.25 cup wheat flour, 5.5 cups water, 0.625 cup yogurt, 1 inch ginger julienned, 0.25 cup coriander leaves, 2 green chilies, 1 lemon
instructions: 1. Add all the whole spices except rose petals to a pan. Toast them on a low heat for 3 to 5 mins, until aromatic & crunchy. Transfer to a plate to cool down. Add rose petals and toast just until slightly hot. 2. Add the whole spices to a grinder jar and make a fine powder. Reserve 2 tablespoons of this powder in a small bowl. Add the rose petals and grind to a fine powder. Keep covered until required. 3. Pour oil to a deep pot and saute onions until light golden. Add the mutton and saute on medium for 6 to 7 mins. 4. Stir in the ginger garlic and saute for 1 minute until the raw smell goes away. Turn down the heat, add turmeric, red chili, salt and half of the nihari masala. 5. Pour hot water (not boiling) and give a good stir. Bring to a gentle boil and cover. Simmer on a low heat, until the meat is tender and falls off the bone easily. The time taken depends on the heat level. (about 1 hour 45 mins on a medium heat or 2 hours 45 mins on low heat) 6. Add flour, rest of the nihari masala, yogurt, half of the water to a small bowl. Mix well to make a smooth slurry, adding more water. Pour this gently into the nihari, stirring constantly to prevent lumps. 7. Bring it to a boil. Cover and simmer for 30 mins, until thick, yet of pouring consistency or traces of fats are visible over the nihari. If it is runny, cook on a high heat uncovered to evaporate little liquid. 8. Taste test and add salt. Garnish with chopped chilies, ginger juliennes and chopped coriander. 9. Squeeze in some lemon juice and serve with Tandoori roti, Naan or any rustic or soft breads.

title: Nippattu
ingredients: 1 cup rice flour, 3 tbsp peanuts, 3 tbsp fried gram, 1 tbsp coconut, 1 tbsp sesame seeds, 0.625 tsp red chilli powder, 0.5 tsp salt, 2 sprigs curry leaves, 1 tbsp oil, 0.25 cup water, 1 tbsp maida, 1 tbsp chiroti rava
instructions: 1. Dry roast peanuts lightly until the skin begins to come off. You can cool and deskin them, I went ahead without removing the skin. Do not deep roast the peanuts as they burn when deep fried. Coconut is another optional ingredients. 2. Pluse all the 3 to a coarse mixture in a mixer. It is good to have some split peanuts here and there. 3. Add rice flour to a mixing bowl. If you prefer then use chiroti rava (optional). 4. Next add 1 tbsp maida. This is optional. 5. Add the crushed peanuts & sesame seeds. 6. Then add salt, red chilli powder and curry leaves. 7. Mix all of them. Heat 1 tbsp oil in a kadai until very hot. Pour this to the flour. 8. The oil must sizzle. This will make them crispy. 9. Dry roast peanuts lightly until the skin begins to peel off. Cool them and pulse twice in a blender along with dried coconut and fried gram. The mixture must be coarse with some split peanuts here and there. 10. Add rice flour to a large mixing bowl. Also add crushed peanut powder, sesame seeds, curry leaves, red chili powder and salt. If using rava and maida you can add them as well. 11. Mix up everything well. 12. Heat 1 tbsp oil until very hot in a kadai. Pour this to the flour mixture. The oil must sizzle when it is added. 13. Pour ¼ cup water and mix to make a non sticky stiff dough without kneading. If needed sprinkle more water as needed. 14. After making about 2 to 3, begin to heat oil in a kadai or deep pan on a medium flame. 15. Regulate the flame to medium high. Gently remove and drop nippattu in hot oil. You can fry about 2 to 3 in each batch. 16. Avoid disturbing them for a minute. When firm and golden, flip and fry on the other side until golden and crisp. The bubbles will reduce when they are done. 17. Remove them to a mess basket or colander. Avoid tissue. Continue making and frying all of them.

title: Oatmeal Pancakes (Oat Pancakes)
ingredients: 1.5 cups oats flour, 0.25 tsp salt, 1.5 tsp baking powder, 0.125 tsp cinnamon, 2 eggs, 1 cup milk, 1.5 tbsp melted butter, 1 tsp vanilla extract, 1 tbsp sugar, 0.25 cup Chocolate syrup
instructions: 1. Add little over 1 ½ cup oats to a blender. You can use any kind of oats. I used organic instant oats. 2. Powder them as finely as possible. 3. Measure 1 ½ cups oats flour, cinnamon (optional),baking powder and salt. 4. Mix everything well. 5. Add eggs, melted butter, milk and sugar to a bowl. Any milk can be used. I accidentally added soft butter here. But please note that for better texture, butter has to be melted and then added. Also do not use cold milk. Butter will clump up together when added to cold milk. 6. Whisk everything well. 7. Add the flour to this. 8. Mix just until well combined. The batter is of a thick pouring consistency. 9. Grease a pan and heat it. Pour a ladle full of batter. Do not spread it much. 10. Drizzle little oil or butter. Allow to cook on a medium heat until the base is firm and done. 11. Flip it and cook on the other side. Serve with chocolate syrup or maple syrup. 12. homemade chocolate syrup recipe Ingredients ¾ cup palm sugar or palm jaggery or dark jaggery ¼ cup cocoa 1 tsp vanilla extract ¾ cup water Method. Mix all the ingredients. If using jaggery, you may need to filter it. Boil for 8 to 9 minutes or until it thickens. Cool and store it in a glass bottle. Use it as a topping on pancakes, poke cakes, ice creams or to make chocolate milk. 13. Oats Recipes f... Oats Upma Recipe Oats idli Oats Dosa Recipe Oats Chilla Recip...

title: Oatmeal Soup
ingredients: 0.25 cup rolled oats, 1.5 cup vegetables, 1 tbsp butter, 1 tsp garlic, 1 tsp ginger, 0.375 pepper, 0.25 tsp red chili flakes, 1.5 to 2 cups stock, 0.5 tsp herbs, Salt, Lemon juice
instructions: 1. Melt butter and saute garlic until it turns aromatic. You can also use ginger if you like the flavor. 2. Add oats and saute for a while. If using steel cut oats, saute for about 3 mins or until they begin to smell nutty. 3. Add all the mixed veggies and saute for 2 to 3 mins. 4. Pour stock/broth or water as needed. 5. Cook it on a medium heat until the veggies are tender. We don’t need to add any thickeners here since oats makes the soup slightly thicker. Add more liquid if required to bring it to a consistency. 6. Add herbs and pepper. You can also use fresh herbs. I used mixed italian herbs. 7. Add salt as needed. Switch off. Add red chili flakes now or while serving. Serve oats soup warm. We prefer to add some lemon juice just before serving.

title: Oats Chilla Recipe
ingredients: 1 cup oats, 0.25 cup besan, 1.25 water, 0.625 teaspoon cumin seeds, 0.5 teaspoon salt, 0.125 teaspoon turmeric, 0.5 cup carrot, 2 tablespoons onion, 1.5 green chilies, 0.5 teaspoon ginger, 2 tablespoons coriander leaves, 1 tablespoon oil
instructions: 1. Add oats to a grinder jar and made a slightly coarse powder. If the powder is too coarse the chillas may break. 2. To a mixing bowl, transfer the ground oats, gram flour, salt, turmeric, green chilies, cumin, ginger, carrot, onions & coriander leaves. 3. Pour 1 cup water and mix to a lump free batter. Pour another ¼ cup water and mix to make a thick batter. It must be of pourable and spreading consistency. 4. Heat a pan on a medium heat. If using a cast iron pan, Once the pan is hot enough drizzle few drops of oil and spread with a tissue or cloth. Let it heat up well. 5. When the pan is hot enough, stir the batter well and pour ¼ cup of batter in the center of the pan. 6. Spread it gently to a slightly thin pancake of about 6 inches. Drizzle half tsp to tbsp oil or ghee around the edges. 7. Let it cook on a medium heat until the base is set and cooked well. 8. Turn it to the other side and cook pressing down with the spatula. You will see brown to golden spots when the oats chilla is cooked. 9. If you prefer crisp chilla, cook them on a low heat for a minute or 2. Remove to serving plate. 10. To make the next chilla, stir the batter well and make sure the pan is hot enough but not very hot. Also the batter thickens as it rests, you can splash 1 to 2 tbsps of water if required as you make them. 11. Serve oats chilla warm or hot with chutney.

title: Oats Chivda Recipe (Oats Mixture)
ingredients: 0.75 cup rolled oats, 1 cup poha, 1.25 tablespoon oil, 3 tablespoons peanuts, 0.5 teaspoon mustard seeds, 0.5 teaspoon cumin seeds, 1 red chili, 1 sprig curry leaves, 1.5 green chilies, 3.5 garlic cloves, 2.5 tablespoons fried garm, 2 tablespoons coconut thinly sliced chopped, 0.5 teaspoon salt, 0.5 teaspoon red chili powder, 0.1875 teaspoon turmeric, 1 pinch asafoetida
instructions: 1. Dry roast oats until slightly golden. They will not turn crisp. Set them aside to a plate. 2. Next roast poha in the same pan. Keep stirring and roast until crisp and light. Set them aside. 3. Heat oil and crackle mustard, cumin and red chili. Roast peanuts until golden & aromatic. 4. Add garlic, green chili and curry leaves. 5. When the garlic begins to smell good. add coconut and fried gram. 6. Fry until the coconut smells good. 7. Add turmeric, chili powder and salt. Switch off the stove. 8. Transfer poha and oats. Mix everything well. 9. When the mixture cools down, add more salt if needed and pour little coconut or olive oil. Toss them. 10. Store oats chivda in a air tight jar and use within 2 months.

title: Oats Dosa Recipe
ingredients: 0.5 cup oats, 0.25 cup rice flour, 0.25 cup semolina, 0.5 teaspoon Salt, 2 green chili, 0.5 teaspoon cumin seeds, 2 tablespoon onions, 1 sprig curry leaves, 0.5 teaspoon ginger, 0.25 cup curd, 1.25 to 1 cups water, 2 tablespoon Oil
instructions: 1. Add half cup oats to a grinder jar and make a fine powder. 2. Transfer it to a mixing bowl along with salt, rice flour, semolina, cumin, curry leaves, ginger, green chilies, chopped onions and coriander leaves. 3. Add curd (optional) and pour 1¼ cup water. Mix all of them together to get a free flowing runny batter. 4. The consistency must be similar to that of the rava dosa batter. 5. If required add more water to get the required consistency. Keep this aside for 10 to 15 mins. 6. If using a non stick pan you can straight away use it. If using a cast iron pan, heat it first until hot enough. Drizzle a few drops of oil and spread it with a kitchen tissue or a cut onion. Heat this well until smoky hot. When you see the smoke turn off. This step creates a non-stick effect on your cast iron pan. Wipe off the excess oil with kitchen tissues or a cloth. 7. Just before you begin to make the dosas, check and adjust the consistency if needed by pouring little more water. 8. Heat the griddle until moderately hot enough. To check you can drizzle a teaspoon of batter on the pan, it should sizzle immediately. This means the pan is hot enough. 9. Stir the batter with a deep ladle as the solids settle at the bottom. Regulate the flame to medium, take a ladle full of batter and begin to pour from the sides /edges across the pan and then in the center. 10. Do not spread the batter. Just pour it and fill up the gaps. 11. Drizzle 1 teaspoon ghee or oil all over including the edges. 12. Cook the oats dosa on a medium heat until it turns golden on the base. 13. When it is cooked well, the edges will begin to leave the pan. Flip it to the other side and cook for 1 minute. If you have a trouble making these, then refer my pro tips mentioned in the post. 14. Again flip it back and cook until crisp and golden. Remove oats dosa to a plate and serve with chutney. 15. To make the next dosa, the pan has to be hot enough. if needed you can add few drops of oil and rub with a cut onion. Then make the next oats dosa. 16. Also stir the batter well before you pour it to the pan, as the solids settle at the bottom. 17. If you have some left over plain dosa batter, then powder some oats. For 1 cup dosa batter add ¼ cup powdered rolled or quick cook oats. 18. Stir the powder in a small bowl along with little water. Mix to make a lump free batter. 19. Mix the oats batter with dosa batter. 20. Bring it to thick dosa batter consistency by pouring more water if needed. 21. Heat a greased tawa and pour the batter spreading it well to make a thin and crisp dosas like we spread the traditional dosa. 22. Pour 1 teaspoon oil around the edges. Flip it and fry the oats dosa on the other side as well.

title: Oats Egg Omelet
ingredients: 2 eggs, 0.25 cup oats, 3.5 tablespoons milk, 0.25 teaspoon salt, 1 pinch turmeric, 0.25 teaspoon oregano, 0.25 teaspoon black pepper, 1.5 to 2 teaspoon oil, 2 tablespoon onions, 2 tablespoon carrot, 2 tablespoon capsicum, 2 tablespoon tomato, 2 green chilies, 1 tablespoon coriander leaves
instructions: 1. Wash and chop veggies as needed. I use 2 tbsp onions, 2 tbsp capsicum, 2 tbsp grated carrot, 2 tbsp deseeded and chopped firm tomato. 2 green chilies chopped, cilantro (coriander leaves) chopped finely. You can also use spring onions and cabbage. Prepare and keep these aside. 2. To a bowl add oat flour, salt, turmeric, pepper and oregano or your preferred seasoning. To make oat flour process ¼ cup oats to a fine flour in a mixer or processor. You will get around ¼ cup (heaped) flour. 3. Add 3 tbsp milk to the flour and make a lump free batter. If you follow the ayurvedic food combining rules, then egg and milk do not go well as they are not compatible with each other. So you can use plant milk. Oat milk goes very well for this. 4. Break 2 eggs to the bowl. 5. Beat them all together with a fork until lightly frothy. The mixture must be of a pouring consistency. If your eggs are very small, you may need to add in 1 to 2 tbsp. more milk. 6. Add 1½ tsp oil, butter or ghee to a pan. Heat it. 7. Set the flame to medium. When the pan is hot enough, pour the egg mixture. 8. Mix all the veggies and layer them. Gently press the veggies with a spatula. 9. When the base is firm enough, flip it. Cook until the other side is fully cooked. 10. If you like you can sprinkle pepper and chaat masala. Serve oats egg omelette hot. 11. Oats Recipes f... How to Cook Oatme... Masala Oats Recip... Oats Chilla Recip... Savory Steel Cut ... Oatmeal pancakes ...

title: Oats idli
ingredients: 1 cup oats, 0.5 cup rava, 0.25 cup curd, 1 cup water, 0.5 teaspoon salt, 0.5 tablespoon lemon juice, 0.5 teaspoon soda-bi-carbonate, 2 tablespoon carrot, 3 tablespoon water as needed for consistency, 0.25 teaspoon mustard seeds, 0.5 teaspoon cumin seeds, 1 teaspoon urad dal, 0.5 teaspoon channa dal, 0.5 teaspoon ginger, 4 cashews, 1.5 tablespoon oil, 1 pinch hing, 1 green chili, 6 curry leaves, 2 tablespoon coriander leaves
instructions: 1. Add oats to a blender or grinder and make a coarse powder. Set aside. 2. Heat oil or ghee in a pan. If using cashews (optional), fry them until golden. Remove and set aside. Add mustard, cumin, chana dal and urad dal. 3. Fry until the dals turn golden. Then add curry leaves, green chilies, hing and ginger. Saute for 30 seconds. 4. Pour rava and fry until aromatic. Add powdered oats. 5. Fry until the mixture turns aromatic. Add salt and mix. Cool this completely. 6. Once cool, pour water and curd to it. Mix to make a thick batter. Set this aside for 5 to 8 mins. 7. Heat a steamer with 2 cups water or as required. Meanwhile drizzle a few drops of oil over the idli plates and grease them. Place fried cashews optionally. 8. After 10 mins, the batter turns slightly thick. At this stage add lemon juice and more water if needed to bring it to a consistency like that of idli batter. 9. The batter must be thick and not of pouring consistency. But must be smooth and not lumpy. 10. Add lemon juice, carrots, coriander leaves. Mix a good mix. 11. Make sure the water is getting hot. Then sprinkle half teaspoon soda or 1 teaspoon fruit salt - eno to the batter. Gently mix it. Do not over mix. 12. Fill the greased plates with batter. This must go right away for steaming. Delaying will make the idlis dense. 13. When the water in the steamer begins to boil rapidly, place the oats idli stand in the cooker and steam for about 11 minutes. Switch off the heat. 14. If cooked properly then a skewer inserted in comes clean or with slight crumbs. 15. Allow them to cool down a bit. Remove them with the help of a spoon. 16. Serve oats idli hot with a tomato chutney or idli-sambar.

title: Oats Khichdi Recipe
ingredients: 0.5 cup moong dal, 1 cup rolled oats, 1 tomato, 0.5 cup carrots, 0.5 cup green peas, 0.3333333333333333 cup green beans, 0.25 teaspoon red chilli powder, 4 cups water, 2 tablespoon ghee, 1 bay leaf, 0.5 teaspoon cumin seeds, 1 teaspoon ginger, 1 green chili, 0.5 teaspoon salt, 0.125 teaspoon turmeric, 1 pinch asafoetida
instructions: 1. Rinse moong dal well a few times. Drain the water. 2. Rinse and chop tomato, carrot, green beans & green chili. Grate ginger. 3. Heat a pot or pressure cooker on a mdeium heat. When the ghee melts, add cumin seeds and bay leaf. As soon as the seeds begin to sizzle, add ginger and green chili. 4. Quickly mix and add hing, carrots, beans and peas. Saute for 1 to 2 minutes. Add tomatoes and saute for 2 minutes. Add salt and red chili powder. Give a good stir. 5. Pressure cooker: Add the drained lentils and oats. Pour water. Give a good stir. Cover and pressure cook for 3 whistles on a medium flame. Let the pressure release naturally. 6. Cooking in a pot: Add the drained lentils and pour water. Bring the water to a rolling boil and then simmer until the lentils are soft and tender. Keep stirring. When the lentils soften, add oats. If there is not enough water, pour some hot water. 7. Open the lid. Oats and lentils both must be softened and the texture has to be thick, mushy and of pouring consistency. I usually pour some boiling hot water if required at this stage and simmer for a minute becaue the khichdi becomes too thick as it cools down. 8. Taste test and add more salt if required and garnish with coriander leaves. Drizzle some ghee if you want. Squeeze some lemon juice and serve hot. 9. Perform all the same steps as done for the stovetop cooker. Pressure cook for 9 mins on porridge mode. Wait for the pressure to release naturally for 8 mins. Release the rest manually. Pour some hot boiling water for perfect consistency.

title: Oats Pongal
ingredients: 1 cup rolled oats, 0.5 cup moong dal, 2.25 cups water, 9 cashews, 0.5 teaspoon black pepper corn, 0.5 teaspoon salt, 1 sprig curry leaves, 1 teaspoon ginger, 0.125 teaspoon turmeric, 0.5 teaspoon cumin seeds, 2 tablespoon ghee, 0.125 teaspoon hing, 1 green chilli
instructions: 1. Dry roast oats in a pot or pressure cooker for 3 to 4 minutes and remove them to a bowl. This step helps to make less sticky oats pongal. 2. To the same pot or cooker, add moong dal and dry roast for 5 to 6 minutes on a medium heat until aromatic. This makes your oats pongal aromatic. 3. Pour water and rinse the dal well at least thrice. Pour 1½ cups water and pressure cook for 3 whistles. If cooking in a pot, cook until moong dal becomes soft and mushy. If needed add more water while you cook. 4. Once the pressure releases, open the lid and check. Dal has to be soft cooked. Pour ¾ to 1 cup hot water and add roasted oats. 5. Cover and cook on a low to medium heat until oats are softened. Rolled oats do not take too long to cook as they are pre-steamed. 6. Add salt as required and taste test it to adjust. 7. Heat a pan with ghee. Add ginger and green chilli (optional). Saute for 30 to 40 seconds, then add cumin seeds, cashew nuts, curry leaves and black pepper. 8. Fry until the cashews become light golden. Then add hing and turmeric. Turn off the stove. 9. Pour the tempering to the cooked oats and moong dal. Stir and cover for 5 minutes before serving. 10. Press the saute button and add ghee to the steel insert. Fry the cashews until golden and remove them to a bowl for garnishing later. 11. Add ginger, green chilies (optional), cumin seeds, black pepper and curry leaves. Saute until you begin to smell the curry leaves good. 12. Then add hing and oats. Roast for 2 to 3 mins. If the IP becomes too hot, press cancel in between and continue. 13. Add rinsed moong dal, turmeric and salt. Continue to sauté for 4 to 5 minutes. I found this step makes the dish aromatic and less sticky. 14. Press cancel. Pour 4 cups water and deglaze the pot. 15. Secure the instant pot with the lid. Press pressure cook and set the timer to 8 minutes. 16. After the IP beeps, wait for natural pressure release for 8 minutes. Release the rest of the pressure manually. 17. Stir oats pongal. I prefer to top with little ghee. Serve with papads, pickle or chutney.

title: Oats Scrambled Eggs
ingredients: 0.75 cup oats, 1.5 egg, 1 tbsp oil, 1 green chili, 1 tsp ginger, 0.5 tsp cumin, 0.25 cup vegetables, 1 pinch Turmeric, Salt, 1 tsp Lemon juice, 2 tbsps Coriander leaves, 0.25 tsp garam masala
instructions: 1. Crackle cumin in hot oil. Saute onions, ginger and green chilies. If using ginger garlic paste , fry the onions until golden and then saute ginger garlic paste until the raw smell goes away. 2. When the onions turn slightly golden or pink, then add mixed veggies. I used carrots, peas and corn. You can also use capsicum and beans. You can also skip the veggies. 3. Saute for a minute. Then cover and cook on a low flame until soft and tender. If needed you can also sprinkle little water and cook. 4. Add oats, salt, turmeric and garam masala (optional). I used quick cook oats. If using rolled oats, add them to a colander. Rinse them under running water. Drain completely. Then add to the pan. 5. Saute for 2 to 3 mins, they do not turn crunchy due to the moist veggies. 6. Mix everything well scrambling the egg. 7. Pour the egg to pan. If using quick oats, immediately sprinkle 3 to 4 tbsp water (about ¼ cup)evenly in the pan. Add more if it looks dry. You can also beat the egg and then add to the pan. Avoid sprinkling water if using rolled oats. 8. Cover and cook on a very low flame until the oats and egg are completely cooked. Just for 2 to 3 mins. Check by tasting little, oats must be soft now. If oats remain under cooked, you can also sprinkle little more water. 9. Sprinkle coriander leaves and lemon juice as needed. Lemon juice helps to remove the smell of egg, recommended for those new to eating eggs. 10. 1 green chili chopped 11. 1 tsp ginger chopped or ginger garlic paste 12. ¼ cup vegetables fine chopped mix 13. 2 tbsps Coriander leaves chopped finely 14. Heat oil in a pan until hot. Then add cumin and let it splutter. 15. Next stir fry the onions, ginger and green chili until the onions turn golden. 16. Next add the chopped vegetables and saute for a min. 17. Cook covered until tender. If needed sprinkle some water to help them cook. 18. Add in oats, salt, garam masala and turmeric. If using rolled oats, add them to a colander and rinse under running water. 19. Drain the water completely, then add to the pan. 20. Fry for 2 mins. Then beat the egg until frothy and pour it to the pan. 21. Sprinkle 3 to 4 tbsps of water if using quick oats. Avoid water if using rolled oats. 22. Scramble the egg. Cook covered on a low flame until oats and egg are cooked well. Check salt and add more if desired. 23. Add lemon juice and coriander leaves. Stir well and serve oats srambled eggs hot. 24. .kadence-column173268_d8ec65-ba > .kt-inside-inner-col,.kadence-column173268_d8ec65-ba >
