title: Oats Upma Recipe
ingredients: 1 cup rolled oats, 1 cup water, 1.5 tablespoons oil, 1 onion, 0.625 cup mix vegetables, 1.5 green chilies, 1.5 tablespoons lemon juice, 2 tablespoons coriander leaves, 0.5 teaspoon salt, 2 tablespoons coconut, 1 sprig curry leaves, 0.5 teaspoon ginger, 1 pinch hing, 0.25 teaspoon mustard seeds, 0.5 teaspoon cumin, 0.5 teaspoon chana dal, 0.5 teaspoon urad dal, 2 tablespoons peanuts, 0.25 teaspoon turmeric
instructions: 1. Dry roast 1 cup oats in a wide pan for about 5 minutes until crisp. They do not become very crisp. Set this aside to cool. 2. Heat a pan with 1½ tablespoon oil. Add ½ teaspoon cumin, ¼ teaspoon mustard seeds, 2 tablespoons peanuts, ½ teaspoon chana dal & ½ teaspoon urad dal. 3. Fry them stirring often till the peanuts turn golden and aromatic. 4. Next add 1 pinch hing, ½ teaspoon ginger, 1 to 2 slit green chilies & 1 sprig curry leaves. Fry all of them till the curry leaves turn crisp. 5. Then add 1 small chopped onions and saute until golden. 6. Optional - If using veggies. Then add ½ to ¾ cup vegetables and fry for 2 mins. 7. Sprinkle 2 tbsp water. Cover and cook until the veggies are slightly soft. 8. Next sprinkle some ½ teaspoon salt and ¼ teaspoon turmeric. 9. If using rolled oats then pour 1 cup water. or if using quick cook oats use ½ cup water. 10. Mix well and then taste the water. The water has to be slightly salty. If needed add more salt. Bring it to a boil. 11. Transfer the roasted oats & give a gentle mix to incorporate the water with the oats. 12. With in 1 to 2 mins the water will be absorbed. Cover and cook on a low flame for 2 mins or until all the moisture is evaporated. 13. Add 2 tablespoons chopped coriander leaves. Mix well. 14. When the temperature comes down, pour 1 tablespoon lemon juice. You can top it with some fresh grated coconut if you have. It tastes good as it is. 15. Oats upma is best served with yogurt.

title: Oats uttapam
ingredients: 0.5 cup oats, 0.25 cup rice flour, 2 tablespoons curd, salt as needed, water as needed, 2 tablespoons onions, 2 tablespoons carrots, 2 tablespoons tomatoes, 2 green chilies, 2 tablespoons coriander leaves
instructions: 1. Add half cup quick cook oats or rolled oats to a bowl along with ¼ th cup rice flour or sooji, salt. If using rolled oats, measure half cup first and then powder it. I used quickcookoats so did not powder. If using sooji instead of rice flour, then powder the oats. 2. Add 2 tbsp. curd/ yogurt or 1.5 tsp lemon juice along with a pinch of soda. Pour enough water to make a batter of pouring consistency. Stir well. Rest for 5 mins. 3. Grease and heat a pan/ tawa. Pour a laddle full of batter and lightly spread it. Usually uttapam are made thick, but here oats may lend a sticky texture, so make them medium thick. Add chopped onions, carrots, coriander and green chilies. You can also add deseeded tomatoes, thinly sliced capsicums. Press with a wooden spoon gently. 4. When the base is cooked firm, flip it and cook for a while. 5. Flip it to the other side, Fry for a while on a low flame for the uttapam to turn crisp. If you have any pickle or chutney podi, you can sprinkle evenly. I like to use flax seed podi. Serve with any chutney or pickle. 6. Add dosa batter to a bowl. 7. Add quick cookoats and little salt. 8. Add water as needed to bring it to the right pouring consistency. 9. Pour a ladle full of batter on a hot pan. Spread it to suit your liking. Add chopped veggies. I made this using only carrot. 10. Sprinkled sesame seeds spice powder. 11. ½ cup oats (rolled or quick cook) 12. 2 tablespoons onions fine chopped 13. 2 tablespoons tomatoes fine chopped 14. 2 green chilies chopped 15. 2 tablespoons coriander leaves fine chopped 16. Add oats to a blender jar and make a fine powder. 17. If you do not have rice flour then add sooji along with oats to the blender and powder finely. 18. Transfer the oats flour, rice flour or semolina, salt, yogurt or lemon juice + soda to a mixing bowl. 19. Mix together with water to make a batter similar to the dosa batter consistency. 20. Check salt and add more if needed. 21. Meanwhile rinse and chop onions, tomatoes, chilies and coriander leaves. 22. Mix together all the veggies on the chopping board and keep them ready. 23. Grease a tawa with few drops of oil and heat it on a medium flame. 24. Stir the batter well and adjust the consistency if needed by adding little water. 25. Pour a ladle of batter and spread a bit to make a thick oats uttapam. 26. Sprinkle the veggies as desired and gently press them with a wooden spatula. 27. Cook until the base is done well & slightly crisp. You can also cover and cook until the base is set. 28. Flip and cook well on the other side as well. 29. Flip again and fry until the base is slightly crisp. 30. Remove the oats uttapam to a serving plate. 31. Serve oats uttapam with any chutney, podi or pickle. 32. We usually sprinkle the gun powder or the peanut podi over them. 33. .kadence-column173268_d8ec65-ba > .kt-inside-inner-col,.kadence-column173268_d8ec65-ba > title: Okra Curry (South Indian)
ingredients: 25 grams okra, 1 sprig curry leaves, 1 cup onion, 0.75 cup tomatoes, 4.5 garlic cloves, 1 medium sweet potato chop to inch slices, 2.5 tablespoons oil, 0.25 teaspoon mustard seeds, 0.5 teaspoon cumin seeds, 2.5 tablespoon sambar powder, 0.5 teaspoon red chili powder, 1.5 tablespoon Tamarind paste chutney, 0.625 teaspoon sea salt, 0.5 1 cup water, 1 tablespoon jaggery, 0.29166666666666663 cup thick coconut milk, 2 tablespoons coriander leaves cilantro
instructions: 1. Gather the following ingredients: 2. Rinse, drain & chop 250 grams /9 ounce okra to 1 inch pieces (don’t need to wipe dry before chopping) 3. Rinse & pat dry 1 sprig fresh curry leaves (omit if you don’t have) 4. Fine chop 1medium onion to make 1 cup (120 grams) (red or yellow onion) 5. Chop 1 medium tomato to make ¾ cup (50 grams) (deseed if preferred) 6. Slice 4 to 5 large garlic cloves (10 grams) (don’t chop too small) 7. Optional – Rinse & scrub 1 medium sweet potato (150 grams). Chop to ½ inch thick slices & immerse in a bowl of water 8. ½ teaspoon Kashmiri red chili powder (adjust to heat preference) 9. 1 to 2 tablespoon Tamarind paste or sour tamarind chutney (or 2 tbsp tamarind soaked in ⅓ cup hot water) 10. optional – ¼ to ⅓ cup thick coconut milk ( or grind ¼ cup grated coconut with ¼ cup water or use coconut chutney, adjust to taste) 11. On a medium flame, heat 2 tablespoons oil in a pan and add mustard seeds and cumin seeds. 12. When the seeds begin to pop, add the curry leaves, onions and garlic. Saute for 3 mins. 13. Add the okra and saute for another 5 mins. 14. Stir in the red chili powder, sambar powder/ curry powder and salt. 15. Add the tomatoes, tamarind and ½ cup water. (If using soaked tamarind, squeeze well & strain using a strainer/filter) 16. Also add the sweet potatoes. 17. The consistency should be like seen in the following image else the sambar powder/curry powder can burn at the bottom. 18. Mix well and cook covered for 5 mins. The curry gets thicker fast due to the ground spices, so keep an eye. This step is crucial to cook down the gooey texture in okra. 19. Pour 1 more cup water and cook covered till okra and sweet potatoes are fork tender. Add more water if required while they cook. 20. Mine takes about 15 to 20 mins. It may depending on the kind of cookware, intensity of the flame and the okra. Mature okra take longer to cook. 21. Stir in jaggery and coconut milk if using. Mix well and taste test to adjust salt and tamarind to your preference. It should be ideally tangy, spicy and lightly sweet. Bring to a boil and turn off the heat. 22. Garnish Okra Curry with coriander leaves if you want and serve with brown rice, millets or eat it on its own like a meal. Flatbreads like chapati, ragi roti, roti are also some options.

title: Onion Pakoda | Onion Pakora
ingredients: 2 cups onions, 1.25 cup besan, 1.5 tablespoon rice flour, 0.75 teaspoon salt, 0.5 teaspoon ginger finely chopped, 0.5 teaspoon carom seeds, 2.5 green chilies, 1 sprig curry leaves, 1 mint leaves chopped, 0.25 cup cashews, Oil for deep frying
instructions: 1. Slice onions moderately thin and add them to a mixing bowl. 2. Add chilies, ginger, curry leaves, pudina and salt to the same mixing bowl. 3. Nicely squeeze the onions with your fingers few times without mushing them up. 4. Leave it aside for 5 minutes. This helps the onions to release some moisture. 5. Then sprinkle besan, ajwain, cashews and more salt if needed. 6. Mix everything well to coat the onions with the flour. The mixture will be fairly dry at this moment. 7. Add water 2 tbsps each time and mix the flour to make a moist mass of dough. 8. The dough must be tight yet very moist and not soggy like batter else they absorb lot of oil while frying. 9. Heat 1 tablespoon oil in a kadai until very hot. 10. Be careful as it is too hot. Pour this to the dough. 11. Immediately the oil will sizzle. Mix the dough with a spoon and not hand as the oil is too hot. 12. Heat oil in the same kadai or fry pan for deep frying the pakoda. 13. Check if the oil is hot enough in the kadai by sliding a tiny piece of dough first. 14. It has to rise and not sink in the oil. Next it should not brown too quickly. This is the right temperature. 15. When the oil is hot enough, regulate the flame to medium high. 16. Take a small amount of dough and make small pakoda with your fingers and slide them gently in the hot oil. 17. Do not add too many pakodas in the pan. They should have some space in the pan to swim around & fry well. 18. While frying stir & if needed flip the onion pakodas occasionally for even frying. 19. Fry them till they turn crisp & golden. Then transfer them with a ladle to an absorbent tissue placed in a plate or to a colander. 20. Continue to make more onion pakoda in batches with the rest of the dough. 21. Serve onion pakoda hot with a cup of tea or coffee. You can also serve them with Coriander Chutney, Mint Chutney or Green chutney. 22. Or sprinkle some chaat masala or any ground seasoning of your choice.

title: Onion Raita Recipe
ingredients: 0.5 cup onions, 1 cup curd, 2 green chilies, 1 tablespoon coriander leaves, salt to taste
instructions: 1. Rinse onions under running water. peel off the skin and rinse them again. Rinse green chilies. Add coriander leaves to a bowl filled with water. rinse and repeat the rinse. Drain them. 2. Slice onions, chop chilies and coriander leaves, discarding the fibrous stalks. 3. Add yogurt & salt to a bowl. Whisk the yogurt until smooth. 4. Add onions, green chilies, coriander leaves to the bowl. Mix well. Taste test and add more salt if required. 5. Optionally add more yogurt if you prefer more. Add more sliced onions if you prefer more onions. Serve onion raita with biryani or pulao.

title: Overnight Oats Recipe | Dates Nut Overnight Oats Recipe
ingredients: 0.5 cup rolled, Milk, 1 handful almonds, sweetener, 2.5 tbsp coconut
instructions: 1. Add half cup rolled or ¾ cup quick oats to the jar. Quick ones are precooked and dehydrated, so if possible use rolled oats. But the nutrition in rolled and quick variety is almost the same. 2. Pour milk just enough to immerse them. You can use any vegan milk too like organic soya, coconut or almond milk. You can use any fresh fruit juice too instead of milk. 3. I prefer to add in some nut since soaking enhances the nutrition. You can add any of your favorite nuts. I added few broken walnuts. 4. Add some chopped dates. Top with little more milk. You can skip dates and add any sweetener. 5. Cover and refrigerate over night or at least for 4 hours. 6. The next morning all the ingredients are soaked well. 7. You can add your favorite toppings. I like to add some fresh grated coconut. You can also use some frozen coconut. 8. Then lot of chopped nuts, seeds and then few more chopped dates. I used some pumpkin seeds, almonds and dates. You can even add some choco chips, chopped chocolate, sesame seeds or sunflower seeds.

title: Pakora Recipe | Vegetable Pakora
ingredients: 0.5 cup besan, 0.25 cup rice flour, 0.5 teaspoon salt, 3 green chili peppers, 2 tablespoons mint leaves, 1 teaspoon ginger garlic paste, 0.375 teaspoon garam masala powder, 0.5 teaspoons carom seeds, oil for deep frying as needed, 1 medium carrot, 0.25 cup capsicum, 1 cup cabbage, 1 medium onion, 6 french beans, 1 cup spinach
instructions: 1. Wash the veggies and cut to thin 2 inch long strips. Check the pictures in the post. 2. Add them to a bowl along with ginger garlic, green chilies (or chilli flakes), salt, garam masala, mint or other herbs. Mix well & squeeze them gently to release moisture. Keep aside for 10 mins. 3. Add the gram flour, rice flour (or corn starch) along with ajwain (optional). Mix well to a slightly sticky dough. If the dough is too dry, sprinkle a few tablespoons of water and mix. It must be of a sticky dough consistency and not batter consistency. 4. Taste test and add more salt, garam masala or green chilies. 5. Heat oil in a deep pan on a medium heat. Test by dropping a small portion of dough, it must sizzle and come up but not brown. This is the right temperature. 6. Take small portions of dough and flatten with your fingers to 1½ inch size portions and gently slide to the hot oil. You won't shape it or drop it in lumps. 7. Regulate the flame to medium. Do not disturb for a minute or 2 until they firm up a bit. Then stir them and fry until golden, crisp and aromatic. 8. Remove the vegetable pakoras to a cooling rack or steel colander. To make the next batch, ensure the oil is hot enough but not smoking hot. Fry in batches until you finish all of the prepared dough. 9. Serve vegetable pakora hot with a cup of masala tea, Coriander chutney or green chutney. 10. To keep them crispy for longer, place them on a wired rack in a low oven setting. You can also reheat them in air fryer.

title: Palak Chicken (Chicken Saag Recipe)
ingredients: 1.1 pounds Boneless Chicken, 1 tablespoon lemon juice, 0.29166666666666663 teaspoon black pepper, 0.29166666666666663 teaspoon Kashmiri red chilli powder, 0.25 teaspoon salt, 8.5 ounces Baby Spinach, 3 green chilies, 18 cashews, 2 tablespoons oil, 0.875 cup onions, 0.875 cup tomatoes, 2 teaspoons ginger garlic paste, 0.625 teaspoon garam masala, 1 tablespoon kasuri methi, 0.875 teaspoon salt, 1.5 tablespoons whipping cream, 1 Indian bay leaf, 0.5 teaspoon cumin seeds, 3 green cardamoms, 3.5 cloves 3 to 4 cloves, 1 inch cinnamon piece, 1 black cardamom
instructions: 1. Add chicken to a large mixing bowl. Sprinkle ¼ teaspoon salt, black pepper, red chilli powder and lemon juice. Mix and set aside until we need it. 2. Pour 1 teaspoon oil to a hot pan and add cashews and green chilies. Stir fry until the chilies blister. Add spinach (cleaned, rinsed and drained) and stir fry on a medium heat until wilted and fully cooked, for 6 mins. Turn off the heat & cool this. 3. Pour ½ cup cold water to a blender and transfer the spinach, chilies and cashews. You may set aside some of the green chilies which you can adjust later. 4. Make a smooth puree and taste test. If you want more heat add back the green chilis that we set aside and blend. 5. Add the rest of the oil (along with ghee if using) to a hot pan. Add all the whole spices and let them sizzle. Stir in the chopped onions and saute until light brown & really soft. 6. Stir in the ginger garlic and saute for a minute, until aromatic. 7. Add tomatoes and salt. Stir well and cook until the tomatoes lose their raw flavor. If you want you may cook covered at this stage. 8. Add the marinated chicken and stir fry on a medium high heat, until pale. Stir in the garam masala. 9. Pour ½ to ¾ cup hot water and mix well. Cook covered on a medium heat, until chicken is fully cooked through. 10. Stir in the Spinach puree. If the palak chicken gravy is too thick , you may pour little hot water. 11. Simmer until bubbling hot and cook for 3 to 4 minutes. Taste test and adjust salt. Take kasuri methi in your palms and crush it well. Sprinkle all over. Turn off the heat. Keep the gravy slightly runny if you want to serve it with rice. It thickens slightly upon cooling. 12. Garnish Palak Chicken optionally with cream. Squeeze lemon juice while serving. Serve with Steamed rice, Naan, Roti or Chapati. 13. This is not a part of the recipe but if you love authentic Indian flavors, make a nice tadka and pour it over the dish. Heat 1 tablespoon ghee on a medium heat. Add 2 to 3 sliced garlic cloves and 2 dried red chillies. Fry on a low heat for a minute, until garlic turns light golden. Add ¼ teaspoon hing (optional). Turn off and pour it over palak chicken.

title: Palak Corn Recipe
ingredients: 8 cups spinach, 0.25 cup coriander leaves, 1.5 cup corn kernels, 1.5 tablespoon oil, 2 green cardamoms, 4 green chilies, 1 inch ginger, 4 garlic, 1 cup yellow onion, 0.5 cup tomatoes, 1 teaspoon salt, 18 cashew nuts, 1.25 teaspoon garam masala, 0.5 teaspoon roasted cumin powder, 1 teaspoon coriander powder, 2 teaspoons kasuri methi, 0.5 cup water to cook, Lemon juice, 2.5 tablespoons heavy cream, 1.5 to 2 tablespoon ghee, 2 red chilies, 3 garlic, 0.5 teaspoon cumin seeds, 0.25 teaspoon hing, 0.5 teaspoon Kashmiri red chili powder, 1 grams paneer
instructions: 1. Heat oil in a pot and add the cardamoms, onions, green chilies, ginger, garlic, and salt. Saute for 4 to 5 mins until transparent. Add the cashews, tomatoes and water. Bring to a rolling boil on a high heat. Reduce to medium and cook covered until very soft, for 10 mins. 2. Open the lid & reserve half of the green chilies aside so you can adjust the heat levels as you blend in the next steps. 3. Stir in garam masala, salt, spinach and coriander leaves. Saute until wilted and cooked through, for 3 to 4 mins. Turn off and stir in the rest of the water. 4. Blend using an immersion blender or a stand blender to a smooth or coarse puree, as per your preference. (cool down first, if using a stand blender). Taste test and adjust the green chilies/heat level at this stage. 5. Return the palak mixture to the pot and add corn kernels. Bring to a boil and let simmer for 3 to 4 mins. If you are using mature spinach, you may cook for another 3 to 4 mins. Taste test and adjust salt & garam masala. Crush kasuri methi in your hands and sprinkle. 6. Turn off and pour the cream. Corn palak is ready to serve at this stage. You may serve it with a squeeze of lemon juice if you don't want to make the tadka. 7. Heat 1 teaspoon ghee in a non-stick pan and place the paneer cubes. Fry until golden on a medium high heat, tossing them around gently. Stir in half to the corn palak and reserve the rest. 8. Turn off the heat and add the remaining ghee, cumin seeds and red chilies. When the seeds sizzle, turn on and add the garlic. 9. Let fry on a low heat until light golden. Turn off and stir in the hing, red chili powder and the rest of the fried paneer. Mix well and pour this immediately over the corn palak. Garnish with more heavy cream if you want. 10. Perform the first step on saute mode and pressure cook for 5 mins after pouring water. I cook basmati rice along with this, placed on a high legged trivet. Gently release the pressure immediately. Follow the rest as mentioned above.

title: Palak Paratha (Spinach Paratha)
ingredients: 1.5 cups Whole wheat flour, 1.5 cups spinach, 0.5 inch ginger, 0.25 tsp garam masala, 1 green chili, 0.5 tsp salt, 1.5 tbsp water, 1 tbsp Oil, 2 tbsp Ghee
instructions: 1. Wash spinach and drain completely. 2. Add them to a blender with chili, ginger or garlic and make a fine puree with the help of 1 to 2 tbsp water. Use water only if needed. 3. Add the puree to the flour along with salt and spice powders. 4. Knead together to make a dough. 5. If the dough turns sticky, just sprinkle more flour & knead to a non sticky dough. 6. If the dough turns too dry then, sprinkle water & knead. 7. Add little oil and knead well to make a soft pliable dough. 8. Set this aside for 20 to 30 minutes. Make 8 equal sized balls. Cover them. 9. Flour the rolling area and flatten a ball. Sprinkle flour. 10. Roll it to make slightly thick palak paratha. 11. Sprinkle flour over the paratha as needed to prevent sticking. It has to be slightly thicker than roti. 12. Heat a tawa until hot enough. Transfer a paratha to the tawa. 13. When the paratha begins to get bubbles over it. 14. Flip it to the other side. Gently press down the paratha with a spatula to puff it. Cook until golden spots appear. 15. Once done smear little ghee. 16. Place a kitchen tissue on a plate. Stack the palak parathas to keep them soft. 17. Serve palak paratha with chutney, dal or curry. Or use them to wrap as rolls with veggie or egg fillings.

title: Palak Rice Recipe (Spinach Rice)
ingredients: 1 cup rice, 1.5 tablespoon oil, 0.5 teaspoon cumin seeds, 1 bay leaf, 2 cups spinach, 2 green chilies, 2 garlic cloves, 1 onion, 1 medium potato, 0.625 garam masala, 0.125 teaspoon turmeric, 0.625 teaspoon salt, 11 mint leaves, 1 tbsp lemon juice
instructions: 1. Rinse rice and cook in a pot or cooker. Rice must be grainy and not mushy. 2. Wash palak thoroughly in lots of water. Drain completely. Add them to a small blender and puree to a smooth paste. You can also add few tablespoons of water to help blend smooth or alternately fine chop and use. 3. Heat a pan with oil, add cumin seeds and bay leaf. 4. When the cumin begins to sizzle, add garlic & saute until it turns fragrant for about 40 to 60 seconds. 5. Add onion and green chilli . Saute until the onions turn golden. 6. Then add potatoes. Saute until they become tender. If needed sprinkle some water and cook. 7. Sprinkle garam masala, salt and turmeric. Pour palak puree and saute until the raw smell goes off. 8. Add mint leaves and saute for a min or two. Switch off the stove. 9. Transfer the cooled rice, squeeze in the lemon juice. Mix everything well. Taste test and add more salt if needed 10. Serve palak rice hot or warm. 11. Heat oil in a pot. Add cumin and bay leaf. When the cumin sizzles, saute ginger garlic paste. 12. Then add onions and green chilies. Fry until transparent. Add potatoes or any other veggies you intend to use. Stir fry for 2 to 3 mins. 13. Stir in the garam masala, salt and turmeric. Transfer the pureed palak and saute until the raw smell goes away. 14. When the mixture turns thicker, add uncooked rice. 15. If using pressure cooker, pour 1¾ cups water for basmati rice. (2 cups if using normal rice). If cooking in a pot use 2 cups water. 16. Pressure cook on a medium high flame for 1 whistle for basmati rice. If using normal rice pressure cook for 2 whistles. 17. Release the pressure manually with the help of a spatula if using basmati rice. 18. Fluff up palak rice with a fork.

title: Paneer 65 Fry Recipe
ingredients: 2 grams paneer, 1.5 to 2 teaspoon ginger garlic paste, 0.5 teaspoon cumin powder, 0.75 teaspoon garam masala, 0.75 teaspoon coriander powder, 0.625 teaspoon red chilli powder, 0.125 teaspoon turmeric, 1 sprig curry leaves, 0.25 teaspoon salt, 1.5 teaspoon lemon juice, 0.1875 teaspoon salt, 3 tablespoons cornstarch, 2 tablespoons rice flour, 0.25 cup water, 0.75 cup oil, 1.5 sprigs curry leaves, 4.5 green chilies, 1.5 tablespoon oil, 1 teaspoon garlic, 1.5 green chilies, 1 sprig curry leaves, 1 teaspoon red chilli powder, 0.5 tablespoon garlic paste, 0.25 teaspoon salt, 0.25 teaspoon sugar, 1.5 teaspoons vinegar, 3 tablespoons water
instructions: 1. Dice paneer to ¾ inch pieces and add them to a mixing bowl. Add all the ingredients mentioned for marinade – ginger garlic paste, cumin powder, coriander powder, garam masala, red chilli powder, turmeric, chopped curry leaves and salt. 2. Toss the paneer with all the ingredients we just added so the spices coat all the paneer well. Sprinkle lemon juice or vinegar and 2 to 3 tablespoons of water. Toss again and set aside as long as you like. (up to 48 hours in the fridge.) 3. Tempering paneer 65 is optional but this makes a lot of difference to the dish. If you want to skip you may skip this section and head over to frying paneer section directly. 4. Prepare the red chilli paste by mixing the red chilli powder, garlic paste, sugar, salt, vinegar (or yogurt) and water in a small bowl. Keep this aside. 5. Pour 1 ½ tablespoons oil to a wok and heat it. Add the chopped garlic, green chilies and curry leaves. Fry until the garlic begins to smell good. 6. Lower the flame. Stir and pour the sauce we made. Cook this on a medium heat until oil separates from the thick sauce. At this stage the sauce should be essentially thick to keep your final paneer 65 crisp and not runny. (check stepwise pictures in the post) Turn off the heat. 7. Add flour and salt to a small bowl and pour water. Mix together to make a thick batter, it should be of neither too thick nor runny. If you dip a spoon into the batter, it should coat the back of the spoon. (Check stepwise pictures in the post) To make the air fryer version, it needs to be more on the thicker side as this prevents the batter from dripping. 8. Pour this batter over the marinated paneer and give a gentle mix. If you feel the batter is too thick and does not spread, add a splash of water. It may need a few tablespoons at this stage. 9. Heat oil in a deep pot or pan for frying. If you want you may shallow fry in 3 tablespoons of oil. Test if the oil is hot enough by dropping a small portion of batter to the hot oil, it should sizzle and come up without turning brown. 10. Regulate the flame to medium. Gently pick up each pieces of paneer, making sure it is well coated with the batter, gently drop it to hot oil. Make sure you put them away from each other so they don’t stick. 11. Do not crowd the pan by adding a lot of pieces. Do not disturb for 3 minutes, meaning do not stir. 12. Later gently turn them to the other side and fry until crisp and golden. Remove to a wired rack or steel colander. 13. Fry the paneer in batches until all of them are done. Turn off the heat and place the curry leaves (pat dry first) in the frying skimmer and gently down to the hot oil. They will fry and become crisp, remove to the plate. 14. Repeat frying by placing the slit and deseeded green chilies (pat dry first) in the skimmer and lower them to the hot oil. Fry them until blistered. 15. If you don’t want to temper, you may serve the paneer 65 fry as is. If you have made the spicy tempering, add your paneer 65 to it and toss until well coated. Serve it immediately. 16. Garnish paneer 65 with fried green chilies and curry leaves, Squeeze some lemon juice and serve alongside fresh cut onions. 17. Keep the tempering ready first. 18. Preheat your air fryer for 10 mins at 400 F or 200 C. Spray oil and begin to place each piece of paneer in the basket/tray, spacing them apart. For the air fry version, I have felt keeping the pieces slightly bigger and the batter a bit thicker works well. 19. Air fry for 6 to 7 mins. Turn them to the other side and continue to air fry for another 3 mins , until crisp and cooked through. Adjust the air frying time depending on the size of your fryer. Smaller fryer requires a bit lesser temperature and lesser cook time. 20. Toss the paneer 65 in the tempering and serve immediately.

title: Paneer Bhurji Recipe
ingredients: 2 grams paneer, 1.5 tablespoon oil, 0.5 teaspoon cumin seeds, 1 medium onion, 1 green chilli, 1 teaspoon ginger garlic paste, 1 medium tomato, 0.25 cup capsicum, 0.125 teaspoon turmeric, 0.25 teaspoon salt, 0.625 teaspoon red chilli powder, 0.75 teaspoon garam masala powder, 0.5 teaspoon kasuri methi, 1 teaspoon lemon juice, 2 tablespoon Coriander leaves
instructions: 1. Pour 4 cups milk to a pot and bring it to a boil. Add 1 tbsp vinegar or lemon juice and Stir. When the milk begins to curdle well, turn off the stove. If the milk does not curdle then add 1 tbsp more vinegar. 2. Drain it to a colander lined with a muslin cloth. Rinse it well under running water. Squeeze off the excess whey and make a knot with the cloth. 3. Hang the paneer for about 10 to 15 minutes to remove excess whey. Meanwhile chop the onions & tomatoes finely. 4. Pour oil to a hot pan and add cumin seeds. When they begin to sizzle, add finely chopped onions and chopped green chili. 5. Saute until the onions turn golden to light brown. Add ginger garlic paste and saute until the raw smell has gone, for about a minute. 6. Add chopped tomato, salt and turmeric. Saute until the tomatoes turn soft and mushy. 7. Stir in the red chili powder, kasuri methi & garam masala. 8. Mix and saute until the raw smell goes off from the mixture & the masala leaves the sides of the pan. 9. Stir in peas or capsicum if using. Fry until capsicum turns slightly soft. Crumble paneer well and add to the pan. 10. Stir and fry till everything blends well just for 2 minutes. Taste it and add more salt and spices if required. 11. Turn off the stove. Add 2 tbsp chopped coriander leaves. Serve paneer bhurji with roti, paratha or bread. Sprinkle some lemon juice before serving.

title: Paneer Bhurji Sandwich
ingredients: 4 slices bread, 2 tsps butter, 1 tbsp oil, 0.5 tsp cumin, 1 tsp garlic, Salt, 1 Pinch turmeric, 0.625 tsp red chilli powder, 0.375 tsp garam masala, 0.25 tsp amchur, 1 onion, 1 tomato, 0.3333333333333333 cup paneer
instructions: 1. Smear softened butter or ghee over the bread slices and toast them until golden. If you are making 2 servings, Take a larger skillet and toast them all at one time or two. If you prefer to toast or grill the sandwiches then skip doing it on the tawa. 2. While the bread toasts, slice an onion and tomato. Keep aside all the toasted bread slices. 3. Add 1.5 tsp oil to the pan and saute chili, garlic and cumin. You can also use ginger in place of garlic. Saute until the garlic turns golden. 4. Add the crumbled paneer. Switch off the stove. 5. Add salt, turmeric, red chili powder, garam masala, amchur powder or lemon juice and chopped coriander leaves. 6. Mix everything well. Taste test and adjust salt and other spices to your taste. 7. Place this on the bread. 8. Then layer onions & tomatoes. You can also add very thin slices of yellow and red bell peppers. 9. You can place lettuce or coriander leaves. You can also add green chutney or chili sauce. 10. Cover with another slice of bread. Gently press down. You can also grill these now until golden if you have not toasted at step 1. Halve the paneer bhurji sandwich and serve.

title: Paneer Biryani Recipe (Restaurant Style)
ingredients: 2 cups aged basmati rice, 1.5 to 2 teaspoons salt, 1 tablespoon oil, 1 tablespoon lemon juice, 1.5 bay leaf, 2 star anise, 6 green cardamoms, 2 inch cinnamon piece, 2 black cardamoms, 1 strand mace, 325 grams paneer, 6 tablespoons ghee, 2 cup onions, 1.5 green chilli, 1.5 tablespoon ginger garlic paste, 0.5 cup tomatoes, 1.5 to 2 teaspoon Kashmiri chilli powder, 1.5 to 2 teaspoons biryani masala, 1 tablespoon coriander powder, 1 teaspoon roasted cumin powder, 0.25 teaspoon turmeric, 0.75 teaspoon salt, 0.75 cup mint leaves, 0.75 cup coriander leaves, 1.25 cup yogurt, 3.5 tablespoons water, 2 cashews, 3.5 tablespoons raisins, 0.25 cup whole milk, 2.5 pinches saffron, 1 teaspoon kewra water
instructions: 1. I prefer to use 20 to 25 whole cashews (split open later) and 2 to 3 tablespoons sweet raisins for garnish. You may skip them if you want. 2. Heat ¼ cup milk in cup or sauce pan and add 2 to 3 large pinches of saffron strands. Optionally, also add ½ tbsp. of kewra or rose water to it and keep aside. Whisk 1¼ cup fresh yogurt (not sour) well with a whisk or fork and set aside. (To use Greek yogurt whisk 1 cup Greek yogurt with ¼ cup milk.) 3. Puree ½ cup tomatoes (sweet tomatoes) in a small grinder. Slice 2 cup onions thin and evenly so they fry evenly. I use 1 large onion, slice and then halve the slices so they fry faster. You will slice and then squeeze them gently to separate the layers completely, then measure. 4. Rinse & drain ¾ to 1 cup each of mint leaves and coriander leaves. Chop them later. Slice 1 green chili (optional, deseed if needed) & set aside 1½ tablespoons ginger garlic paste (or gratings). 5. You will need the following ground spices: 6. Rinse 2 cups aged basmati rice well and soak it in water for at least 30 minutes. You can soak up to 50 minutes but not more as the rice can easily break and turn mushy. 7. Heat 1 tablespoon ghee in a heavy bottom pan. When it is hot enough, place the paneer block/s (250 to 400 grams) gently and fry it on a medium high heat until golden on both the sides, turn it when one side is done. (If you put the paneer in cold ghee or cold pan, it will get stuck, so wait until hot) 8. Remove to a plate and cool down. Later dice to desired sizes. I dice each block to 10 portions. To garnish I dice to smaller pieces. 9. To the same pan, add cashews and fry until light golden. Stir in the raisins. If you want you may add a tsp of ghee while frying. 10. Let fry until they turn plump. Remove to a plate and keep aside. I prefer to wipe the pan with some kitchen tissues to prevent burnt bits of nuts or paneer. 11. Pour 4 tablespoons ghee and add the sliced onions. Begin to fry on a medium high heat. Keep stirring often & spread them in the pan. Continue to fry for a few minutes, then reduce the flame as needed to medium or low. 12. After about 8 to 9 minutes, onions begin to caramelize. At this stage turn down the heat to lowest so they don’t burn. If using dutch oven, turn off the heat. Saute until the onions caramelize and turn evenly deep golden. Be very attentive at this stage as they can burn in few seconds. Burnt onions taste bitter. It took me 12 minutes for this entire task, at varying heat levels. Turn the heat to low. Remove 2 tbsps of these fried onions to a plate and set aside. 13. Add the following spices 14. bay leaf 2 star anise 6 green cardamoms 2 inch cinnamon 2 Black cardamoms 1 large strand of mace / javitri 15. Quickly sauté 1½ ginger garlic paste & slit green chilli until slightly aromatic. Be cautious as this can burn in a flash. 16. Stir in salt, Biryani/ garam masala, Coriander powder, Cumin powder, Kashmiri red chilli powder & turmeric. 17. Pour the half cup tomato puree. 18. Cook until it turns thick & aromatic. 19. Make sure your yogurt is smooth and whisked well. To the yogurt bowl, stir in 2 tbsps of the prepared masala. 20. Turn the heat to low and add the spiced yogurt to the pot. 21. Stir well and cook on a low heat until the mixture turns thick and fats begin to separate. 22. Turn off the heat. Pour 3 to 4 tablespoons hot water and stir well. Taste test this and add more salt or garam masala if you want. Add ¾ of the mint leaves, ¾ of the coriander leaves you prepared and ¾ of the fried paneer (save some for kids or garnish if you want). Do not add all 400 grams here. You can add max 300 grams. 23. Cover and keep aside until the rice is prepared.

title: Paneer cheese toast recipe | Paneer cheese sandwich recipe video
ingredients: 5.5 bread slices, 1.25 to 1 cups Paneer, 1 tbsp oil, 0.5 tsp cumin, 1 medium onion, 1 green chili, 1 tsp ginger paste, 1 medium tomato, 0.25 tsp turmeric, 0.625 tsp red chili powder, 0.625 tsp garam masala, 0.5 tsp kasuri methi, Salt, 1 tbsp Butter, 18 grams mozzarella cheese, Red chili flakes, 1 Handful coriander leaves
instructions: 1. Add jeera to hot oil. 2. Fry the onions until golden. 3. Add the ginger garlic paste and fry till the raw smell goes away. 4. Add tomatoes, turmeric and salt. 5. Fry the tomatoes until mushy. 6. Next add in the chilli powder and garam masala. 7. Saute till the raw smell of the chili powder goes away. 8. Add paneer. I made fresh paneer the previous night. Mix up the paneer with the masala. 9. Add kasuri methi and coriander leaves. 10. Regulate the flame to low. Place the bread with butter on the pan. Spread paneer over it. 11. Sprinkle cheese, red chili flakes and any herbs or coriander leaves. 12. Cover with a lid and cook on a low flame until the cheese melts. If you do it on a high flame the bread can burn so keep it at lowest. You can also put this in the oven for 3 to 4 mins. Serve paneer cheese toast immediately. 13. Heat a pan with oil and add cumin. 14. When they begin to crackle, add onions and chilies. 15. Saute well until the onions turn golden. 16. Add ginger garlic paste and saute till the raw smell goes away. 17. Add tomatoes and sprinkle salt, turmeric. 18. Cook until the tomatoes turn mushy. You can also cover and cook at this stage to speed up the process. 19. Add garam masala and red chili powder. Mix well until well combined. 20. Add panir. Mix to spread the masala very well. 21. Add coriander leaves and kasuri methi. Saute for a minute and switch off. 22. Add very hot paneer bhurji over the bread and then the grated cheese over it. 23. Cover with a lid and toast on a low to medium heat until the cheese melts. Keep checking in between to prevent burning. 24. Just microwave it on high heat for 1 minute. 25. Put the bread along with cheese topped in a 170 C preheated oven. Grill for 8 to 10 mins or until golden. 26. Serve paneer cheese toast immediately.

title: Paneer Curry Recipe (Dhaba Style)
ingredients: 225 grams Paneer, 0.5 teaspoon Kashmiri red chilli powder, 0.5 teaspoon garam masala, 0.25 teaspoon salt, 1 teaspoon crushed kasuri methi, 2 tablespoons oil, 1 teaspoon ghee, 1 bay leaf, 0.5 teaspoon cumin seeds, 1 black cardamom, 2 green cardamoms, 1 inch cinnamon, 3 cloves 3 cloves, 2 red chilies, 1 cup onions, 1 teaspoon ginger, 1 teaspoon garlic, 1.25 cup tomatoes, 1.25 teaspoon Kashmiri red chilli powder, 0.875 teaspoon garam masala, 1 teaspoon coriander powder, 0.25 teaspoon roasted cumin powder, 1.5 tablespoon roasted besan, 3 tablespoons curd, 1.5 cups water, 0.75 teaspoon salt, 2 tablespoons coriander leaves
instructions: 1. Mix together salt & spices (mentioned under marinade) in a bowl. Place the paneer pieces and coat them gently with the spices. If it is too dry, add a splash of water. Keep aside until needed later. 2. Fine chop onions or mince in a FP. Begin to cook while you prepare the rest of the ingredients. 3. Heat oil in a pan and add the whole spices - bay leaf, black cardamom, green cardamoms, dried red chillies, cumin, cinnamon & cloves. 4. Once they begin to sizzle, add the onions and stir fry on a medium high heat for 4 to 5 minutes, stirring often. 5. Later reduce the heat and cook on a medium to low heat until the onions begin to caramelize & turn light brown but not burnt. 6. Reduce the heat to low and stir in the ginger garlic. Saute for a minute until the raw flavor is gone. 7. Stir in the dry spices, roasted besan and salt. Stir well and fry for a minute or 2 until the mixture turns aromatic. If you want you may add ghee at this stage. 8. Add the chopped or grated tomatoes and mix well. Cook until the onion tomato masala blends well and turns thick. 9. Take 1 tablespoon of this onion masala and mix it well into the whisked yogurt or cultured cream. Pour this into the pan and cook until you see traces of oil being separated from the masala. 10. Pour 1½ cups water to the same pan and bring it to a boil on a high heat. Reduce the heat and cook covered until you see traces of oil on top of the curry. Taste test and adjust salt & garam masala if you want. 11. While that cooks, in another non-stick pan heat a teaspoon ghee. Place the paneer pieces in the hot pan and coat them with ghee. Fry on a medium heat to bring out the flavors of the spices. Take a spoon and stir them. Do not over fry, it just takes a few minutes. 12. Add this paneer along with the ghee to the cooked curry. Make sure your curry isn't too thick. It has to be slightly flowy so the extra moisture liquid goes into the paneer keeping it soft. 13. Give a gentle stir and cook covered on a low heat for 2 mins. Transfer to a serving bowl and garnish with coriander leaves. Serve with plain rice, roti, paratha, naan or with jeera rice.

title: Paneer Dosa (Street Style)
ingredients: 2 tablespoons oil, 1 cup onion, 1 green chili, 1 heaped teaspoon ginger garlic paste, 0.5 cup tomato, 1 teaspoon Kashmiri chili powder, 0.625 teaspoon garam masala, 0.625 teaspoon sea salt, 225 grams paneer, 1 teaspoon kasuri methi, 2 tablespoons coriander leaves, 2.25 cups dosa batter, 1.5 to 2 tablespoons softened unsalted butter ghee oil
instructions: 1. Heat oil in a pan and add onions & green chili. Saute until golden for 6 to 7 mins and add ginger garlic. Saute for a minute and add chili powder, garam masala and salt. 2. Add the tomatoes & cook until they lose the raw smell, for 4 to 5 mins. 3. Stir in ¼ cup water and cook until the masala is thick & aromatic. Mix in the diced paneer. Cook for 2 mins, until heated through. Taste test and adjust salt to taste. 4. Mix in kasuri methi & turn off. Cool down slightly and sprinkle coriander leaves. 5. Bring your dosa batter to a thick yet of pouring and spreading consistency. (I usually add little water and salt at this stage) 6. Heat a dosa pan on a medium flame until really hot. 7. Pour a ladle full of batter in the center and spread it from the center to the edges of the pan, making concentric circles. 8. Add diced butter or sprinkle ghee across the dosa. Cook on a medium high heat until golden, crisp and the edges begin to come off the pan. 9. Scoop out ¼ portion of the prepared paneer to one half of the dosa. Cover it half way and remove to a serving plate. 10. Serve paneer dosa hot. You don't need any sides but coconut chutney or any other will go well. 11. Make the next dosa when the pan is hot enough, but not smoking hot. If it is too hot, splash little water to cool down slightly.

title: Paneer fried rice
ingredients: 1 cup rice, 2 tablespoons oil, 0.875 cup paneer, 2.5 tablespoons Thick curd, 0.5 teaspoon ginger garlic paste, 0.5 teaspoon red chilli powder, 0.375 teaspoon garam masala, 0.125 teaspoon turmeric, 0.25 teaspoon salt, 0.375 cup spring onions, 0.5 cup carrot, 0.375 cup bell peppers, 0.375 teaspoon black pepper, 2 cloves garlic, 1 green chilli, 1.25 tablespoon soya sauce
instructions: 1. If you have precooked rice, you may skip this step. You will need about 2 to 2.5 cups cooked rice for this recipe. Soak 1 cup rice for 15 to 30 mins and cook al dente in lots of water. Do not overcook and make the rice mushy. Drain off the water and set this rice aside to cool. If you are a beginner, you can check my fried rice recipe post to learn about cooking perfect rice for fried rice. 2. To a bowl, add 3. ¼ to ½ teaspoon garam masala. Mix all of the ingredients. 4. Add ¾ to 1 cup cold paneer cubes. Mix well. and set aside for at least 15 mins 5. Meanwhile chop and prepare 6. Heat large pan with 1 tablespoon oil. Add marinated paneer and cook on medium high till the marinade dries up and clings on to the paneer. Once done remove to a plate. 7. Pour another tablespoon oil to the same pan. Add 1 tablespoon chopped garlic and 1 chopped green chilli. Saute for 30 to 60 seconds. Then add spring onion whites, carrots and bell peppers. Fry on a high heat until they are half done yet crunchy. 8. Add fried paneer and green onions, toss everything well. 9. Add cooled rice, sprinkle a pinch of salt and ¼ teaspoon crushed black pepper. Pour 1 tablespoon soya sauce around the sides in the hot pan. You will see the soya sauce sizzles and the entire paneer fried rice turns aromatic. You can also add any hot sauce at this stage if you want. Soya sauce also has salt so add salt with caution. 10. Mix and fry on high heat for 2 mins. Taste test and add more sauce or salt as required. Serve paneer fried rice hot.

title: Paneer Jalfrezi Recipe (Restaurant Style)
ingredients: 275 grams paneer, 1 medium onion, 1.5 cup tomatoes, 1 cup bell peppers capsicum, 1 green chili slit, 2 teaspoon ginger garlic paste, 2.5 tablespoons oil, 0.625 teaspoon cumin seeds, 0.75 teaspoon Kashmiri red chili powder, 0.25 teaspoon turmeric, 1 teaspoon garam masala, 0.75 teaspoon sea salt, 0.875 tablespoon vinegar, 1 teaspoon sugar jaggery, 1 teaspoon Kasuri methi, 2.5 tablespoons coriander leaves
instructions: 1. Cut paneer to square or rectangles of preferred sizes. I cut mine to ¾ * 2 inches long pieces. If using fresh tomatoes, chop and puree them in a blender. 2. On a medium flame, heat oil or ghee in a pan. Add cumin seeds and chilies. 3. Fry them for a minute and add the chopped/sliced onions. Saute until light golden, for 5 mins. Stir in the ginger garlic and saute for 1 to 2 mins. 4. Turn down the heat to low, stir in the chilli powder, garam masala, coriander powder (optional), turmeric and salt. Pour the tomato puree immediately. 5. Cook on a medium high heat until it becomes slightly thick, for 5 to 7 mins. (If using store bought puree it takes only 2 to 3 mins) 6. Add the bell peppers & diced onions. Stir fry on a medium high heat, for 4 to 5 mins until slightly tender, yet crunchy. If your pan dries out, add 1 to 2 tablespoons of water. 7. Taste test and stir in more salt, vinegar/ tomato paste (only if needed) and sugar if using. 8. Reduce the heat and add paneer. Crush kasuri methi in your hands and sprinkle. Gently stir in to coat the paneer with the jalfrezi. 9. Cover and cook on a very low heat just for 2 to 3 mins, until heated through. Sprinkle coriander leaves and serve paneer jalfrezi with rice or roti. It also goes well to make kathi rolls and sandwiches.

title: Paneer Kathi Roll Recipe
ingredients: 25 grams paneer, 1 teaspoon Kashmiri chili powder, 1 teaspoon garam masala, 1 teaspoon coriander powder, 0.5 teaspoon cumin powder, 0.5 teaspoon chaat masala, 0.41666666666666663 teaspoon salt, 2 tablespoons Greek Yogurt, 1 teaspoon ginger garlic paste, 1 teaspoon lemon juice, 1 teaspoon kasuri methi, 1 tablespoon oil, 1 tablespoon oil, 1 cup onions, 1 cup bell peppers, 0.5 cup carrot, 4 or 6 medium chapati roti paratha, 2 tablespoons cilantro chutney, 0.25 cup onions, 2 green chilies, 1 lemon
instructions: 1. In a mixing bowl, stir together all the ingredients mentioned for marinade except paneer. Mix well and taste test. Adjust salt and spice levels. 2. Add the paneer to the marinade. Mix well and rest until you prepare the rest. You can marinate this up to 24 hours in the refrigerator. 3. When you are ready to make the Paneer Kathi Rolls, slice onions, chilies, carrots and bell peppers thinly. 4. Add the sliced onions, and green chilies (called to assemble) to a small bowl. Sprinkle little salt and squeeze the lemon juice. Cover and rest aside until we need them to assemble later. 5. Cook the parathas following the instructions on your pack or make them from my post. Stack them on a rack or on a plate lined with tissue. This keeps them soft. 6. Heat oil in a skillet and add the onions, carrots and bell peppers. Sauté them on a high heat until they lose the raw flavor, for 3 to 4 mins. Remove them to a plate. 7. Add the marinated paneer to the same hot pan and spread them in a single layer. Let cook until the marinade no more tastes raw, for about 4 to 5 mins, stirring half way. 8. Add the bell pepper onion mixture and mix well with the paneer. Taste test and add a sprinkle of salt, garam masala and red chili powder for more heat if you want. 9. Mix well and turn off. Cool down slightly. 10. Place the paratha on a tray/plate. Spread the cilantro chutney. If you are making this ahead to serve later, drizzle the green chutney later, at the end, before rolling. 11. Add paneer in the center, followed by some sliced onions, green chilies. 12. Bring the sides to the center and wrap them in a parchment paper. If you don’t have a paper, simply spread the chutney on the edges and assemble the kathi roll.

title: Paneer Korma | Paneer Kurma
ingredients: 275 grams paneer, 1.25 cup onions, 0.75 cup tomatoes, 0.25 teaspoon turmeric, 0.5 tablespoon ginger garlic paste, 1.125 teaspoon garam masala, 1 teaspoon coriander powder, 0.875 teaspoon red chili powder, 2 tablespoons oil, 1 mint leaves, 1 bay leaf, 0.25 teaspoon cumin seeds, 2.5 green cardamoms, 1 star anise, 2 inch cinnamon piece, 2.5 cloves 2 to 3 cloves, 1.25 cup water, 12 cashew nuts, 0.25 cup coconut, 7 tablespoons water
instructions: 1. Skip this step if you want to use coconut milk in the recipe. Add cashews or poppy seeds to a grinder jar and make a fine powder first. Add fresh coconut and repeat grinding. Pour 6 to 8 tbsps water to make a smooth paste. If required add a little more water. 2. Heat a pan with oil on a medium heat. Once the oil is slightly hot, add whole spices - bay leaf, star anise, cumin, cloves, cinnamon and cardamoms. 3. When the spices begin to sizzle, add ginger garlic paste and fry till it turns slightly aromatic for about 60 seconds. Do not over fry, it can make the gravy bitter. 4. Add onions, sprinkle salt and fry till they turn transparent or golden. 5. Add tomatoes , salt and turmeric. Fry till tomatoes turn mushy. If required splash a bit of water and cook covered until the tomatoes turn mushy. 6. Next add red chili powder, garam masala, coriander powder and mint leaves or coriander leaves. Carefully saute for two to three minutes without burning. 7. Add the smooth ground paste. Fry for three to four minutes on a medium flame stirring occasionally. It must begin to release a nice aroma of coconut and poppy seeds. [If using coconut milk stir it together with ¾ cup water] 8. Pour water (or diluted coconut milk) and mix well. Bring this to a boil and reduce the heat, cook till the gravy thickens & you see specks of oil floating on the korma. Keep stirring occasionally. 9. Once the curry reaches a desired thick consistency, add paneer and gently stir. 10. Turn off the heat. Let paneer korma rest for a while so it absorbs the flavors. Serve with rice, roti or naan. 11. Perform all the steps following the instant pot saute mode, except for adding the coconut paste or coconut milk. Reserve this for adding later. Also you will use only 1 cup water for cooking the gravy. 12. Pressure cook for 5 mins and wait for 2 mins. Release the pressure manually. 13. Stir in the ½ to ¾ coconut milk or the coconut paste. If you think your korma is too thick, pour little boiling water. Taste test and add more garam masala and salt if required. 14. Let cook on saute mode until aromatic and thick for 3 mins. 15. Turn off the IP and add paneer. Cover paneer korma with a lose lid and serve after 10 mins. Garnish with coriander leaves.

title: Paneer Lababdar Recipe (Instant Pot and Stovetop)
ingredients: 2 cups tomatoes, 0.5 cup water, 15 cashews, 2 inch cinnamon piece, 4 cloves 4 cloves, 4 cardamoms, 4 red chilies, 2 tablespoons oil, 1 bay leaf, 0.5 cup onions, 1 tablespoon ginger garlic paste, 1.25 teaspoon coriander powder, 0.5 teaspoon garam masala, 0.5 teaspoon red chili powder, 0.75 teaspoon salt, 0.875 cup water, 0.75 teaspoon sugar, 1 green chilli, 0.625 teaspoon kasuri methi, 2 grams paneer, 2.5 tablespoon cream, 2 tablespoon coriander leaves, 3.5 tablespoon paneer
instructions: 1. Add tomatoes, dried red chilies, cashews, cinnamon stick, cloves and cardamoms to a pot or pan. 2. Pour water and cook covered until tomatoes turn soft and pulpy. 3. Cool this and add to a blender. Blend well till you get a smooth puree. The puree has to be smooth. If needed you may add little water and blend. 4. Heat oil in a pan. Then add add bay leaf and saute for a min. 5. Add finely chopped onions and saute until they turn completely and uniformly golden. 6. Next add ginger garlic paste and saute till the raw smell goes away. This takes about 1 to 2 mins. 7. Pour the tomato and spice puree to the pan. 8. Add red chili powder, salt, coriander powder and garam masala. 9. Mix and saute for about 2 to 3 mins. Cook covered for another 2 to 3 mins until the gravy thickens slightly. 10. Pour water and add sugar. Mix well. Cook covered on a medium heat until the gravy thickens and you see traces of oil over the gravy. 11. Taste test and add more salt if needed. Take kasuri methi to your palm, crush it and add it here. Also add grated paneer, cubed paneer and green chili. 12. Mix everything well and lower the flame completely. Cook just for 1 to 2 mins. Turn off the stove. 13. Pour cream and mix. Add coriander leaves and mix. 14. Keep the pan covered for 5 mins so paneer absorbs the flavors. Transfer to a serving bowl and garnish with coriander leaves. You can also garnish with more grated paneer & cream. 15. Serve paneer lababdar hot with roti, garlic naan or rice.

title: Paneer Makhani Recipe
ingredients: 17 cashewnuts, 2.25 cups tomatoes, 2 cups cups paneer, 1.5 tablespoons butter, 2 green cardamoms, 1 inch cinnamon piece, 2 cloves 2 cloves ((optional, but recommended)), 1 green chili, 1 teaspoon ginger garlic paste, 1 teaspoon red chilli powder, salt as needed, 0.75 teaspoon sugar, 0.125 teaspoon turmeric, 1.25 teaspoon garam masala, 0.5 teaspoon kasuri methi, 0.25 cup cream
instructions: 1. If using store bought paneer, then soak it in hot water for 15 to 20 mins. 2. Drain the water and use. This will keep the paneer soft. 3. Blend together tomatoes and cashews to a smooth puree. 4. If you do not have a powerful blender then just soak cashews for 10 to 15 mins in hot water. 5. The tomato puree has to be smooth. 6. If you find your puree coarse, then pass it through a strainer. 7. Heat a pan with butter. saute cloves, cinnamon, cardamoms and green chili. 8. Then saute ginger garlic paste for 2 mins until the raw smell has gone. 9. Transfer the tomato puree to the pan. 10. The tomato puree will begin to splash so be cautious. 11. Cover partially and cook until the puree thickens. 12. Add chili powder, turmeric and salt. 13. Saute until the mixture leaves the sides of the pan. 14. Next add garam masala and saute for 1 to 2 mins. 15. Pour 1 cup water and sugar. Adjust the amount of water as needed to make a gravy. 16. Cook until the paneer makhani gravy thickens & you see traces of butter or grease on top. 17. Add the paneer & kasuri methi to the makhani gravy. 18. Stir gently and cook covered for 2 mins on a low heat. 19. Pour the cream and allow to bubble. 20. Transfer to a serving bowl and garnish with cream and coriander leaves. 21. Serve paneer makhani with roti, naan or jeera rice.

title: Paneer Manchurian Recipe
ingredients: 2 grams paneer, 0.25 cup all-purpose flour, 3 tablespoons corn flour, 0.25 teaspoon Salt, 0.5 teaspoon red chilli powder replace with pepper powder, Water, Oil, 1 tablespoon oil, 0.625 tablespoon garlic, 0.625 tablespoon ginger, 0.25 cup spring onion, 0.375 cup capsicum, 1 teaspoon corn flour, 0.5 cup water, 1.5 tablespoons soya sauce, 2 tablespoons tomato sauce, 0.875 teaspoon sugar, 0.625 teaspoon black pepper, 0.625 teaspoon vinegar, Salt, 2 tablespoons Spring onion
instructions: 1. Prepare all the ingredients for making the sauce and set aside. Heat oil in a kadai for deep frying paneer. 2. While the oil heats, add corn flour, maida, salt and chilli powder to a bowl. Add water just enough to make a free flowing yet thick batter. The consistency must be moderately thick, not too thick or too runny. 3. Test if the oil is hot by dropping a small portion of the batter to the oil. It should immediately sizzle and come up to the surface without turning brown. This is the right temperature. 4. Dip the paneer cubes in the batter and gently drop to the hot oil. Do not disturb them for a minute or 2 until the coating set firmly over the paneer. 5. Stir often and fry on a medium heat until golden and crisp. Remove them to a steel colander. 6. To a mixing bowl add 1 tablespoon soya sauce, 2 teaspoons ketchup (tomato sauce), little sugar and mix well. Add paneer and give a good stir. 7. To a small bowl, add 1 tablespoon corn starch, 1 tablespoon all-purpose flour and 2 to 3 pinches of black pepper. Mix together and sprinkle all over the paneer. 8. Coat the paneer well with the flour. If the mixture is too dry, sprinkle little water. Each piece of paneer should be well coated with a thick batter. 9. Heat a tablespoon of oil in a non-stick pan. When the oil turns hot, place each piece in the oil, each atleast an inch apart. Fry until crisp on both the sides, turning to the other after one side turns golden. 10. To air fry, place them on a sheet and air fry for 6 to 8 mins at 350 F or 180 C, turn the pieces and air fry for another 5 mins. 11. Add 1 teaspoon corn starch to a bowl and add ½ cup water. Mix and make a lump free slurry. Keep this aside. 12. Heat 1 tbsp of oil in a wok. Regulate the flame to high. Add ginger garlic and saute for a min for the nice aroma to comes out. 13. Next add spring onion whites and fry for a min or two. Add capsicum and saute for another minute. 14. Pour soya sauce, tomato sauce and corn slurry. Make sure you stir the corn slurry once before pouring to the pan. 15. Add more water if needed. Reduce the flame to medium and add sugar. Stir and check the taste test. 16. Add black pepper, salt and vinegar if needed. Let the sauce boil and cook down to a thick consistency. 17. Add the fried paneer and spring onion greens. Toss the paneer in the sauce well to coat evenly. 18. Serve paneer manchurian hot with noodles or fried rice.

title: Paneer Pakora Recipe
ingredients: 2 grams paneer, 0.25 tsp red chili powder, 0.5 tsp chaat masala, 0.5 tsp garam masala, Oil for deep frying, 0.5 cup besan, 2 tbsps rice flour, 0.25 tsp red chili powder, Salt as needed, 0.875 tsp ajwain, 1 tsp ginger green chilli paste, Water as needed
instructions: 1. Cut paneer to desired sizes. 2. Sprinkle red chilli powder, garam masala and chaat masala. 3. Toss them well in the bowl. Set this aside. 4. Heat oil in a deep kadai on a medium heat. 5. Add besan, rice flour, salt, ajwain, red chilli powder, ginger chilli paste to a bowl. 6. Pour water just enough to make a thick but free flowing batter. 7. When the oil is hot enough, dip each paneer piece in the batter, swipe off the excess dripping batter. 8. Gently slide to the hot oil. You can use your fingers or a table spoon to do this. 9. Do not disturb them for about a minute. 10. Gently keep stirring & flipping to fry evenly until they turn golden. 11. Drain them to a colander. Sprinkle some chaat masala. 12. Serve paneer pakora hot with chutney or a cup of tea.

title: Paneer Paratha Recipe (Plain Layered and Stuffed)
ingredients: 2 cups wheat flour, 0.25 teaspoon sea salt, 1 tablespoon oil, 0.625 cup water, 2.5 tablespoons ghee, 25 grams Paneer, 3 tablespoons coriander leaves, 1.5 green chilies, 0.5 teaspoon sea salt, 0.625 teaspoon Kashmiri red chilli powder, 0.875 teaspoon garam masala, 0.75 teaspoon roasted cumin powder, 0.625 teaspoon amchur powder, 0.3333333333333333 teaspoon dry ginger powder, 0.375 teaspoon ajwain, 1 tablespoon Kasuri methi
instructions: 1. Make Dough: Add flour, salt, oil and ½ cup water to a large mixing bowl. Begin to mix, adding more water as required to form a soft dough, (not sticky). Cover and rest the dough until you prepare the filling or for 20 mins. 2. Take ¼ cup flour in a bowl and set aside to help you roll the parathas. 3. Make the filling: Mix together grated paneer, salt, herbs and all the spices in another bowl. Taste test to adjust salt and spices. Divide the filling roughly to 8 portions. 4. Roll the paratha: Divide the dough to 8 portions. Roll the dough in your palms to form balls. Keep them covered until used. Sprinkle little flour over the rolling area & take a ball and dip it in the flour. 5. Method 1 – Dust off the excess in the bowl & roll to a 5 inch disc or roti. Or shape the dough to a small cup with your fingers, starting from the center. (Refer stepwise pics in the post) 6. Place a portion of paneer stuffing in the center. Push down the stuffing & gently bring the sides of the roti up, shaping like a cup. Repeat the steps of pushing the stuffing inside with your finger and bring the dough up until the edges come over the stuffing. (Refer video). 7. Bring the edges together over the stuffing and join them to seal carefully. 8. Method 2 – Roll a 5 inch roti and place the paneer stuffing in the center, hold the edges and make small pleats, across the roti. Bring all of them on top and join them together to seal the stuffing. Remove the excess dough if any on top. 9. Dip the dough ball in flour, shake off the excess and flatten it gently to spread the stuffing evenly. This ensures the stuffing does not come out. 10. Sprinkle some flour on the rolling area. Place the paneer paratha disc on the rolling area with the sealed side down. Begin to roll evenly all over without putting pressure. 11. Do not roll the paneer paratha continuously. Instead rotate in clock-wise direction a little after every 4 to 6 rolls to ensure it does not tear or get stuck at the bottom. Sprinkle flour as needed and avoid using excess. If you see the stuffing coming out, gently smear some flour to seal it. 12. Roll the paneer paratha as thin or thick as per your preference without tearing. 13. Transfer the rolled ones to a dry counter space. Once you are done with 3 to 4 begin to fry them. 14. Method 3 – With this method, you will be able to make only 6 large parathas. So divide the dough to 12 and stuffing to 6 parts. Roll 2 rotis of 6 inches each. Spread the paneer mixture on one of the roti, leaving a quarter inch on the edges. Wet the edges with little water. 15. Place the other roti on top of it. Press down gently on the sides to seal both the rotis. Then sprinkle flour and roll it evenly as thin as you want. 16. Heat a pan/tawa or griddle to cook the paneer parathas. The griddle should be hot enough else the parathas will turn hard. 17. When the pan is hot, gently transfer a rolled paneer paratha over it. (Make sure you dust off the excess flour before frying) 18. Let cook for 2 to 3 mins, turn it to the other side and cook for another 2 mins. Turn it again. 19. You will see the paneer paratha begins to puff and cook well. Drizzle ghee or oil all over and turn it to the other side and cook. 20. Smear some ghee on this side as well and press down the edges to cook thoroughly, until you see golden to light brown spots. 21. Remove the paneer paratha to a plate and stack them. 22. Top paneer paratha with some butter and serve with coriander chutney, mint chutney, pickle or yogurt if preferred.

title: Paneer Pasanda Recipe
ingredients: 2.5 tablespoons oil, 1.75 cups onions, 0.5 inch ginger, 4 medium garlic, 1.5 cups tomatoes, 2 soaked cashews, 0.5 cup curd, 1.25 tablespoon butter, 1 green chili, 1 bay leaf, 1 strand mace, 1 black cardamom, 4 green cardamoms, 4 cloves 4 cloves, 1.5 inch cinnamon, 0.5 teaspoon Kashmiri red chili powder, 0.75 teaspoon garam masala, 1.5 to 1 cups hot water, 0.875 teaspoon salt, 0.375 teaspoon sugar, 0.25 teaspoon black pepper, 1 teaspoon kasuri methi, 0.125 teaspoon nutmeg, 1 pinch saffron, 4 grams Paneer, 1 teaspoon ghee, 1.5 tbsps cashew nuts, 0.125 teaspoon garam masala, 0.125 teaspoon cumin powder, 0.125 teaspoon red chili powder, 0.125 teaspoon ginger, 0.125 teaspoon amchur, 0.125 teaspoon salt, 1.25 tablespoon coriander chutney, 1 tablespoon heavy cream, 1 tablespoon coriander leaves
instructions: 1. Heat oil in a deep pot and saute onions on a medium heat for 10 minutes. Add the ginger and garlic and continue to saute on a low heat until deep golden (caramelized) but not burnt. 2. Cool this and add to a blender along with tomatoes, soaked cashews and curd. Make a smooth paste. 3. Add butter to the same pot and heat it on a low heat. Add the whole spices – bay leaf, mace, cinnamon, cloves, green and black cardamoms and green chilies. 4. Let them sizzle in the butter for a minute and stir in the red chili powder and garam masala, followed by the onion tomato puree. 5. Be careful as this can splutter. Quickly cover and cook for 8 minutes on a low to medium heat (leaving the spoon in & stir after every 3 minutes without uncovering). 6. Turn down the heat and stir in the hot water. Cover and simmer until the gravy turns thick and creamy. 7. Meanwhile, trim the edges of the paneer blocks to cut down about 35 to 40 grams ( ¼ cup crumbled). Cube the rest of the paneer to 8 portions. 8. Make a slit on the paneer portions, keeping one side intact (refer the pictures in the post). 9. Mix together the crumbled paneer with salt, coriander chutney, chopped cashews, red chili powder, amchur, garam masala and dried ginger. Taste test and add more spice to your taste. The mixture has to be moist. 10. Carefully fill the paneer sandwiches with this mixture, making sure to keep one side intact. (do not break). Keep them aside. 11. Check the gravy, it should be thick and creamy. Add nutmeg, kasuri methi, black pepper, sugar and salt. Taste test and adjust all the spices if you want. 12. Sprinkle saffron or rose water or kewda. Turn off and keep covered for at least 5 to 10 minutes. 13. Heat a pan with ghee or oil and place the paneer sandwiches. Fry them on a medium to medium-high heat until golden, using a wooden tong or 2 spatulas, hold the paneer sandwiches and turn them to the other side. 14. Fry them until crisp and golden. 15. The gravy becomes thick after cooling down a bit. Serve the gravy with Basmati rice, naan or roti. Place the sandwiches in the gravy and garnish with coriander leaves & cream.

title: Paneer popcorn
ingredients: 1.25 cup paneer, 0.25 cup flour, 1.5 tsp ginger garlic paste, Salt as needed, 0.625 tsp red chilli powder, 0.375 tsp pepper, 0.75 tsp garam masala, 0.75 tsp herbs, 0.5 cup bread crumbs, Salt as needed, 0.25 tsp red chili powder
instructions: 1. Cut paneer to one by half inch cubes. Keep these aside. 2. Add red chili powder, salt and bread crumbs to a wide bowl. Mix everything well and set aside. 3. Add flour, salt, ginger garlic paste, pepper, herbs, red chili powder and garam masala to a mixing bowl. 4. Add water little by little and make a thick batter. Too thick batter will make them hard so it has to be moderately thick. 5. Add the paneer cubes to this and coat them well with the batter. 6. Roll each of these batter coated paneer cubes in bread crumbs. 7. Gently press down the crumbs to the paneer. 8. Refrigerate for about 15 mins. 9. Heat oil in a kadai. When the oil is hot enough, add the bread coated paneer cubes in the hot oil. 10. Regulate the flame to medium and fry stirring until golden. 11. Drain them when golden and crunchy. To fry the next batch, discard the bread crumbs that settle at the bottom of the kadai. Then add the next batch. 12. Serve paneer popcorn with red chilli sauce or schezwan sauce.

title: Paneer Pulao Recipe (Paneer Rice)
ingredients: 1.5 cups basmati rice, 2.5 cups water for stovetop pressure cooker, 0.5 teaspoon salt, 2 tablespoons oil, 1 onion, 1.5 green chilies, 0.5 cup green peas, 0.5 cup carrots, 1 tablespoon ginger garlic paste, 0.25 cup coriander leaves, 13.5 mint leaves, 1 bay leaf, 0.5 teaspoon cumin seeds, 3.5 green cardamoms, 6 cloves 6 cloves, 1 inch cinnamon, 1 star anise, 1.5 cup paneer, 1.5 tablespoon oil, 0.75 teaspoon ginger garlic paste, 0.25 teaspoon red chili powder, 0.5 teaspoon garam masala, salt as needed, 1 tablespoon gram flour, 0.375 kasuri methi
instructions: 1. Prep-up paneer: Add all the marination ingredients including 1½ tablespoon oil to paneer. Mix and set aside. If it is too dry, sprinkle 1 tbsp water. Rinse and soak rice in water for 15 mins. 2. Make pulao rice: Heat oil or ghee in a pot or cooker. Add all the whole spices. When they begin to sizzle, add sliced onions & green chilies. Saute them until golden. 3. Saute ginger garlic paste till the raw smell is gone, for 1 minute. Add all the chopped veggies, mint and coriander leaves. Saute for 2 to 3 mins. Pour water and stir in salt. Taste test and adjust salt to make it slightly salty. 4. Bring it to a boil. Add soaked and drained rice and pressure cook for 1 whistle on a medium flame. If cooking in a pot, cover and cook on a low flame until the rice is done. 5. Fry Paneer: Meanwhile, heat a non-stick pan and place the marinated paneer cubes. Fry them until golden. If the pan looks too dry add some oil. 6. Assemble Paneer Pulao: When the pressure releases naturally, open the lid and fluff up. Remove to a serving plate and top the fried paneer. Serve paneer pulao with a raita or a salad.

title: Paneer Sandwich Recipe (3 Variations)
ingredients: 6 slices brown bread, 1 tablespoons butter, 0.5 cup paneer, 1 tablespoon sauce, 2 tablespoon tomatoes, 3 tablespoon sweet corn, 0.25 cup carrot, 2 tablespoon cucumber, 2 tablespoon coriander leaves, 1 green chilli, 0.25 teaspoon salt, 1 teaspoon lemon juice, 2 tablespoon green chutney, 6 slices bread, 1.5 tsp oil, 0.25 tsp cumin, 1 onion, 0.75 tsp ginger, 1 tomato, 1 Pinch turmeric, Salt, 0.25 tsp red chilli powder, 0.375 tsp garam masala powder, 0.625 cup paneer, 2 tbsp coriander leaves, 0.5 cup carrots, 0.25 cup capsicum, 0.5 tsp kasuri methi
instructions: 1. To a mixing bowl add crumbled paneer, carrots, cucumber, tomatoes, green chilies, coriander leaves and salt. 2. Add 1 tbsp sauce or green chutney or thick curd along with chaat masala. Mix well. 3. Taste this add more salt or sauce if needed. 4. Butter the bread slices on the outer side. Spread sauce or mayo or curd or green chutney on the inner side. 5. Spread the paneer mixture over the sauce and cover with another slice. Toast the paneer sandwich in a toaster or tawa until golden. 6. Heat 1 tsp oil in a pan. Add a pinch of cumin & fry until they begin to sizzle. 7. Add 1 chopped garlic and fry until fragrant. 8. Then add ¼ to ½ tsp red chili powder, ¼ to ½ tsp garam masala, ¼ to ½ tsp kasuri methi and salt. 9. Add 1/3 cup crumbled paneer and fry just for 1 minute. Sprinkle coriander leaves. 10. Butter 4 bread slices on one side. Spread the paneer on the other side. 11. Place some onion slices, tomatoes & cucumbers. You can also smear some green chutney or tomato sauce on the inner side of bread slices. Toast on the same tawa. 12. Heat oil and add cumin seeds. When they splutter add onions and fry until golden. 13. Add grated ginger & fry for a minute. Next put in tomatoes and salt. Saute untill soft. 14. Stir in red chili powder, garam masala, kasuri methi & amchur powder. Mix and fry for a minute. 15. Turn off the stove and add in crumbled paneer and coriander leaves. Check salt and spice add more if needed. Remove this to a bowl. 16. Grilling on tawa or griddle - Apply ghee or butter on one side of the bread slice. Toast bread slices till golden. Then place the prepared paneer. Layer with some grated carrots, beets & onions. Cover with another slice and press down the paneer sandwiches with a flat wooden spoon. 17. Grilling in toaster - You can also grill these in a toaster. Cut and serve paneer sandwich.

title: Paneer Tikka Masala Recipe
ingredients: 25 grams paneer, 0.5 cup Hung curd, 1 teaspoon ginger garlic paste, 0.375 teaspoon Kashmiri red chili powder, 0.5 teaspoon garam masala, 0.25 teaspoon Kasuri Methi, 0.125 teaspoon turmeric, 0.25 teaspoon salt, 1 teaspoon lemon juice, 1 teaspoon oil, 0.5 bell pepper, 0.5 onion, 2 tablespoons oil, 2 green cardamoms, 1 cup onions, 1.5 cup tomatoes, 1.5 teaspoon ginger garlic paste, 14 cashew nuts, 0.875 teaspoon red chili powder less spicy kind, 0.875 teaspoon garam masala, 0.25 teaspoon salt, 1 teaspoon sugar, 0.75 cup water, 1 teaspoon kasuri methi, 3 tablespoons Heavy cream, 2 tablespoons coriander leaves
instructions: 1. To a mixing bowl, add yogurt, red chili powder, ginger garlic paste, garam masala, turmeric, kasuri methi, oil, lemon juice & salt. 2. Mix well and taste test it. Add more salt if required. Add paneer cubes & gently mix well to coat the paneer with the marinade. 3. Optional - If using onions & bell peppers, dice them to the same size as that of paneer. Separate the layers in the onion. Add them along with bell pepper and marinate gently. Set this aside until the gravy is done. 4. Heat half of the oil and add the chopped onions. Saute them well until transparent or golden on a medium heat. 5. Add tomatoes, cashews, sugar and salt. Saute all of these well until the tomatoes turn mushy and soft. 6. Stir in red chilli powder & garam masala, Saute for another 2 mins, until the masala smells good & aromatic. 7. Cool this completely and add to a blender. Pour water and blend to a smooth puree. 8. Heat the rest of the oil in a pan on a low flame and add the whole spices. When they sizzle, add ginger garlic paste & saute for 1 to 2 mins, until the raw smell disappears. 9. Pour the onion tomato puree. (Careful as it may splash). At this step, taste test and add more red chilli powder if you want to achieve a brighter color. 10. Pour water to bring this to a gravy consistency. Cook covered until the gravy turns thick for about 10 mins and you see traces of oil on top. Keep stirring in between. 11. Once the gravy is thick yet of a pouring consistency, you may remove and discard the whole spices. Crush the kasuri methi in your palms and sprinkle it. (optional). Taste the gravy & add more salt if needed. Rest this aside. 12. Option 1 - Grill in oven. Preheat the oven to 430 F or 220 C for at least 15 mins. Line a baking tray with foil. Thread paneer, onion & bell peppers alternately on skewers. Place the skewers in the baking tray & grill for 8 to 10 minutes, turning them to the other side after 5 mins. Remove them when golden and crisp. 13. Option 2 - To make on griddle. Heat a tsp oil in a wide pan on a high flame, coat each paneer piece with the marinade & place on the griddle. Cook on a high flame till the marinade dries up and paneer turns golden. Similarly place the bell peppers and marinated onions and fry until the marinade dries up. 14. Option 3 - Air fryer. If your air fryer needs preheating, you may preheat it for 5 mins first. Thread paneer, onion, bell pepper on skewers. Air fry the skewers placed in a tray at 390 F or 200 C for 5 mins. Brush them with the marinade and then air fry for another 2 mins. The marinade has to partially dried up. Also adjust the air fryer time as needed. 15. Transfer grilled paneer tikka along with veggies & onions to the tikka masala gravy. 16. Pour the cream. Gently mix. Garnish with cream and coriander leaves. 17. Serve paneer tikka masala with Roti, Butter Naan, Basmati Rice or Jeera rice

title: Paneer Tikka Recipe in Oven, Air Fryer, Stovetop
ingredients: 1.5 tablespoon oil, 1.25 teaspoon red chilli powder, 0.625 teaspoon salt, 0.25 teaspoon turmeric, 1 teaspoon garam masala, 1.25 teaspoon coriander powder, 1 teaspoon chaat masala, 1 teaspoon kasuri methi, 1.25 teaspoon ginger garlic paste, 6 tablespoons greek yogurt, 2 grams paneer, 1 onion, 1 red capsicum, 1 green capsicum, 1 tablespoon mustard oil, 1 tablespoon gram flour, 0.375 teaspoon carom seeds
instructions: 1. To make this recipe, we need greek yogurt or hung curd (thick curd). If you do not have thick curd, you can check this post on how to make hung curd . I have used thick curd here. If you use runny yogurt, it will let out lot of whey while grilling and all the masala will come off to the tray. 2. Cube 200 to 250 grams paneer to 1½ inch cubes. Keep aside. Then choose 1 large onion and bell pepper. Rinse and cube them to almost the same size of paneer. Do not chop them small as they shrink after grilling. Separate the layers of onions. 3. To a mixing bowl, add 4. tablespoons thick curd 1 to 1½ teaspoon red chili powder 1/8 teaspoon turmeric 1 to 1½ teaspoon coriander powder ½ teaspoon salt 1 teaspoon garam masala 1 teaspoon chaat masala 5. Next add 6. Optional: If making on a griddle or tawa add 1 tablespoon roasted besan or sattu (roasted gram flour). Dry roast besan until aromatic. It should lose the raw flavor, cool and add it. Do not skip using besan it helps to hold the curd well to the paneer tikka. It also brings out a good flavor and will avoid messing up the tawa. 7. Add 1½ tablespoon oil. I have used mustard oil, you can also use any other oil. 8. Mix everything well. The marinade must be thick. Taste the marinade and add more salt and spices if needed. 9. Add capsicum and onions. Coat them well with marinade. 10. Move the veggies to one side of the bowl and add 200 grams cubed paneer and gently coat them as well. Handle with care they may break. 11. Cover and rest them in the fridge for minimum of 30 mins or an hour. If you leave them for too long, sometimes they break when you thread them on to the skewer later. If you prefer to keep them longer, thread them first and then refrigerate. 12. Next begin to thread them on skewers. With the quantities mentioned in the recipe card you can thread them on 3 large or 4 medium skewers. Paneer tikka on tawa and oven were made at 2 different times so the number of skewers seen in the pictures will not match the recipe. 13. Pour 1½ tablespoons mustard oil to a pan. Add 2½ tablespoons of besan. I highly recommend using mustard oil here for flavor. But I have used regular cooking oil in the pictures. 14. Mix well to a smooth mixture. Turn on the stove and fry this on a low heat, stirring continuously until fragrant. The mixture begins to bubble up and turn frothy. Once you smell it good and fragrant, turn off the heat. 15. Add 16. to 1 ¼ teaspoon red chilli powder ½ teaspoon salt ¼ teaspoon turmeric 1 to 1¼ teaspoon garam masala ½ to 1 teaspoon coriander powder 1 tablespoon crushed kasuri methi / dried fenugreek leaves 1 teaspoon ginger garlic paste ½ to ¾ teaspoon ajwain / carom seeds 17. Mix all of these and let them cool down completely. Then add 6 tablespoons thick curd or greek yogurt and add 1 tablespoon lemon juice or ¾ to 1 teaspoon chaat masala. Skip the lemon juice if your yogurt is even slightly sour. 18. Mix everything. Taste test and adjust any the seasoning. The marinade has to be thick as you see in the picture here. 19. Add 200 to 250 grams of paneer, 1 medium onion and 1 cup cubed bell peppers. Mix them gently. Cover and refrigerate in the fridge for at least 20 to 30 mins. To store longer I thread them on the skewers and then store. 20. To grill them on direct fire, in oven or air fryer, thread them on the skewers, alternating paneer, bell peppers and onions.

title: Paratha Recipe (Flaky South Asian Flatbread)
ingredients: 2.5 cups wheat flour, 0.875 cup warm water, 0.5 teaspoon salt, 1 tablespoon neutral oil, 3.5 tablespoons melted ghee
instructions: 1. Stir flour, salt & oil in a mixing bowl (or food processor). Pour water (start with lower amount) & mix to form a soft non-sticky dough, adding more water as required. 2. Knead/process until the dough is soft and pliable, for 3 mins. Smear a few drops of oil, cover and let rest for 20 mins to 1 hour. 3. Add ¼ cup flour to a medium sized plate or bowl & reserve aside to roll the parathas. Divide the dough to 8 portions. Smoothen and roll them to balls in between your palms. Keep covered until the end. 4. Sprinkle a tsp of flour over the rolling area and place the dough ball over it. Flatten it with fingers and coat with flour on both the sides. 5. Roll to a 5 to 6 inch paratha. Apply ⅓ tsp melted ghee/ oil all over and sprinkle 2 pinches of flour all over. 6. Triangle Parathas – Fold it to half, to make a semicircle. Apply ghee over the semicircle & sprinkle a pinch of flour. Fold it again to get a triangle. 7. Square Parathas - Fold the left ⅓ part over the middle ⅓. Fold the right ⅓ part over the middle. Apply a few drops of ghee all over the visible part & sprinkle a pinch of flour. Fold the top ⅓ over the middle ⅓ and fold the bottom ⅓ over the middle, to get a square. 8. Sprinkle flour on both the sides lightly and roll to a 6 to 7 inch square or triangle paratha. When you are done rolling 4 to 5, you may begin to fry. 9. Heat a tawa/ pan on a medium flame, until it is medium hot. Dust off any excess flour from the paratha & transfer to the tawa. 10.
