When you see bubbles on top (takes me about 40 to 60 seconds), turn it to the other side and drizzle with ¼ teaspoon ghee or oil. Gently spread the ghee all over with a spatula. 11. When it begins to puff (within a minute), turn it to the other side and drizzle more ghee. Press down gently with the spatula, until it puffs and light brown spots appear. Press down on the edges to cook well. 12. Remove and stack the parathas in a clean kitchen cloth lined in a casserole or on a wired rack. To fry the next paratha, make sure the pan is hot enough, but not smoking hot. 13. Serve parathas with your favorite curry. Store the leftovers in an airtight container and refrigerate up to 3 days. Heat up on a medium hot pan.

title: Pasta soup recipe
ingredients: 1 tbsp butter, 1 tbsp garlic, 1.5 cups veggies, 1.5 medium tomato, 1 cup pasta, 2 cups water, Salt as needed, 0.5 tsp Herbs, 0.5 tsp Pepper, Any other seasoning as desired
instructions: 1. Pour 6 cups of water to a large pot and bring it to a boil. Cook pasta until al dente. Drain and rinse them well in a colander. 2. Add butter to a hot pot. 3. Saute garlic until it smells good. 4. Saute the veggies along with garlic for 3 mins. I did not have potatoes so didn’t use, but I suggest using since they help to slightly thicken the soup. You can also use corn. 5. Puree tomatoes and add them to the pot. This step is optional. I used since there was no lemon at home. I usually add lemon juice to the warm soup just before serving. 6. Cook for 3 to 4 mins. I prefer to do this on a high flame until the tomatoes dry up. 7. Pour 2 cups of water. 8. Simmer covered with a small vent open to let out the steam. Cooking on a low heat makes the soup tasty. 9. Once the veggies are tender, add the cooked pasta and stir. Add more water if needed to suit your consistency. 10. Cook for 2 to 3 mins. 11. Add pepper. 12. Add oregano. Serve hot or warm with spicy roasted boiled eggs or toasted bread. You can also serve with Garlic cheese toast French toast egg bread toast 13. You can also skip this step and cook the pasta along with the veggies. To get rid of the excess starch I have preboiled them first. Bring 6 cups of water to a boil and add pasta. Cook until al dente. Drain to a colander and rinse them well. 14. Heat butter in a pot, saute garlic until you begin to smell the garlic good. 15. Saute the veggies for about 2 to 3 mins until you smell them good. 16. Add tomato puree and saute on high for 3 to 4 mins. If using lemon juice, skip using tomato. 17. Pour water and cook on a low heat until the veggies are soft and tender. 18. Add salt and pasta. Adjust water as needed. 19. Cook further for 2 to 3mins. 20. Add pepper and herbs. Serve pasta soup hot / warm. Add lemon juice just before serving. 21. Sprinkle your favorite seasoning if desired.

title: Pav Bhaji Masala Recipe
ingredients: 3 tablespoons coriander seeds, 2.5 tablespoons cumin seeds, 2 teaspoons pepper corn, 1 tablespoon fennel seeds, 5.5 red chilies, 3 inch cinnamon, 1 cloves 10 cloves ((laung)), 2 black cardamoms, 1 tablespoon amchur powder, 1 star anise, 4 green cardamoms, 1.5 bay leaves, 0.5 teaspoon ginger powder
instructions: 1. Add a large star anise to a pan and let it heat up gently. Dry roast until you get a nice floral aroma. Remove to a plate to cool. I prefer to dry roast these slowly, separately & for a little longer. With this step your pav bhaji masala won’t have a lot of star anise grits. 2. To the same pan, add 5 to 6 dried red chilies (deseeded & broken), 3 inch cinnamon, 2 black cardamoms, 1 to 2 bay leaf and 3 tablespoons coriander seeds. 3. Dry roast on a low heat until they turn crunchy and aromatic. Transfer to the plate for cooling. 4. Add 2 ½ tablespoons cumin seeds, 1 tablespoon fennel seeds, 1 to 2 teaspoons black pepper corn (depending on your tolerance). Also add 10 cloves and 4 green cardamoms (optional). 5. Dry roast until the cumin seeds turn nutty, crunchy and well roasted. They must be roasted well like it is done for Roasted Jeera powder . Transfer to the plate and cool all of them. If your amchur & dry ginger are from refrigerator, you may gently warm them up in the same pan. This helps in better shelf life of the pav bhaji masala. 6. Add all the roasted and cooled ingredients. 7. I personally love using a little Kashmiri red chilli powder as it imparts a better color than the dried red chilies. This is optional. I use about a teaspoon. 8. Add half teaspoon dried ginger powder and 1 tablespoon amchur. You can also add 1/4 teaspoon hing if you want but I don’t. 9. Grind to a fine powder. Make sure you don’t grind too long, until the pav bhaji masala becomes hot. I always find the star anise to be a little gritty but after cooking it just seems fine. If you want you can sieve it. Cool completely.

title: Pea Shoots Recipe
ingredients: 2 grams pea shoots, 1 tablespoon garlic, 1 green chili, 1 tablespoon oil, 0.125 teaspoon salt
instructions: 1. Fresh pea shoots won’t require a lot of cleaning because they are grown in hygienic and cleaner atmosphere. But still you may simply scan through to find and discard grass or unwanted greens. This is how mine looks before the clean-up. If you find some of them with fibrous stems, you may trim them off. But It is tedious to do that, so invest a little time in choosing very young shoots at the stores. 2. Here is how I rinse and drain them – Fill water in a large bowl and stir in 2 tablespoons of vinegar and 1 tablespoon sea salt. Add the pea shoots and let rest for 2 to 3 mins and rinse off 2 to 3 times with fresh water. Remove them to a colander and shake off gently to bring out the excess moisture. Let drain completely, until all the excess water is out of the pea shots. 3. I simply let mine sit in the colander for 30 mins while I cook other dishes for the meal. If required pat dry with a cotton towel or kitchen papers. It is very essential not to cook them without draining well else you will end up with a pool of water at the bottom of the pan. If you want you may chop them finely before cooking. 4. Pour oil to a wok and add 1 tablespoon minced garlic and 1 deseeded & minced green chili or ¼ tsp chili flakes. (I do not wait for the oil to heat up here.) 5. Let the garlic and chili cook on a low heat for a minute, until aromatic. Do not brown. 6. Add the pea shoots and sprinkle ⅛ teaspoon salt. I usually don’t add salt because they are sweet and I don’t feel salt is required. If you want you may add a teaspoon of organic soya sauce or tamari but I am not using it here. 7. Increase the flame to high and stir fry just for a minute, until wilted. Turn off and do not overcook. Remove to a serving plate immediately and serve.

title: Peanut Chutney Recipe (Groundnut Chutney)
ingredients: 0.5 cup raw peanuts, 1.5 teaspoon oil, 1 garlic clove, 1.5 green chilies, 0.25 teaspoon salt, 0.5 teaspoon cumin seeds, 0.625 cup water, 1.5 tablespoons urad dal, 0.5 teaspoon tamarind, 1.5 sprigs curry leaves, 1 teaspoon oil, 0.25 teaspoon mustard, 1 red chili, 1 pinch hing, 1 garlic, 1 sprig curry leaves
instructions: 1. Pour 1 tsp oil to a pan or kadai and heat it. Add 2 to 3 slit green chilies, 1 garlic clove or (¼ inch peeled ginger) and 1½ tablespoon urad dal or chana dal (Bengal gram). To make Andhra style peanut chutney, garlic is not fried but added directly to the chutney jar later. So if you want you can also add it directly to the jar. Dal lends a nutty aroma to the chutney. However you can skip it here. 2. Begin to fry them on a medium to low heat until dal turns golden & aromatic. 3. Remove the fried ingredients to a plate. 4. Pluck the mint leaves from the stems and add them to a bowl of water. Rinse them & set aside to drain completely.Heat oil in a pan and fry the chilies for 2 to 3 mins until they get blistered. Remove to a plate. 5. Add the peanuts to the pan and fry them on a medium heat until golden & aromatic. Then turn off and add cumin. Fry for 30 seconds 6. I always use the raw mint leaves to make this chutney. But if you do not like to use them raw then fry them for about 1 to 2 mins. 7. Cool and transfer all the ingredients to a blender jar. Add ginger or garlic, salt and little water. 8. Blend to smooth chutney adding more water if needed. 9. This chutney needs no tempering. If you prefer then heat 1 tsp oil. Add a pinch of mustard and a broken red chilli. 10. When the seeds splutter, then add curry leaves. The leaves will turn crisp then add hing. Pour this over the peanut chutney. Serve with idli, dosa, paratha. 11. Ensure you use cubed onions instead of chopped. The flavor and taste is best with cubed onions. 12. Heat oil in a pan. Fry the peanuts on a medium heat until golden & aromatic. 13. Add cumin and garlic to the hot pan and saute for 1 to 2 mins. Remove these and set aside. I used rinsed peanuts here so the skin got charred too quickly before they were roasted. 14. Add another teaspoon oil and fry the onions and slit green chilies for 3 to 4 mins. Or until the onions turn pink to golden. The raw smell of the onion should go away. You can also add curry leaves along with the onions if you want. 15. Cool all the ingredients. Blend them together with salt and water as needed. If using fried gram and tamarind paste you can add them to the jar now. 16. Make a smooth chutney adding more water as needed. Taste test and add more salt. Pour more water if needed to bring it to consistency. Remove this to a serving bowl. 17. Tempering is optional. But you can if you prefer. Heat 1 tablespoon oil and add a pinch of mustard seeds, 1 pinch of cumin seeds (optional), 1 pinch of urad dal. When the dal turns golden, add curry leaves and red chilli. Turn off when the curry leaves turn crisp. Add a pinch of hing. 18. Pour the tempering to the chutney. Serve peanut chutney with dosa, idli and uthappam.

title: Peanut Rice Recipe
ingredients: 1.5 cups rice, 0.25 cup peanuts, 1.5 tbsp chana dal, 1 tbsp urad dal, 1 garlic clove, 2.5 sprigs curry leaves, 2.5 red chilies, 0.625 tsp cumin, 1 tbsp desiccated coconut, 2 tbsps oil
instructions: 1. Dry roast dal until golden on a medium to low heat and set aside. I usually roast the chana dal and urad dal in bulk for my breakfast chutney. So I just used them up. 2. Dry roast peanuts well along with garlic until half roasted. Garlic is roasted here to remove the pungent smell. 3. Then add curry leaves and red chilies. Roast until the peanuts emit a nice good aroma. Curry leaves and red chilies should become crisp. 4. Add cumin, coconut or sesame seeds at this stage and switch off the stove. 5. Cool them completely and add to a blender or mini blender jar. You can also set aside some peanuts for garnish. 6. Powder coarse or finely to suit your taste. We like the coarse texture so I powdered the chana dal and urad dal first to a fine powder. Then coarsely ground the rest of the ingredients. 7. Cook and cool the rice as you would do regularly. There are 2 ways you can make the peanut rice. 8. First method: Heat oil in a kadai and temper with half tsp mustard seeds, 1 broken red chili, curry leaves and then hing. Later add the powder along with salt and switch off the stove. Mix the cooked and cooled rice. 9. Second method: This is the method I most often follow for the Lunch box. Cool the cooked rice completely. Add the peanut powder, salt and oil. I use either cold pressed organic sesame, peanut or olive oil. 10. Mix everything well. Serve peanut rice with a vegetable salad.

title: Peas Pulao (Matar Pulao Recipe)
ingredients: 1.5 cups aged basmati rice, 1.125 cup green peas, 0.75 cup onion, 1.5 green chilies, 1 tablespoon ginger garlic paste, 0.25 cup mint leaves, 2 tablespoons ghee, 1 cashews, 0.75 teaspoon salt, 1 teaspoon cumin seeds, 4.5 green cardamoms, 4.5 cloves 4 to 5 cloves, 1 bay leaf, 2 inch cinnamon piece, 1 star anise, 1.5 strands mace, 2.5 to 3 cups hot water, 1.75 cups water, 2.5 cups water
instructions: 1. Wash 1½ cups (275 grams) aged basmati rice a few times until the water runs clear. Soak it while you prepare the rest. Later drain and set aside. Prepare the following : 2. Rinse and drain 1 to 1¼ cup (160 grams) fresh or frozen green peas (matar) 3. Slit 1 to 2 green chilies (omit for low heat) 4. Fine chop ¼ cup mint leaves or coriander leaves (cilantro) 5. On a medium flame, heat 2 to 2½ tablespoons oil or ghee in a pressure cooker (or instant pot on saute mode). Optionally fry 10 cashews until golden and remove to a plate. Add the following whole spices: 6. bay leaf (Tej patta) 1 star anise 2 inch cinnamon piece 4 to 5 green cardamoms 1 strand mace 1 teaspoon cumin seeds (or shahi jeera/ Indian caraway seeds) 4 to 5 cloves 7. When the spices begin to sizzle, add sliced onions & green chili. 8. Saute the onions on a medium flame until deep golden and begin to caramelize, for 7 to 8 mins. (to cook in instant pot, saute only for 5 mins) 9. Add ginger garlic paste. 10. Saute on a low flame until a nice aroma comes out, for about 30 to 40 seconds. 11. Add green peas and fine chopped mint leaves. Saute for a minute or 2 until the mint leaves begin to smell good. 12. Add drained rice and optionally fry on a high flame for 2 to 3 minutes. Do not over mix or mix vigorously as the rice grains will break. 13. Add ¾ teaspoon salt and 2½ cups (600 ml) water to cook in stovetop pressure cooker or 1¾ cups (420 ml) water to cook in instant pot . Give a good mix and taste the water. It has to be slightly salty. 14. Add 2 to 2½ tbsp oil or ghee to a hot pot. Add 10 cashews, if you want split them and use. Fry them until golden on a medium to low flame and remove to a plate. Reserve to garnish. 15. To the same pot, add the following spices and let them sizzle for a few seconds. Do not burn. 16. star anise / chakri phool 1 strand mace/ javitri 4 to 5 green cardamoms / elaichi 4 to 5 cloves / lavang 1 tsp jeera / cumin (or shahi jeera) 1 bay leaf / tej patta 2 inch thin cinnamon piece. If using true cinnamon like me, you can use up to 3 – 2 inch pieces. 17. Add ¾ cup sliced onions and 1 to 2 slit green chilies. 18. Saute until they turn golden, for 6 to 8 mins. 19. Lower the flame and add 1 tablespoon ginger garlic paste. 20. Saute until the raw smell is gone. Add mint leaves here and fry for 2 mins. I forgot at this stage so added later. 21. Add 1 to 1¼ cup green peas. 22. Add soaked and drained rice. 23. Fry on a high heat for 2 to 3 mins without stirring a lot. 24. Pour hot water. Be cautious with the amount of water you use. You will need anywhere from 2½ cups (600 ml) to 3 cups (720 ml) cups. Since I use aged rice, I use 3 cups. If in doubt start with 2.5 cups and add more hot water as you cook. 25. Add salt. Stir and taste the water. It must be medium salty. 26. Since I forgot to add the mint leaves earlier I added them here. 27. Allow to cook on a medium heat for 8 mins, until most of the water is absorbed. But it must be still soggy as you see below. 28. Cover the pot at this stage, lower the flame and cook for 8 to 10 mins until all the water is absorbed and the rice is cooked through. Switch off the stove and allow to rest for a while. 29. Fluff it up with a fork.

title: Peda Recipe
ingredients: 1 tablespoon ghee, 0.5 cup milk, 1 cup milk powder, 0.375 teaspoon cardamom powder, 5.5 tablespoons powdered sugar, 1 pinch saffron, 1.5 cups crumbled mawa, 0.25 teaspoon cardamom powder, 1 teaspoon ghee, 0.5 tin condensed milk, 1 tablespoons pistachios, 1 pinch saffron strands
instructions: 1. Pour ½ tablespoon ghee and ½ cup milk to a non-stick pan. 2. When the milk turns warm, add the milk powder and mix well to make a smooth mixture. 3. Begin to heat on a medium flame and cook until it thickens and becomes sticky. 4. Then add ½ tablespoons ghee. Scrape off the sides with the spatula. Cook stirring continuously until it becomes thick and becomes a mass. (Check video). At this stage it will also leave the the pan. 5. Transfer this to a mixing bowl. Cool down. 6. When it comes down to warm temperature, add powdered sugar and cardamom powder. Mix well to incorporate the sugar. Taste test and add more sugar if needed. 7. Divide the dough to 8 portions and roll them to balls. Flatten and tuck a sliced pistachio. 8. Cool the milk pedas completely and store at room temperature for a day. Or refrigerate for longer shelf life. 9. Bring khoya to room temperature. Crumble or grate it and then measure. 10. Add it to a non-stick pan. Next add condensed milk along with cardamom powder and saffron milk (optional). 11. Mix and begin to cook on a medium flame. The mixture melts and turns gooey. 12. Let it cook till it thickens and becomes a mass. Overcooking can make it dry. Turn off and cool down. 13. The mixture looks smooth on its own, so don’t have to knead it. Grease your palms and make 12 balls. Next flatten them slightly. Tuck the chopped pistachios in the center. 14. Store pedas in a air tight container and refrigerate.

title: Pepper Chicken Recipe
ingredients: 1 cup onions, 0.75 tsp ginger garlic paste, 1 cup tomatoes, salt, 1.25 tsp pepper corn, 0.25 tsp red chili powder, 0.875 tsp garam masala powder, 1 sprig curry leaves, 0.5 tsp cumin, 2.5 green cardamoms, 1 inch cinnamon, 3 cloves 3 cloves (or laung), 2.5 tbsp oil, 5 grams chicken, 0.75 tsp ginger garlic paste, 0.125 tsp turmeric, salt little, 3.5 tbsp coconut milk
instructions: 1. Marinate chicken with ingredients under marination. Keep it aside until needed. 2. Pour oil to a hot pan. Add cumin, cinnamon, cloves and cardamom. Allow them to sizzle. 3. Next add curry leaves and ginger garlic paste. Fry till the raw smell of ginger garlic goes off. 4. Add onions and fry till they turn golden. 5. Then add tomatoes and salt. Fry till the tomatoes turn mushy and soft. 6. Add red chili powder, garam masala and coarsely crushed pepper corn. 7. Fry everything well till the mixture looses all the moisture. 8. Add chicken and fry for about 3 to 4 minutes. 9. Cover and cook on a low flame till the chicken turns tender. 10. If the chicken doesn't let out enough moisture to cook, then add little water. 11. Cover and cook until soft. If you want to have gravy, 12. You can take it off while there is moisture. You can also pour 3 to 4 tbsp coconut milk. 13. If you prefer a dry version, cook without lid and evaporate the moisture in the chicken. fry till all the moisture dries up. 14. Serve pepper chicken hot with rice and Rasam. Squeeze in some lemon juice before serving.

title: Perugu vada
ingredients: 0.5 cup urad dal, 0.25 teaspoon salt, oil, 0.5 cup curd, 1.5 cups water, 0.5 teaspoon salt, 2.5 cups curd, 1 sprig curry leaves, 0.25 teaspoon mustard seeds, 0.5 teaspoon cumin seeds, 1 red chili, 2 green chilies, 1 teaspoon ginger, 1 pinch hing, 0.5 teaspoon salt, 1 tablespoon oil, 0.25 teaspoon turmeric, 0.25 cup khara boondi, 2 tablespoons coriander leaves
instructions: 1. Rinse urad dal at least 3 to 4 times in lots of water. Wash it well, rubbing with your hand. Drain the water and soak them in enough water for at least 5 hours. 2. To make the batter in the right consistency, follow this link to medu vada recipe. Also make and deep fry the garelu following the instructions there. 3. For 1 cup urad dal, you will be able to make 14 to 16 garelu depending on the size. Here I made perugu garelu with only 7 of them. If you want to make with all of them, double the full recipe. 4. Make the tempering – Heat 1 tablespoon oil in a small pan. When the oil becomes hot, add mustard, cumin and red chilies. I was running out of cumin, so added very little. 5. When the spices splutter, then add curry leaves, green chilies and ginger. Saute until the leaves turn crisp. Turn off. Lastly add hing and turmeric. Cool this completely. 6. Add yogurt to a bowl and whisk it well with some salt. If it is too thick then you may add little water. If the yogurt is too thick, it won’t be absorbed by the vadas. So add accordingly, it must not be too thick or too runny. I did not since mine had lot of whey in it. 7. Next add the cooled tempering to the curd and mix well. Taste test and add more salt and green chilies if needed. If you want add more turmeric to the tadka pan and then pour little yogurt into it. Pour that back here. 8. Drain the water from dal and add it to a grinder jar along with salt and 3 tablespoons water. You can also add half teaspoon cumin and 1 green chili. Blend it for a few minutes. Scrape the sides and sprinkle more water only if needed and blend to a smooth fluffy batter. Take care not to make it very runny. 9. The batter consistency must be thick and not very runny. If your batter becomes runny, you can add 2 to 3 tablespoons rice flour. Let it sit for 5 to 10 mins it turns thick. If you make them with runny batter, they will soak up oil. Mix and beat the batter well in clock-wise direction for 1 to 2 minutes to make it fluffy. 10. Fill a bowl with water or butter milk and stir some salt to it. Since these are smaller in size they are okay even if dropped in salted water. Set aside away from your stove along with the seasoned yogurt. 11. Heat oil in a pan for deep frying. Check if the oil is hot enough by gently dropping a small portion to the oil. It has to rise without browning. This is the correct temperature. Gently slide in a tbsp of batter each time in the hot oil. You can use your fingers to shape them or use a tablespoon. Fry them on a medium heat until crisp and golden. 12. Drop these hot punukulu in water or buttermilk. 13. After 1 to 2 mins, remove the fritters from water and squeeze each of them gently by pressing in between your palms. 14. Add these to the seasoned perugu/ yogurt. Set this aside in the refrigerator for a couple of hours until they soak well. 15. oil as needed for deep frying 16. 2 green chilies chopped 17. 1 teaspoon ginger fine chopped 18. ¼ cup khara boondi or mixture 19. 2 tablespoons coriander leaves chopped 20. Rinse urad dal thrice and soak them in water for 5 to 6 hours. 21. Just before making the fritters, heat a wide pan with 1 tablespoon oil. When it is hot enough, add cumin seeds, mustard and red chilli. When the seeds splutter, add curry leaves, ginger and green chilies. Soon the leaves will become crisp. Turn off the stove. Add turmeric and add hing. Cool this completely. 22. Once it cools down, add yogurt to this along with ¼ teaspoon salt. Whisk it well until smooth. If it is too thick, then add little water to bring it to consistency. It should be neither too thick nor too runny. Taste test and add more salt if required. Set this aside. 23. Drain the water from dal and add it to the grinder/blender along with salt and 3 tablespoons water. 24. Blend it for a few minutes. Then scrape the sides and sprinkle more water only if needed and blend to a smooth and fluffy thick batter. 25. Ensure the blender does not become hot while you blend. Keep sprinkling water as needed and blend. 26. The consistency of the batter has to be thick, fluffy and light. ( If it is runny then add 2 to 3 tablespoons rice flour and mix. Make punugulu instead of garelu) 27. In a large wide serving bowl, whisk together curd and salt. Then add water to make buttermilk and set aside. 28. Heat oil in a pan for deep frying on a medium heat. Check if it is hot by gently dropping a small portion of batter to the oil. It has to rise without browning a lot. 29. Let them fry and firm up for a few minutes, then stir and fry them evenly until crisp and golden. 30. Remove and add them directly to the bowl of buttermilk. Immerse them fully by pushing down with your fingers. 31. Once they soak up the buttermilk and soften in about 2 to 3 mins. Remove them one by one and gently squeeze to remove excess buttermilk. 32. Drop them in the seasoned perugu/ yogurt. Gently stir the yogurt to coat them. They will be delicate so handle them carefully. 33. Cover and rest for a couple of hours in the refrigerator until they become soft. Avoid leaving this in hot place as the yogurt will turn sour. You can keep it in the refrigerator. 34. .kadence-column173268_d8ec65-ba > .kt-inside-inner-col,.kadence-column173268_d8ec65-ba > title: Pesarattu Recipe
ingredients: 1 cup whole moong beans, 0.75 inch ginger piece, 2 green chilies, 0.75 teaspoon cumin seeds, 1.5 tablespoon raw rice, 0.25 teaspoon salt, 2 tablespoons ghee, 1 tablespoon oil, 0.5 teaspoon cumin seeds, 2 medium onions, 2 green chilies
instructions: 1. Add green gram and rice (optional) to a large bowl and rinse them well a few times. 2. Pour fresh water and soak them in for about 4 to 6 hours. Drain the water and rinse them well. 3. Add moong dal, green chilies, cumin seeds, ginger and salt to a blender jar along with little water just enough to make a thick batter. 4. Blend the ingredients to a slightly coarse or smooth batter to suit your liking. I usually blend to a coarse fine semolina texture. 5. Pesarattu batter must be of pouring consistency yet thick & spreadable. 6. If needed pour more water to bring the batter to a consistency. Do not make the batter runny as the pesarattu will not turn crispy. 7. Optional - topping: heat 1 tablespoon ghee or oil in a pan. Add cumin seeds, onions and green chilies. Saute them on a medium high flame until transparent yet crunchy. This will bring out the sweet flavor from the onion. They should not turn soft. Cool these completely. 8. Heat a tawa or non-stick griddle on a medium flame. 9. If using a cast iron pan, when it turns hot, drizzle few drops of oil or ghee and rub with a piece of sliced onion. Wipe off the excess. This prevents the pesarattu from sticking to the pan. If using non-stick pan you don't need to grease it. 10. Test by sprinkling little water. Immediately water should sizzle rapidly. When the water evaporates, reduce the flame to medium heat. 11. Pour ½ to ¾ cup of batter on the tawa. Then quickly spread it to a thin layer beginning from the center, moving towards the edges of the pan. 12. On a low to medium heat let the pesarattu cook for a few minutes. Pour 1 teaspoon ghee around the edges and the center too. 13. This step is optional for a thin pesarattu. Lightly scrape off the thick parts with a wooden spatula to the thinner parts making it even. (Check my step-by-step photos above) 14. When the pesarattu is done, you will see the edges begin to leave the pan. It also cooks to golden color & crisp on the outside. 15. Then flip and cook on the other side just for a minute or so. When you see the batter is no more raw, then flip back and roast just for a minute until crisp. Optional - Sprinkle 1 to 2 teaspoons of the fried onions here as much as you like. 16. Fold it and remove to serving plate. Do not over cook until it browns or discolors as the taste will alter. 17. To make the next pesarattu, ensure the pan is not too hot. Reduce the flame and cool down a bit. If using cast iron pan, you can rub it well with the onion and few drops of oil. 18. Stir the batter well before making the next one and then use it. 19. These pesarattu are best served hot with this ginger chutney. It is eaten alongside rava upma.

title: Potato fry (Aloo fry)
ingredients: 1.25 cup potatoes, 1.5 tablespoon oil, 0.25 teaspoon mustard seeds, 0.5 teaspoon cumin seeds, 1.5 garlic, 0.5 teaspoon urad dal, 1 sprig curry leaves, 1 red chili, 0.125 teaspoon asafoetida, 0.125 teaspoon turmeric, 0.5 teaspoon red chili powder, 0.375 teaspoon garam masala, 0.3333333333333333 teaspoon salt
instructions: 1. Wash potatoes and peel. Cube to small bite sizes about half inch cubes. Keep them in a bowl of water until used. This helps to remove the excess starch and prevents them from sticking to each other while frying. 2. Crush or chop garlic. Rinse curry leaves and set aside. 3. Pour oil to a heavy bottom non stick or cast iron pan. When the oil turns hot, add cumin, mustard, red chili and dal (optional). Fry until the dal turns golden. 4. Add curry leaves and garlic. When the garlic is lightly fried, add hing. 5. Drain the water from potatoes and then add them to the pan. Sprinkle turmeric. Mix and fry potatoes for about 3 to 4 minutes on a medium heat stirring often. 6. For crispy aloo fry, keep stirring and fry them without covering until fork tender. You can also add more oil for better roasting. 7. For soft potatoes, lower the flame. Cover and cook till the potatoes are fork tender. Stir in between to prevent burning. 8. When the potatoes are done, prick them with a fork to check if they are cooked completely. You shouldn't feel them hard from inside. Otherwise cook them for a little longer. 9. Then sprinkle chili powder, salt and garam masala. Toss everything well and fry for a minute or two to blend well. I usually add a tsp of oil at this stage so the spice powders coat all of the potatoes easily. 10. Serve potato fry with rice and rasam, sambar.

title: Potato Kurma (Aloo Kurma)
ingredients: 3 potatoes, 2 tablespoons oil, 1.25 cup onions, 0.75 cup tomatoes, 1.5 teaspoon ginger garlic paste, 1.5 green chili, 0.625 teaspoon salt, 0.125 teaspoon turmeric, 0.875 teaspoon red chili powder, 1.25 teaspoon garam masala, 0.25 teaspoon fennel seeds powder, 2 tablespoons coriander leaves, 2 tablespoons pudina, 3 tablespoons coconut, 2 tablespoons poppy seeds, 0.5 teaspoon Shahi jeera, 2 green cardamoms, 1 star anise, 1 inch cinnamon, 4 cloves 4 cloves ((laung)), 1 strand mace, 1 bay leaf
instructions: 1. To make the kurma paste, add grated coconut and poppy seeds (or cashews) to a grinder jar. Pour little water and make a fine paste. Or If you want to use yogurt, add it to a bowl and whisk well with a fork. Keep this aside. 2. If using yogurt, you need to add the diced potatoes to a pot and cover them partially with water. Cook until 90% done. Skip this step if using coconut. 3. Pour oil to a hot pan. When the oil becomes hot, reduce the flame to low and add all the whole spices - bay leaf, star anise, cloves, cinnamon, green cardamoms, mace and shahi jeera or cumin. 4. When they begin to sizzle, add the fine chopped onions. Increase the heat to medium and saute them until golden. 5. While the onions fry, chop the tomatoes and the potatoes. keep the tomatoes aside & the potatoes in a bowl of water. 6. When the onions are golden, add ginger garlic paste. Fry until the raw smell goes away, for about 40 to 60 seconds. 7. Transfer the tomatoes and sprinkle salt and turmeric. Fry until the tomatoes turn completely soft and blend well with onions. 8. Next add mint and fry for a minute. 9. Add garam masala, red chili powder and crushed fennel. Saute for 2 to 3 minutes until the raw smell of chili powder goes away. 10. Add the coconut paste and mix well. If using yogurt in place of coconut, add 2 tablespoons of the onion tomato masala (from the previous step) to the yogurt and mix well. Lower the heat and pour this to the pan slowly. 11. Mix well and fry until the mixture begins to smell good. Add potatoes and saute for 2 minutes. 12. Pour 1½ to 1¾ water and stir. Bring it to a rapid boil. Lower the heat to medium and cook covered till the potatoes are fully cooked and oil rises to the top. You can also pressure cook on a low flame for one whistle. 13. If the aloo kurma is runny, increase the flame and cook uncovered to evaporate some of the water. Turn off the heat when the kurma reaches a thick gravy consistency. Taste test and adjust salt. 14. Add chopped coriander and Serve potato kurma with biryani, pulao or chapathi.

title: Potato Sandwich Recipe (Aloo Sandwich)
ingredients: 1 potato, 1 green chili, 0.625 teaspoon ginger, 0.375 teaspoon red chili powder, 0.375 teaspoon garam masala powder, 0.125 teaspoon turmeric, 1 tablespoon lemon juice, 0.29166666666666663 teaspoon salt, 1 tbsp coriander leaves, 1 teaspoon ghee, 4 slices bread, 2 tbsp butter, 0.25 cup green chutney
instructions: 1. Wash and peel 300 grams potatoes (1 large or 2 medium). I prefer to quarter them and steam over a pot just until done. You can also boil the whole potatoes in a pressure cooker for 3 whistles or for 5 to 9 mins in the instant pot depending on the size of your potatoes. 2. To a mixing bowl, add ½ teaspoon grated ginger 1 green chili chopped (optional) 1 tbsp chopped coriander leaves ½ teaspoon red chili powder ¼ teaspoon salt (adjust to taste) 1/8 teaspoon turmeric ¼ to ½ teaspoon garam masala (adjust to taste) ½ teaspoon coriander powder. Pour 1 to 2 tsp ghee or oil over the spices. I prefer to use hot ghee as it helps to bring out the aroma of the other ingredients used. However it is optional. 3. Mix up everything. You can also add lemon juice or amchur now. 4. Add mashed potato. Mix up everything well. Taste test and add more spices, salt or lemon juice to taste. 5. Smear softened butter or ghee on one side of the bread. I prefer ghee. Turn the bread slices to the other side. If you want you can also smear some green chutney or schezwan sauce and then spread the aloo masala. Cover with another slice and smear butter or ghee on the outer side. You can also smear chutney or sauce on both the slices. 6. Heat the sandwich griller and grill the sandwiches for 3 to 5 mins until crisp. Alternately you can also toast them on a hot griddle on both the sides until crisp & golden. Cut aloo sandwiches to squares or triangles and serve with green chutney or schezwan sauce. 7. Add 1 tablespoon oil to a pan and heat it. Then add cumin sees. When the seeds splutter, add half teaspoon ginger paste or grated ginger and fry till you smell good. Add 1 medium sliced onion, 2 to 3 curry leaves and 1 chopped green chili. Saute till the onions turn lightly golden. While the onions fry, peel and mince 1 large raw potato or mash boiled potato, whichever you prefer. 8. Add minced raw potato or mashed boiled potato. Fry for 3 to 4 minutes. 9. Skip this step if using boiled potatoes. If using raw mined potatoes, add water just enough to cover the potatoes.Cover and cook till the potatoes are soft cooked and tender. Since they are mined they cook faster. 10. Add 1/8 teaspoon turmeric, ¼ teaspoon fennel powder (optional), ¼ teaspoon pepper powder(optional), ¼ teaspoon amchur (optional), ½ teaspoon garam masala and ½ teaspoon red chili powder. I added chili powder later, since I forgot. 11. Fry everything well till you get a nice aroma of the masala and the fennel seeds powder. Everything should blend well, potatoes soft cooked and fairly dry. 12. Smear butter on one side of the bread for crust. On the inner side, spread the curry in a thin layer, sprinkle some coriander leaves if you like. If you do not have a toaster, you can toast on a tawa on both the sides. Gently flip the bread & toast on the other side. The filling tastes so good on its own that you can simply spread it over toasted bread slices and serve. 13. If making in toaster. Cover with another slice and toast following the manufacturers guidelines. Serve potato sandwich with tea or with any of your favorite dips. 14. Sandwich Recip... Mayonnaise Sandwi... Vegetarian Club S... Veg Sandwich Reci... Bombay Sandwich R... Veg Grilled Sandw...

title: Potlakaya Perugu Pachadi | Snake Gourd Yogurt Pachadi
ingredients: 2 cups snake gourd pieces, 0.75 cup sour curd, 1 tbsp shredded ginger, 1 sprig curry leaves, 1 tbsp oil, 0.5 tsp mustard, 0.5 tsp cumin, 3 green chilies, 1 red chili, Coriander leaves, 1 Pinch hing, Salt
instructions: 1. Wash potlakaya under running water. With the help of a knife scrape off the whistish skin. Rinse thoroughly. Chop to cubes, remove the pith and seeds. Chop them to cubes. 2. Pressure cook the chopped gourd for 1 to 2 whistles, depends on how tender your gourd is. You can even cook them in a pot. 3. Once the vegetable is cooled, puree it. Can alternately skip pureeing cooked gourd and use them directly. 4. Heat oil in a pan, add curry leaves, green chilies, mustard, cumin and allow them to splutter. 5. Add ginger and red chili, fry to get an aroma. 6. Add turmeric and hing. 7. Pour the pureed gourd and fry for a min on high. 8. Once cool, transfer potlakaya perugu pachadi to the yogurt bowl and mix well. Add coriander leaves. 9. Ready to serve with rice or phulka

title: Prawn biryani (shrimp biryani)
ingredients: 1.25 cups basmati rice, 1 bay leaf, 2.5 green cardamoms, 0.5 teaspoon shahi jeera, 25 grams shrimp, 1 teaspoon ginger garlic paste, 0.125 teaspoon turmeric, 0.5 teaspoon biryani masala, 0.5 teaspoon red chili powder, 0.125 teaspoon salt, 2.5 tablespoon oil, 1 bay leaf, 1 inch cinammon, 1 star anise, 2 green cardamoms, 4.5 cloves 4 to 5 cloves, 0.5 teaspoon shahi jeera, 1 onion, 1 teaspoon ginger garlic paste, 1 medium tomato, 2 tablespoon coriander leaves, 2 tablespoon mint leaves, 1 teaspoon biryani masala, 0.5 teaspoon red chili powder, salt, 0.25 cup curd
instructions: 1. Drain the water completely from prawns, add chili powder, turmeric, salt & masala powder. 2. Marinate and rub them gently for a while with the marinade. Set aside for at least 30 minutes. You can also do this the previous night and refrigerate. 3. Wash and soak rice for about 30 minutes. Drain off completely. Bring 3 to 4 cups of water to a boil with salt, a bay leaf, 4 green cardamoms, 1/2 tsp shahi jeera. Add rice to the pot and cook until fully done but al dente. It should remain grainy, firm and not mushy. 4. Heat oil in a pan, Add bay leaf, shahi jeera, cloves, cardamom, star anise and cinnamon. Use just an inch of cinnamon, i used ceylon cinnamon hence used a bit more. 5. This shrimp biryani needs a little more oil than the other non-veg biryani recipes since prawns do not release much fats when cooked. Stick on to the quantity mentioned in the recipe else biryani may turn dry. 6. When they begin to splutter, add sliced onions. Separate the layers of onions before adding. Fry until brown or golden. 7. Browned onions lend a good flavor, so don’t skip this. You can set aside a few for garnishing. Add ginger garlic paste and saute till the raw smell goes off. 8. Add chopped tomatoes, mint and coriander leaves. Cook until the tomatoes turn soft. 9. Next add turmeric, red chili powder, biryani masala powder . I also added a bit of coriander powder since my masala powder was very strong. 10. Cook until the oil begins to separate. 11. Add marinated prawns. 12. Fry just for a minute. 13. Let the flame remain low. Pour slightly thick coconut milk and stir. If you do not follow any food combining rules of compatibility you can use yogurt/curd as well instead of coconut milk. 14. Cook until the gravy thickens & prawns are cooked through. Prawns take just 3 minutes to cook. They curl up to C shape when cooked through. Off the stove and set the pan aside. The color of the gravy is dark due to the brown onions.

title: Prawn Curry (Prawn Masala)
ingredients: 2.25 tablespoons oil, 0.75 cup onion, 1 teaspoon ginger garlic paste, 0.75 cup tomato, 0.625 teaspoon sea salt, 0.25 cup raw coconut, 0.125 teaspoon turmeric, 0.625 teaspoon Kashmiri red chili powder, 1 teaspoon coriander powder, 0.625 teaspoon garam masala, 0.25 teaspoon black pepper, 0.25 teaspoon cumin seeds, 1 green chilli, 1 sprig curry leaves, 1 garlic, 25 grams prawns, 1.25 cup water, 2 tablespoons coriander leaves, 0.75 teaspoon tamarind paste optional
instructions: 1. Devein and wash the prawns well with running cold water. Drain the water completely. Optionally toss them in a pinch of salt, chili powder and garam masala if you want. I do not do that. 2. Heat a tablespoon oil in a pan and saute ¾ cup sliced onions (1 medium onion) until they turn pink, for 5 to 6 mins. Add ¾ tsp each chopped ginger and garlic (or 1 teaspoon ginger garlic paste). Saute for a minute. 3. Add ¾ cup chopped tomatoes (1 large tomato), sprinkle ½ teaspoon sea salt and ⅛ teaspoon turmeric. Saute till the tomatoes turn mushy & break down completely, for about 5 mins. 4. Add ½ to ¾ teaspoon garam masala, 1 teaspoon coriander powder, ½ to ¾ teaspoon Kashmiri red chili powder and ¼ cup grated raw coconut. (I used tightly pressed frozen raw coconut). You can also use ¼ cup coconut milk or 10 cashew nuts. 5. Saute on a medium heat till the mixture is nicely roasted, for about 3 to 5 minutes. Take care not to burn. Cool down completely. 6. Transfer to a blender with ¾ cup water. If you want add half to 1 tsp of tamarind paste (not concentrate) and blend to a smooth puree (or slightly coarse paste if you want). Be careful with tamarind as there is no way back. 7. Heat the same pan with 1 to 1½ tablespoon oil. This step is optional and you may instead cook the prawns directly in the gravy later. Place the prawns in the pan and cook just for 2 mins, 1 min on each side. I also added a few curry leaves with the prawns for flavor. Remove to a plate. 8. Add ¼ teaspoon cumin seeds, 1 slit green chili (optional) and 1 chopped garlic clove. Wait till the garlic is lightly roasted then add 1 sprig curry leaves. Fry till the garlic turns aromatic and the leaves turn crispy. 9. Pour the onion tomato mixture and mix well. If required add more water or coconut milk to bring to a slightly runny consistency. Bring to a rolling boil and cook until it is thick and traces of oil are seen on the curry. Reduce the heat completely, and add the prawns. Simmer just until the tails curl up to “C” shape, meaning they are fully cooked. 10. Overcooking will make them hard. Switch off the stove. Taste test and add more salt or ¼ teaspoon black pepper if you want. If you have not used tamarind like me, sprinkle some lemon juice for a tang. Garnish prawn curry with coriander leaves. Serve with rice or roti, naan or chapathi, Ghee rice or Jeera rice . 11. Then transfer them to a bowl and pour 2 to 3 cups of cold water. Drain and repeat rinsing them a few times. If using frozen prawns defrost them the same way and avoid thawing or defrosting in microwave or using hot water as the prawns lose their taste and texture. 12. Rinse each prawn well, drain the water & place them in a bowl.

title: Prawn pulao (shrimp pulao)
ingredients: 1 cup rice, 2 grams prawns, 1 onion, 1 tsp ginger garlic paste, 1 Handful mint leaves, 1.5 green chili, 0.625 tsp Garam masala, 0.625 tsp coriander powder, 0.25 tsp Red chili powder, 1 pinch turmeric, Salt, 2.5 tbsps coconut milk, Oil, Lemon juice, 1 bay leaf, 3 green cardamoms, 5 cloves 4 to 6 cloves, 2 inch cinnamon stick, 0.5 tsp Shahi jeera, 1 star flower, 1 strand mace
instructions: 1. Wash and soak rice for about 30 mins. 2. Cook it in whatever way you like either in the pot, pressure cooker or electric cooker. 3. Cook rice till al dente. You can also add few drops of oil to the rice pot to keep the rice grainy. Rice should be grainy fully cooked but not mushy. 4. Devein and clean prawns. Rinse them well. You can also wash them in vinegar to remove the fishy smell. Drain them completely and set aside. 5. While the rice soaks and cooks, you can marinate prawns with ginger garlic paste, garam masala, coriander powder, red chili powder, salt and turmeric. 6. Heat a pan with oil and saute all the pulao spices until fragrant. 7. Add onions and saute until golden and cooked well. Transfer the prawns and saute for 2 to 3 mins or until the raw smell from the ginger garlic goes away. Add mint and saute for 2 mins. 8. Cover and cook on a low heat until the prawns are completely cooked. Add the coconut milk or water and allow to thicken. You must be left with a thick curry like prawn mixture. At this stage if the prawn are swirled it means they are cooked completely. If not sprinkle little water and cook further. Overcooking makes them hard. So be sure not to overcook. 9. Add cooked and cooled rice along with lemon juice. Mix well and serve prawn pulao hot with fresh onion, cucumber wedges and papad.

title: Prawns Fry Recipe
ingredients: 2 grams prawns, 2 teaspoons lemon juice, 0.5 tablespoon oil, 1.5 teaspoon ginger garlic paste, 0.25 teaspoon sea salt, 0.125 teaspoon turmeric, 0.625 teaspoon Indian red chili powder, 0.625 teaspoon garam masala, 0.5 teaspoon coriander powder, 1 tablespoon oil, 1 green cardamom, 0.5 teaspoon cumin seeds, 1 medium onion, 1 green chili slit, 1.5 sprigs curry leaves, 0.5 teaspoon Indian chili powder
instructions: 1. Devein and clean prawns. I used about 10 tiger prawns (200 grams). Rinse them thoroughly. To remove fishy smell, they can be rinsed with water and little vinegar. You can also use lemon juice and salt to rinse. If using frozen prawns, you will need to soak in hot water for a while. This will help to soften them. Drain off water completely. Add 2 teaspoons lemon juice and ½ tablespoon oil. Mix well. 2. Add 1½ teaspoon ginger garlic paste, ½ to ¾ teaspoon red chili powder, ⅛ teaspoon turmeric, ¼ teaspoon salt, ½ to ¾ teaspoon garam masala and ½ teaspoon coriander powder (optional). Mix and set aside for at least 10 minutes, while you chop the onions and green chili. 3. Heat 1 tablespoon oil or ghee in a hot pan. Add ½ teaspoon cumin seeds and a green cardamom (optional). 4. When the seeds begin to sizzle, stir in the onions and slit chilies. Sprinkle a pinch of salt. 5. Saute until the onions turn transparent or light golden and add the marinated prawns. 6. Add curry leaves and stir fry until they swirl up to a C shape. 7. The prawns will swirl up completely, meaning they are cooked thoroughly. If you want add more red chili powder, salt and stir fry for just a minute. 1 tbsp fresh grated coconut can also be added at this stage. Stir fry until the coconut begin to smell good. 8. Serve prawns fry with rice. You can also add some cooked basmati rice, little more ghee, salt to the pan. Mix and stir to make prawn ghee rice.

title: Pudina Chutney (Mint Chutney)
ingredients: 1 cup mint leaves, 0.5 cup coriander leaves, 0.5 teaspoon sugar, 0.5 teaspoon salt, 0.25 inch ginger, 1 garlic clove, 1.5 green chilies, 1 cup yogurt, 0.625 teaspoon roasted cumin powder, 0.625 teaspoon chaat masala, 0.5 cup coconut, 0.375 cup pudina, 1 tablespoon roasted bengal gram, 0.5 teaspoon cumin seeds, 0.5 inch ginger, salt as needed, 2.5 green chilies, 1 tablespoon lemon juice, 1 teaspoon Oil, 1 red chili, 0.25 teaspoon mustard seeds, 0.5 teaspoon urad dal, 1 pinch hing, 1.5 cup mint leaves, 0.5 teaspoon roasted cumin powder, 0.5 inch ginger, 1.5 green chilies, salt as needed, 0.5 teaspoon lemon juice, 0.375 teaspoon chaat masala, 1.5 tablespoon water, 1.5 tablespoon fried gram
instructions: 1. Pluck 1 cup mint leaves (tightly packed, 15 grams). We don’t want to use stalks here. Add them to a bowl of water and rinse them very well a few times. Rinse half cup coriander leaves with tender stalks (tightly packed, 15 grams), a few times. Drain completely and add them to a grinder jar. 2. Add ¼ inch ginger, 1 small garlic clove and 1 to 2 green chilies. 3. Add ½ teaspoon sugar and 1/3 teaspoon salt. 4. Add 1 tablespoon chilled thick yogurt. Do not use runny yogurt. If you double or triple the recipe then add yogurt accordingly. Do not add too much yogurt at this stage. 5. Grind until smooth. Do not over blend. Over blending may cause the mint leaves to release oils and can taste bitter. Keep this aside. If you want you can serve this as is or even refrigerate at this stage for later. 6. Add the rest of the thick yogurt (1 cup minus 1 tbsp) to a bowl. Add ½ to ¾ teaspoon roasted cumin and ½ to ¾ teaspoon chaat masala. Skip chaat masala if you don’t have. If you want you can reduce the quantity of yogurt but this is what we like at home. 7. Whisk very well until smooth. 8. Add the mint chutney to the yogurt. 9. Mix well and taste test. Adjust salt and spice to taste. Serve mint chutney as a side. 10. Wash pudina in a pot filled with clean water. Repeat the wash a few times and drain them in a colander. 11. Dry roast ¾ tbsp each of chana dal and urad dal on a low flame until golden. Add ½ tsp cumin to the hot pan and switch off. Cool these. If using bhuna chana skip roasting. You can also saute pudina in oil for 2 to 3 mins first and use. You can also use peanuts. Just roast the peanuts until aromatic and golden. 12. Add ½ cup chopped coconut, 2 to 3 green chilies, ½ inch ginger or 2 small garlic cloves, pudina, roasted dal, cumin and salt. You can also add a tsp of tamarind paste if you like the tang. 13. Blend them all together first for pudina chutney. 14. Add water as needed and blend to a smooth or coarse chatni to suit your liking. I prefer to use lemon juice now. Transfer the pudina chutney to a serving bowl. 15. Pudina chutney can be served without tempering too. To temper heat a small pan with little oil on a low flame. Add red chili, ¼ tsp mustard and ½ tsp urad dal. When the dal turns golden, add hing and switch off the stove. Pour this to the chutney. Mix well. Serve pudina chutney with south Indian breakfasts like idli dosa evening snacks

title: Pudina Rice (Mint Rice Pulao)
ingredients: 1 cup rice, 0.75 cup mint leaves, 0.25 cup coriander leaves, 1.5 green chilies, 0.75 inch ginger, 2 medium garlic, 0.3333333333333333 cup raw coconut, 0.3333333333333333 teaspoon sea salt, 1.5 to 2 tablespoon Oil, 1.25 to 2 cups water, 1 tablespoon lemon juice, 1 cup mix veggies carrots peas potatoes beans, 1 bay leaf, 1 star anise, 0.3333333333333333 teaspoon cumin seeds, 3.5 cloves 3 to 4 cloves (/laung), 1 inch cinnamon, 2.5 green cardamoms, 1 strand mace
instructions: 1. Wash 1 cup aged basmati rice or any short rice. Soak it for about 15 minutes, until you pluck and prepare the mint and coriander leaves. Pluck ¾ cup mint leaves (without stalks) and ¼ cup coriander leaves (optional, can include tender stalks). Wash them in plenty of water, a few times. Remove and place them in a colander, shake off to drain as much water as possible. 2. To a grinder jar, add the coriander and mint leaves along with 3. ⅓ cup grated raw coconut (or ¼ cup chopped or ⅓ cup yellow onion cubed) 4. Grind to a fine paste, as fine as possible. Avoid adding water. 5. Heat 1½ to 2 tablespoons ghee or oil in a pan or cooker. If using Instant pot, cook on a saute mode. Add the following 6. small bay leaf / tej patta 1 star anise /chakri phool ⅓ teaspoon cumin seeds or shahi jeera 3 to 4 cloves /laung 1 inch cinnamon /dalchini 2 to 3 green cardamoms /elaichi 1 small strand mace /javitri (optional) 10 to 15 split cashews (optional) 7. Add the paste and saute for 3 to 4 minutes, until it turns aromatic and loses the raw flavor. If you want you may add 1 cup mixed veggies, such as potatoes, beans, carrots, peas etc & saute for 2 to 3 mins. These are optional and I have not used it here. 8. Firstly pluck mint leaves, add them to a large pot filled with water. Rinse them well in more water. Drain them in a colander. 9. Soak rice for at least 15 to 20 minutes. 10. Cook it and set aside to cool completely. You can cook rice in electric cooker, pressure cooker or pot. The rice must be grainy and not mushy. 11. While the rice cooks, add the mint leaves to a small blender jar. Blend it little coarsely without adding water. Set this aside. 12. To make pudina rice, heat oil in a pan. Next add peanuts, chana dal and urad dal when they are lightly golden, add mustard and red chili. Lower the heat. When mustard splutters, add curry leaves, ginger, green chilies. 13. Ginger may get burnt, be quick. Add the pudina. Saute for 1 minute. Switch off the stove. 14. Add turmeric to the pan and add this to the cooled rice. Lastly sprinkle salt and squeeze off lemon all over the rice. Mix well. I also like to add some extra virgin olive oil before the rice is mixed.

title: Pulao Recipe | Veg Pulao | Vegetable Pulao | Pulav
ingredients: 1.5 cups basmati rice, 2 tablespoons oil, salt, 2.5 cups water, 1.5 teaspoons ginger garlic paste, 1 medium onion, 2 green chilies, 1 medium carrot, 4 medium french beans, 0.5 cup green peas, 1 potato, 3 tablespoons mint, 1 bay leaf, 0.75 teaspoon shahi jeera, 4 green cardamoms, 4 cloves 4 cloves ((laung)), 2 inch cinnamon, 1 star anise, 1 strand mace, 1 pinch nut meg, 2 pinches stone flower, 0.5 teaspoon fennel seeds powder
instructions: 1. Add basmati rice to a bowl and rinse it a few times until water runs clear. 2. Soak it for at least 20 minutes. After 20 mins, drain off the water completely and set aside. 3. While the rice soaks, rinse carrots, beans, peas, potatoes, onions, chilies & mint leaves. 4. Peel the carrots and potatoes. Nip off both the ends of beans and chop all of them to bite sized pieces. 5. Slice onions and slit green chill. Fine chop the mint leaves. (If you want you can also make a fine paste of ginger garlic, green chilies and mint.) Set all of these aside. 6. On a medium flame, heat 2 tablespoons ghee or oil in a pot or pressure cooker. 7. When the oil is slightly hot, add all the whole spices. 8. When the spices begin to sizzle, add the onions & chilies. Saute until onions turn golden. 9. Next saute ginger garlic paste, for 30 to 60 seconds, until the raw smell goes off. 10. Add all the veggies and mint. Saute for 2 to 3 minutes. 11. Pour water and add salt too. Taste the water, it has to be slightly salty. 12. Bring the water to a rolling boil. Next add drained rice and stir. 13. If cooking in pot, cover and cook on a low heat until all the water is absorbed and the rice grains are cooked. When the rice is done, turn off the stove. Cover and let the veg pulao rest in the pot for 10 to 15 mins. This helps the rice grains to become fluffy. 14. If making in pressure cooker, cover the cooker with the lid. Then cook on a medium high flame for 1 whistle. 15. Switch off the stove. When the pressure releases, remove the lid and fluff up the veg pulao rice with a fork. 16. Serve veg pulao hot or warm with a simple raita or gravy. 17. Press SAUTE button on the instant pot. Pour ghee or oil to the inner pot. Saute the spices in the hot oil for 30 seconds. 18. Fry onions & green chilies until transparent for 2 mins. Then fry ginger garlic paste just for 30 seconds. 19. Next add in all the chopped veggies and mint. Saute for 2 mins. Add the drained rice (1 ½ cups) and salt. Press CANCEL button. 20. Pour 1¾ cups water and mix. Taste the salt level and add more if needed. 21. Scrape the bottom gently with the spatula to release any bits of food stuck. This prevents a burn notice. Secure the instant pot with the lid. 22. Press PRESSURE COOK button (high pressure) & set the timer for 5 mins. Position the steam release handle to sealing. 23. Instant pot beeps when it is done. Let the pressure release naturally for 5 mins. 24. Release the rest manually by moving the steam release handle from sealing to venting with a spoon. 25. Fluff up the vegetable pulao with a fork & serve with yogurt raita.

title: Pulihora Recipe
ingredients: 1.5 cups aged rice, 1 tablespoon sesame oil, 0.25 teaspoon Turmeric, 0.5 teaspoon Salt, 1 handful tamarind, 0.75 teaspoon Jaggery, 0.5 teaspoon mustard powder, 2 tablespoons Oil, 0.5 teaspoon mustard, 1 tablespoon urad dal, 1 tablespoon channa dal, 0.25 cup peanuts, 2 red chilies, 2 Green chilies, 0.5 teaspoon Ginger, 2 sprigs curry leaves, 1 pinch hing
instructions: 1. Cooking rice - Add rice to a bowl & wash it well a few times. Pour 3 cups water. Place the bowl in a pressure cook and cover with a lid. Pressure cook for 2 to 3 whistles on a medium flame. When the pressure drops remove the bowl and transfer to a wide plate. Do not mash it. 2. Preparing rice - The rice has to be grainy and not mushy. Sprinkle salt and turmeric over the rice. Then pour 1 tbsp sesame oil. Let cool down and mix well. If needed add more turmeric to get the color you desire. 3. Making mustard powder (optional) - If you wish to use mustard powder, roast the seeds until slightly hot and cool. Powder and set aside. or You can roast sesame seeds until they begin to splutter. Cool and powder. Do not use both, use either of them. 4. Soaking Tamarind - Add tamarind to a small bowl and rinse it. Pour hot water just enough to immerse it. When it turns soft, squeeze and make a thick paste. Pass it through a filter, discard stones and pulp. 5. Heat a pan with oil. Add mustard, peanuts, chana dal & urad dal. Fry till the peanuts turn golden & aromatic. Add curry leaves, green chilies, red chilies and ginger (optional). Fry till the red chilies turn crisp 6. Add hing, followed by tamarind pulp, jaggery and little more salt. 7. Mix everything well & cook till the mixture turns thick & begins to leave the oil on the side. If you take off the mixture a bit early your pulihora will turn very moist & mushy. Turn off the stove. 8. The rice must be cool by now. Sprinkle mustard powder or sesame powder all over the rice. Then transfer ¾ of the cooked pulihora mixture to the rice. 9. Gently mix and add more if needed. If offering to God do not taste it. Else you can taste and add more salt as needed. If the pulihora is too dry add more oil & mix. 10. Serve pulihora with fresh homemade curd.

title: Puliyogare Recipe | Tamarind Rice Recipe
ingredients: 1 cup rice, 4 tablespoons tamarind, 1 tablespoon jaggery, 0.5 teaspoon salt, 0.5 teaspoon mustard seeds, 2.5 tablespoons peanuts, 0.625 tablespoons chana dal, 0.625 tablespoons urad dal, Salt, 1.5 green chilies, 1 red chili, 2.5 tablespoons oil, 1 sprig curry leaves, 0.125 teaspoon asafoetida, 0.75 tablespoons chana dal, 0.75 tablespoons urad dal, 2.5 red chilies, 1 tablespoons coriander seeds, 0.5 tablespoons cumin, 0.5 teaspoon mustard seeds, 0.25 teaspoon methi seeds, 0.25 teaspoon pepper corn, 0.125 teaspoon asafoetida, 1.5 tablespoons sesame seeds, 2.5 tablespoons coconut
instructions: 1. Wash rice a few times and cook to keep the rice grainy. Mushy rice doesn't go well to make puliyogare. 2. I usually cook rice with 2 cups of water in a pressure cooker for 3 whistles following the pot-in-pot method. 3. Spread the rice and cool it completely. 4. Soak tamarind in ½ to ¾ cup water. Add jaggery and stir until it dissolves. 5. When the tamarind turns soft, squeeze it well with your fingers and extract juice. 6. Taste test this and if you want it slightly more sweeter, add a bit more of jaggery. The sweetness reduces as you cook down. I prefer to pass it through a filter. Set this aside. 7. Heat a pan on a low to medium flame. Dry roast chana dal, urad dal and red chilies until lightly golden. 8. Add pepper corn. methi seeds and coriander seeds. 9. Continue to roast stirring often until methi begins to smell good. 10. Add cumin, mustard, sesame seeds and coconut. Continue to roast stirring continuously. 11. When the coconut begins to smell good. Add hing. 12. Switch off the stove and cool this completely. 13. Powder this as finely as possible in a blender. Set this aside. 14. Heat the same pan with oil and add mustard seeds. 15. When they begin to crackle, add dals and peanuts. 16. Roast them until lightly golden. Then add curry leaves, green chilies and red chilies. 17. When the peanuts and dal turn completely golden, add hing. 18. Pour the tamarind and jaggery water and stir. 19. Add turmeric and salt. Stir and begin to cook until slightly thick. 20. Then add the powder little by little and mix. 21. If the mixture becomes too thick, then add some more water to the tamarind and squeeze. Pour this water back to the pan. 22. Stir and cook on a medium heat until it thickens. 23. You can add 1 tbsp oil at this moment. 24. Cook on a low heat until the mixture turns thick and begins to leave oil. Add more salt if needed. 25. Add this to the cooled rice and mix. Do not add the entire mixture at one time. 26. Add little by little as needed and mix to prevent too much of puliyogare gojju in your rice. 27. You may be left with some extra puliyogare mix, which you can use up the next few days. 28. Allow puliyogare to absorb the flavors for about 30 mins then serve. 29. Usually tamarind rice is served without any accompaniment. You can serve it with some plain curd.

title: Pumpkin Curry Recipe
ingredients: 35 grams pumpkin, 1 cup chickpeas, 1.25 cup onions, 1.25 cup tomatoes, 1 green chilli, 0.5 tablespoon ginger paste, 0.5 tablespoon garlic paste, 3 tablespoons oil, 1.25 teaspoon salt, 1.25 cups water, 0.75 cup coconut milk, 0.25 cup coriander leaves, 1.5 teaspoons Kashmiri Red chilli powder, 1.5 teaspoon garam masala, 0.5 teaspoon cumin powder, 1.5 teaspoons coriander powder, 0.25 teaspoon turmeric, 0.5 teaspoon fennel powder, 1 tablespoon Kasuri methi, 1.25 teaspoon amchur powder, 1 teaspoon jaggery, 1 Indian bay leaf, 2 inch cinnamon piece, 1 star anise
instructions: 1. Heat a pot with oil and add bay leaf, cinnamon and star anise. 2. When they begin to sizzle in the oil, add onions and green chilies. 3. Stir fry them until deep golden, switching between medium to low flames & vice versa as required. 4. Lower the flame, add ginger garlic and saute for about a minute, until the raw smell has gone. 5. Stir in ½ tsp salt and ground spices – red chilli powder, 1 teaspoon garam masala, cumin powder, coriander powder, turmeric and fennel powder. 6. Quickly add the pumpkin and mix well. Stir in the tomatoes and stir fry for 2 to 3 mins. 7. Cover and cook on a medium to low flame until the tomatoes turn mushy. This takes about 5 to 7 mins. 8. Pour 1 cup hot water and add chickpeas (if using). Pour Coconut or almond milk. 9. Rise the flame and bring the curry to a rolling boil. Reduce the flame and cook covered until the pumpkin is fork tender and about to get mushy. As you cook, if required add more boiling hot water, this depends on your pumpkin. I had to add another ½ cup hot water. 10. Mix well and taste test. Add the following ingredients according to taste – ½ to 1 teaspoon salt, ½ teaspoon garam masala, kasuri methi, jaggery (sugar) and amchur (or tamarind paste). Crush the kasuri methi before adding to the pan. 11. Pumpkin curry should be thick, yet of pouring consistency as it thickens further after cooling. If your curry is runny, cook uncovered on a high heat for a few minutes so some of the liquid evaporates. 12. Turn off and rest the pumpkin curry covered until serving. Garnish with fresh coriander leaves. If you want you may squeeze in some lemon juice while serving. 13. Serve with Poori, Roti, Paratha, Naan, plain steamed rice, quinoa or millets.

title: Quinoa Pulao (Vegetable Quinoa Recipe)
ingredients: 1 cup quinoa, 1.5 to 2 tablespoon oil, 1 onion, 1 green chili pepper, 0.5 teaspoon ginger garlic paste, 0.375 cup carrots, 0.25 cup green peas, 2.5 green beans, 1 tomato, 11 mint leaves chopped finely, 0.625 teaspoon garam masala, 0.25 teaspoon turmeric, 0.25 teaspoon red chilli powder, 0.5 teaspoon salt, 1 bay leaf, 0.5 teaspoon cumin seeds, 2 green cardamoms, 1 inch cinnamon, 2 cloves 2 cloves, 1.25 cups water, 2 cups water
instructions: 1. Pour quinoa to a large bowl and pour 3 to 4 cups of water. Rub them well for a few seconds and drain the water. 2. Pour more water and rinse them again. I rinse them thrice. Lastly drain them to a colander and set aside. Rinse and chop all the veggies. 3. Press saute button and pour oil to the steel insert. Add all the whole spices. When they sizzle, add onions and chilies. Saute until transparent for 2 mins. 4. Saute ginger garlic paste for 30 seconds and stir in turmeric, salt, red chili powder, garam masala, mint and mixed veggies. Saute until the tomatoes breakdown. 5. Add quinoa and saute for 30 seconds. Press Cancel and pour water. Mix well to release any bits of food stuck. Cover the IP and position the steam release valve to sealing. 1¼ cups water 6. Add quinoa and saute for 30 seconds. Press Cancel and pour water. Mix well to release any bits of food stuck. Cover the IP and position the steam release valve to sealing. 7. Pressure cook for 1 min in new IP or 2 to 3 mins in old IP (read notes). Let the pressure drop naturally before opening the lid. 8. Let the quinoa pulao cool down slightly. Taste test and add more salt and lemon juice before serving. Do not stir a lot when it is hot. 9. On a low to medium flame heat oil in a pot. Add all the whole spices. 10. When they begin to sizzle add sliced or chopped onions and chilies. Saute until they turn golden or transparent. 11. Add ginger garlic and fry until you get a nice aroma. Do not burn. Reduce the heat and stir in salt, garam masala, turmeric and chilli powder. 12. Add the chopped veggies and mint. Stir fry for 60 seconds. If using tomato then saute until they break down and turn mushy. 13. Transfer drained quinoa and saute for 30 to 60 seconds. Pour water and give a good stir. Taste test and add more salt if needed. 2 cups water 14. Transfer drained quinoa and saute for 30 to 60 seconds. Pour water and give a good stir. Taste test and add more salt if needed. 15. Bring the water to a rolling boil on a high heat & then reduce the flame to medium. Cook until the quinoa turns tender and absorbs all the water. 16. When quinoa is cooked fully they look transparent. Cover and reduce the flame to low. Let the quinoa pulao rest on the stove for 5 to 7 mins. Squeeze some lemon juice if desired. Serve with raita.

title: Rabri Recipe (Rabdi)
ingredients: 1.5 liter full fat milk, 4.5 tablespoons sugar, 0.5 teaspoon cardamom powder, 1 strands saffron, 1 teaspoon rose water, 1 almonds, 17.5 pistachios
instructions: 1. Grease or rinse a heavy bottom pot and pour milk to it. 2. Bring it to a boil then reduce the flame to low. Let the milk simmer until it reduces approximately to half the original quantity (to 3 cups). 3. While the milk simmers, bring 1 cup water to a boil in a small pot. Add almonds and pistachios. 4. Turn off the heat and rest the nuts until the skin loosens (for about 10 to 15 mins). Drain them and peel the skin. Chop them finely and keep aside. 5. Transfer 2 to 3 tablespoons of hot milk from the pot to a small bowl. Add saffron strands and leave them to soak. 6. If you do not want to have layers of cream in your rabdi, you can skip this step. After simmering the milk for a while, a layer of cream forms on top of the boiling milk. Gently move that to the sides of the pot and stick up. (check the step-by-step pictures above or video) 7. Next stir once to prevent the milk from getting scorched at the bottom. 8. Continue to simmer the milk, slide the layer of cream to the sides of the pot every time it forms and repeat sticking it up to the side. Stir again once. This process of sliding and sticking to the sides should happen every time a layer of cream forms until the milk reduces to half the original quantity. I.e 3 cups in this case. 9. Do not forget to stir at the bottom every time you slide the cream. 10. When the milk reduces to half, add sugar and then scrape off the layers of cream from the sides of the pot. If the milk cream has dried up, pour some hot milk (from the pot) over the sides. This will soften it and loosen. Add it back to the milk. 11. Simmer until the quantity reduces to 2 cups. Add nuts, saffron milk, cardamom powder & rose water. Turn off the heat. 12. Chill rabri and garnish with nuts just before serving. It can be served with gulab jamun, malpuas & jalebi.

title: Ragi cake
ingredients: 0.75 cup ragi flour, 0.75 cup wheat flour, 2 tbsps cocoa powder, 1 cup sugar, 0.3333333333333333 cup curd whisked, 0.6666666666666666 cup butter, 0.75 cup milk, 0.5 tbsp vinegar, 1 tbsp vanilla extract, 1 tsp baking powder, 0.5 tsp baking soda, 0.125 tsp salt, 1 cup full fat milk, 1 tsp vanilla extract, 2.5 tbsp cocoa powder, 0.25 cup sugar
instructions: 1. Pour milk. You can use any milk. 2. Add vinegar & vanilla extract. 3. Pour melted butter. You can also use oil, I had used virgin coconut oil before it works well without the flavor of oil in the cake. 4. Whisk the curd and pour it. 5. Begin to mix gently. 6. There should be no lumps. Do not over mix. 7. Transfer this to the greased cake pan. Knock a few times to the counter. 8. Preheat the oven to 170 C for at least 15 mins. If using a fan forced oven, preheat at 160 C. 9. Fluff up the flour in the jar with a fork. Spoon it to a measuring cup. Level the flour. Do the same while you measure the cocoa. Sieve together ragi flour, wheat flour, salt, baking powder, baking soda and cocoa. Sieve thrice. 10. To the same bowl, add sugar or jaggery. 11. Pour milk, vinegar, vanilla, curd, and butter to the bowl. Whisk the curd before adding it. 12. Mix everything well just until combined. Do not over mix. 13. Pour the mixture to the greased tray. 14. Bake for 25 to 30 mins. Mine was done at 30 mins. When the cake is done a skewer inserted comes out clean. 15. Pour milk to a pot or pan. 16. Add cocoa and sugar. Mix well and boil stirring often. 17. Within few minutes the mixture turns thick. Add vanilla extract and turn off the heat. 18. Place the cake on a deep serving plate. Pour the chocolate sauce over the cake. 19. Gently spread with a spatula. Sprinkle some white chocolate or sprinkles. 20. Slice ragi cake and serve.

title: Ragi Cookies (Ragi Biscuits)
ingredients: 0.5 cup ragi flour, 0.5 cup wheat flour, 5 tbsp sugar, 0.625 tsp baking powder, 0.25 tsp cardamom powder, 7 tbsp ghee, 2 tbsp milk
instructions: 1. Firstly powder 4.5 to 5 tbsp sugar. It will give you about half cup. Measure and use it. 2. Add ragi and wheat flour to a mixing bowl. Next add in baking powder or cardamom powder. Mix them well until combined. 3. Melt the ghee and cool it. Pour 6 tbsp of ghee to the flour mixture. 4. Begin to mix well until combined. If the dough doesn't come together, then pour 1 to 2 tbsp of more ghee or milk. Use just as needed. I have used only 7 tbsp ghee. 5. Bring the dough together and make a ball. Cover and keep in the fridge for 30 mins. You can also keep in the freezer for 10 mins. 6. Preheat the oven to 170 C or 350 F for 15 mins. Divide the dough to 14 to 16 balls. Flatten them slightly and press down with a fork. 7. Place them on a baking tray, 1 inch apart from the other as they become big after baking. 8. Baking in Oven - Bake for 12 to 14 mins depending on your oven and thickness of the biscuit.Baking in cooker - Preheat the cooker with salt at the bottom. Remove the gasket and weight. When the cooker turns hot, place the tray in the cooker and bake on the lowest flame for 12 to 15 mins. You can bake in 2 to 3 batches. 9. When done the biscuit top will be non sticky. They will be slightly soft in the center but when they cool down they will firm up. 10. Transfer to a wire rack and cool completely. Store ragi biscuits in a air tight jar and use up to 3 weeks.

title: Ragi Dosa Recipe
ingredients: 0.5 cup ragi flour, 0.25 cup rice flour, 2 tablespoons semolina, 2 cups water, 0.29166666666666663 teaspoon salt, 0.5 teaspoon cumin seeds, 1 green chili, 1 teaspoon ginger, 5.5 curry leaves, 2 tablespoons onions, 2 tablespoons coriander leaves, 2 tablespoons ghee oil, 0.5 cup rice, 0.5 cup ragi, 0.5 cup urad dal, 0.25 teaspoon methi seeds, salt as needed, 2 tablespoon oil
instructions: 1. In a mixing bowl, stir together ragi flour, semolina, rice flour, onions, chilies, curry leaves, coriander leaves, cumin, salt and ginger. Pour half of the water and begin to mix to a batter. Pour the remaining water in batches and mix to a lump free batter. 2. The consistency of the batter should be very runny for a thin dosa, slightly thicker for a thicker dosa. Rest aside for 10 to 15 mins while you make your chutney. 3. Grease your pan and heat it on a medium high flame until really hot. A few drops of water sprinkled on the pan should sizzle and evaporate. 4. Stir the batter very well as the solids settle at the bottom. Regulate the flame to medium high and begin to pour the batter, moving forward - starting from the edges in a circular pattern to cover the entire pan. 5. You will have some unfilled pockets, fill them up with the batter. (Check the pictures) 6. Drizzle 1 teaspoon ghee or oil across the edges & over the dry spots. Let cook for 3 mins on a medium high until the edges begin to leave the pan. 7. Gently turn with a spatula to the other side and cook for another 1 min. Lastly turn it again and cook until the base is crisp, for another minute. This is just a guideline for timing, it may vary depending on the kind of stove and cookware used. 8. Remove to a serving plate. Before you make the next ragi dosa, make sure the pan is really hot (not smoking) and give the batter a good stir else the solids settle at the bottom. 9. Rinse ragi and rice together and soak them for 8 hours. Later wash urad dal & methi seeds thoroughly and soak for at least 4 to 5 hours. 10. Drain them and rinse well. Blend the urad dal with water as needed until frothy and thick. Transfer this to a bowl. 11. To the same jar, add ragi (or ragi flour) and rice. Blend well with water to a slightly coarse medium batter (not too thick or too runny). Mix this well with little salt. You can avoid salt now and add after fermenting. 12. Ferment this batter for 6 to 8 hours depending on the climate. The batter must rise at least slightly. 13. Pour water and bring to a desired consistency. To make crisp dosas keep the batter slightly thick. For thin dosas make the batter slightly thin. 14. Grease the pan and heat up. The pan must turn hot enough. 15. Pour 1 ladle of ragi dosa batter and spread it as you like thick or thin. Drizzle some oil or ghee. When the sides leave the pan, flip it and cook on the other side.

title: Ragi idli recipe
ingredients: 0.5 cup urad dal, 1.5 cups ragi, 1 teaspoon methi seeds, 0.625 teaspoon non-iodized salt
instructions: 1. Add urad dal and methi seeds to a bowl and rinse them well thrice. Soak for 5 hours. 2. To another bowl, add ragi and rinse them a few times very well. Add fresh water and soak them for 5 hours. 3. If using poha then soak them 30 minutes before blending. 4. After 5 hours drain the water. Add the urad dal, methi seeds (or soaked poha) to a blender jar. 5. I always add salt while blending. If you live in a hot place, then you may add after fermenting. 6. Pour chilled water during summers and blend the batter until thick, fluffy and frothy. The batter should be thick yet of pouring consistency. 7. Transfer this to a large bowl. 8. Add ragi to the blender and add little water. Blend to a slightly coarse paste. I do not make it very fine as this gives the texture of rava. 9. Next add more water as needed while blending. 10. Pour this to the blended urad dal batter and give a good mix. The batter should be thick like the regular idli batter. If needed sprinkle some water at this stage. 11. Cover and keep it in a warm place. Leave overnight to 14 hours until it ferments and rises well. 12. When fermented, you should not have the raw smell of urad dal or ragi in the batter. But should have a mild fermented flavor, yet should not have a sour smell. This is the correct fermentation for our taste. 13. When the batter is ready, pour 1 cup water in your steamer or cooker. Bring it to a boil on a medium heat. 14. Meanwhile grease the plates and pour the batter in the moulds. 15. When the water begins to boil rapidly, open the lid and place the ragi idli stand in it. 16. Cover and steam for exactly 10 to 12 mins. You may need to adjust the timing based on your pot. 17. Switch off and allow to cool slightly. 18. Dip the edge of a spoon in water and remove the ragi idlis using it. 19. Serve ragi idli hot with sambar or chutney.

title: Ragi laddu
ingredients: 1 cups ragi flour, 0.25 cup peanuts, 0.375 cup almonds, 0.25 cup desiccated coconut, 4 green cardamoms, 15 grams jaggery, 2 tbsp Ghee
instructions: 1. Heat ghee and fry the flour on a low heat till you get a nice aroma of ragi. Set this aside to cool. 2. Dry roast peanuts, cool, remove the skin. It is optional to remove the skin. 3. Roast almonds or sesame seeds until you smell them lightly roasted. Set aside ½ tbsp. of seeds aside (optional). 4. Dry roast desiccated coconut lightly. set aside to cool. 5. Add peanuts, sesame seeds, coconut and cardamoms to a grinder and make a fine powder. You can even powder each of these separately if doing in large quantity. 6. Add the cooled ragi flour and jaggery to the grinder. 7. Blend for about 1 to 2 minutes for the oil to ooze out from the mix. Make balls while the mix is still warm. If needed add melted warm ghee and make ragi ladoo. (check video) 8. Tip: If making in large quantity, you can mix the blended nut powder, jaggery, ragi flour and sesame seeds in a mixing bowl. Blend this in batches. 9. Store ragi ladoo in a air tight jar.

title: Ragi Malt Recipe (Ragi Java, Ragi Porridge)
ingredients: 4 tablespoon ragi flour finger millet flour, 1.5 tablespoon sugar, 1.5 cups Water, 1 cup Milk, 4 drops ghee, 0.25 teaspoon cardamom powder, 6 almonds, 4 tbsp finger millet Ragi flour, Salt, 1.5 cup Water, 1 cup Buttermilk, 1 tsp Oil, 1 pinch Cumin, 1 sprig Curry leaves, 1 tbsp Coriander leaves, 1 Green chili
instructions: 1. Add 4 tablespoons ragi flour to a heavy bottom pan or pot. 2. Pour 1½ cups water. Remember the stove is off yet and we are not cooking it at this stage. 3. Mix well and ensure there are no lumps in the ragi flour mixture. Turn on the stove and begin to cook stirring often on a medium flame to prevent lumps. 4. Keep stirring continuously and cook until the mixture turns thick and glossy. You will see there are no lumps. 5. Then add jaggery or any other sweetener. Mix well and turn off the stove. 6. I went ahead using 1 cup milk here. You can use even lesser. Slowly incorporate the milk to the ragi malt and mix well. I used preboiled milk here. If you use cold milk, slowly bring it to a boil and turn off. 7. Stir well to incorporate the milk well with the ragi malt. Add almond powder or mixed dry fruits powder if using. You will see smooth ragi malt. 8. For salted version – After cooking it in water, cool it completely and season it or add butter milk to bring it to the desired consistency and season it. You can find the detailed recipe in the recipe card. Serve ragi malt warm or cold. We usually top the ragi malt with ghee and nuts. 9. Most times I cook the ragi malt with water as it turns too heavy with milk. 10. Cooking ragi with milk may hinder the absorption of iron from ragi. So it is good to cook and consume it without any milk products. But it is up to you to judge. 11. You can skip the sweetener and add chopped dates or maple syrup to sweeten the ragi malt. 12. To enhance the nutrition you can add dry fruits powder. I usually blanch almonds, dry them completely and make almond meal for my toddler. Add a tsp of this to the ragi malt. 13. You can also blend together mixed nuts and few dates in a small chutney jar. Add this to the cooked ragi malt. 14. Consuming ragi with fruits high in vitamin C helps in absorption of iron from ragi. So adding an apple or banana to your ragi malt helps in absorption of iron. Avoid using milk in this case. 15. Aged or elderly : For the aged or elderly people with digestion problems, soak ragi overnight and discard the water. Give a good rinse and blend well until smooth with water. Cook this to a thick porridge with water. Ragi porridge rec... Ragi laddu 16. Nac... Ragi Cookies (Rag... Ragi Dosa Recipe Ragi idli recipe ...

title: Ragi Mudde Recipe
ingredients: 1 cup ragi flour, 2 cups water, 0.3333333333333333 teaspoon salt, 1.5 teaspoon ghee, 1 tablespoon ragi flour, 3 tablespoons water
instructions: 1. In a small bowl, mix together 1 tablespoons ragi flour with 3 tablespoons water. The mixture should be free of lumps. Set this aside. 2. To a heavy bottom pot, pour 2 cups water. Add ⅓ teaspoon salt and ½ teaspoon ghee. Both these are optional. 3. Bring the water to a boil, then stir the ragi mixture with a spoon and pour it straight into the boiling water. 4. Give a good mix with the spoon and let it come to a boil again on a medium heat. 5. You will see the mixture begins to thicken and bubble, then lower the flame completely and add the flour. 6. Mix with a sturdy wooden stick or a spatula to incorporate the flour into the water. Within 60 to 90 seconds you should see no streaks of flour. 7. Continue to mix until no lumps remain.
