Scrape off the stick with a spoon to loosen all of the ragi stuck to it. 8. Cover the pot and let it cook on the lowest heat for 5 mins. 9. After 5 minutes, give a mix again for 30 to 60 seconds. Remove the stick and cover back. Turn off and let it steam in the residual heat for another 5 minutes. (with the stove turned off) 10. Perfectly done ragi mudde will be non-sticky when you touch it with your fingers or a spoon. 11. Grease or moisten a wide plate with few little water. This helps the dough not to stick to the plate. 12. Transfer the dough to the plate. Grease your fingers with ghee or dip your fingers in a bowl of water. 13. Begin to knead the dough for 30 to 60 seconds. Then divide to 2 to 3 equal portions and roll to small balls. 14. Serve ragi mudde hot with a gravy or soupy lentil dish. 15. We will need ½ cup flour for this recipe. Stir in 1 tbsp ragi flour in 1 cup water. Also add little salt to taste and bring the water to a rolling boil. 16. Add the rest of the flour in 2 parts. If you are making more ragi mudde then add it in 3 to 4parts. 17. Take the pot away from the stove and place on the kitchen counter. Stir well with the wooden stick or spoon to prevent any lumps. Put it back to the stove and cook until it turns thick. Scrape off the sides and get the dough to the center of the pot. 18. Cover and cook on a lowest heat for about 2 to 3 minutes, making sure it does not burn. 19. Turn off the stove and let the dough rest for few minutes. Keep the pot covered at the steam helps to cook it fully. 20. When the temperature comes down, either grease your hand with ghee or moisten with water and roll it to balls. 21. Serve ragi mudde hot with any gravy or sambar.

title: Ragi Rava Upma
ingredients: 1 cup ragi rava, 2.5 cups water, 0.41666666666666663 teaspoon salt, 2 tablespoons oil, 1 sprig curry leaves, 2 green chilies, 1 pinch hing, 0.5 teaspoon mustard, 0.5 teaspoon cumin, 0.25 cup onion, 1 cashew nuts, 1 teaspoon ginger, 0.5 tablespoon chana dal, 1 teaspoon urad dal, 2 tablespoons coriander leaves, 1 lemon
instructions: 1. Wash and soak ragi rava for about 30 mins. Drain completely and squeeze off the excess water using your hands. Set this aside. 2. Heat a pan with oil. Add cumin, mustard, chana dal, urad dal and cashew nuts. Fry them until the lentils turn golden. 3. Add hing, onions, curry leaves, ginger and green chilies. Fry them until the onions turn transparent. 4. Transfer the rava and saute for a while for about 3 to 4 minutes until you begin to smell the rava good. 5. Meanwhile stir water and salt in a pot. Taste test the water. It should be slightly salty. Bring this to a boil. Turn down the heat and slowly pour the water with one hand, while you stir the rava with another hand. Be careful as the water can splash at this stage. 6. Cover and cook on a low to medium heat until all the water is absorbed and cooked through. If you think it is still uncooked, simply add more boiling water. Stir in between a few times. Turn off and let rest for 10 to 15 mins. 7. Sprinkle some lemon juice and garnish with coriander leaves. Serve ragi rava upma hot.

title: Ragi Roti Recipe (Ragi Rotti)
ingredients: 0.5 cup ragi flour, 0.5 cup water, 0.25 teaspoon salt, 1 teaspoon oil, 0.25 teaspoon cumin seeds, 2 tablespoons onion, 1 green chili, 2 tablespoons carrot, 2 tablespoons coriander leaves
instructions: 1. Pour water to a pot and add salt & oil. Bring to a rolling boil and add the flour. 2. Turn off the heat and using a sturdy spoon or a silicon spatula begin to incorporate the flour into the water until no more dry flour is visible. If it is too dry, splash 1 to 2 tablespoons of water. 3. Scrape off all the dough from the spoon and add to the bowl. Cover and let rest until the temperature cools down to a slightly hot or warm temperature (not cold). Add little flour to a wide small plate and keep aside. 4. Remove the dough to the rolling board and mix well with your hand until it looks very smooth. Divide to 4 equal portions and keep them covered in the same pot. 5. Smoothen each portion & roll to a ball, flatten it and dip in flour. Shake off the excess and roll to a 6 inch moderately thick ragi roti, moving it often to prevent sticking. (check pics or video) 6. If the dough sticks, sprinkle some flour lightly as you roll. Transfer to the counter and make the rest. 7. Heat a tawa/griddle on a medium high heat. Dust up the excess flour if any from the surface of the roti and transfer to the pan. 8. Let cook for 30 to 35 seconds, turn/flip it to the other side and press down gently all over with a spatula. Turn it again and cook until puffed. If it does not puff, press down again all over until it begins to puff. 9. Remove to a wired rack and spread ghee if you want. Repeat toasting the ragi roti the same way. Serve them hot or cold with a vegetable curry or a meat curry accompanied by plenty of veggies. 10. To make Masala roti, make the dough as mentioned above and add the other ingredients and mix well. 11. Divide the dough to 3 portions. Place a dough ball over a greased parchment paper or banana leaf. Begin to spread with your fingers to a thin roti of 6 to 7 inches. If you want make 3 holes across the center. 12. Heat a pan and invert the ragi roti over it (parchment paper up). Press down gently with your hand and pull off the parchment carefully. 13. Pour 1 teaspoon ghee or oil all over including into the wholes. On a medium heat cook covered for 4 to 5 mins, turning to the other side after 3 mins. Remove when they have golden spots.

title: Raita Recipe
ingredients: 1 cup curd, 0.5 cup cucumbers, 3 tablespoons onion, 0.25 teaspoon salt, 1.5 green chilies, 1 tablespoons coriander leaves, 0.5 teaspoon cumin powder
instructions: 1. Wash cucumbers, onion (if using) and green chilies under running water. Peel the cucumbers if you prefer and chop them to bite sized pieces. Deseed chilies if you prefer low heat and chop them. Fine chop coriander leaves and onions. 2. Add yogurt and salt to a mixing bowl and whisk well until smooth. Add cucumbers, onions, coriander leaves, chilies, salt and cumin powder. Mix well and taste test to adjust salt. If it is too thick for your liking add more yogurt. 3. Garnish with cumin powder or black pepper. Serve raita with biryani, pulao, kebabs or paratha.

title: Rajma Recipe (Rajma Masala)
ingredients: 1 cup rajma, 2.5 tablespoons ghee, 1 bay leaf, 0.5 teaspoon cumin seeds, 1 cup onions, 1 green chilli, 1.5 teaspoon ginger garlic paste, 1.5 cups tomatoes, 0.75 teaspoon red chilli powder, 0.75 teaspoon garam masala, 1.5 to 2 teaspoons coriander powder, 0.875 teaspoon cumin powder, 0.25 teaspoon turmeric, 0.5 teaspoon salt, 0.75 tablespoon kasuri methi, Few Ginger juliennes, 2 tablespoons coriander leaves, 2.5 tablespoons heavy cream
instructions: 1. Add rajma to a large bowl and rinse them thrice. Soak them uncovered in 4 cups water for at least 6 hours to overnight. Discard the water and rinse them well. 2. Transfer them to a stovetop pressure cooker or instant pot. Pour 1½ cups water and pressure cook until soft, for 4 to 5 whistles. Or in the instant pot for 12 to 16 mins, depending on the age of rajma. 3. Once cooked they should be soft enough yet hold there shape. If you mash a rajma with your fingers it should get mashed easily. 4. Heat ghee or oil in a pot. Add cumin seeds and bay leaf. When they begin to sizzle add the onions and green chili. 5. Saute on a medium heat until golden. Stir in the ginger garlic and saute for a minute, until aromatic. 6. Pour the tomato puree & cook until the onion tomato mixture turns thick, for about 3 min. 7. Stir in the spices – red chili powder, garam masala, coriander powder, cumin powder and turmeric. Saute for 2 to 3 minutes, until the masala begins to smell aromatic. 8. Add the rajma along with the stock (rajma cooked water). Pour more hot water as required (about ½ to 1 cup, only as required) to make a gravy. (If using canned beans, drain the liquid from the can and rinse them before adding). 9. Mix well and simmer for 10 to 15 mins, until slightly thick. To make the gravy thick, if you want mash a tbsp of the rajma and add back. 10. Taste test and add more salt, ¼ teaspoon garam masala if required and crushed kasuri methi. Optionally you may stir in cream and garnish with coriander leaves and ginger juliennes. You can also make a dhaba style tadka garnish or a restaurant style creamy finish from the post. 11. Squeeze some lemon juice before serving. Serve rajma with chawal (rice). 12. Press Saute button and pour ghee to the steel insert. When the ghee melts, add cumin seeds, bay leaf, chopped onions, green chili and salt. 13. Saute for about 5 minutes stirring constantly to cook evenly until golden, about to caramelize but not burnt. 14. Add ginger garlic & saute for 30 seconds to 1 minute. 15. Add all the spice powders – chili powder, garam masala, turmeric, coriander powder and cumin powder. 16. Press cancel. Add rajma and pour 2 cups water. Deglaze to release any bits of food stuck at the bottom. 17. Place a trivet and keep the bowl of tomato puree over that. Secure the IP with the lid and pressure cook for 18 mins (for softer beans, if you prefer al dente – 16 mins). 18. When the pressure drops, open the IP. Rajma should super soft, yet hold shape. Pour the tomato puree and cook for 2 to 3 mins on saute mode. For a thicker consistency, mash some of the rajma. (If required pour about ½ to ¾ cup water to bring to a runny consistency.) 19. Taste test and add more salt, ¼ teaspoon garam masala, cream (optional) and crushed kasuri methi. Press cancel. 20. Garnish with coriander leaves and ginger juliennes. Serve rajma with chawal. Drizzle some lemon juice.

title: Rasam Powder Recipe | Rasam Podi
ingredients: 0.75 cup Coriander seeds, 0.25 cup Toor dal, 0.25 cup Chana dal, 2 tbsp Cumin, 1.5 tsp Pepper corn, 5 red chilies, 0.375 tbsp Fenugreek seeds, 0.5 tsp hing, Handful fresh curry leaves
instructions: 1. Dry roast chana dal and toor dal on a medium heat. Since I made in small quantity I have roasted both the dals together. If making in larger quantity, dry roast them separately. 2. Keep stirring and fry until golden to light brown and aromatic. 3. Pour the coriander seeds and red chilies. 4. Keep stirring and roast until the coriander emits a nice aroma. The red chilies will also turn crisp. Set them aside to a plate. 5. To the same pan, add methi seeds and roast until they turn aromatic. 6. They will turn slightly dark in color. 7. Add jeera and pepper. Very soon jeera turns aromatic. Transfer these as well to a plate. 8. Add the curry leaves and roast. You can also fry them in little oil. I always avoid using oil for any spice powders including for rasam powder. 9. When the leaves turn crisp, remove them. 10. Cool all these completely. 11. Clean all the ingredients first and roast them one by one on a low to medium flame until aromatic. Do not combine 2 ingredients and roast. 12. When done in small quantities like I have done below, they can be added one after the other & roasted. But when made in large quantities they have to be roasted separately as the roasting time is different for each ingredient. 13. Cool the ingredients in a large wide plate . Make sure no moisture from steam is trapped in them. 14. Send the entire mixture to a flour mill to make the rasam powder. Do not cover the container until the rasam powder is completely cool. 15. For many years I used aluminum foil to pack all the foods I would bring from India. In the recent years I have stopped that as well and began to use washed & sun dried small cloth bags. Pack the foods & then wrap the bag in cling wraps so that the rasam powder does not come out.

title: Rasam Recipe | South Indian Hotel Style
ingredients: 2.5 medium tomatoes chopped, 0.125 teaspoon turmeric, 0.625 teaspoon salt, 2.5 to 3 cups water, 1 teaspoon Tamarind, 1.5 tablespoon jaggery, 0.25 cup coriander leaves, 1.5 tablespoon oil, 0.5 teaspoon mustard seeds, 0.5 teaspoon cumin seeds, 1 red chili, 1 sprig curry leaves, 1 pinch hing, 3.5 cloves garlic, 0.25 teaspoon fenugreek seeds, 0.375 teaspoon pepper corn, 1.5 teaspoon cumin seeds, 1 red chili
instructions: 1. To a pan, add ¼ teaspoon methi seeds & one dried red chilli. On a low flame, dry roast them until methi turns slightly aromatic. Then add 1½ teaspoon cumin seeds (jeera) and ¼ to ½ tsp pepper corn. 2. Roast all of them until methi seeds turn slightly dark in color and aromatic. If making for baby or toddler skip red chili and use less pepper. Cool them and add to a grinder jar. 3. You can also coarsely crush it in a mortar pestle. Set aside. You can also skip this roasting part & just powder the spices directly but the aroma is great when roasted. 4. Add 1½ tablespoon oil to a hot pot. When the oil turns hot, add half broken red chili, ½ teaspoon cumin seeds and ½ teaspoon mustard seeds. 5. Soon they begin to splutter, then add 3 to 4 crushed garlic, 1 sprig curry leaves and then a pinch of hing. Saute well until the leaves turn crisp. 6. Add 1 to 1½ cups chopped or mashed tomatoes or pureed (about 2 to 3 medium tomatoes). Sprinkle ½ teaspoon salt and 1/8 teaspoon turmeric. 7. Cover and cook or saute until the tomatoes turn mushy. 8. Add spice powder or rasam powder from step 1. 9. Mix and saute for 2 to 3 minutes. The spice powder must smell good. 10. Pour 2½ to 3 cups water. Add 1 teaspoon tamarind. Less is fine but don’t a lot. 11. Add jaggery to taste. I use about 1 to 2 tablespoons to balance the flavors. So adjust to your taste. 12. Bring it to a boil & let simmer for 5 minutes. Rasam boiled on a low flame tastes good. Stir well and take little rasam in a tablespoon. Cool it and taste test. Add more salt, tamarind and jaggery if needed. 13. Add ¼ cup chopped coriander leaves with stalks. Cover and turn off the stove. Serve Rasam with steamed rice and some papad, stir fry veggies. 14. Pour ½ cup water to a pot and heat it. Add a lemon sized ball of tamarind and let it soften. Then add another 1½ cups water and extract the juice. Discard the pulp. 15. To the same bowl, add the spice powder we made in the recipe along with 8 well crushed medium garlic cloves. Add jaggery and salt to taste. Bring this to a boil and then reduce the flame to low. Simmer it for 5 minutes. Add coriander leaves and turn off. 16. Make a tempering in 1 tsp hot ghee by adding ½ teaspoon mustard seeds, 4 crushed garlic cloves, 1 red chilli & 1 sprig curry leaves. When the garlic cloves turn slightly golden, add 1/8 tsp hing. Pour this to the garlic rasam.

title: Rasgulla recipe
ingredients: 1 liter milk, 2.5 tablespoons lemon juice, 1 liter ice water, 1.5 cups refined white sugar, 4 cups water, 3 green cardamom pods, 0.5 teaspoon rose water, 1 pinch saffron stands kesar for garnishing
instructions: 1. Bring milk to a boil in a pot. 2. If using non-homogenized milk (milk from milkman), then keep the pot aside. Wait for 5 mins. 3. If using homogenized milk from packets or cartons then reduce the flame to low. 4. Add 2 tbsp lemon juice & stir until the milk curdles. 5. If it doesn't curdle, add more & stir. 6. When you see the milk curdled completely, Switch off the stove. Rest for 2 mins. 7. Then pour cold water to the pot. This will stop the chena from cooking further & keeps it soft. 8. Place a colander over a large bowl & line with a thin cloth. Drain the curdled milk. 9. Rinse the chenna under running water to remove the acidic flavor. 10. Tie the cloth. Squeeze it well to remove the excess whey as much as possible. 11. Hang it for 1 to 1½ hours. Chenna should not have any excess whey. It must be crumbly. 12. Add sugar, cardamoms & water to a wide pan or pot. 13. Make sure you use a wide pot good enough to hold all the rasgullas. 14. The balls will cook & almost double in size only if there is enough space in the pot. 15. Stir to dissolve the sugar. Bring it to a boil. 16. Knead the chenna well to make it a smooth dough for 3 to 5 mins. I do it just for 3 mins. 17. Do not over knead to the extent that the chenna turns greasy or soggy. 18. When you see the mixture turns uniformly smooth and no more grainy, then stop kneading. 19. Take small portions of this and roll to tiny balls. 20. They should be tiny and not big as they expand in size after boiling. I made about 16. 21. Add rose water to the sugar syrup. (optional). 22. Bring the sugar syrup to a rolling boil on a medium flame. 23. Remove the cardamom pods. Then add the balls one after the other gently. 24. Cover the pot immediately with a lid. 25. The syrup must be boiling steadily at a constant heat. 26. Cook on a medium flame for 9 to 10 mins . The syrup must be bubbling & boiling steadily through out this time. So adjust the heat as needed depending on the kind of pot & stove used. 27. After 5 mins gently stir the sugar syrup once with a skewer without touching the rasgullas. Cover immediately. 28. This ensures even cooking and puffing. During the cooking time if you feel the heat is too much reduce the heat slightly, but ensure it is still bubbling & boiling. 29. After 10 mins, remove the pot immediately from the stove to prevent rasgulla from cooking further. 30. Keep the lid closed always otherwise they will shrink or fall flat. Do not open at least for 20 mins. 31. They double in size and also sink in syrup when cooked completely. 32. Allow rasgulla to rest and cool completely. Serve them chilled. If desired garnish with saffron. 33. Once the rasgullas have rested for a while, taste the sugar syrup and check for sweetness. 34. If you prefer more sweeter rasgullas or syrup, then add ¼ cup sugar to a small pot. 35. With the help of a ladle transfer about 3/4 cup sugar syrup to the pot and make a sugar syrup. Cool this and pour to the rasgulla pot or pan.

title: Rasmalai Recipe
ingredients: 1.25 liter milk, 1.5 tbsp lemon juice, 1 liter ice water, 1 cup refined white sugar, 3.5 cups water, 0.25 tsp cardamom powder, 0.75 liter milk, 0.5 cup sugar, pinch saffron, 2 tbsp pista
instructions: 1. Pour 3 cups milk to a heavy bottom pot and boil. 2. Add saffron and sugar when the milk comes to a boil. 3. Reduce the flame to medium. Stir every 2 to 3 minutes to prevent the milk from scorching at the bottom. 4. When a layer of cream forms, just move it aside. 5. Boil until the milk thickens and reduces to half the quantity. Set this aside to cool. 6. Cool half of this and chill in the fridge. Keep the other half warm. 7. Bring 5 cups milk to a boil in a pot. Turn off the stove. 8. Pour lemon juice and keep stirring until the milk has curdled completely. 9. If it doesn't curdle then add some more and stir. 10. Next if it fails to curdle then turn on the stove & regulate to a low flame. Stir until it curdles. 11. Turn off the stove. Pour ice water or add ice cubes to the pot and set aside for 2 mins. 12. Drain to a muslin cloth lined over a colander. 13. Pour fresh water over the chena and rinse well. You can also do it under running water for the sour taste to vanish. 14. Wrap the chena in the cloth. Squeeze off excess whey and make a knot. 15. Next hang it for about 45 minutes to 1 hour for the excess whey to drain off. 16. Chena should not have any excess dripping whey & should not be completely dry. It must be moist and grainy but not very sticky or dry. Please check the images above the recipe card. 17. Transfer the chenna to a plate. At this stage chenna should not have any excess whey in it. 18. Knead well for about 3 to 5 minutes until smooth. Chenna should not be grainy but must be smooth. Do not go by the time but focus on the texture - smooth, non grainy and non-sticky texture is required. 19. Make 12 equal balls and flatten them to make discs. 20. If the discs are slightly cracked on the sides, roll the sides gently. Set these aside. 21. Bring 3½ cups of water to a boil with 1 cup sugar. 22. Stir until the sugar dissolves completely. Then add cardamom powder. 23. When the sugar syrup begins to boil rapidly, then add the discs gently. 24. Cook covered for about 9 to 10 minutes on a moderately high flame. 25. Turn off the stove. Remove the pot from the stove to avoid cooking further. 26. Keep the lid closed for the next 20 mins to avoid them turning flat or shrinking. 27. When done they will sink in the syrup. 28. Take off the discs to a plate and cool them. 29. Squeeze them gently in between your palms to remove sugar syrup. 30. Add these to the half of the warm thickened milk or rabri. 31. Rest them for 3 to 4 hours. Pour the other half of the chilled rabri to this just before serving. 32. Garnish rasmalai with chopped nuts.

title: Rava Kesari Recipe (Kesari Bath)
ingredients: 1 cup semolina, 0.875 cup sugar, 2.5 cups water, 0.25 teaspoon cardamom powder, 2 pinches natural kesari color, 5.5 tablespoons Ghee, 12 Cashews, 12 raisins
instructions: 1. Heat 2 tbsp ghee in a heavy bottom pot and fry cashews until light golden. 2. Then add raisins and fry until they swell up. Remove to a plate and set aside. 3. In the same ghee add the rava and fry on a low to medium heat until aromatic and crunchy. 4. Do not brown or discolor the rava as the aroma of the rava kesari will change. 5. While the rava is getting roasted, pour water to another pot and bring it to a boil. 6. When the water turns boiling hot, slowly pour it to the rava by consistently stirring to prevent lumps. 7. The water will be absorbed by the semolina soon. Keep stirring and make sure there are no lumps. 8. When you see there is no excess water in the pan, then cover and cook on a low flame for 2 to 3 mins. This is just to ensure rava is cooked completely and turns fluffy. 9. Add sugar and mix well. Sugar will dissolve and the entire rava kesari mixture turns gooey. 10. Keep stirring and cook until the mixture thickens and the moisture is evaporated. 11. Then sprinkle cardamom powder, saffron or kesari and ghee. I used about 3 tbsp ghee. 12. Mix well and cook for 2 to 3 mins until the rava kesari leaves the pan. Cover and rest for 2 to 4 mins. 13. Grease a mould or round cup and then scoop some rava kesari to it. Level the top. 14. Invert the bowl or mould to a plate. Gently shake off. Garnish rava kesari with cashews and nuts. 15. You can also fluff up the rava kesari with a fork to make it fluffy.

title: Rava Laddu Recipe | Rava Ladoo
ingredients: 1 cup rava, 0.75 cup sugar, 0.25 cup desiccated coconut, 0.25 cup ghee, 1 teaspoon cardamom powder, 1 raisins, 1 cashews
instructions: 1. Add semolina & desiccated coconut to a pan and dry roast it on a medium to low flame stirring constantly for 6 to 7 mins. 2. It has to be evenly light golden without browning or burning. When it turns aromatic, light and crunchy, turn off the stove. Transfer this to a plate & cool down. 3. To a grinder jar, add sugar and cardamoms. Grind it to a fine powder and rest it in the jar itself for 2 to 3 mins. 4. When the semolina cools down, add it to the same grinder jar. 5. Grind to a slightly coarse powder. Taste test this. It has to be slightly coarse and not very coarse. If it is too coarse the ladoos will not bind.Transfer this to a mixing bowl. 6. Pour ghee in a hot pan. Add cashews & fry on a medium flame until light golden. 7. Then add the raisins and stir for 30 seconds. Turn off the heat. 8. Pour this hot ghee along with nuts and raisins to the rava laddu mixture. 9. Stir it with a spoon and not use your hand yet as it is too hot. 10. Once the ghee incorporates well, use your hand and mix everything well. 11. Take small portions of this mixture to your palm and press down hard with your finger to form a ball. (Check video). 12. Form the ladoos while the mixture is still hot. You will be able to make 10 to 11 rava laddus. If the mixture does not bind well, heat more ghee or milk in the same pan and add it to the mixture, 1 tablespoon at one time until you are able to do it. I did not use any more. 13. Store them in an air tight jar at room temperature and use up within a month.

title: Rava Uttapam (Sooji Uttapam)
ingredients: 1 cup semolina, 0.5 cup water, 0.5 cup curd, 0.41666666666666663 teaspoon salt, 0.25 teaspoon unflavored fruit salt, 1.25 tablespoon oil, 1 onion, 1.5 green chilies, 2 tablespoons carrot, 1 tomato, 2 tablespoons capsicum, 2 tablespoons coriander leaves
instructions: 1. Add semolina, water, salt and yogurt to a mixing bowl. Mix well to a batter and cover it. Rest for 20 mins. 2. Meanwhile chop the veggies and season the pan or tawa with oil. Wipe off excess with a kitchen tissue. 3. After 20 mins the batter turns thicker as it absorbs moisture. The consistency should be medium thick, of pouring consistency (not runny) 4. If the batter is too thick you may splash a bit of water - 1 to 2 tablespoons & stir. (Check pics in the post to know the consistency) 5. Heat a pan on a medium high flame. Drizzle a few drops of oil on the pan and smear with a kitchen tissue carefully. 6. Optional - if you want you may stir in eno or baking soda at this point. 7. When the pan is hot, pour ⅓ cup of batter and spread a bit gently. Do not spread it a lot. It has to be thick. Sprinkle the veggies and press with a spatula. 8. Add ½ to ¾ teaspoon oil or ghee around the edges and cook on a medium heat until you see the edges loosen from the pan. 9. Turn the uttapam carefully to the other side with a spatula. 10. Cook until the veggies are partially done or brown to your liking. Turn back and cook until the rava uttapam turns lightly crisp. Transfer to a serving plate. Before you make the next uttapam, make sure your pan is hot enough. 11. If you want you can also sprinkle some Podi or cheese. Serve rava uttapam hot or warm with chutney or masala tea.

title: Raw Banana Fry (Aratikaya Vepudu)
ingredients: 3 Raw banana, 2 pinches turmeric, 0.25 tsp mustard, 0.5 tsp Cumin, 1 tsp Urad dal, 2.5 Green chilies, 0.5 inch ginger, 4 cloves garlic, 1 onion, 1 sprig curry leaves, 0.625 tsp garam masala powder, 2 tbsp Oil
instructions: 1. Peel raw banana. Cut to half vertically. Slice and transfer them to a bowl full of water. The pieces should not be too thin or too thick. 2. Make a paste of green chilli, ginger and garlic. Set this aside. 3. Coarsely crush or pulse onions in a blender. 4. Heat a deep Kadai or pan with oil. 5. Add urad dal, mustard and cumin. Let the dal fry till lightly golden without burning. Add ginger garlic and chili paste. 6. Fry till you begin to get a nice aroma. It should begin to turn slightly golden. 7. Drain the water from raw banana and add them to the Kadai. Add turmeric. 8. Mix and fry on medium flame till they are almost cooked. Stirring constantly to prevent burning. 9. Sprinkle salt evenly and fry on a medium flame till they turn soft, cooked and tender. Do not add salt until the banana is almost cooked, otherwise it will not get cooked further. 10. Add the coarsely crushed (pulsed) chili onions paste. Fry on high till the onions begin to caramelize. Before adding onions make sure the banana is cooked well. 11. Add garam masala, red chili powder (optional) and fry on a low flame till they firm up. Since there is green chili used you can use very little red chili powder for the color. But I haven’t used since we like the flavor of green chili in this. Raw banana fry Vazhakkai Fry (Po... Bajji Recipe (Ara... Banana Curry (Pla... Green Banana Reci... Eggless chocolate...

title: Red Sauce Pasta Recipe (Red Pasta Recipe)
ingredients: 1.5 cups fusilli, 2.5 tablespoons extra virgin olive oil, 1 teaspoon sea salt, 3.5 garlic cloves, 0.25 cup shallot, 25 grams tomatoes, 0.5 cup red bell peppers, 0.75 teaspoons red chili flakes, 0.5 teaspoon mixed Italian herbs, 0.25 teaspoon black pepper, 6 cups water, parmesan
instructions: 1. Spread tomatoes and bell peppers on a baking tray and add a tbsp. of oil. Bake for 15 mins at 425 F - 220 C, until the tomatoes are softened. Or alternately, pan fry the tomatoes and bell peppers on a medium high heat until blistered & soft, for about 5 to 6 mins. Or Air fry for 9 mins at 390F / 200 C. 2. Cool down and blend to a smooth sauce with chili flakes. 3. Bring water to a rolling boil and add the pasta along with salt (if required, check the package). On a medium high heat, cook al dente following the timings on the pack. Mine takes 11 mins. 4. Reserve half cup pasta stock and drain to a colander. Cover and keep aside. 5. Heat a pan with oil and add garlic. Saute for a minute and add the onions. 6. Saute until transparent. Stir in the chili flakes and pour tomato puree. Saute for 3 to 4 mins and pour the reserved pasta stock. 7. Cook for another 3 to 4 mins until thick (check pics) and add the herbs, black pepper (optional) and salt. Taste test and add sugar if your sauce is too tart. 8. Stir the pasta into the red sauce. Garnish with parmesan and black pepper if you want.

title: Reshmi Kabab | Chicken Malai Kabab
ingredients: 25 grams chicken, 2 tablespoons cream, 2 tablespoons greek yogurt, 1.25 tablespoon lemon juice, 0.5 teaspoon black pepper, 2 tablespoons coriander leaves, 1.5 teaspoon ginger garlic paste, 0.5 teaspoon salt, 1 teaspoon oil, 2 tablespoons almond meal, 2 tablespoons cheese, 1 tablespoon butter, 0.5 cup Mint Chutney
instructions: 1. Add chicken to a bowl and pat dry any excess moisture. This prevents the chicken malai tikkas from letting out lots of moisture. 2. In a separate bowl, mix together almond meal, fresh cream, thick yogurt, cheese (optional), lemon juice, oil, salt, black pepper, ginger garlic and coriander leaves. 3. Mix them well and taste test. If you want you may add more salt and black pepper to adjust to your taste. This mixture should be ideally thick. 4. Pour this over the chicken and mix well. Cover and set aside in the refrigerator for atleast 4 hours to overnight. 5. Preheat the oven to the highest. At 240 C or 470 F for at least 15 mins. 6. Thread the chicken on the skewers and place them on a prepared baking tray. Grill for 15 mins. 7. Turn them to the other side and grill again for 7 to 8 mins. Check after 22 to 23 mins of grilling to prevent burning. 8. The last 2 minutes grill them on a broil mode for charring. Remove the grilled chicken malai tikkas from the oven and brush butter over them. 9. Serve chicken malai kababs with mint chutney.

title: Ridge Gourd Recipe
ingredients: 5 grams ridge gourd, 1 tbsp Oil, 1 sprig curry leaves, 0.5 tsp mustard, 0.5 tsp cumin, 1 Pinch hing, 2 garlic cloves, 2 onions, 1 green chili, 0.5 cup tomatoes, Salt, 0.125 tsp turmeric powder, 0.5 tsp red chili powder, 0.75 tbsp chana dal, 0.75 tbsp urad dal, 1 garlic, 1 tbsp coconut, 1 tsp sesame seeds, 0.5 tsp jeera, 1 red chilli, 1 pinch methi seeds
instructions: 1. Dry roast red chilli, chana dal and urad dal until the dal turns golden and aromatic. 2. Then add garlic, sesame seeds, jeera and coconut. Fry until coconut turns aromatic. 3. Cool completely and make a fine powder. Set this aside 4. Wash ridge gourd well, then peel off the skin. If using organic gourd, then you can use the peel to make chutney. 5. Chop the ridge gourd to half inch pieces and set aside. 6. Wash and chop onions & tomatoes finely. I do not use onions for this recipe just to cut down the cook time. 7. Heat the pot with oil, then add mustard and cumin. When they begin to crackle, add hing and curry leaves. Then add garlic and chilli. Saute for 1 minute. 8. If using onions add them and fry till transparent. Then add the ridge gourd and fry on a medium flame for 3 to 4 minutes. 9. Add tomatoes, salt, red chili powder and turmeric. Mix and fry for about 2 minutes. 10. Cover and cook on a low flame. After a while the gourd will begin to ooze out moisture. Keep stirring occasionally to prevent burning, continue cooking till the tomatoes and gourd are soft cooked. 11. Some gourds do not let out any moisture in that case sprinkle 3 to 4 tbsps water and cook. I do not use water at all. 12. When the ridge gourd is finished cooking, you should be left with little thin gravy. 13. Sprinkle the spice powder made earlier. 14. Cover and cook until the curry turns slightly thick for about 4 to 5 mins. Taste the curry and add more salt as desired. 15. Serve ridge gourd curry with rice or roti.

title: Rogan Josh Recipe (Lamb Rogan Josh)
ingredients: 1.1 lbs bone-in mutton, 2.5 tablespoons ghee, 0.25 teaspoon asafoetida, 0.5 cup curd, 0.75 teaspoon salt, 1.25 tablespoon Kashmiri red chili powder, 1 cup water, 1.25 cup sliced shallots, 1 bay leaf, 3.5 cloves 3 to 4 cloves ((or ¼ teaspoon ground cloves)), 1 inch cinnamon, 3 green cardamoms, 1 black cardamom, 2 teaspoon fennel seeds powder, 1 teaspoon ginger powder, 1 teaspoon garam masala
instructions: 1. This step is optional. But it helps to thicken the rogan josh. Heat 1.5 tablespoons oil in a pan and saute shallots/onions on a medium heat until golden, about to caramelize but not burnt. Cool them and blend with 2 to 3 tablespoons of yogurt to a smooth paste. Keep this aside. 2. Heat oil or ghee in a heavy bottom pot and add bay leaf, green cardamoms, cinnamon, cloves and black cardamoms. (If you opt for the ground spices, add them later.) 3. When the spices begin to sizzle in oil, add mutton and sear it for 1 to 2 minute on a high heat. 4. Reduce the heat to medium and fry for another 4 to 5 minutes until the meat is slightly brown. 5. While you fry the meat, add yogurt to a mixing bowl. Also add ground spices - red chilli powder, fennel powder, dried ginger powder and salt. 6. Whisk all of them well. If your yogurt is too thick (like Greek yogurt), add 3 to 4 tablespoons of water or stock and whisk it well until smooth. 7. Once the meat is light brown in color, add hing to the oil. Take the pot off the stove and slowly stir in the spice and yogurt mixture. 8. Mix well and place the pot back on the stove. Cook covered for 3 to 4 minutes on a medium heat. 9. Pour 1 cup hot water or stock. Cook covered on a low heat until tender, adding more hot water if required. 10. Stir rogan josh every 7 to 8 minutes. When it is done, the mutton pieces should be soft, tender and fall off the bone. (Optional - During the last 20 minutes of cooking add the blended onion paste and mix well.) 11. The cook time depends on the kind of pot, source of heat and the intensity of heat. Do not forget to add more hot water (if required) as you cook. It takes me 2 hours for the mutton to get tender. 12. The gravy in Rogan josh is neither too runny nor too thick but is slightly thick as the some of the bone marrow seeps into the liquids. This results can be achieved only by slow cooking the dish. 13. Taste test and add more salt or garam masala if required. Turn off the heat and rest for few hours before serving. This helps to develop the flavors. 14. Serve rogan josh with plain steamed basmati rice, Saffron Rice, Tandoori Roti, Butter Naan, Paratha or Roti.

title: Royyala iguru
ingredients: 25 grams prawns, 2 medium onions, 1 green chili, 1 sprig curry leaves, 0.25 tsp cumin, Salt, 0.125 tsp turmeric, 0.5 tsp ginger garlic paste, 0.25 tsp red chili powder, 1 tbsp Lemon juice, 1 tsp garam masala powder, 1 tbsp Coriander leaves, 1.5 tbsp oil
instructions: 1. Clean and wash prawns. Drain water thoroughly, marinate with ½ tsp. ginger garlic paste, ¼ tsp. red chili powder, little salt and lemon juice. Set this aside till you prepare the rest. 2. Heat a pan with oil, add cumin, and curry leaves and green chili. Sauté till the curry leaves turn crisp 3. Add onions, sprinkle salt and fry till they turn golden, reduce the flame to minimum (sim), continue to fry till they caramelize. Take care not to burn them. 4. Add ginger garlic paste and give a quick stir. Since the onions are already caramelized the ginger garlic can get burnt. Take care 5. Add the marinated prawns and fry for 2 to 3 mins 6. Add the rest of the red chili powder, turmeric and garam masala. Give a good stir and fry for a min 7. Sprinkle some water just enough to bring in some moisture for the prawns to get cooked. I used almost 90 ml water. 8. Cover and cook on a medium flame till they are completely cooked. I understand them to be cooked when they curl up and turn pink or orange. If there is more water left, you can evaporate by cooking further without a lid 9. Garnish royyala iguru with coriander leaves

title: Saag Aloo Recipe
ingredients: 25 grams potatoes, 15 grams baby spinach, 0.5 cup onion, 0.3333333333333333 cup tomatoes, 1.5 green chilies, 1 teaspoon ginger, 1 teaspoon medium garlic, 0.375 teaspoon Kashmiri red chili powder, 0.125 teaspoon turmeric, 0.5 teaspoon Garam masala, 0.5 teaspoon coriander powder, 0.25 teaspoon cumin powder, 0.41666666666666663 teaspoon sea salt, 0.5 teaspoon cumin seeds, 2 tablespoons oil, 0.5 tablespoon kasuri methi, 0.5 cup water, 2 tablespoons coriander leaves, 1.5 tablespoon lemon juice
instructions: 1. Use only the leaves and avoid stems of the leafy greens. If using kale, destem them as well. 2. Rinse your greens in plenty of water and drain them to a colander. Shake off to drain the water completely. Keep aside to use later. Prepare the other ingredients. 3. Heat oil/ghee in a wide pan. Add cumin seeds. When the seeds begin to sizzle add onions and saute for 4 to 5 minutes. 4. Add potatoes, ginger, garlic and green chilies. Saute for another 1 minute. Stir in salt, chili powder, turmeric, coriander powder, cumin powder and garam masala. 5. Pour water and cook covered until the potatoes are al dente, not fully cooked or mushy. If required add more water as they cook. Taste test and add more salt or spices to taste. While the potatoes cook, chop the greens roughly & keep them ready. 6. Add tomatoes and cook on a medium high heat until they lose the raw flavor. Avoid over stirring here. 7. Optional - Add chopped fenugreek leaves/kale/mustard greens if using and stir fry until they wilt completely. 8. Add spinach in batches and saute until wilted. Add crushed kasuri methi, microgreens if using and turn off. Mix well to wilt the microgreens. 9. Let cool down a bit and squeeze in lemon juice and garnish with coriander leaves/ microgreens if you want.

title: Saag Paneer Recipe
ingredients: 25 grams paneer, 4 cups baby spinach, 4 cups mustard greens, 1.5 to 2 tablespoon ghee, 0.75 cup onions, 1.5 green chilies, 0.5 inch ginger, 3 medium garlic, 0.5 cup tomatoes, 0.3333333333333333 cup carrot, 0.125 teaspoon turmeric powder, 0.875 teaspoon coriander powder, 0.5 teaspoon cumin powder, 0.25 cup yogurt, 0.25 cup water, 0.5 teaspoon salt, 0.5 teaspoon sugar, 0.875 teaspoon garam masala, 0.25 cup heavy cream, 1 lemon, 1 tablespoon unsalted butter, 0.125 teaspoon asafoetida, 1.5 garlic, 1.5 red chili, 0.625 teaspoon Kashmiri red chili powder
instructions: 1. Rinse the spinach and mustard greens at least thrice in plenty of water. Drain them to a colander. If using kale prepare reading the notes below. 2. Add ½ tablespoon ghee to a non-stick pan, spread and heat it. Place the cold paneer on the pan and fry on a medium high heat, until the bottom turns golden. Turn the pieces to the other side and repeat frying until golden. Keep this aside 3. Heat the rest of the ghee in a pan and add onions, green chilies, ginger and garlic. Saute until the raw flavor from onions disappear, for about 7 to 8 minutes. 4. Stir in coriander, turmeric and cumin. Add tomatoes and yogurt. Mix well. Cover and cook until the tomatoes break down to a mush. The onions should completely become soft. 5. Add carrot and saute for 2 mins, to bring out the sweet flavors. 6. Add spinach and mustard greens. Let them steam/cook or saute until wilted & cooked. It should take you about 5 to 6 minutes. 7. Pour water & reserve a green chili aside. Blend to a slightly coarse puree using an immersion blender or a blender. If using a blender, cool down first. If the mixture is too thick, use a splash of water. Taste test and if you want add the reserved chili & blend. 8. Mix together saag puree and salt in the pan. Bring it to a gentle simmer and cook on a medium heat for 6 to 7 mins, or until it no more smells raw. If it is too thick to your liking, add some hot water during this process. 9. Add garam masala, fried paneer (retain some to garnish) and sugar (if you need). Mix together very well first and then stir in the cream. Cook for another 3 to 4 minutes, until aromatic. 10. To Temper – Add butter to a pan. Turn on the stove & regulate to low heat. Before the butter melts, add garlic and let cook for 2 minutes on a very low heat to infuse the flavors. Do not brown the garlic, add dried red chili & fry until crisp. 11. Turn off the heat. Add hing and red chili powder or flakes. Pour this over Saag Paneer & garnish with fried paneer. Serve with Tandoori Roti or Basmati Rice. Squeeze lemon juice while serving.

title: Saffron Rice (with Basmati)
ingredients: 1 cup basmati rice, 1.5 to 2 cups water, 1.5 tablespoons unsalted butter, 0.3333333333333333 teaspoon sea salt, 0.25 teaspoon saffron stands, 0.5 teaspoon ginger, 0.25 teaspoon cardamom, 0.25 teaspoon cinnamon, 0.25 teaspoon nutmeg, 0.25 cup toasted pistachios, 0.25 cup sweet raisins
instructions: 1. Add rice to a colander or bowl and rinse well thrice. Drain completely. Mix together cardamom, nutmeg and cinnamon in a small bowl. Keep this aside. Crush the saffron in a mortar pestle to a fine powder. 2. Melt butter in a saucepan and add raisins. Fry them on a medium heat, until plump. Remove to a small bowl, retaining most of the ghee in the pan. 3. Stir in the rice gently to coat it with the rest of the butter and let toast on a high heat for 2 mins. Stir gently only 1 to 2 times (taking care not to overdo else the grains can break) and toast for another 1 to 2 mins. 4. Add crushed saffron, ginger, salt and pour water. Give a gentle mix and bring the water to a rolling boil, on a high heat. 5. Reduce the heat to low and cook covered until the water is fully absorbed and the rice is cooked through. (It takes me about 13 to 15 mins but can vary depending on the cookware and intensity of heat) 6. Sprinkle the ground spices all over and cover back the pot. Let rest for 10 mins. Later fluff up the rice gently with a fork to spread the spices all over. 7. Garnish Saffron Rice with chopped toast pistachios, raisins or pomegranate arils. Serve with curry, lentils, kababs, grilled veggies or kofta.

title: Sagu Recipe
ingredients: 2 potatoes, 0.25 cup green peas, 1 sprig curry leaves, 2.5 green chilies, 0.25 inch ginger, 0.25 cup fried gram, 0.25 teaspoon mustard, 0.25 teaspoon cumin, 1 pinch hing, 1 pinch turmeric, Salt, 2 tablespoons Oil, 2 tablespoons lemon juice
instructions: 1. Peel and chop potatoes to small cubes. The smaller, the quicker the curry gets cooked. I chop them to almost half inch. Heat oil in a deep pan, add mustard, cumin and curry leaves. When the spices begin to crackle, add hing, ginger, slit green chilies, potatoes and peas. Fry for 2 to 3 minutes. Make sure you add enough green chilies since there is no red chili powder used. The heat comes only from green chilies. If you want to a use onions, add them before the potatoes and saute for 4 to 5 mins. 2. Pour water to cover the potatoes and bring the water to a rolling boil. Reduce the heat to medium and cook until the potatoes are fork tender/ fully cooked. 3. While the potatoes cook, make a fine powder of the fried gram. 4. Add very little water to make a thick paste but of pouring consistency. Add salt and turmeric to the paste. 5. Once the potatoes are soft cooked, pour this mix and let it simmer till the gravy thickens. If your gravy becomes runny, you can mix some more fried gram powder with very little water and pour it to the gravy. When it is thick, yet of pouring consistency, tun off the heat. It will thicken further upon cooling. Taste test and adjust salt. 6. Add lemon juice and stir well. Garnish with coriander leaves if you want.

title: Salmon Curry Recipe
ingredients: 1 lb salmon, 2 tablespoons oil, 0.25 teaspoon mustard seeds, 1.5 sprigs curry leaves, 2.5 garlic cloves, 1 cup onion, 1 green chili slit, 0.25 cup tomato, 0.625 teaspoon salt, 1.125 teaspoon red chilli powder, 0.25 teaspoon turmeric, 0.5 teaspoon garam masala, 1.125 teaspoon coriander powder, 1.5 tablespoons tamarind, 1.25 cups water, 0.5 cup coconut milk, 2 sprigs coriander leaves
instructions: 1. Bring salmon to room temperature. Rinse it well under running water and pat dry with a kitchen tissue. 2. If using tamarind, soak it in half cup hot water. 3. Pour 1 tbsp oil to a nonstick pan and heat it. 4. When the oil turns hot, place the salmon with the skin side down. 5. Sprinkle 1 pinch of each - salt, red chilli powder and garam masala. Fry it for 1 to 2 mins & then carefully flip. 6. Repeat sprinkling salt, red chili powder and garam masala on the other side. Add curry leaves towards the sides in the oil. 7. Fry just for another 1 to 2 mins depending on the thickness of the fillet. 8. Salmon should be partially cooked, not fully. Gently remove it to a plate along with crispy curry leaves and set aside. 9. Pour 1 tablespoon oil to the pan and heat it. 10. Add mustard seeds. When they pop, add curry leaves and garlic. 11. Saute until the garlic smells fragrant. 12. Then saute onions and green chilies until onions turn golden. 13. Then add tomatoes, salt, turmeric, red chilli powder, garam masala and coriander powder. 14. Saute all of these until tomatoes break down and the masala turns fragrant. The mixture should leave the sides of the pan as well. 15. Pour 1 cup water and ¼ cup tamarind water. Mix well and cook until the onions turn completely soft. The curry should thick yet slightly be runny at this stage. 16. Taste the curry and add more salt and tamarind if needed. 17. Optional - Pour coconut milk and stir. 18. Gently place the salmon (skin side down) and pour some of the curry over the fillets. 19. Cover and cook on the lowest flame until the salmon is cooked completely. When it is done you can see the color changes. Press down the fillet gently, if done it flakes. 20. Sprinkle coriander leaves. Rest the salmon curry for atleast 2 hours before serving.

title: Salmon Rice Bowl
ingredients: 35 grams salmon, 1 tablespoon oil, 0.5 teaspoon paprika, 0.75 teaspoon garlic powder, 0.75 teaspoon onion powder, 0.1875 teaspoon black pepper, 0.29166666666666663 teaspoon salt, 0.625 teaspoon garam masala, 0.25 teaspoon sugar, 1 teaspoon soya sauce, 0.5 teaspoon rice vinegar, 1 cup basmati rice, 1.5 to 2 cups water, 0.5 teaspoon salt, 0.625 tablespoon butter, 0.5 teaspoon turmeric, 0.75 teaspoon garlic powder, 0.625 teaspoon garam masala, 0.25 teaspoon black pepper crushed, 1 cucumber, 1 medium ripe tomato, 0.25 cup parsley, 1 red onion, 1 teaspoon extra virgin olive oil, 1 teaspoon lemon juice, salt if required, 0.25 teaspoon black pepper, 1 cup yogurt, 0.75 cup mint leaves, 0.5 cup cilantro, 1 garlic clove, 0.25 inch ginger, 0.29166666666666663 teaspoon salt, 0.375 green chili pepper
instructions: 1. Rinse rice at least thrice and drian the water completely. To a pot/pan, add butter and melt it on a low flame. Stir in turmeric and salt, followed by water/stock. 2. Cover and bring the water to a rapid boil. Add the rice and increase the heat to bring to a boil. 3. Reduce the heat completely and cook covered until all of the water is absorbed by the rice. This takes about 12 to 14 mins depending on your stove. 4. Once the water is absorbed, open the lid and sprinkle the garlic powder, black pepper and garam masala. Do not stir. Cover and let rest for 15 mins. Later taste test and add more salt and seasoning if required. 5. While the rice cooks, pluck the mint and coriander leaves, rinse, drain and add them to a small grinder/blender. Add ginger garlic salt pepper or chili and 1 tablespoon yogurt. 6. Blend until smooth and stir in the rest of the yogurt. Mix well with a whisk, do not blend after adding more yogurt. Taste test and adjust salt. 7. Wash and chop the cucumbers, tomatoes, onions and herbs. Transfer them to a bowl. Sprinkle lemon juice, oil and black pepper just before serving. 8. Mix together soya sauce and vinegar in a bowl. Keep aside. Add all the seasoning spices to a bowl and mix well. Keep aside. 9. Pat dry the salmon cubes. Heat a non-stick pan with oil and spread it all over the pan. Place the salmon cubes (with the skin side down if it has skin). 10. Let cook for 3 to 4 mins on a medium to medium high heat. Using a spatula or a tong gently turn them to the other side and pan-fry until cooked through, for another 3 to 4 mins, depending on the thickness of the salmon. 11. Salmon is considered fully cooked when the internal temperature of the thickest part reaches 145 F. If you do not have a meat thermometer, you will know it is done when it flakes easily with the touch of a fork. 12. Pour soya sauce/tamari and vinegar all over the salmon. Immediately sprinkle the spices all over. Turn off the heat and gently turn the pieces if required to coat the sauce and spices in the pan. Taste test and adjust the seasoning. 13. Fluff up the rice in the pot with a fork and divide the rice to 2 large serving bowls. Place the salmon, salad and drizzle yogurt mint sauce as preferred. 14. Serve salmon rice bowls hot or warm.

title: Sambar Powder Recipe (Sambar Masala)
ingredients: 1.5 tablespoons bengal gram, 1.5 tablespoon black gram, 1 tablespoon toor dal, 1 red chilies, 0.25 cup coriander seeds 4 tbsps of 22 to 25 grams, 0.75 teaspoon methi seeds, 2 sprigs curry leaves, 2 teaspoon cumin, 1 teaspoon pepper corn, 0.125 teaspoon asafoetida, 0.25 teaspoon turmeric
instructions: 1. Heat a pan. If making sambar powder in large quantity then dry roast each ingredient separately as the cooking time is different. Since this recipe is to make little sambar powder I have roasted them together but in batches. 2. Pour 1 ½ tablespoon chana dal, 1 ½ tablespoon urad dal, 1 tablespoon toor dal and 8 to 12 dried red chilies. You can also use more red chilies than mentioned in the recipe. But with the amount I mentioned it was too hot for my kids. So if you have kids do adjust. Dry roast them on a low flame until the dal is golden in color & begin to turn aromatic. 3. Add ¼ cup coriander seeds and ¾ teaspoon methi seeds. Roast until coriander seeds turn crunchy and the methi seeds are deep roasted & aromatic. 4. When you smell the roasted methi seeds, add 2 sprigs curry leaves and roast until they turn crisp. By now the methi seeds will turn slightly darker in color. 5. Add 2 teaspoon cumin seeds and roast till crunchy. You will smell the cumin good very soon. Turn off the stove. If you prefer to use pepper corn, you can add 1 teaspoon black pepper corn along with cumin. We use pepper only for rasam powder. 6. Add ¼ teaspoon turmeric and 1/8 teaspoon hing to this. Turmeric is used here as a preservative to keep the sambar masala good. Transfer all these to a wide plate and cool completely.

title: Sambar rice
ingredients: 0.25 cup toor dal, 0.5 cup rice, 2 cups water, 1 sprig curry leaves, 1 pinch asafoetida, 1.5 tbsp ghee, 2 pinches turmeric, 0.25 tsp red chilli powder, 9 shallots, 1 drumstick, 1 carrot, 1 tomato, 1 tbsp tamarind, 0.5 tsp salt, 0.5 tsp chana dal, 0.5 tsp urad dal, 1 red chilies, 0.5 tsp cumin seeds, 1.5 tsp coriander seeds, 0.75 tbsp coconut, 0.125 tsp methi
instructions: 1. Add ½ cup rice and ¼ cup plus 2 tbsp dal to a pressure cooker. I have used a mix of toor and moong dal. Wash and soak for about 15 minutes. Cook with 2 cups water for 1 to 2 whistles on a medium flame. You can use a combination of white and brown rice or white rice and fox tail millet. If using millets, soak at least for 2 hours. 2. If using brown rice, the texture turns out perfect even when the dal and rice are cooked together. Since brown rice doesn’t turn very mushy but the dal turns very soft and hence adds a smooth texture to the sambar rice. Mash the dal and rice. Dal must become soft and blend well with rice. 3. If you have ready sambar powder, skip this one and the next 2 steps. But i suggest making this powder to get a very flavorful dish. Dry roast the ingredients for making the powder. You will find double the quantity of ingredients as i doubled it to make this recipe twice. First roast dals with red chilies until lightly golden, add coriander seeds. When they get roasted, add methi, cumin. Switch off the stove when you begin to smell the methi good. 4. Add coconut and toss in the hot pan for a while. You can also skip coconut if you do not have. 5. Cool these and powder finely. 6. Heat ghee or oil in a pan. Add red chili, mustard and cumin. When they splutter add curry leaves and hing. When the curry leaves turn crisp, i set aside half of this seasoning to use at the last step. 7. Add shallots, carrots, beans and drumsticks. If you are short of time skip drumsticks since they lot of time to cook. Chayote/ chow chow or mullangi can also be used. 8. Saute well for 2 to 3 minutes. Add tomatoes, red chilli powder (optional) and turmeric. You can also skip tomatoes. Saute for 3 to 4 minutes. 9. Add 1 cup water and cook until the veggies are soft cooked. 10. Make sure the veggies are cooked completely before adding tamarind. so check the drumstick & then proceed. 11. Add salt. 12. Add the sambar powder. 13. Simmer well for few minutes until the spice powder begins to smell good. Check if there is enough sour, salt and spice. You can adjust now. 14. Add the mashed rice. 15. Stir well and simmer until dal rice blends with the sambar masala. Taste test and add more salt, tamarind or sambar powder if needed. To add more sambar powder, simply stir it with little hot water and pour. Add more hot water if needed to bring it to a consistency. 16. Add the tempering that was set aside. This doesn’t give any special tadka flavor but i still prefer. Serve sambar rice with papad and ghee.

title: Savory Steel Cut Oats
ingredients: 0.25 cup steel cut oats, 0.25 cup moong dal, 1 tablespoon ghee, 0.5 teaspoon cumin, 1 bay leaf, 0.75 teaspoon ginger, 0.125 teaspoon turmeric, 0.5 teaspoon salt, 0.25 teaspoon red chilli powder, 2 tablespoon onions, 1 medium tomato, 0.25 cup mix vegetables, 2 cups water, 1 pinch hing, 0.25 teaspoon garam masala
instructions: 1. Rinse steel cut oats and lentils thrice and soak them until you chop and prep up the ingredients. 2. Heat ghee in a pressure cooker or pot. Add cumin and bay leaf. When the cumin splutters, add hing. Add onions and saute until pink, stir in ginger and saute for 1 minute. 3. Add tomatoes, veggies, turmeric and salt. Saute until the tomatoes turn mushy. Stir in the red chili powder, garam masala, oats and lentils. 4. Saute for 2 mins and pour water. Give a good mix and pressure cook on a medium heat for 2 to 3 whistles. To cook in a pot without cooker, bring to a rolling boil. Reduce the heat and cook until the lentils and oats are tender. Add some hot water if it is to thick. 5. Open the lid when the pressure drops and give a good mix. Taste test and adjust salt. Serve steel cut oats hot or warm topped with a tsp of ghee or lemon juice 6. Press saute button on the IP. When it displays hot add ghee or oil. Add cumin and bay leaf. Saute for 30 seconds and quickly add onions. 7. Saute for 2 mins and then add hing, ginger, tomatoes and salt. Saute until the tomatoes break down and turn soft. 8. Saute veggies, turmeric and chili powder for a minute. Then add dal, steel cut oats and pour water. Mix well. 9. Secure the insta pot lid and set the steam release handle to sealing. 10. Press the porridge button. It is set to 20 mins. You can also pressure cook on high for 10 mins. 11. Wait for the natural pressure release. When the pin drops, open and stir the steel cut oats. Taste test and adjust salt to taste. Add little ghee and lemon juice.

title: Schezwan Fried Rice Recipe
ingredients: 1.5 cups rice, 0.25 cup spring onions, 0.75 cup carrots, 0.75 cup french beans, 0.75 cup capsicum, 1.5 tablespoons oil, 0.5 tablespoon ginger, 1 tablespoon garlic, 1 medium onion, 2 tablespoons celery, 1.5 tablespoons oil, 1.5 tablespoons soya sauce, 1 tablespoon tomato ketchup, 0.625 tablespoons vinegar, 0.875 tablespoon red chili powder, 0.5 tablespoon sugar, 0.625 teaspoon salt, 0.875 teaspoon Sichuan pepper corn, 3 tablespoons water
instructions: 1. Wash rice and soak for 20 to 30 mins. Bring 6 cups water to a boil. 2. When the water begins to boil rapidly, then add rice and 1 teaspoon oil. Cook on a medium high flame till al dente. 3. Al dente rice is firm but fully cooked. Make sure rice is not over cooked. 4. When the rice is cooked al dente, drain it to a colander. 5. While the cooked rice cools completely, chop the vegetables. 6. Pour oil to a hot wok. Fry ginger & garlic till they smell good. 7. Next add onions and fry until golden. 8. Skip this step if you have the sauce ready. Stir in red chili powder with water. Add this to the pan along with soya sauce, vinegar, ketchup, sugar, salt and schezwan pepper. Cook until it thickens. Pour 3 tbsps more water and cook till it thickens, Remove to a bowl. To the same pan, add 1½ tablespoon more oil. 9. On a high flame, fry spring onion whites, carrots, beans & capsicum until half cooked yet crunchy. 10. Next add Schezwan sauce, cooled rice and spring onion greens. 11. Mix and stir fry schezwan rice for 2 minutes. Taste test and adjust salt and spice if needed. 12. Serve schezwan fried rice hot or warm with any Indo chinese side.

title: Schezwan Sauce Recipe
ingredients: 26.5 red chilies, 1 cup water, 0.25 cup oil, 2 tbsps garlic, 1 tbsp ginger, 9 sichuan peppercorn, 3 tbsps onions, 0.5 cup water, 1.25 tbsp sugar, 0.875 tsp salt, 0.875 tbsp soya sauce, 0.875 tbsp vinegar, 0.25 tsp pepper powder, 0.25 cup celery, 2 tbsp tomato sauce
instructions: 1. Add the red chilies to a bowl and soak them in hot water for 30 mins. 2. To reduce the heat you can deseed and use them. 3. Meanwhile chop onions, ginger and garlic. 4. After 30 mins, add the red chilies to a blender jar. 5. Make a fine paste. If needed you can add 1 to 2 tbsp water. Set this aside. 6. Heat oil in a pan. Saute garlic, ginger and sichuan pepper for 2 mins. If using celery, add it now. 7. Add onions and fry until they turn transparent. Do not brown the ginger garlic as they may lend a bitter taste. 8. Stir in the chili paste, sugar, salt and pepper powder. Saute for 2 mins. Pour half cup water and mix well. 9. Cover and cook on a medium heat till the sauce turns thick. 10. Open the lid and cook further stirring often until oil begins to separate. 11. When you see the mixture begins to leave the sides of the pan, add soya sauce and vinegar. The sauce must taste hot with hints of sour and sweetness. 12. Optional - If using tomato sauce add it now. 13. Mix well and cook just for 1 to 2 mins. Taste it and add more salt or vinegar if needed. 14. Cool schezwan sauce completely and store it in a glass jar. Refrigerate for better shelf life. 15. The prepared sauce can be enjoyed as a side dip with snacks. It can be used to make noodles, fried rice or schezwan snacks. 16. To make fried rice or noodles, heat 1 tbsp oil in a wok. Fry onions and bell peppers on a high flame. Then add the rice or noodles and sauce as desired. Mix and fry on a high flame for 2 mins.

title: Set Dosa Recipe (Sponge Dosa)
ingredients: 2 cups raw rice, 1 cup poha, 0.5 cup urad dal, 1.5 teaspoons fenugreek seeds, 0.5 teaspoon salt, oil
instructions: 1. Soaking : To make set dosa recipe, firstly add rice to a large bowl and wash very well a few times. Then soak in ample amount of water for about 5 hours. Add urad dal and fenugreek seeds to another bowl and rinse them a few times. Then soak in ample amount of water for about 5 hours. Wash poha just before grinding the batter. 2. Making batter: Drain the water from from both the bowls. Add urad dal, salt, fenugreek seeds and poha to a blender jar along with 1 cup water. Blend them till bubbly. If needed add more water to grind the batter smooth & frothy. Do not add too much water, batter should not be very runny. 3. Transfer the batter to a large batter bowl. Add rice to the jar along with 1/4 cup water. Grind to a smooth batter. Pour this to the urad dal batter. Mix everything well till well combined. 4. Fermenting : Set aside in a warm place for 8 to 12 hours for the batter to ferment and rise well. 5. Add few drops of oil to a tawa. Using a tissue or cut onion spread the oil very well. Heat the tawa. Mix the batter gently. Taste test and add more salt if required. 6. Pour one ladle full of batter and dab it gently to spread the batter do not spread like a normal dosa. Add oil across the edges. 7. Cover and cook for a while until it is cooked inside. You can flip and cook it for a minute on the inner side as well. 8. To get a crispy set dosa, lower the flame and cook for a while. Sprinkle idli podi if you have it or serve hot with chutney. Repeat making more set dosa on the tawa.

title: Shahi Korma Recipe
ingredients: 1 kg bone-in chicken, 2 tablespoons lemon juice, 1 teaspoon salt, 1 tablespoon Kashmiri red chili powder, 1 tablespoon garlic paste, 1 tablespoon ginger paste, 1.5 teaspoon korma masala, 1 tablespoon coriander powder, 0.25 teaspoon turmeric, 0.29166666666666663 cup oil, 2.75 cups onions, 1 cup yogurt, 1.125 tablespoon korma masala, 0.25 cup heavy cream, 1 almonds, 15 whole cashew nuts, 0.29166666666666663 cup water, 0.29166666666666663 teaspoon nutmeg, 0.25 teaspoon saffron, 0.5 teaspoon rose water, 0.25 cup ghee, 0.75 cup onions, 1.5 tablespoons toasted almond flakes
instructions: 1. Soak almonds & cashews in hot water for 25 mins. Later, drain and add to a blender or grinder. If you want you may peel the almonds. Pour fresh water. Make a smooth, thick & creamy puree. Make korma masala from the recipe notes. 2. Rub chicken with salt, lemon juice and ginger garlic paste. Add chili powder, turmeric, coriander powder and korma masala. Marinate well, cover and keep aside for a minimum of 30 mins to max of 60 mins. 3. Heat ghee or oil in a wide pan and add the chopped onions. Fry them on a medium heat, until transparent for 10 mins. Reduce to medium low, continue to saute until golden & light brown. It takes me another 10 mins. Deglaze with 2 tablespoons water and cook until jammy, for 2 mins. 4. Add the chicken and saute on a medium high heat, for 5 to 6 mins. 5. Lower the heat and stir in the whisked yogurt in 2 batches. Mix and cover the pan. (It’s alright if the yogurt splits.) Cook for 5 to 6 mins. 6. Pour hot water as needed, depending on how much liquid your chicken released. (I use ¾ cup). Cover and cook until the chicken is fully cooked. (Your meat thermometer shoud read 175F / 79 C.). (It takes me another 8 mins) 7. Stir in the nut puree and cook until thick, for another 4 mins. Stir in rest of the korma masala and cook for 3 mins. Taste test and adjust salt. 8. Turn off the heat & stir in the heavy cream & nutmeg. Sprinkle the kewra/rose water & saffron water all over. Keep covered. 9. In a small wide pan, heat ghee and fry the sliced onions until golden and light brown, but not burnt. Remove to a plate, spread to cool down, for 2 mins. 10. Pour the hot ghee all over the shahi korma. Once cooled, separate the fried onions and drizzle them on top. 11. Serve Shahi Korma with Butter Naan, Tandoori Roti, Parathas, pulao or with saffron rice.

title: Shahi Paneer Recipe (Restaurant Style)
ingredients: 12 whole cashew nuts, 1 almonds, 2.5 green cardamoms, 1.5 cup yellow, 1 inch ginger, 4 garlic cloves, 1.5 tomatoes, 1 green chili slit, 0.3333333333333333 cup water, 237.5 grams paneer, 3 tablespoons ghee, 1 bay leaf, 0.5 teaspoon shahi jeera, 2 inch cinnamon piece, 2 cloves 2 cloves, 0.375 teaspoon Kashmiri red chili powder, 0.25 teaspoon turmeric, 0.5 teaspoon salt, 0.875 teaspoon garam masala, 0.5 cup yogurt, 0.875 cup water, 0.1875 teaspoon black pepper, 1 pinch saffron strands, 3 drops Kewra water, 3 tablespoons heavy cream
instructions: 1. On a medium flame, fry cashews, almonds and cardamoms in 1 tablespoon ghee or oil, for 2 to 3 mins (read notes 1). Stir in onions, ginger, garlic and green chili if using. 2. Saute for 5 to 6 mins, deglaze with 3 to 4 tablespoons water and cook further for 3 to 4 mins. Remove the green chili for a low heat option. Cool down and blend with water to a smooth puree/paste. 3. On a medium flame, heat the rest of the ghee in the pan and add bay leaf, jeera, cinnamon and cloves (if using). When they begin to sizzle, add the puree along with chili powder, garam masala, turmeric and salt. 4. Saute for 3 to 4 minutes. Meanwhile whisk yogurt in a bowl and add 1 to 2 tbsps of the sautéed paste. Mix well to a smooth mixture. 5. Reduce the heat to low and incorporate it to the rest of the sautéed paste/sauce in 2 batches, mixing between each addition. 6. Saute on a medium heat for 3 to 4 mins and pour water. Mix well and cook covered until thick, creamy and traces of fats are visible on top. (takes me about 10 mins, depends on the cookware) 7. Optionally, remove the whole spices (except jeera) at this stage and taste test to adjust salt. Add the paneer and black pepper for spice/heat or more garam masala for flavor. Cook for a minute and turn off the heat. Stir in the heavy cream if using. 8. Sprinkle crushed saffron, kewra/ rose water and cover. Rest for a minimum of 5 to 7 minutes. 9. Shahi Paneer goes well with Saffron rice, Turmeric rice, Jeera rice, plain basmati, roti, naan or plain parathas.

title: Shami Kabab (Shami Kebab)
ingredients: 45 grams chicken, 0.3333333333333333 cup chana dal, 1 teaspoon red chili powder, 0.25 teaspoon turmeric, 1.5 inch cinnamon stick, 2.5 green cardamoms, 2 red chilies, 1 inch ginger, 3 garlic cloves, 1 teaspoon roasted cumin, 1 teaspoon garam masala, Water, Salt, 1 onion, 2 eggs, 2.5 tablespoons coriander leaves, 2 tablespoons mint leaves, 1 green chili, Oil
instructions: 1. Wash and soak chana dal for about 2 to 3 hours. Drain the water and add them to a large pot/ Dutch oven along water. 2. Add ginger, garlic, salt, turmeric, red chili powder, whole red chilies, garam masala, cumin, cinnamon stick, green cardamoms and meat. 3. Bring this to a gentle boil, Cover the pot partially and cook until lentils and the meat is completely tender. Add more hot water, only as required as you cook. 4. Once the meat and lentils are tender, evaporate the excess water completely. 5. Cool this completely and discard the whole spices and bones (if any). 6. Add this to a food processor or an onion chopper and grind to a slightly coarse mixture. 7. Add green chilies, onions, mint and coriander leaves. Taste this and adjust salt and heat levels to your preference. 8. Break an egg to a bowl and whisk with a fork to a uniform mixture. Add it to the mixture in batches and mix well to form a non-sticky dough. 9. Divide to 10 equal parts and shape them to balls. Flatten them on your palm, to shape like kebab/patty. Transfer to a tray. You can also freeze these at this stage. 10. Stovetop: Heat oil for shallow frying or pan frying in a wide skillet. Dip one kebab each time in the egg and lift it with a fork. 11. Gently shake off the excess egg and gently place these in the hot oil. Fry on a medium heat until golden, turn them to the other side & fry until crisp. Transfer to a wired rack or to absorbent tissues. 12. Air Fryer: Grease your air fryer basket and preheat it. Air fry the kebabs for 8 to 10 mins at 370 F/ 185 C, until crisp. 13. Bake in Oven: Place the egg washed shami kabab on a prepared tray (with parchment) and bake at 400 F - 200 C for 14 to 16 mins, turning them halfway. 14. Serve chicken shami kabab hot with mint chutney.

title: Sheer Khurma Recipe
ingredients: 4 cups milk, 1 cup thin vermicelli, 0.25 cup sugar, 8 dates, 2 tablespoons chironji seeds, 15 almonds, 15 cashew nuts, 2 pistachios, 3 tablespoons ghee, 0.5 teaspoon cardamom powder, 1 teaspoon edible rose water, 0.375 cup mawa
instructions: 1. Bring 2 cups water to a boil. Pour half of the water to another small bowl. Soak almonds, pistachios and cashews in one bowl. Soak chironji seeds in another bowl. 2. After 30 mins, drain the hot water and pour cold water in both the bowls. 3. Meanwhile chop the dates to bite sized pieces and set aside. 4. Drain the water completely and peel the almonds and pistachios. Discard the skin and soak them in fresh room temperature water for at least 1 to 2 hours. 5. Drain the water from the chironji seeds and transfer them to a clean cloth. Pat them dry and rub gently. The skin begins to peel off. Discard the skin and any brown seeds that seem to be rancid. Keep the seeds in a small bowl and set aside. 6. When the nuts are soaked well after 1 to 2 hours, chop them very finely. Keep them aside. Ensure the nuts are dry before frying them. 7. Fine vermicelli is usually long and not broken. You will need to break them a bit and measure 1 cup (50 grams) and keep aside. 8. Heat 2 tbsps ghee in a heavy bottom pot. Fry cashews and almonds in hot ghee for about 1 to 2 mins. 9. Then add pistachios and chironji seeds. Fry all of them until crisp and golden. This takes about 5 to 6 mins as the nuts are soaked. When done, remove to a plate. 10. To the same pot, add dates and fry until they begin to smell good. Remove to a plate. 11. Pour 1 tbsp more ghee and add the seviyan. Saute on a low to medium heat until golden. Remove to a plate and keep aside. 12. To the same pot, pour 4 cups milk and bring it to a boil on a medium to low heat. 13. When the milk begins to boil, simmer for 5 minutes stirring often. Later add the roasted vermicelli, half of the fried nuts, chironji seeds and dates. 14. Stir and cook until the vermicelli is cooked. You have to keep stirring every few mins to avoid the milk scorching. This took about 10 mins on a medium heat. 15. Add cardamom powder & sugar or condensed milk (optional). If using mawa (optional) add it now. 16. Simmer for a few more minutes until slightly thick yet runny. Sheer khurma will thicken upon cooling so turn off when it is of runny consistency. Add cardamom powder & rose water (optional). 17. Serve warm or chilled. Garnish with fried nuts, chironji seeds and dates.

title: Sheera Recipe | Suji Halwa
ingredients: 1 cup suji, 1 cup sugar, 0.5 cup ghee, 2.5 cup water, 0.5 teaspoon green cardamom powder, 15 cashew nuts, 15 raisins
instructions: 1. Pour 2½ cups water to a pot & begin to heat it. 2. Meanwhile on another burner heat ghee in a pot. When the ghee turns hot, add cashews and fry until light golden. 3. Then add the raisins and fry until they swell. Remove to a plate. 4. Now add rava to the ghee and begin to fry on a medium heat stirring constantly. 5. Fry until the rava turns golden. If you prefer deep roasted suji halwa, then continue to fry for few more minutes. 6. Bring the water to a rolling boil. 7. Pour the hot water to the hot rava pan stirring constantly. Be careful as it splatters. 8. Keep the flame to medium and keep stirring until all the water is absorbed. Sheera is thick at this stage. 9. Cover and cook on a low flame for 1 to 2 mins. 10. Next add the sugar and cardamom powder. Mix well to dissolve the sugar. It dissolves & makes the entire sheera gooey. 11. Within a minute the gooey sheera turns thick again. Keep stirring until it begins to leave the pan. 12. When it cools down fluff up with the spatula. Transfer to serving bowls and garnish with cashews and raisins. 13. You can also press it down in a small bowl and invert on serving plates.

title: Shorba (Biryani gravy)
ingredients: 2 tbsp Oil, 1 onion, 2 medium tomatoes, 2 green chilies, 0.75 tsp ginger garlic paste, 0.5 tsp salt, 0.25 tsp turmeric, 4 tbsp coconut, 3 tbsps mint, 1 tsp red chili powder, 1 tsp biryani masala powder, 1.5 to 2 cups water, 1.5 tbsp curd, 1 bay leaf, 1 strand mace, 3 green cardamoms, 2 petals star anise, 3 cloves 3 cloves ((laung)), 1 inch cinnamon, 0.5 tsp shahi jeera
instructions: 1. Heat 1 tbsp. oil in a pan, add onions and chilies , fry till onions turn slightly golden. 2. Add ginger garlic paste and sauté till you get an aroma. 3. Add tomatoes and salt, fry till they turn completely mushy. 4. Add grated coconut. Fry for 3 to 5 mins without burning the mix. Set this aside to cool. Grind this to a smooth puree with 1 ½ to 2 cups of vegetable stock. You could use ice cold stock to grind the hot mix if you want to cut down the cooling time. Pour the ice cold stock in the jar first and then add the hot mix. 5. Heat the same pan with some more oil, add the dry spices and fry till you get a nice smell for a min.Do not burn the spices. 6. Add pudina / mint, sauté for a min. 7. Pour the ground mix, add red chili powder, masala powder, turmeric. Mix well. Adjust the quantity of water to bring it to the consistency you desire. 8. When the mix begins to boil, add yogurt and simmer for at least 8 to 10 mins. If your gravy becomes too watery, you can increase the heat to high and evaporate the water, so it comes to the desired consistency. 9. If you wish to refrigerate it, for best taste, refrigerate it immediately after it cools down and before 2 hours of cooking. Serve biryani shorba or sherva with hot biryani or pulao.

title: Shrimp Curry Recipe
ingredients: 45 grams Shrimp, 2 tablespoons oil, 1 cup onion, 1 teaspoon ginger grated, 1.5 teaspoon garlic, 1 cup tomatoes, 0.75 teaspoon sea salt, 0.25 teaspoon turmeric, 1.5 teaspoon Kashmiri red chili powder, 1 tablespoon coriander powder, 0.5 teaspoon cumin powder, 4 ml 13 5 oz canned coconut milk, 0.5 cup stock water, 1.5 tablespoon tamarind paste, 1 tablespoon coriander leaves
instructions: 1. Heat 2 tablespoons oil (30 ml) in a pan and add 1 cup fine chopped onions (1 medium, 130 grams). Optionally If you have fresh curry leaves you may add 8 to 10 leaves. 2. Saute till golden, for 6 to 7 mins and add 1 teaspoon grated ginger and 1½ teaspoon pressed/grated garlic. (or alternately use 1½ tsps ginger garlic paste). Saute for 1 to 2 mins, until aromatic. 3. Stir in 4. 1 to 2½ teaspoon Kashmiri red chili powder (I use 2½, or use paprika for low heat) 5. Deglaze with 3 tablespoons water and add 1 cup chopped tomatoes (2 medium, or ½ cup tomato puree). 6. Saute until they become mushy and break down. 7. Pour 400 ml/ 13.5 oz canned coconut milk (1⅔ cups, or use raw grated coconut, read notes in recipe card at the end of the post) 8. If you are using small shrimp or any frozen shrimp, you won’t need any extra liquid. But if you are using jumbo/large size fresh shrimp, stir in ½ cup stock/water with 1½ tablespoon tamarind paste/pulp (or 1 tsp concentrate). Pour it to the pan. 9. Bring to a gentle boil and simmer for 3 mins for the flavors to develop. Taste test to adjust the seasoning. I felt mine needed a bit of sweetener to balance the tamarind so I added a tsp of jaggery powder (optional).

title: Shrimp Pakora (Shrimp Fritters)
ingredients: 3 tablespoons gram flour, 2 tablespoons cornstarch, 1 onion, 1 sprig curry leaves, 1 green chilli, 1 teaspoon ginger garlic paste, 0.375 teaspoon garam masala powder, 0.3333333333333333 teaspoon salt, oil, 1 shrimp, 0.25 teaspoon red chili powder, 0.125 teaspoon turmeric powder, salt
instructions: 1. Peel, devein and rinse the shrimps or prawns very well. Drain water completely. Add red chili powder, half of the salt and turmeric. I also added a bit of lemon juice. 2. Mix well and keep aside for only 10 minutes, no longer as the shrimp can turn mushy. 3. On a medium flame, heat oil in a deep pan to fry. Add ginger garlic paste, green chilies, curry leaves, sliced onions, flour, garam masala and a rest of the salt. Mix gently to coat the prawns well with all the ingredients. If you are using small prawns, omit the onions and fine chop chilies and curry leaves. 4. I forgot to add garam masala at this step so I added later. I did not add water, the moisture from the prawns and lemon juice was sufficient to coat the prawns well. But if your mixture looks dry, just sprinkle a tsp of water and mix. 5. When the oil is hot, turn the flame to medium high. Take each prawn along with some onions and curry leaves batter and drop it to the hot oil. Do not add too many at one time. 6. To Bake: Preheat the oven to 200 C / 400 F. Place the shrimps on a prepared baking tray and spray with oil. Bake them for 10 mins, turning after 6 mins, spray with oil again. Largest ones take about 13 to 14 mins. Keep an eye after 12 mins. They can get overcooked and turn chewy very soon. They are done, when the tails are curled. 7. Prawns tend to get burnt faster, so be attentive. Flip them to the other side and fry till golden. Fry all the prawns in batches. 8. This is an optional step. When you finish frying all the prawn fritters, add them back to hot oil and repeat frying them for a minute, this gives a good crunchy texture to the pakora. Remove to a cooling rack or steel colander. Serve them with a sprinkle of chaat masala or with your favorite sauce or mint chutney. 9. 1 sprig curry leaves or 8 mint leaves finely chopped 10. 1 green chilli fine chopped 11. oil to deep fry 12. Marinate prawns: Devein, peel and clean prawns/ shrimp. Rinse well and drain them completely. Coat them with red chilli powder, turmeric, garam masala and salt. If you want add a tsp of lemon as well. Leave them aside for 10 mins while you prep up the rest. 13. Prep up to fry: Heat oil in a deep pan to deep fry. Coat the prawns with ginger garlic, green chilies, gram flour, cornstarch, more salt and curry leaves. If it looks too dry, add a tsp of water to coat the prawns well with all the ingredients. 14. Test if oil is hot: When the oil is hot enough, to test drop a small portion of the dough to the oil. It should rise steadily and not brown too quickly. This is the right temperature. Your thermometer should read 350 F. 15. Fry shrimp pakora: Regulate the flame to medium high, hold each prawn along with some onions, curry leaves batter and gently slide to the hot oil. Let fry until golden and crisp. You know they are cooked when the tails are curled. 16. Remove them to a cooling rack or steel colander. Fry all the shrimp in batches. If you want refry for a minute, for extra crisp texture. 17. Shrimp fritters are best served hot, they begin to lose the crunch upon cooling. 18. .kadence-column173268_d8ec65-ba > .kt-inside-inner-col,.kadence-column173268_d8ec65-ba >
