ingredients: 1.5 lbs bone-in mutton, 1 tablespoon ghee, 1 teaspoon black pepper, 1 teaspoon cumin seeds, 1 tablespoon coriander seeds, 1 tablespoon fennel seeds, 6 green cardamoms, 1 black cardamom, 6 cloves 6 cloves, 2 inch piece of cinnamon, 1 medium yellow, 4 cloves garlic, 1 inch ginger, 1.5 teaspoon sea salt, 3 cups water, 2 cups aged extra long basmati rice, 3 tablespoons ghee, 1 bay leaf, 1.5 star anise, 0.75 teaspoon shahi jeera, 2 inch cinnamon piece, 6 green cardamoms, 1 tablespoon ginger garlic paste, 3 green chilies, 1 medium red onion, 0.25 cup Greek yogurt, 0.5 teaspoon garam masala, 1 teaspoon fennel powder, 2 tablespoons coriander leaves
instructions: 1. Heat a tablespoon of ghee in the pot or pressure cooker and add the mutton. Sear for 5 mins on a medium high heat. 2. Stir in the whole spices mentioned for yakhni and let them sizzle for a minute. Add salt, onions, ginger & garlic. Pour hot water & mix well. 3. Pressure cooker: Cover with a lid and pressure cook for 3 whistles on a medium heat or in the instant pot for 8 to 10 mins. When the pressure drops, open the lid, it should be fully cooked. 4. Cook in a pot: Bring to a rolling boil and skim off the froth. Cook until the meat is tender. It should be cooked fully but not overcooked. 5. Remove the mutton and reserve aside in a bowl & cover. Strain the yakhni and press down with a spoon to extract the flavors. Discard the spices, onions etc in the strainer. Measure the yakhni and add more water to make it 4 cups (945 ml). (read notes) 6. When you are ready to make the pulao, rinse rice thrice and soak in 4 cups of water for only 30 mins. Later drain to a colander. 7. Heat a wide pot with ghee and add the sliced onions. Saute for 8 to 9 mins on a medium heat, until deep golden. 8. Stir in the whole spices, followed by ginger garlic paste & green chilies. Saute for 1 to 2 mins and add the mutton. Saute the mutton for 2 to 3 mins. 9. Stir in the garam masala, fennel powder, a generous pinch of salt and yogurt. Cook until the moisture evaporates. Mix in the yakhni and taste test to adjust salt. It should taste slightly salty. 10. Bring to a rolling boil and add the drained rice. Mix and cook uncovered on a medium high heat for 7 mins. (should be boiling rapidly) 11. Cover & dum cook on a low heat for 10 to 12 mins, until all the water is absorbed. 12. Rest for 15 mins before uncovering. Garnish with fine chopped coriander leaves & fried onions. Serve yakhni pulao hot with mirchi ka salan, raita and vegetable salad.

title: Yogurt Sandwich Recipe (Hung Curd Sandwich)
ingredients: 4 slices bread, 0.29166666666666663 cup Greek Yogurt, 0.25 teaspoon Salt, 1 garlic clove, 0.375 teaspoon black pepper, 0.25 teaspoon mustard, 0.5 teaspoon red chili flakes, 1 green chili, 2 tablespoons herbs, 1.25 tablespoon butter, 1 teaspoon extra virgin olive oil, 0.25 cup carrots, 2.5 tablespoons onion, 0.25 tablespoons bell peppers
instructions: 1. To make 2 sandwiches, you will need ¼ to ⅓ cup Greek Yogurt (hung curd), ¼ to ½ teaspoon black pepper, ¼ teaspoon Salt and ¼ teaspoon ground mustard (optional or organic mustard sauce). 2. You will need half cup grated & chopped veggies or for convenience use store bought organic coleslaw mix. Here is what I use 3. 2 to 3 tablespoons fine chopped onion (or ¼ cup blanched, drained & shredded cabbage) 4. ¼ cup bell peppers fine chopped (or boiled sweet corn kernels) 5. 2 tablespoons chopped coriander or parsley 6. 1 green chilli optional chopped 7. Add mustard, salt, pepper, red chili flakes (optional). 8. Optional If you want you may add some healthy cold pressed organic oil of choice. I used 1 tablespoon extra virgin cold pressed olive oil. 9. Mix and taste test. If you want add more yogurt or more veggies and spices to taste. 10. Butter your bread slices or drizzle some ghee and toast them on a griddle. If you want to make these in air fryer or sandwich toasted, read my instructions in the recipe card below. You can also skip this toasting step and make the sandwich with fresh bread. 11. Turn to the other side and toast until golden. Turn off the heat. 12. Let the bread cool down a bit. Then spread the yogurt filling you made. 13. Cover with another slice and serve right away or with in 2 hours. Yogurt sandwich tends to become sour if you leave it at room temperature for too long and this can also grow bacteria. Slice the Hung curd sandwich and serve.

title: Zucchini Stir Fry Recipe
ingredients: 1.5 cups zucchini, 1 onion, 0.5 cup bell peppers, 0.75 tablespoon garlic, 0.5 teaspoon ginger, 1.25 tablespoons oil, 1 teaspoon soya sauce, 1 teaspoon vinegar, 0.75 teaspoon black pepper, 1 teaspoon toasted sesame seeds
instructions: 1. Rinse all the veggies. Slice onions and bell peppers thin. Chop zucchini to ¼ inch thickness. Chop ginger and garlic. 2. Pour oil to a hot pan. Stir fry ginger and garlic for 30 seconds. 3. Add onions and fry until transparent. Then stir fry bell peppers for 30 seconds. 4. Next add zucchini and stir fry on a high heat until half done yet crunchy. Do not over cook as they tend to turn too soft. 5. Then pour soya sauce and vinegar. Add black pepper /steak seasoning/garam masala. Stir fry just for 30 to 60 seconds. 6. Turn off the heat and sprinkle toasted sesame seeds or roasted & crushed peanuts. Taste test and sprinkle salt if you want. 7. Serve zucchini stir fry as a side or in your wraps and sandwiches.
